/app/src/ast.rs:
    1|       |//! Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) for C11 compiler.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the core AST data structures and APIs for representing
    4|       |//! C11 programs. The AST is designed as a flattened storage system for efficiency,
    5|       |//! with index-based references to child nodes and types.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! ## Architecture
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! The AST is organized into focused submodules for better maintainability:
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! - [`nodes`]: Node definitions, constructors, and builder patterns for AST nodes
   12|       |//! - [`types`]: Type system representation and utilities for semantic analysis
   13|       |//!
   14|       |//! ## Key Features
   15|       |//!
   16|       |//! - **Flattened Storage**: All AST nodes are stored in contiguous vectors with index-based references
   17|       |//! - **Interior Mutability**: Uses `Cell` for type annotations without requiring mutable AST access
   18|       |//! - **Builder Patterns**: Ergonomic constructors for complex AST nodes
   19|       |//! - **Type System**: Canonical types distinct from syntactic type specifiers
   20|       |//!
   21|       |
   22|       |use std::num::NonZeroU32;
   23|       |
   24|       |/// Represents an interned string using symbol_table crate.
   25|       |/// Alias for GlobalSymbol from symbol_table crate with global feature.
   26|       |pub type NameId = symbol_table::GlobalSymbol;
   27|       |pub type StringId = NameId;
   28|       |
   29|       |use crate::semantic::{QualType, ScopeId, SemanticInfo, SymbolRef, TypeRef, ValueCategory};
   30|       |pub use crate::source_manager::{SourceId, SourceSpan};
   31|       |
   32|       |// Submodules
   33|       |// Submodules
   34|       |pub mod dumper;
   35|       |pub mod literal;
   36|       |pub mod literal_parsing;
   37|       |pub mod nodes;
   38|       |pub mod parsed;
   39|       |pub mod parsed_types;
   40|       |
   41|       |// Re-export commonly used items for convenience
   42|       |pub use nodes::*;
   43|       |pub use parsed::*;
   44|       |pub use parsed_types::*;
   45|       |
   46|       |// Re-export operators that are used throughout the codebase
   47|       |pub use nodes::{BinaryOp, UnaryOp};
   48|       |
   49|       |/// The flattened AST storage.
   50|       |/// Contains all AST nodes, types, symbol entries in contiguous vectors.
   51|       |#[derive(Clone, Default)]
   52|       |pub struct Ast {
   53|       |    pub kinds: Vec<NodeKind>,
   54|       |    pub spans: Vec<SourceSpan>,
   55|       |    pub semantic_info: Option<SemanticInfo>, // Populated after type resolution
   56|       |}
   57|       |
   58|       |impl Ast {
   59|       |    /// Create a new empty AST
   60|    780|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
   61|    780|        Ast::default()
   62|    780|    }
   63|       |
   64|       |    /// Add a node to the AST and return its reference
   65|  9.33k|    pub(crate) fn push_node(&mut self, kind: NodeKind, span: SourceSpan) -> NodeRef {
   66|  9.33k|        let index = self.kinds.len() as u32 + 1; // Start from 1 for NonZeroU32
   67|  9.33k|        self.kinds.push(kind);
   68|  9.33k|        self.spans.push(span);
   69|  9.33k|        NodeRef::new(index).expect("NodeRef overflow")
   70|  9.33k|    }
   71|       |
   72|       |    /// Add a dummy node to the AST and return its reference
   73|  9.09k|    pub(crate) fn push_dummy(&mut self, span: SourceSpan) -> NodeRef {
   74|  9.09k|        self.push_node(NodeKind::Dummy, span)
   75|  9.09k|    }
   76|       |
   77|       |    /// Get node kind by reference
   78|  38.6k|    pub(crate) fn get_kind(&self, node_ref: NodeRef) -> &NodeKind {
   79|  38.6k|        &self.kinds[node_ref.index()]
   80|  38.6k|    }
   81|       |
   82|       |    /// Get node span by reference
   83|  1.55k|    pub(crate) fn get_span(&self, node_ref: NodeRef) -> SourceSpan {
   84|  1.55k|        self.spans[node_ref.index()]
   85|  1.55k|    }
   86|       |
   87|       |    /// get root node ref
   88|    956|    pub(crate) fn get_root(&self) -> NodeRef {
   89|    956|        NodeRef::ROOT
   90|    956|    }
   91|       |
   92|  1.13k|    pub(crate) fn scope_of(&self, node_ref: NodeRef) -> ScopeId {
   93|  1.13k|        match &self.kinds[node_ref.index()] {
   94|    360|            NodeKind::TranslationUnit(data) => data.scope_id,
   95|    367|            NodeKind::Function(data) => data.scope_id,
   96|      1|            NodeKind::FunctionDecl(data) => data.scope_id,
   97|    397|            NodeKind::CompoundStatement(data) => data.scope_id,
   98|      8|            NodeKind::For(data) => data.scope_id,
   99|      0|            _ => unreachable!("ICE: Node {:?} does not have a scope", self.get_kind(node_ref)),
  100|       |        }
  101|  1.13k|    }
  102|       |

  105|    423|        self.semantic_info = Some(semantic_info);

  112|       |

  117|  19.0k|        NonZeroU32::new(value).map(Self)
  118|  19.0k|    }

  121|  84.8k|        self.0.get()
  122|  84.8k|    }

  125|  64.6k|        (self.get() - 1) as usize
  126|  64.6k|    }

  128|       |    /// Create an iterator over a range of consecutive NodeRefs, starting from `self`.
  129|       |    #[inline]
  130|  6.33k|    pub(crate) fn range(self, len: impl Into<u32>) -> NodeRefRange {
  131|  6.33k|        NodeRefRange {
  132|  6.33k|            current: self.get(),
  133|  6.33k|            end: self.get() + len.into(),
  134|  6.33k|        }
  135|  6.33k|    }
  136|       |}
  137|       |
  138|       |/// An iterator over a contiguous range of `NodeRef`s.
  139|       |#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
  140|       |pub struct NodeRefRange {
  141|       |    current: u32,
  142|       |    end: u32,
  143|       |}
  144|       |
  145|       |impl Iterator for NodeRefRange {
  146|       |    type Item = NodeRef;
  147|       |
  148|       |    #[inline]
  149|  15.8k|    fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {
  150|  15.8k|        if self.current < self.end {
  151|  9.53k|            let node = NodeRef::new(self.current).expect("NodeRef overflow in range");
  152|  9.53k|            self.current += 1;
  153|  9.53k|            Some(node)
  154|       |        } else {
  155|  6.33k|            None
  156|       |        }
  157|  15.8k|    }
  158|       |
  159|       |    #[inline]
  160|     22|    fn size_hint(&self) -> (usize, Option<usize>) {
  161|     22|        let len = (self.end - self.current) as usize;
  162|     22|        (len, Some(len))
  163|     22|    }
  164|       |}
  165|       |
  166|       |impl ExactSizeIterator for NodeRefRange {}
  167|       |

  172|  3.85k|    }

                                   ^0

   48|       |pub struct TypeRegistryDisplay<'a> {
   49|       |    pub ast: &'a Ast,
   50|       |    pub registry: &'a TypeRegistry,
   51|       |}
   52|       |
   53|       |impl<'a> fmt::Display for TypeRegistryDisplay<'a> {
   54|      3|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   55|       |        // Collect all TypeRefs used in the AST
   56|      3|        let mut used_type_refs = HashSet::new();
   57|       |
   58|     53|        for kind in &self.ast.kinds {
                          ^50
   59|     50|            AstDumper::collect_type_refs_from_node(kind, &mut used_type_refs);
   60|     50|        }
   61|       |
   62|      3|        if used_type_refs.is_empty() {
   63|      0|            return Ok(());
   64|      3|        }
   65|       |
   66|       |        // Print header
   67|      3|        writeln!(f, "\n=== TypeRegistry (Used TypeRefs) ===")?;
                                                                           ^0
   68|       |
   69|       |        // Sort TypeRefs for consistent output
   70|      3|        let mut sorted_type_refs: Vec<_> = used_type_refs.into_iter().collect();
   71|     48|        sorted_type_refs.sort_by_key(|ty_ref| ty_ref.get());
                      ^3               ^3
   72|       |
   73|       |        // Dump each used TypeRef with user-friendly formatting
   74|     18|        for ty_ref in sorted_type_refs {
                          ^15
   75|     15|            let formatted_type = self.registry.display_type(ty_ref);
   76|     15|            writeln!(f, "TypeRef({}): {}", ty_ref.base(), formatted_type)?;
                                                                                       ^0
   77|       |        }
   78|      3|        Ok(())
   79|      3|    }
   80|       |}

  101|       |    /// Collect TypeRefs from a NodeKind
  102|     50|    fn collect_type_refs_from_node(kind: &NodeKind, type_refs: &mut HashSet<TypeRef>) {
  103|     50|        match kind {
  104|      0|            NodeKind::Designator(_) => {}
  105|      3|            NodeKind::Function(data) => {
  106|      3|                type_refs.insert(data.ty);
  107|      3|            }
  108|      1|            NodeKind::Param(data) => {
  109|      1|                type_refs.insert(data.ty.ty());
  110|      1|            }
  111|      1|            NodeKind::FunctionDecl(func_decl) => {
  112|      1|                type_refs.insert(func_decl.ty);
  113|      1|            }
  114|      1|            NodeKind::RecordDecl(record_decl) => {
  115|      1|                type_refs.insert(record_decl.ty);
  116|      1|            }
  117|      2|            NodeKind::FieldDecl(field_decl) => {
  118|      2|                type_refs.insert(field_decl.ty.ty());
  119|      2|            }
  120|      1|            NodeKind::EnumDecl(enum_decl) => {
  121|      1|                type_refs.insert(enum_decl.ty);
  122|      1|            }
  123|      3|            NodeKind::EnumMember(_) => {}
  124|       |
  125|       |            // QualType usage (contains TypeRef)
  126|      1|            NodeKind::Cast(qual_type, _) => {
  127|      1|                type_refs.insert(qual_type.ty());
  128|      1|            }
  129|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinTypesCompatibleP(t1, t2) => {
  130|      0|                type_refs.insert(t1.ty());

  132|      0|            }

  228|       |    /// Format a DesignatedInitializer for display

  234|      2|                    Designator::FieldName(name) => {
  235|      2|                        result.push_str(&format!(".{}", name));
  236|      2|                    }
  237|      2|                    Designator::ArrayIndex(index) => {
  238|      2|                        result.push_str(&format!("[{}]", index.get()));
  239|      2|                    }
  240|      0|                    Designator::GnuArrayRange(start, end) => {
  241|      0|                        result.push_str(&format!("[{} ... {}]", start.get(), end.get()));
  242|      0|                    }
  243|       |                },
  244|      0|                _ => result.push_str("<invalid designator>"),
  245|       |            }
  246|       |        }
  247|       |
  248|      5|        if init.designator_len > 0 {
  249|      4|            result.push_str(" = ");
  250|      4|        }
                      ^1
  251|       |
  252|      5|        result.push_str(&init.initializer.get().to_string());
  253|      5|        result
  254|      5|    }

  256|       |    /// Dump a single parsed parsed node kind
  257|    283|    fn dump_parsed_node_kind(f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>, kind: &ParsedNodeKind, ast: &ParsedAst) -> fmt::Result {
  258|    283|        match kind {
  259|     59|            ParsedNodeKind::Literal(literal) => match literal {
  260|     44|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Int { val, suffix, base } => {
  261|     44|                    writeln!(f, "LiteralInt({:?}, {:?}, base={})", val, suffix, base)
  262|       |                }
  263|      3|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Float { val, suffix } => {
  264|      3|                    writeln!(f, "LiteralFloat({}, {:?})", val, suffix)
  265|       |                }
  266|      9|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::String(s) => writeln!(f, "LiteralString(\"{}\")", s),
  267|      3|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Char(c) => writeln!(
  268|      3|                    f,
  269|      3|                    "LiteralChar('{}')",
  270|      3|                    char::from_u32(*c as u32).unwrap_or(char::REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER)
  271|       |                ),
  272|       |            },
  273|     34|            ParsedNodeKind::Ident(name) => writeln!(f, "Ident({})", name),
  274|       |
  275|       |            // Expressions
  276|      3|            ParsedNodeKind::UnaryOp(op, node) => writeln!(f, "UnaryOp({:?}, {})", op, node.get()),
  277|     11|            ParsedNodeKind::BinaryOp(op, l, r) => {
  278|     11|                writeln!(f, "BinaryOp({:?}, {}, {})", op, l.get(), r.get())
  279|       |            }
  280|      1|            ParsedNodeKind::TernaryOp(c, t, e) => {
  281|      1|                writeln!(f, "TernaryOp({}, {}, {})", c.get(), t.get(), e.get())
  282|       |            }
  283|      1|            ParsedNodeKind::GnuStatementExpression(stmt, expr) => {
  284|      1|                writeln!(f, "GnuStatementExpression({}, {})", stmt.get(), expr.get())
  285|       |            }

                                  ^23         ^23          ^23                          ^23                 ^23  ^23

  414|       |        }
  415|    283|    }
  416|       |
  417|       |    /// Dump a single AST node kind
  418|    212|    fn dump_parser_kind(
  419|    212|        f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>,
  420|    212|        kind: &NodeKind,
  421|    212|        ast: &Ast,
  422|    212|        symbol_table: Option<&SymbolTable>,
  423|    212|    ) -> fmt::Result {
  424|    212|        match kind {
  425|     15|            NodeKind::TranslationUnit(tu_data) => {
  426|     15|                let start = tu_data.decl_start.get();
  427|     15|                if tu_data.decl_len > 0 {
  428|     15|                    let last = start + tu_data.decl_len as u32 - 1;
  429|     15|                    writeln!(f, "TranslationUnit(decls={}..{}) (parser kind)", start, last)
  430|       |                } else {
  431|      0|                    writeln!(f, "TranslationUnit(decls=[]) (parser kind)")
  432|       |                }
  433|       |            }
  434|     47|            NodeKind::Literal(literal) => match literal {
  435|     30|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Int { val, suffix, base } => {
  436|     30|                    writeln!(f, "LiteralInt({:?}, {:?}, base={})", val, suffix, base)
  437|       |                }
  438|      1|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Float { val, suffix } => {
  439|      1|                    writeln!(f, "LiteralFloat({}, {:?})", val, suffix)
  440|       |                }
  441|     16|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::String(s) => writeln!(f, "LiteralString({})", s),
  442|      0|                crate::ast::literal::Literal::Char(c) => writeln!(
  443|      0|                    f,
  444|      0|                    "LiteralChar('{}')",
  445|      0|                    char::from_u32(*c as u32).unwrap_or(char::REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER)
  446|       |                ),
  447|       |            },
  448|     20|            NodeKind::Ident(sym, _) => writeln!(f, "Ident({})", sym),
  449|      1|            NodeKind::UnaryOp(op, operand) => writeln!(f, "UnaryOp({:?}, {})", op, operand.get()),
  450|      7|            NodeKind::BinaryOp(op, left, right) => {
  451|      7|                writeln!(f, "BinaryOp({:?}, {}, {})", op, left.get(), right.get())
  452|       |            }
  453|      1|            NodeKind::TernaryOp(cond, then, else_) => {
  454|      1|                writeln!(f, "TernaryOp({}, {}, {})", cond.get(), then.get(), else_.get())
  455|       |            }
  456|      1|            NodeKind::PostIncrement(expr) => writeln!(f, "PostIncrement({})", expr.get()),
  457|      0|            NodeKind::PostDecrement(expr) => writeln!(f, "PostDecrement({})", expr.get()),
  458|      3|            NodeKind::Assignment(op, lhs, rhs) => {
  459|      3|                writeln!(f, "Assignment({:?}, {}, {})", op, lhs.get(), rhs.get())
  460|       |            }
  461|      1|            NodeKind::FunctionCall(call_expr) => {
  462|      1|                let start = call_expr.arg_start.get();
  463|      1|                if call_expr.arg_len > 0 {
  464|      1|                    let last = start + call_expr.arg_len as u32 - 1;
  465|      1|                    writeln!(
  466|      1|                        f,
  467|      1|                        "FunctionCall(callee={}, args={}..{})",
  468|      1|                        call_expr.callee.get(),
  469|       |                        start,
  470|       |                        last
  471|       |                    )
  472|       |                } else {
  473|      0|                    writeln!(f, "FunctionCall(callee={}, args=[])", call_expr.callee.get())
  474|       |                }
  475|       |            }
  476|       |
  477|      3|            NodeKind::MemberAccess(obj, field, is_arrow) => writeln!(
  478|      3|                f,
  479|      3|                "MemberAccess({}, {}, {})",
  480|      3|                obj.get(),
  481|       |                field,
  482|      3|                if *is_arrow { "->" } else { "." }
                                             ^1            ^2
  483|       |            ),
  484|      1|            NodeKind::IndexAccess(array, index) => {
  485|      1|                writeln!(f, "IndexAccess({}, {})", array.get(), index.get())
  486|       |            }
  487|      1|            NodeKind::Cast(ty, expr) => writeln!(f, "Cast({}, {})", ty, expr.get()),
  488|      1|            NodeKind::SizeOfExpr(expr) => writeln!(f, "SizeOfExpr({})", expr.get()),
  489|      1|            NodeKind::SizeOfType(ty) => writeln!(f, "SizeOfType({})", ty),
  490|      1|            NodeKind::AlignOf(ty) => writeln!(f, "AlignOf({})", ty),
  491|      1|            NodeKind::CompoundLiteral(ty, init) => {
  492|      1|                writeln!(f, "CompoundLiteral({}, {})", ty, init.get())
  493|       |            }
  494|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinVaArg(ty, expr) => {
  495|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinVaArg({}, {})", ty, expr.get())
  496|       |            }
  497|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinVaStart(ap, last) => {
  498|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinVaStart({}, {})", ap.get(), last.get())
  499|       |            }
  500|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinVaEnd(ap) => {
  501|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinVaEnd({})", ap.get())
  502|       |            }
  503|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinVaCopy(dst, src) => {
  504|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinVaCopy({}, {})", dst.get(), src.get())
  505|       |            }
  506|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinExpect(exp, c) => {
  507|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinExpect({}, {})", exp.get(), c.get())
  508|       |            }
  509|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinPopcount(exp) => {
  510|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinPopcount({})", exp.get())
  511|       |            }
  512|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinClz(exp) => {
  513|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinClz({})", exp.get())
  514|       |            }
  515|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinCtz(exp) => {
  516|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinCtz({})", exp.get())
  517|       |            }
  518|      1|            NodeKind::BuiltinOffsetof(ty, expr) => {
  519|      1|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinOffsetof({}, {})", ty, expr.get())
  520|       |            }
  521|      0|            NodeKind::BuiltinTypesCompatibleP(t1, t2) => {
  522|      0|                writeln!(f, "BuiltinTypesCompatibleP({}, {})", t1, t2)
  523|       |            }
  524|      0|            NodeKind::AtomicOp(op, args_start, args_len) => {
  525|      0|                let start = args_start.get();
  526|      0|                if *args_len > 0 {
  527|      0|                    let last = start + *args_len as u32 - 1;
  528|      0|                    writeln!(f, "AtomicOp({:?}, args={}..{})", op, start, last)
  529|       |                } else {
  530|      0|                    writeln!(f, "AtomicOp({:?}, args=[])", op)
  531|       |                }
  532|       |            }
  533|       |
  534|      1|            NodeKind::GenericSelection(gs) => {
  535|      1|                let start = gs.assoc_start.get();
  536|      1|                if gs.assoc_len > 0 {
  537|      1|                    let last = start + gs.assoc_len as u32 - 1;
  538|      1|                    writeln!(

  540|      1|                        "GenericSelection(control={}, associations={}..{})",
  541|      1|                        gs.control.get(),
  542|       |                        start,
  543|       |                        last
  544|       |                    )
  545|       |                } else {
  546|      0|                    writeln!(f, "GenericSelection(control={}, associations=[])", gs.control.get())
  547|       |                }
  548|       |            }
  549|      2|            NodeKind::GenericAssociation(ga) => {
  550|      2|                writeln!(
  551|      2|                    f,
  552|      2|                    "GenericAssociation(ty={:?}, result_expr={})",
  553|       |                    ga.ty,
  554|      2|                    ga.result_expr.get()
  555|       |                )
  556|       |            }
  557|      1|            NodeKind::GnuStatementExpression(compound_stmt, result_expr) => {
  558|      1|                writeln!(
  559|      1|                    f,
  560|      1|                    "GnuStatementExpression({}, {})",
  561|      1|                    compound_stmt.get(),
  562|      1|                    result_expr.get()
  563|       |                )
  564|       |            }
  565|     22|            NodeKind::CompoundStatement(cs) => {
  566|     22|                let start = cs.stmt_start.get();
  567|     22|                if cs.stmt_len > 0 {
  568|     18|                    let last = start + cs.stmt_len as u32 - 1;
  569|     18|                    writeln!(f, "CompoundStatement(stmts={}..{})", start, last)
  570|       |                } else {
  571|      4|                    writeln!(f, "CompoundStatement(stmts=[])")
  572|       |                }
  573|       |            }
  574|      1|            NodeKind::If(if_stmt) => writeln!(
  575|      1|                f,
  576|      1|                "If(condition={}, then={}, else={})",
  577|      1|                if_stmt.condition.get(),
  578|      1|                if_stmt.then_branch.get(),
  579|      1|                if_stmt
  580|      1|                    .else_branch
  581|      1|                    .map(|r| r.get().to_string())
                                           ^0      ^0
  582|      1|                    .unwrap_or("none".to_string())
  583|       |            ),
  584|      1|            NodeKind::While(while_stmt) => writeln!(
  585|      1|                f,

  587|      1|                while_stmt.condition.get(),
  588|      1|                while_stmt.body.get()
  589|       |            ),
  590|      1|            NodeKind::DoWhile(body, cond) => {

  592|       |            }
  593|      1|            NodeKind::For(for_stmt) => writeln!(
  594|      1|                f,
  595|      1|                "For(init={}, condition={}, increment={}, body={})",
  596|      1|                for_stmt.init.map(|r| r.get().to_string()).unwrap_or("none".to_string()),
                                                    ^0      ^0
  597|      1|                for_stmt
  598|      1|                    .condition
  599|      1|                    .map(|r| r.get().to_string())
                                           ^0      ^0
  600|      1|                    .unwrap_or("none".to_string()),
  601|      1|                for_stmt
  602|      1|                    .increment
  603|      1|                    .map(|r| r.get().to_string())
                                           ^0      ^0
  604|      1|                    .unwrap_or("none".to_string()),
  605|      1|                for_stmt.body.get()
  606|       |            ),
  607|      4|            NodeKind::Return(expr) => writeln!(
  608|      4|                f,
  609|      4|                "Return({})",
  610|      4|                expr.map(|r| r.get().to_string()).unwrap_or("void".to_string())
  611|       |            ),
  612|      2|            NodeKind::Break => writeln!(f, "Break"),
  613|      1|            NodeKind::Continue => writeln!(f, "Continue"),
  614|      1|            NodeKind::Goto(label, _) => writeln!(f, "Goto({})", label),
  615|      1|            NodeKind::Label(label, stmt, _) => writeln!(f, "Label({}, {})", label, stmt.get()),
  616|      2|            NodeKind::Switch(cond, body) => {
  617|      2|                writeln!(f, "Switch(condition={}, body={})", cond.get(), body.get())
  618|       |            }
  619|      1|            NodeKind::Case(expr, stmt) => writeln!(f, "Case({}, {})", expr.get(), stmt.get()),
  620|      1|            NodeKind::CaseRange(start, end, stmt) => {
  621|      1|                writeln!(f, "CaseRange({}, {}, {})", start.get(), end.get(), stmt.get())
  622|       |            }
  623|      1|            NodeKind::Default(stmt) => writeln!(f, "Default({})", stmt.get()),
  624|      6|            NodeKind::ExpressionStatement(expr) => writeln!(
  625|      6|                f,
  626|      6|                "ExpressionStatement({})",
  627|      6|                expr.map(|r| r.get().to_string()).unwrap_or("none".to_string())
  628|       |            ),
  629|       |
  630|       |            // Declaration and FunctionDef removed
  631|     15|            NodeKind::Function(data) => {
  632|     15|                let func_name = Self::get_function_name(data.symbol, symbol_table);
  633|     15|                let start = data.param_start.get();
  634|     15|                if data.param_len > 0 {
  635|      1|                    let last = start + data.param_len as u32 - 1;
  636|      1|                    writeln!(
  637|      1|                        f,
  638|      1|                        "Function(name={}, symbol={:?}, ty={}, params={}..{}, body={})",
  639|       |                        func_name,
  640|       |                        data.symbol,
  641|       |                        data.ty,
  642|       |                        start,
  643|       |                        last,
  644|      1|                        data.body.get()
  645|       |                    )
  646|       |                } else {
  647|     14|                    writeln!(
  648|     14|                        f,
  649|     14|                        "Function(name={}, symbol={:?}, ty={}, params=[], body={})",
  650|       |                        func_name,
  651|       |                        data.symbol,
  652|       |                        data.ty,
  653|     14|                        data.body.get()
  654|       |                    )
  655|       |                }
  656|       |            }
  657|      2|            NodeKind::Param(data) => {
  658|      2|                writeln!(f, "Param(symbol={:?}, ty={:?})", data.symbol, data.ty)
  659|       |            }
  660|      0|            NodeKind::EnumConstant(name, value) => writeln!(
  661|      0|                f,
  662|      0|                "EnumConstant({}, {})",
  663|       |                name,
  664|      0|                value.map(|r| r.get().to_string()).unwrap_or("auto".to_string())
  665|       |            ),
  666|      1|            NodeKind::StaticAssert(cond, msg) => {
  667|      1|                let message_str = if let NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::String(s)) = ast.get_kind(*msg) {
  668|      1|                    s.to_string()
  669|       |                } else {
  670|      0|                    "<invalid>".to_string()
  671|       |                };
  672|      1|                writeln!(f, "StaticAssert(condition={}, message=\"{}\")", cond.get(), message_str)
  673|       |            }
  674|     17|            NodeKind::VarDecl(var_decl) => {
  675|     17|                writeln!(
  676|     17|                    f,
  677|     17|                    "VarDecl(name={}, ty={}, storage={:?})",
  678|       |                    var_decl.name, var_decl.ty, var_decl.storage
  679|       |                )
  680|       |            }
  681|      0|            NodeKind::FunctionDecl(func_decl) => {
  682|      0|                writeln!(
  683|      0|                    f,
  684|      0|                    "FunctionDecl(name={}, ty={}, storage={:?})",
  685|       |                    func_decl.name,
  686|      0|                    func_decl.ty.get(),
  687|       |                    func_decl.storage
  688|       |                )

   57|       |/// Parse C11 floating-point literal syntax
   58|     78|pub(crate) fn parse_c11_float_literal(text: &str) -> Option<(f64, Option<FloatSuffix>)> {
   59|     78|    if text.is_empty() {

   61|     77|    }

                                     ^3

   69|       |    };

   76|     65|        text_without_suffix.parse::<f64>().ok().map(|val| (val, suffix))

   80|       |/// Parse hexadecimal floating-point literal (C99/C11)
   81|       |/// invariant: first 2 character must be 0x or 0X
   82|     12|fn parse_hex_float_literal(text: &str) -> Option<f64> {
   83|       |    // Format: 0[xX][hexdigits][.hexdigits][pP[+|-]digits][fFlL]
   84|     12|    let mut chars = text.chars().peekable();
   85|       |
   86|       |    // Skip "0x"
   87|     12|    chars.next(); // '0'
   88|     12|    chars.next(); // 'x' or 'X'
   89|       |
   90|     12|    let mut result = 0.0f64;
   91|     12|    let mut exponent = 0i32;
   92|     12|    let mut has_dot = false;
   93|     12|    let mut fraction_digits = 0;
   94|     12|    let mut has_digits = false;
   95|       |
   96|       |    // Parse significand
   97|     29|    while let Some(&c) = chars.peek() {
                                  ^27
   98|     27|        if let Some(digit) = c.to_digit(16) {
                                  ^11
   99|     11|            result = result * 16.0 + digit as f64;
  100|     11|            if has_dot {
  101|      3|                fraction_digits += 1;
  102|      8|            }
  103|     11|            has_digits = true;
  104|     11|            chars.next();
  105|     16|        } else if c == '.' {
  106|      7|            if has_dot {
  107|      1|                return None;
  108|      6|            } // Double dot
  109|      6|            has_dot = true;
  110|      6|            chars.next();
  111|      9|        } else if c == 'p' || c == 'P' {
                                            ^1
  112|      8|            break;
  113|       |        } else {
  114|      1|            return None; // Invalid character
  115|       |        }
  116|       |    }
  117|       |
  118|     10|    if !has_digits {
  119|      3|        return None;
  120|      7|    }
  121|       |
  122|       |    // Parse exponent
  123|      7|    if let Some('p' | 'P') = chars.peek() {
  124|      7|        chars.next();
  125|      7|        let mut exp_sign = 1;
  126|      7|        if let Some(&c) = chars.peek() {
                                   ^5
  127|      5|            if c == '-' {
  128|      1|                exp_sign = -1;
  129|      1|                chars.next();
  130|      4|            } else if c == '+' {
  131|      2|                chars.next();
  132|      2|            }
  133|      2|        }
  134|       |
  135|      7|        let mut exp_val = 0i32;
  136|      7|        let mut has_exp_digits = false;
  137|     10|        while let Some(&c) = chars.peek() {
                                      ^4
  138|      4|            if let Some(d) = c.to_digit(10) {
                                      ^3
  139|      3|                exp_val = exp_val.checked_mul(10)?.checked_add(d as i32)?;
                                                               ^0                     ^0
  140|      3|                has_exp_digits = true;
  141|      3|                chars.next();
  142|       |            } else {
  143|      1|                break;
  144|       |            }
  145|       |        }
  146|      7|        if !has_exp_digits {
  147|      4|            return None;
  148|      3|        }
  149|      3|        exponent = exp_val * exp_sign;
  150|      0|    }
  151|       |
  152|       |    // Apply adjustments
  153|      3|    if fraction_digits > 0 {
  154|      2|        result /= 16.0f64.powi(fraction_digits);
  155|      2|    }
                  ^1
  156|      3|    if exponent != 0 {
  157|      2|        result *= 2.0f64.powi(exponent);
  158|      2|    }
                  ^1
  159|       |
  160|      3|    Some(result)
  161|     12|}

  185|    103|fn parse_escape_sequence(chars: &mut Peekable<Chars>, result: &mut String) {
  186|    103|    match chars.peek() {

  196|      2|            chars.next();
  197|      2|            result.push('\r');
  198|      2|        }

  200|      1|            chars.next();

  232|      6|        Some('u') | Some('U') => parse_ucn_escape(chars, result),
  233|     13|        Some(c) if c.is_digit(8) => parse_octal_escape(chars, result),

  236|      1|            result.push(*c);
  237|      1|            chars.next();

  239|      1|        None => {
  240|      1|            result.push('\\');
  241|      1|        }
  242|       |    }

  245|     11|fn parse_hex_escape(chars: &mut Peekable<Chars>, result: &mut String) {
  246|     11|    chars.next(); // consume 'x'
  247|     11|    let mut val: u64 = 0;
  248|     11|    let mut has_digits = false;
  249|       |
  250|     30|    while let Some(&ch) = chars.peek() {
                                  ^23
  251|     23|        if let Some(digit) = ch.to_digit(16) {
                                  ^19
  252|     19|            val = val.saturating_mul(16).saturating_add(digit as u64);
  253|     19|            has_digits = true;
  254|     19|            chars.next();
  255|     19|        } else {
  256|      4|            break;
  257|       |        }
  258|       |    }
  259|       |
  260|     11|    if has_digits {
  261|     10|        let char_val = if val > 0x10FFFF { 0xFFFD } else { val as u32 };
                                                         ^0
  262|     10|        result.push(char::from_u32(char_val).unwrap_or(char::REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER));
  263|      1|    } else {
  264|      1|        result.push('\\');
  265|      1|        result.push('x');
  266|      1|    }
  267|     11|}
  268|       |
  269|     12|fn parse_octal_escape(chars: &mut Peekable<Chars>, result: &mut String) {
  270|     12|    let mut val = 0u32;
  271|     33|    for _ in 0..3 {
  272|     29|        if let Some(&ch) = chars.peek() {
                                   ^22
  273|     22|            if let Some(digit) = ch.to_digit(8) {
                                      ^21
  274|     21|                val = val * 8 + digit;
  275|     21|                chars.next();
  276|     21|            } else {
  277|      1|                break;
  278|       |            }
  279|       |        } else {
  280|      7|            break;
  281|       |        }
  282|       |    }
  283|     12|    result.push(char::from_u32(val).unwrap_or(char::REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER));
  284|     12|}
  285|       |
  286|      6|fn parse_ucn_escape(chars: &mut Peekable<Chars>, result: &mut String) {
  287|      6|    let is_u = chars.next() == Some('u'); // consume u/U
  288|      6|    let digits_needed = if is_u { 4 } else { 8 };
                                                ^5         ^1
  289|      6|    let mut hex_str = String::new();
  290|       |
  291|      6|    for _ in 0..digits_needed {
  292|     25|        if let Some(&ch) = chars.peek() {
                                   ^23
  293|     23|            if ch.is_ascii_hexdigit() {
  294|     22|                chars.next();
  295|     22|                hex_str.push(ch);
  296|     22|            } else {
  297|      1|                break;
  298|       |            }
  299|       |        } else {
  300|      2|            break;
  301|       |        }
  302|       |    }
  303|       |
  304|      6|    if hex_str.len() != digits_needed {
  305|       |        // Invalid, preserve as raw
  306|      3|        result.push('\\');
  307|      3|        result.push(if is_u { 'u' } else { 'U' });
                                            ^2           ^1
  308|      3|        result.push_str(&hex_str);
  309|      3|        return;
  310|      3|    }
  311|       |
  312|      3|    if let Some(c) = u32::from_str_radix(&hex_str, 16).ok().and_then(char::from_u32) {
                              ^2
  313|      2|        result.push(c);
  314|      2|        return;
  315|      1|    }
  316|       |
  317|       |    // Invalid codepoint
  318|      1|    result.push(char::REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER);
  319|      6|}

  321|       |/// Parse a character literal content (e.g. "a", "\n", "\x41") into a codepoint
  322|     32|pub(crate) fn parse_char_literal(s: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  323|     32|    if s.is_empty() {
  324|      1|        return None;
  325|     31|    }
  326|       |    // Optimization: if simple char, just return it
  327|     31|    if !s.contains('\\') {
  328|     19|        let mut chars = s.chars();
  329|     19|        let c = chars.next()?;
                                          ^0
  330|     19|        return Some(c as u32);
  331|     12|    }
  332|       |
  333|     12|    let unescaped = unescape_string(s);
  334|     12|    unescaped.chars().next().map(|c| c as u32)
  335|     32|}

  337|       |#[cfg(test)]
  338|       |mod tests {
  339|       |    use super::*;
  340|       |
  341|       |    #[test]
  342|      1|    fn test_uppercase_hex_literals() {
  343|       |        // Test 0X integer prefix
  344|      1|        assert_eq!(parse_c11_integer_literal("0X10"), Some((16, None, 16)));
  345|       |
  346|       |        // Test 0X float prefix
  347|       |        // 0X1p0 -> 1.0 * 2^0 = 1.0
  348|      1|        assert_eq!(parse_c11_float_literal("0X1p0"), Some((1.0, None)));
  349|      1|    }
  350|       |
  351|       |    #[test]
  352|      1|    fn test_escape_sequences() {
  353|       |        // Test various C escape sequences
  354|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\n"), "\n");
  355|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\t"), "\t");
  356|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\r"), "\r");
  357|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\b"), "\x08"); // BS
  358|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\f"), "\x0C"); // FF
  359|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\v"), "\x0B"); // VT
  360|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\a"), "\x07"); // BEL
  361|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\\"), "\\");
  362|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\'"), "\'");
  363|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r#"\""#), "\"");
  364|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\?"), "?");
  365|       |
  366|       |        // Mixed content
  367|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"Hello\nWorld"), "Hello\nWorld");
  368|      1|    }
  369|       |
  370|       |    #[test]
  371|      1|    fn test_escape_edge_cases() {
  372|       |        // Unknown escape -> keep char (e.g., \q -> q)
  373|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\q"), "q");
  374|       |
  375|       |        // Trailing backslash -> keep backslash
  376|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"foo\"), r"foo\");
  377|       |
  378|       |        // Incomplete UCN -> keep raw
  379|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\u123"), r"\u123");
  380|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\U12345"), r"\U12345"); // too short for U
  381|       |
  382|       |        // Invalid hex digits in UCN -> keep partial raw
  383|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\u12z"), r"\u12z");
  384|       |
  385|       |        // Invalid codepoint in UCN -> replacement char (U+FFFD)
  386|       |        // U+D800 is a surrogate, which is invalid in scalar value
  387|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\uD800"), "\u{FFFD}");
  388|       |
  389|       |        // Empty hex escape -> \x
  390|      1|        assert_eq!(unescape_string(r"\xz"), r"\xz");
  391|      1|    }
  392|       |}

/app/src/ast/nodes.rs:
    1|       |//! AST Node definitions, constructors, and builder patterns.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module contains the core AST node types, including the NodeKind enum
    4|       |//! and associated data structures. It provides constructors and builder patterns
    5|       |//! for creating complex AST nodes ergonomically.
    6|       |
    7|       |use serde::Serialize;
    8|       |
    9|       |use crate::{
   10|       |    ast::{NameId, NodeRef, SymbolRef, TypeRef},
   11|       |    semantic::{QualType, ScopeId},
   12|       |};
   13|       |
   14|       |/// The core enum defining all possible AST node types for C11.
   15|       |/// Variants use NodeIndex for child references, enabling flattened storage.
   16|       |/// Maintained original structure for compatibility, but moved to this module.
   17|       |use crate::ast::literal::Literal;
   18|       |
   19|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
   20|       |pub enum NodeKind {
   21|       |    // --- Literals (Inline storage for common types) ---
   22|       |    Literal(Literal),
   23|       |
   24|       |    // --- Expressions ---
   25|       |    // Ident now includes a resolved SymbolRef after semantic analysis
   26|       |    Ident(NameId, SymbolRef),
   27|       |    UnaryOp(UnaryOp, NodeRef),
   28|       |    BinaryOp(BinaryOp, NodeRef, NodeRef),
   29|       |    TernaryOp(NodeRef, NodeRef, NodeRef),
   30|       |    GnuStatementExpression(
   31|       |        NodeRef, /* compound statement */
   32|       |        NodeRef, /* result expression */
   33|       |    ),
   34|       |
   35|       |    PostIncrement(NodeRef),
   36|       |    PostDecrement(NodeRef),
   37|       |
   38|       |    Assignment(BinaryOp, NodeRef /* lhs */, NodeRef /* rhs */),
   39|       |    FunctionCall(CallExpr),
   40|       |
   41|       |    MemberAccess(
   42|       |        NodeRef, /* object */
   43|       |        NameId,  /* field */
   44|       |        bool,    /* is_arrow */
   45|       |    ),
   46|       |    IndexAccess(NodeRef /* array */, NodeRef /* index */),
   47|       |
   48|       |    Cast(QualType, NodeRef),
   49|       |    BuiltinVaArg(QualType, NodeRef),
   50|       |    BuiltinOffsetof(QualType, NodeRef),
   51|       |    BuiltinVaStart(NodeRef, NodeRef),
   52|       |    BuiltinVaEnd(NodeRef),
   53|       |    BuiltinVaCopy(NodeRef, NodeRef),
   54|       |    BuiltinExpect(NodeRef, NodeRef),
   55|       |    BuiltinTypesCompatibleP(QualType, QualType),
   56|       |    BuiltinPopcount(NodeRef),
   57|       |    BuiltinClz(NodeRef),
   58|       |    BuiltinCtz(NodeRef),
   59|       |    AtomicOp(AtomicOp, NodeRef /* args start */, u16 /* arg count */),
   60|       |    SizeOfExpr(NodeRef),
   61|       |    SizeOfType(QualType),
   62|       |    AlignOf(QualType), // C11 _Alignof
   63|       |
   64|       |    CompoundLiteral(QualType, NodeRef),
   65|       |    GenericSelection(GenericSelectionData),
   66|       |    GenericAssociation(GenericAssociationData),
   67|       |
   68|       |    // --- Statements (Complex statements are separate structs) ---
   69|       |    CompoundStatement(CompoundStmtData),
   70|       |    If(IfStmt),
   71|       |    While(WhileStmt),
   72|       |    DoWhile(NodeRef /* body */, NodeRef /* condition */),
   73|       |    For(ForStmt),
   74|       |
   75|       |    Return(Option<NodeRef>),
   76|       |    Break,
   77|       |    Continue,
   78|       |    Goto(NameId, SymbolRef),                           // resolved symbol after semantic analysis
   79|       |    Label(NameId, NodeRef /* statement */, SymbolRef), // resolved symbol after semantic analysis
   80|       |
   81|       |    Switch(NodeRef /* condition */, NodeRef /* body statement */),
   82|       |    Case(NodeRef /* const_expr */, NodeRef /* statement */),
   83|       |    CaseRange(
   84|       |        NodeRef, /* start_expr */
   85|       |        NodeRef, /* end_expr */
   86|       |        NodeRef, /* statement */
   87|       |    ), // GNU Extension often supported
   88|       |    Default(NodeRef /* statement */),
   89|       |
   90|       |    ExpressionStatement(Option<NodeRef> /* expression */), // Expression followed by ';'
   91|       |
   92|       |    // --- Declarations & Definitions ---
   93|       |    // Removed Parser-only Declaration and FunctionDef variants.
   94|       |    // They are now lowered to semantic nodes immediately or exist only in ParsedAst.
   95|       |    EnumConstant(NameId, Option<NodeRef> /* value expr */),
   96|       |    StaticAssert(NodeRef /* condition */, NodeRef /* message */),
   97|       |
   98|       |    // --- Semantic Nodes (Type-Resolved) ---
   99|       |    // declarations of VarDecl/FunctionDecl/TypedefDecl/RecordDecl
  100|       |    VarDecl(VarDeclData),
  101|       |    FunctionDecl(FunctionDeclData),
  102|       |    TypedefDecl(TypedefDeclData),
  103|       |    RecordDecl(RecordDeclData),
  104|       |    FieldDecl(FieldDeclData),
  105|       |    EnumDecl(EnumDeclData),
  106|       |    EnumMember(EnumMemberData),
  107|       |    Function(FunctionData),
  108|       |    Param(ParamData),
  109|       |
  110|       |    // --- Top Level ---
  111|       |    TranslationUnit(TranslationUnitData),
  112|       |
  113|       |    // --- InitializerList ---
  114|       |    InitializerList(InitializerListData),
  115|       |    InitializerItem(DesignatedInitializer),
  116|       |    Designator(Designator),
  117|       |
  118|       |    // --- Dummy Node ---
  119|       |    Dummy,
  120|       |}
  121|       |
  122|       |impl NodeKind {

  296|       |// Structs for Large/Indirect Variants (to keep NodeKind size small and cache-friendly)
  297|       |// These are stored separately with index-based references.
  298|       |
  299|       |// Control flow statements
  300|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  301|       |pub struct IfStmt {
  302|       |    pub condition: NodeRef,
  303|       |    pub then_branch: NodeRef,
  304|       |    pub else_branch: Option<NodeRef>,
  305|       |}
  306|       |
  307|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  308|       |pub struct WhileStmt {
  309|       |    pub condition: NodeRef,
  310|       |    pub body: NodeRef,
  311|       |}
  312|       |
  313|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  314|       |pub struct ForStmt {
  315|       |    pub init: Option<NodeRef>, // Can be Declaration or Expression
  316|       |    pub condition: Option<NodeRef>,
  317|       |    pub increment: Option<NodeRef>,
  318|       |    pub body: NodeRef,
  319|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
  320|       |}
  321|       |
  322|       |// Semantic node data structures (type-resolved)
  323|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  324|       |pub struct CompoundStmtData {
  325|       |    pub stmt_start: NodeRef,
  326|       |    pub stmt_len: u16,
  327|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
  328|       |}
  329|       |
  330|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  331|       |pub struct TranslationUnitData {
  332|       |    pub decl_start: NodeRef,
  333|       |    pub decl_len: u16,
  334|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
  335|       |}
  336|       |
  337|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  338|       |pub struct InitializerListData {
  339|       |    pub init_start: NodeRef,
  340|       |    pub init_len: u16,
  341|       |}
  342|       |
  343|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  344|       |pub struct FunctionData {
  345|       |    pub symbol: SymbolRef,
  346|       |    pub ty: TypeRef, // function type, not the return type
  347|       |    pub is_noreturn: bool,
  348|       |    pub param_start: NodeRef,
  349|       |    pub param_len: u16,
  350|       |    pub body: NodeRef, // compound statement
  351|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
  352|       |}
  353|       |
  354|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  355|       |pub struct ParamData {
  356|       |    pub symbol: SymbolRef,
  357|       |    pub ty: QualType,
  358|       |}
  359|       |
  360|       |// Semantic node data structures (type-resolved)
  361|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  362|       |pub struct VarDeclData {
  363|       |    pub name: NameId,
  364|       |    pub ty: QualType,
  365|       |    pub storage: Option<StorageClass>,
  366|       |    pub init: Option<NodeRef>,  // InitializerList or Expression
  367|       |    pub alignment: Option<u16>, // Max alignment in bytes
  368|       |}
  369|       |
  370|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  371|       |pub struct FunctionDeclData {
  372|       |    pub name: NameId,
  373|       |    pub ty: TypeRef,
  374|       |    pub storage: Option<StorageClass>,
  375|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
  376|       |}
  377|       |
  378|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  379|       |pub struct TypedefDeclData {
  380|       |    pub name: NameId,
  381|       |    pub ty: QualType,
  382|       |}
  383|       |
  384|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  385|       |pub struct RecordDeclData {
  386|       |    pub name: Option<NameId>,
  387|       |    pub ty: TypeRef,
  388|       |    pub member_start: NodeRef,
  389|       |    /// index where FieldDecl located
  390|       |    pub member_len: u16,
  391|       |
  392|       |    pub is_union: bool,
  393|       |}
  394|       |
  395|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  396|       |pub struct FieldDeclData {
  397|       |    pub name: Option<NameId>,
  398|       |    pub ty: QualType, // object type
  399|       |    pub alignment: Option<u32>,
  400|       |}
  401|       |
  402|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  403|       |pub struct CallExpr {
  404|       |    pub callee: NodeRef,
  405|       |    pub arg_start: NodeRef, // index where CallArg located
  406|       |    pub arg_len: u16,
  407|       |}
  408|       |
  409|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  410|       |pub struct EnumDeclData {

  412|       |    pub ty: TypeRef,
  413|       |    pub member_start: NodeRef,
  414|       |    pub member_len: u16,
  415|       |}
  416|       |
  417|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize)]
  418|       |pub struct EnumMemberData {
  419|       |    pub name: NameId,
  420|       |    pub value: i64,
  421|       |    pub init_expr: Option<NodeRef>,
  422|       |}
  423|       |
  424|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
  425|       |pub enum TypeQualifier {
  426|       |    Const,
  427|       |    Restrict,
  428|       |    Volatile,
  429|       |    Atomic,
  430|       |}

  432|       |// Storage classes
  433|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
  434|       |#[repr(u8)]
  435|       |pub enum StorageClass {
  436|       |    Typedef,
  437|       |    Extern,
  438|       |    Static,
  439|       |    Auto,
  440|       |    Register,
  441|       |    ThreadLocal, // C11 _Thread_local
  442|       |}
  443|       |
  444|       |// Function specifiers
  445|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
  446|       |#[repr(u8)]
  447|       |pub enum FunctionSpecifier {
  448|       |    Inline,
  449|       |    Noreturn, // C11 _Noreturn
  450|       |}
  451|       |
  452|       |// Unary Operators
  453|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
  454|       |#[repr(u8)]

  456|       |    Plus,
  457|       |    Minus,
  458|       |    Deref,
  459|       |    AddrOf,
  460|       |    BitNot,

  463|       |    PreDecrement,
  464|       |    Real,
  465|       |    Imag,
  466|       |}

  468|       |// Binary Operators (includes assignment types)

  470|       |pub enum AtomicOp {

  479|       |    FetchXor,

  482|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
  483|       |#[repr(u8)]
  484|       |pub enum BinaryOp {
  485|       |    Add,
  486|       |    Sub,
  487|       |    Mul,
  488|       |    Div,
  489|       |    Mod,
  490|       |    BitAnd,

  494|       |    RShift,

  496|       |    NotEqual,
  497|       |    Less,
  498|       |    LessEqual,
  499|       |    Greater,
  500|       |    GreaterEqual,
  501|       |    LogicAnd,
  502|       |    LogicOr,
  503|       |    Comma,
  504|       |    Assign,
  505|       |    AssignAdd,
  506|       |    AssignSub,
  507|       |    AssignMul,
  508|       |    AssignDiv,
  509|       |    AssignMod,
  510|       |    AssignBitAnd,
  511|       |    AssignBitOr,
  512|       |    AssignBitXor,
  513|       |    AssignLShift,
  514|       |    AssignRShift,
  515|       |}
  516|       |
  517|       |impl BinaryOp {
  518|  1.49k|    pub(crate) fn is_assignment(&self) -> bool {
  519|    917|        matches!(
  520|  1.49k|            self,
  521|       |            BinaryOp::Assign
  522|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignAdd
  523|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignSub
  524|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignMul
  525|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignDiv
  526|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignMod
  527|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignBitAnd
  528|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignBitOr
  529|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignBitXor
  530|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignLShift
  531|       |                | BinaryOp::AssignRShift
  532|       |        )
  533|  1.49k|    }
  534|       |
  535|    392|    pub(crate) fn without_assignment(&self) -> Option<BinaryOp> {
  536|    392|        match self {
  537|     18|            BinaryOp::AssignAdd => Some(BinaryOp::Add),
  538|      9|            BinaryOp::AssignSub => Some(BinaryOp::Sub),
  539|      5|            BinaryOp::AssignMul => Some(BinaryOp::Mul),
  540|      4|            BinaryOp::AssignDiv => Some(BinaryOp::Div),
  541|      4|            BinaryOp::AssignMod => Some(BinaryOp::Mod),
  542|      2|            BinaryOp::AssignBitAnd => Some(BinaryOp::BitAnd),
  543|      2|            BinaryOp::AssignBitOr => Some(BinaryOp::BitOr),
  544|      2|            BinaryOp::AssignBitXor => Some(BinaryOp::BitXor),
  545|      4|            BinaryOp::AssignLShift => Some(BinaryOp::LShift),
  546|      2|            BinaryOp::AssignRShift => Some(BinaryOp::RShift),
  547|    340|            _ => None,
  548|       |        }
  549|    392|    }
  550|       |}

   15|       |/// The parsed AST storage.

   25|    959|        ParsedAst::default()
   26|    959|    }
   27|       |
   28|  10.1k|    pub(crate) fn push_node(&mut self, node: ParsedNode) -> ParsedNodeRef {
   29|  10.1k|        let index = self.nodes.len() as u32 + 1;
   30|  10.1k|        self.nodes.push(node);

   35|  24.6k|        &self.nodes[(index.get() - 1) as usize]
   36|  24.6k|    }

   42|  3.55k|    }

/app/src/ast/parsed_types.rs:
    1|       |//! Parsed type system for the Parser phase.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module defines the syntactic type representations used during parsing,
    4|       |//! distinct from the semantic type system used during analysis. These types
    5|       |//! are only relevant in the Parser phase and will be converted to semantic
    6|       |//! types during the SymbolResolver phase.
    7|       |
    8|       |use std::num::NonZeroU32;
    9|       |
   10|       |use serde::Serialize;
   11|       |
   12|       |use crate::ast::parsed::{ParsedArraySize, ParsedTypeSpecifier};
   13|       |use crate::ast::{NameId, SourceSpan};
   14|       |use crate::semantic::TypeQualifiers;
   15|       |
   16|       |/// Type reference for parsed base types
   17|       |pub type ParsedBaseTypeRef = NonZeroU32;
   18|       |
   19|       |/// Type reference for parsed declarators
   20|       |pub type ParsedDeclRef = NonZeroU32;
   21|       |
   22|       |/// A parsed type that represents the syntactic structure of a type
   23|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]
   24|       |pub struct ParsedType {
   25|       |    pub base: ParsedBaseTypeRef,   // NonZeroU32
   26|       |    pub declarator: ParsedDeclRef, // NonZeroU32
   27|       |    pub qualifiers: TypeQualifiers,
   28|       |}
   29|       |
   30|       |/// Range for struct members in the arena
   31|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
   32|       |pub struct ParsedStructMemberRange {

   34|       |    pub len: u32,
   35|       |}
   36|       |

   83|       |/// Parsed base type node (the fundamental type specifier)

   87|       |
   88|       |    Record {

  104|       |pub enum ParsedDeclaratorNode {
  105|       |    Identifier,
  106|       |    Pointer {
  107|       |        qualifiers: TypeQualifiers,
  108|       |        inner: ParsedDeclRef,
  109|       |    },
  110|       |

  119|       |        inner: ParsedDeclRef,
  120|       |    },
  121|       |}
  122|       |
  123|       |/// Arena for storing parsed type information
  124|       |/// This provides efficient allocation and referencing for parsed types
  125|       |#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
  126|       |pub struct ParsedTypeArena {
  127|       |    base_types: Vec<ParsedBaseTypeNode>,
  128|       |    declarators: Vec<ParsedDeclaratorNode>,
  129|       |    params: Vec<ParsedFunctionParam>,
  130|       |    struct_members: Vec<ParsedStructMember>,
  131|       |    enum_constants: Vec<ParsedEnumConstant>,
  132|       |}
  133|       |
  134|       |impl ParsedTypeArena {
  135|       |    /// Allocate a new base type and return its reference
  136|    363|    pub(crate) fn alloc_base_type(&mut self, base_type: ParsedBaseTypeNode) -> ParsedBaseTypeRef {
  137|    363|        let index = self.base_types.len() as u32 + 1; // Start from 1 for NonZeroU32
  138|    363|        self.base_types.push(base_type);
  139|    363|        ParsedBaseTypeRef::new(index).expect("ParsedBaseTypeRef overflow")
  140|    363|    }
  141|       |
  142|       |    /// Allocate a new declarator and return its reference
  143|    445|    pub(crate) fn alloc_decl(&mut self, declarator: ParsedDeclaratorNode) -> ParsedDeclRef {
  144|    445|        let index = self.declarators.len() as u32 + 1; // Start from 1 for NonZeroU32
  145|    445|        self.declarators.push(declarator);
  146|    445|        ParsedDeclRef::new(index).expect("ParsedDeclRef overflow")
  147|    445|    }
  148|       |
  149|       |    /// Allocate function parameters and return the range
  150|     16|    pub(crate) fn alloc_params(&mut self, params: Vec<ParsedFunctionParam>) -> ParsedParamRange {
  151|     16|        let start = self.params.len() as u32;
  152|     16|        self.params.extend(params);
  153|     16|        let len = self.params.len() as u32 - start;
  154|     16|        ParsedParamRange { start, len }
  155|     16|    }
  156|       |
  157|       |    /// Allocate struct members and return the range
  158|      5|    pub(crate) fn alloc_struct_members(&mut self, members: Vec<ParsedStructMember>) -> ParsedStructMemberRange {
  159|      5|        let start = self.struct_members.len() as u32;
  160|      5|        self.struct_members.extend(members);
  161|      5|        let len = self.struct_members.len() as u32 - start;
  162|      5|        ParsedStructMemberRange { start, len }
  163|      5|    }
  164|       |
  165|       |    /// Allocate enum constants and return the range
  166|      2|    pub(crate) fn alloc_enum_constants(&mut self, enumerators: Vec<ParsedEnumConstant>) -> ParsedEnumRange {
  167|      2|        let start = self.enum_constants.len() as u32;
  168|      2|        self.enum_constants.extend(enumerators);
  169|      2|        let len = self.enum_constants.len() as u32 - start;
  170|      2|        ParsedEnumRange { start, len }
  171|      2|    }
  172|       |
  173|       |    /// Get a base type by reference
  174|    334|    pub(crate) fn get_base_type(&self, base_ref: ParsedBaseTypeRef) -> ParsedBaseTypeNode {
  175|    334|        let index = (base_ref.get() - 1) as usize;
  176|    334|        self.base_types[index].clone()
  177|    334|    }
  178|       |
  179|       |    /// Get a declarator by reference
  180|    425|    pub(crate) fn get_decl(&self, decl_ref: ParsedDeclRef) -> ParsedDeclaratorNode {
  181|    425|        let index = (decl_ref.get() - 1) as usize;
  182|    425|        self.declarators[index].clone()
  183|    425|    }
  184|       |
  185|       |    /// Get function parameters by range
  186|     13|    pub(crate) fn get_params(&self, range: ParsedParamRange) -> &[ParsedFunctionParam] {
  187|     13|        let start = range.start as usize;
  188|     13|        let end = start + range.len as usize;
  189|     13|        &self.params[start..end]
  190|     13|    }

    1|       |//! MIR to Cranelift IR lowering module
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the mechanical translation from MIR to Cranelift IR.
    4|       |//! The translation follows these rules:
    5|       |//! - No C logic
    6|       |//! - Assume MIR is valid
    7|       |

   41|  1.58k|    match mir_type {

  132|      0|fn f64_to_f128_bytes(val: f64) -> [u8; 16] {
  133|      0|    let bits = val.to_bits();
  134|      0|    let sign = (bits >> 63) & 1;
  135|      0|    let exp = (bits >> 52) & 0x7FF;
  136|      0|    let mant = bits & 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFF;
  137|       |
  138|      0|    let (new_exp, new_mant) = if exp == 0 {
  139|      0|        if mant == 0 {
  140|       |            // Zero
  141|      0|            (0, 0)
  142|       |        } else {
  143|       |            // Subnormal f64 -> Normal f128
  144|       |            // mant is 0.bbbb... (52 bits)
  145|      0|            let lz = mant.leading_zeros() - (64 - 52);
  146|      0|            let shift = lz + 1;
  147|      0|            let normalized_mant = mant << shift;
  148|      0|            let payload = normalized_mant & 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFF; // 52 bits
  149|       |
  150|      0|            let unbiased_exp = -1022 - (shift as i32);
  151|      0|            let biased_exp = unbiased_exp + 16383;
  152|       |
  153|      0|            (biased_exp as u64, (payload as u128) << (112 - 52))
  154|       |        }
  155|      0|    } else if exp == 0x7FF {
  156|       |        // Inf or NaN
  157|      0|        (0x7FFF, (mant as u128) << (112 - 52))
  158|       |    } else {
  159|       |        // Normal
  160|      0|        let unbiased_exp = (exp as i32) - 1023;
  161|      0|        let biased_exp = unbiased_exp + 16383;
  162|      0|        (biased_exp as u64, (mant as u128) << (112 - 52))
  163|       |    };
  164|       |
  165|      0|    let sign_bit = (sign as u128) << 127;
  166|      0|    let exp_bits = (new_exp as u128) << 112;
  167|      0|    let mant_bits = new_mant;
  168|       |
  169|      0|    let result = sign_bit | exp_bits | mant_bits;
  170|      0|    result.to_le_bytes()
  171|      0|}
  172|       |
  173|      8|fn f64_to_x87_bytes(val: f64) -> [u8; 16] {
  174|      8|    let bits = val.to_bits();
  175|      8|    let sign = (bits >> 63) & 1;
  176|      8|    let exp = (bits >> 52) & 0x7FF;
  177|      8|    let mant = bits & 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFF;
  178|       |
  179|      8|    let mut out = [0u8; 16];
  180|       |
  181|      8|    if exp == 0 {
  182|      0|        if mant == 0 {
  183|       |            // Zero
  184|      0|            if sign == 1 {
  185|      0|                out[9] = 0x80;
  186|      0|            }
  187|      0|            return out;
  188|      0|        }
  189|       |
  190|       |        // Subnormal f64
  191|       |        // Calculate leading zeros in the 52-bit mantissa
  192|       |        // 12 zeros because u64 has 64 bits but mant only 52
  193|      0|        let lz = mant.leading_zeros() - 12;
  194|       |
  195|       |        // Shift to make MSB 1 (integer bit for x87)
  196|       |        // We want to put the first '1' at bit 63.
  197|       |        // Currently it is at (51 - lz).
  198|       |        // Shift left by 63 - (51 - lz) = 12 + lz.
  199|      0|        let new_mant = mant << (12 + lz);
  200|       |
  201|       |        // Exponent calculation
  202|       |        // Real exponent of f64 subnormal is -1022.
  203|       |        // We effectively shifted left by (lz + 1) to normalize (because we moved 0.1... to 1.0...)
  204|       |        // So we must subtract (lz + 1) from exponent.
  205|      0|        let real_exp = -1022 - (lz as i32 + 1);
  206|       |
  207|       |        // x87 bias 16383
  208|      0|        let new_exp_biased = (real_exp + 16383) as u16;
  209|       |
  210|      0|        out[0..8].copy_from_slice(&new_mant.to_le_bytes());
  211|      0|        out[8..10].copy_from_slice(&new_exp_biased.to_le_bytes());
  212|      0|        if sign == 1 {
  213|      0|            out[9] |= 0x80;
  214|      0|        }
  215|      0|        return out;
  216|      8|    } else if exp == 0x7FF {
  217|       |        // Inf or NaN
  218|      0|        out[8] = 0xFF;
  219|      0|        out[9] = 0x7F | ((sign as u8) << 7);
  220|       |
  221|      0|        if mant == 0 {
  222|      0|            // Infinity: Integer bit 1, mantissa 0
  223|      0|            out[7] = 0x80;
  224|      0|        } else {
  225|      0|            // NaN: Set integer bit 1
  226|      0|            // f64 mantissa is 52 bits. x87 is 63 bits.
  227|      0|            // Shift left by 11.
  228|      0|            let new_mant = (1u64 << 63) | (mant << 11);
  229|      0|            out[0..8].copy_from_slice(&new_mant.to_le_bytes());
  230|      0|        }
  231|      0|        return out;
  232|      8|    }
  233|       |
  234|       |    // Normal f64
  235|      8|    let unbiased_exp = (exp as i32) - 1023;
  236|      8|    let new_exp = (unbiased_exp + 16383) as u16;
  237|       |
  238|       |    // Explicit integer bit 1 for Normal numbers in x87
  239|      8|    let new_mant = (1u64 << 63) | (mant << 11);
  240|       |
  241|      8|    out[0..8].copy_from_slice(&new_mant.to_le_bytes());
  242|      8|    out[8..10].copy_from_slice(&new_exp.to_le_bytes());
  243|       |
  244|       |    // Sign bit in byte 9, bit 7
  245|      8|    if sign == 1 {
  246|      0|        out[9] |= 0x80;
  247|      8|    }
  248|       |
  249|      8|    out
  250|      8|}
  251|       |

  263|       |/// Helper to emit struct constants
  264|     11|fn emit_const_struct(
  265|     11|    fields: &[(usize, ConstValueId)],
  266|     11|    ty: &MirType,
  267|     11|    output: &mut Vec<u8>,
  268|     11|    ctx: &EmitContext,
  269|     11|    mut module: Option<&mut ObjectModule>,
  270|     11|    mut data_description: Option<&mut DataDescription>,
  271|     11|    base_offset: u32,
  272|     11|) {
  273|       |    let MirType::Record {
  274|     11|        layout: record_layout, ..
  275|     11|    } = ty
  276|       |    else {
  277|      0|        panic!("StructLiteral with non-record type");
  278|       |    };
  279|       |
  280|       |    // Initialize the entire struct with zeros
  281|     11|    let mut struct_bytes = vec![0u8; record_layout.size as usize];
  282|       |
  283|       |    // Emit each field at its proper offset
  284|     34|    for (field_index, field_const_id) in fields {
                       ^23          ^23
  285|     23|        if *field_index < record_layout.field_offsets.len() {
  286|     23|            let field_offset = record_layout.field_offsets[*field_index] as usize;
  287|       |
  288|     23|            let mut field_bytes = Vec::new();
  289|     23|            emit_const(
  290|     23|                *field_const_id,
  291|     23|                &mut field_bytes,
  292|     23|                ctx,
  293|     23|                reborrow_module(&mut module),
  294|     23|                reborrow_data_description(&mut data_description),
  295|     23|                base_offset + field_offset as u32,
  296|       |            );
  297|       |
  298|       |            // Copy the field bytes into the struct buffer
  299|     23|            let required_size = field_offset + field_bytes.len();
  300|     23|            if required_size > struct_bytes.len() {
  301|      0|                struct_bytes.resize(required_size, 0);
  302|     23|            }
  303|     23|            struct_bytes[field_offset..field_offset + field_bytes.len()].copy_from_slice(&field_bytes);
  304|      0|        }
  305|       |    }
  306|     11|    output.extend_from_slice(&struct_bytes);
  307|     11|}

  309|       |/// Helper to emit array constants
  310|     56|fn emit_const_array(
  311|     56|    elements: &[ConstValueId],
  312|     56|    ty: &MirType,
  313|     56|    output: &mut Vec<u8>,
  314|     56|    ctx: &EmitContext,
  315|     56|    mut module: Option<&mut ObjectModule>,
  316|     56|    mut data_description: Option<&mut DataDescription>,
  317|     56|    base_offset: u32,
  318|     56|) {
  319|       |    let MirType::Array {
  320|     56|        element,
  321|     56|        size,
  322|     56|        layout: array_layout,
  323|     56|    } = ty
  324|       |    else {
  325|      0|        panic!("ArrayLiteral with non-array type");
  326|       |    };
  327|       |
  328|     56|    let element_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*element);
  329|     56|    let element_size = lower_type_size(element_type, ctx.mir) as usize;
  330|     56|    let stride = array_layout.stride as usize;
  331|     56|    let padding = stride.saturating_sub(element_size);
  332|       |
  333|    501|    for (i, element_const_id) in elements.iter().take(*size).enumerate() {
                                               ^56      ^56    ^56  ^56    ^56
  334|    501|        let element_offset = (i * stride) as u32;
  335|       |
  336|    501|        emit_const(
  337|    501|            *element_const_id,
  338|    501|            output,
  339|    501|            ctx,
  340|    501|            reborrow_module(&mut module),
  341|    501|            reborrow_data_description(&mut data_description),
  342|    501|            base_offset + element_offset,
  343|       |        );
  344|       |
  345|    501|        if padding > 0 {
  346|      0|            output.extend(std::iter::repeat_n(0, padding));
  347|    501|        }
  348|       |    }
  349|       |
  350|       |    // Fill remaining space with zeros if array is partially initialized
  351|     56|    let count = elements.len().min(*size);
  352|     56|    let emitted_size = count * stride;
  353|     56|    let total_size = array_layout.size as usize;
  354|       |
  355|     56|    if emitted_size < total_size {
  356|      0|        let remaining = total_size - emitted_size;
  357|      0|        output.extend(std::iter::repeat_n(0, remaining));
  358|     56|    }

  361|       |/// Emit a constant value to the output buffer based on its type layout
  362|    598|pub(crate) fn emit_const(
  363|    598|    const_id: ConstValueId,
  364|    598|    output: &mut Vec<u8>,
  365|    598|    ctx: &EmitContext,
  366|    598|    mut module: Option<&mut ObjectModule>,

  386|      3|                dd.write_data_addr(offset, global_val, 0);
  387|      0|            }
  388|      3|            output.extend_from_slice(&0i64.to_le_bytes());
  389|       |        }

  393|      2|            {

  395|      2|                dd.write_function_addr(offset, func_ref);
  396|      2|            }

  397|      2|            output.extend_from_slice(&0i64.to_le_bytes());
  398|       |        }
  399|     11|        ConstValueKind::StructLiteral(fields) => {
  400|     11|            emit_const_struct(fields, ty, output, ctx, module, data_description, offset);
  401|     11|        }
  402|     56|        ConstValueKind::ArrayLiteral(elements) => {
  403|     56|            emit_const_array(elements, ty, output, ctx, module, data_description, offset);
  404|     56|        }
  405|       |    }

  421|       |/// Helper to determine if a type should be packed into registers (I64)
  422|       |/// Returns Some(count) of I64 registers needed (max 2)
  423|    255|fn get_struct_packing(mir_type: &MirType, mir: &MirProgram) -> Option<usize> {
  424|    255|    if !mir_type.is_aggregate() {
  425|    234|        return None;
  426|     21|    }
  427|       |
  428|     21|    let size = lower_type_size(mir_type, mir);
  429|     21|    if size == 0 || size > 16 {
  430|      5|        return None;
  431|     16|    }
  432|       |
  433|     16|    Some(size.div_ceil(8) as usize)
  434|    255|}

  436|       |/// Helper to prepare a function signature for a call
  437|     93|fn lower_call_signature(
  438|     93|    call_conv: cranelift::codegen::isa::CallConv,
  439|     93|    return_type_id: TypeId,
  440|     93|    param_types: &[TypeId],
  441|     93|    args: &[Operand],
  442|     93|    is_variadic: bool,
  443|     93|    use_variadic_hack: bool,
  444|     93|    ctx: &SignatureLoweringContext,
  445|     93|) -> Signature {
  446|     93|    let mut sig = Signature::new(call_conv);
  447|       |    // sig.set_is_variadic(is_variadic); // Try if this method exists

  450|     93|    let return_mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(return_type_id);

                                                           ^0   ^0
  454|     19|        None => None,

                              ^74

  458|     74|    }

  461|     93|    let split_f128 = use_variadic_hack;

  464|     93|    let mut xmm_used = 0;

  467|    188|    for &param_type_id in param_types {
                       ^95

  469|       |
  470|     95|        if is_xmm_argument(mir_type) {
  471|      0|            xmm_used += 1;
  472|     95|        }
  473|       |
  474|       |        // Check for struct packing (HFA workaround: pass as I64s in GPRs)
  475|     95|        if let Some(count) = get_struct_packing(mir_type, ctx.mir) {
                                  ^2
  476|      2|            for _ in 0..count {
  477|      2|                sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  478|      2|            }
  479|      2|            continue;
  480|     93|        }
  481|       |
  482|     93|        if split_f128 && matches!(mir_type, MirType::F80 | MirType::F128) {
                                       ^23      ^23
  483|      0|            sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  484|      0|            sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  485|      0|            continue;
  486|     93|        }
  487|       |
  488|     93|        let param_type = match lower_type(mir_type) {
  489|     92|            Some(t) => t,
  490|      1|            None if mir_type.is_aggregate() => types::I64,
  491|      0|            None => types::I32, // Should not happen for valid MIR
  492|       |        };
  493|     93|        sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(param_type));
  494|       |    }

                      ^68
  498|     68|        let arg_type_id = lower_operand_type_id(arg, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
  499|       |        {
  500|     68|            let type_id = arg_type_id;
  501|     68|            let mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(type_id);
  502|     68|            if mir_type.is_aggregate() {
  503|       |                // For structs/arrays, calculate how many I64 slots we need
  504|      5|                let size = lower_type_size(mir_type, ctx.mir);

  506|     33|                for _ in 0..num_slots {
                                          ^5
  507|     33|                    sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  508|     33|                }
  509|      5|                continue;

                                           ^7       ^8

  514|      1|                sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  515|      1|                continue;
  516|     62|            }

  521|     55|                && ctx.triple.architecture == target_lexicon::Architecture::X86_64

                                 ^44
  523|       |            {

  525|     55|                for _ in 0..needed_padding {
                                          ^11
  526|     55|                    sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::F64));
  527|     55|                }

  529|     11|                xmm_used = 8;
  530|     51|            }
  531|       |
  532|     62|            if is_xmm_argument(mir_type) {
  533|     21|                xmm_used += 1;
  534|     41|            }

  537|       |

  539|      0|            arg_type = types::F64;
  540|     62|        }
  541|       |

  543|       |            // Normalized Variadic Signature hack: promote variadic GPR args to i64
  544|      7|            if is_variadic && arg_type.is_int() && arg_type.bits() < 64 {
  545|      7|                arg_type = types::I64;
  546|      7|            }
                          ^0
  547|     55|        }
  548|       |
  549|     62|        sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(arg_type));
  550|       |    }
  551|       |

  553|       |        // Normalized Variadic Signature hack: Ensure at least 32 slots for variadic functions
  554|       |        // This matches the 32-slot spill area in lower_function_signature
  555|     24|        if is_variadic {
  556|     11|            let current_gpr_count = sig.params.len();
  557|     11|            let total_variadic_slots = 128;
  558|     11|            if current_gpr_count < total_variadic_slots {

                                          ^11
  560|  1.35k|                    sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
  561|  1.35k|                }
  562|      0|            }
  563|     13|        }
  564|     69|    }
