  569|       |/// Helper to resolve arguments for a call, handling variadic struct expansion if needed
  570|     54|fn emit_call_args(
  571|     54|    args: &[Operand],
  572|     54|    fixed_param_count: usize,
  573|     54|    sig: &Signature,
  574|     54|    ctx: &mut BodyEmitContext,
  575|     54|    is_variadic: bool,
  576|     54|    split_f128: bool,
  577|     54|    triple: &Triple,
  578|     54|) -> Vec<Value> {
  579|     54|    let mut arg_values = Vec::new();
  580|     54|    let mut sig_idx = 0;
  581|     54|    let mut xmm_used = 0;
  582|       |
  583|       |    // Count XMM usage from fixed parameters first (checking signatures from ctx.mir logic if needed)
  584|       |    // But here we iterate all args. We need to distinguish fixed params.
  585|       |    // We can assume first `fixed_param_count` args match `sig`'s first params,
  586|       |    // but `sig` might have split params.
  587|       |    // Simpler: iterate fixed_param_count args and check types.
  588|     56|    for i in 0..fixed_param_count {
                              ^54
  589|     56|        if let Some(arg) = args.get(i) {
  590|     56|            let type_id = lower_operand_type_id(arg, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
  591|     56|            let mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(type_id);
  592|     56|            if is_xmm_argument(mir_type) {
  593|      0|                xmm_used += 1;
  594|     56|            }
  595|      0|        }
  596|       |    }
  597|       |
  598|    124|    for (arg_idx, arg) in args.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^54  ^54    ^54
  599|    124|        if sig_idx >= sig.params.len() {
  600|      0|            break;
  601|    124|        }
  602|       |
  603|       |        // Check operand type
  604|    124|        let arg_type_id = Some(lower_operand_type_id(arg, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer));
  605|       |
  606|       |        // Check for struct packing in fixed parameters
  607|    124|        if arg_idx < fixed_param_count
  608|     56|            && let Some(type_id) = arg_type_id
  609|     56|            && let Some(count) = get_struct_packing(ctx.mir.get_type(type_id), ctx.mir)
                                      ^2
  610|       |        {
  611|       |            // Resolve struct to address
  612|      2|            let struct_addr = emit_operand(arg, ctx, types::I64);
  613|      2|            let size = lower_type_size(ctx.mir.get_type(type_id), ctx.mir);
  614|       |
  615|      2|            for i in 0..count {
  616|       |                // If it's the last chunk, we might need partial load
  617|      2|                let offset = (i * 8) as i32;
  618|      2|                let remaining = size.saturating_sub((i * 8) as u32);
  619|       |
  620|      2|                let val = if remaining >= 8 {
  621|      2|                    ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), struct_addr, offset)
  622|       |                } else {
  623|       |                    // Partial load
  624|      0|                    let mut current_val = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, 0);
  625|      0|                    for b in 0..remaining {
  626|      0|                        let byte_val =
  627|      0|                            ctx.builder
  628|      0|                                .ins()
  629|      0|                                .load(types::I8, MemFlags::new(), struct_addr, offset + b as i32);
  630|      0|                        let byte_ext = ctx.builder.ins().uextend(types::I64, byte_val);
  631|      0|                        let shift_amt = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, (b * 8) as i64);
  632|      0|                        let shifted = ctx.builder.ins().ishl(byte_ext, shift_amt);
  633|      0|                        current_val = ctx.builder.ins().bor(current_val, shifted);
  634|      0|                    }
  635|      0|                    current_val
  636|       |                };
  637|      2|                arg_values.push(val);
  638|       |            }
  639|       |
  640|      2|            sig_idx += count;
  641|      2|            continue;
  642|    122|        }
  643|       |
  644|       |        // Check if F128 splitting is needed
  645|    122|        if split_f128
  646|     30|            && let Some(type_id) = arg_type_id
  647|     30|            && matches!(ctx.mir.get_type(type_id), MirType::F80 | MirType::F128)
                             ^29
  648|       |        {
  649|      1|            let val = emit_operand(arg, ctx, types::F128);
  650|       |            // Split val into lo, hi by storing to stack and reloading
  651|      1|            let slot = ctx
  652|      1|                .builder
  653|      1|                .create_sized_stack_slot(StackSlotData::new(StackSlotKind::ExplicitSlot, 16, 0));
  654|      1|            ctx.builder.ins().stack_store(val, slot, 0);
  655|      1|            let lo = ctx.builder.ins().stack_load(types::I64, slot, 0);
  656|      1|            let hi = ctx.builder.ins().stack_load(types::I64, slot, 8);
  657|       |
  658|      1|            arg_values.push(lo);
  659|      1|            arg_values.push(hi);
  660|       |
  661|      1|            sig_idx += 2;
  662|      1|            continue;
  663|    121|        }
  664|       |
  665|       |        // Check if this is a variadic struct argument that needs expansion
  666|    121|        if is_variadic
  667|    107|            && arg_idx >= fixed_param_count
  668|     67|            && let Some(type_id) = arg_type_id
  669|       |        {
  670|     67|            let mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(type_id);
  671|     67|            if mir_type.is_aggregate() {
  672|       |                // Get the struct address
  673|      5|                let struct_addr = emit_operand(arg, ctx, types::I64);
  674|       |
  675|       |                // Calculate how many I64 slots this struct needs
  676|      5|                let size = lower_type_size(mir_type, ctx.mir);
  677|      5|                let num_slots = size.div_ceil(8) as usize;
  678|       |
  679|       |                // Load each I64 chunk from the struct
  680|     33|                for slot in 0..num_slots {
                                             ^5
  681|     33|                    if sig_idx < sig.params.len() {
  682|     33|                        let offset = (slot * 8) as i32;
  683|       |
  684|       |                        // Check if this is the last slot and if we need partial load
  685|     33|                        let current_offset = slot * 8;
  686|     33|                        let remaining_bytes = size as usize - current_offset;
  687|       |
  688|     33|                        let value = if remaining_bytes >= 8 {
  689|     32|                            ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), struct_addr, offset)
  690|       |                        } else {
  691|       |                            // Partial load byte-by-byte to avoid OOB read
  692|      1|                            let mut current_val = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, 0);
  693|      1|                            for i in 0..remaining_bytes {
  694|      1|                                let byte_val =
  695|      1|                                    ctx.builder
  696|      1|                                        .ins()
  697|      1|                                        .load(types::I8, MemFlags::new(), struct_addr, offset + i as i32);
  698|      1|                                let byte_ext = ctx.builder.ins().uextend(types::I64, byte_val);
  699|      1|                                let shift_amt = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, (i * 8) as i64);
  700|      1|                                let shifted = ctx.builder.ins().ishl(byte_ext, shift_amt);
  701|      1|                                current_val = ctx.builder.ins().bor(current_val, shifted);
  702|      1|                            }
  703|      1|                            current_val
  704|       |                        };
  705|       |
  706|     33|                        arg_values.push(value);
  707|     33|                        sig_idx += 1;
  708|      0|                    }
  709|       |                }
  710|      5|                continue;
  711|     62|            }
  712|       |
  713|       |            // HACK: Apply padding to force stack usage for long double
  714|     62|            if !split_f128
  715|     55|                && triple.architecture == target_lexicon::Architecture::X86_64
  716|     55|                && matches!(mir_type, MirType::F80 | MirType::F128)
                                 ^44
  717|       |            {
  718|     11|                let needed_padding = 8usize.saturating_sub(xmm_used);
  719|     11|                for _ in 0..needed_padding {
  720|     55|                    if sig_idx < sig.params.len() {
  721|     55|                        arg_values.push(ctx.builder.ins().f64const(0.0));
  722|     55|                        sig_idx += 1;
  723|     55|                    }
                                  ^0
  724|       |                }
  725|     11|                xmm_used = 8;
  726|     51|            }
  727|       |
  728|     62|            if is_xmm_argument(mir_type) {
  729|     21|                xmm_used += 1;
  730|     41|            }
  731|     54|        }
  732|       |
  733|       |        // Update param_type as sig_idx might have changed due to padding
  734|    116|        if sig_idx >= sig.params.len() {
  735|      0|            break;
  736|    116|        }
  737|    116|        let param_type = sig.params[sig_idx].value_type;
  738|       |
  739|       |        // Non-struct argument (or fixed param)
  740|    116|        let value = emit_operand(arg, ctx, param_type);
  741|    116|        arg_values.push(value);
  742|    116|        sig_idx += 1;
  743|       |    }
  744|       |
  745|       |    // Padding for remaining signature params (variadic slot padding SysV x86_64 hack)
  746|    775|    while sig_idx < sig.params.len() {
  747|    721|        let param_type = sig.params[sig_idx].value_type;
  748|    721|        arg_values.push(ctx.builder.ins().iconst(param_type, 0i64));
  749|    721|        sig_idx += 1;
  750|    721|    }
  751|       |
  752|     54|    arg_values
  753|     54|}

  755|       |/// Helper to get the result of a call, or a zero constant if it has no return value
  756|     54|fn get_call_result(builder: &mut FunctionBuilder, call_inst: Inst, expected_type: Type) -> Value {
  757|     54|    let results = builder.inst_results(call_inst);
  758|     54|    if results.is_empty() {
  759|     13|        builder.ins().iconst(expected_type, 0i64)
  760|       |    } else {
  761|     41|        results[0]
  762|       |    }
  763|     54|}
  764|       |
  765|     54|fn emit_function_call(
  766|     54|    call_target: &CallTarget,
  767|     54|    args: &[Operand],
  768|     54|    expected_type: Type,
  769|     54|    ctx: &mut BodyEmitContext,
  770|     54|) -> Value {
  771|       |    // 1. Determine function properties and callee address if indirect/variadic
  772|     54|    let (return_type_id, param_types, is_variadic, name_linkage, target_addr, use_variadic_hack) = match call_target {
  773|     50|        CallTarget::Direct(func_id) => {
  774|     50|            let func = ctx.mir.get_function(*func_id);
  775|     54|            let param_types: Vec<TypeId> = func.params.iter().map(|&p| ctx.mir.get_local(p).type_id).collect();
                              ^50          ^50           ^50                ^50                                    ^50
  776|     50|            let name_linkage = Some((func.name.as_str(), lower_linkage(func.kind)));
  777|     50|            let is_defined = matches!(func.kind, MirFunctionKind::Defined);
                                           ^35
  778|     50|            (
  779|     50|                func.return_type,
  780|     50|                param_types,
  781|     50|                func.is_variadic,
  782|     50|                name_linkage,
  783|     50|                None,
  784|     50|                is_defined,
  785|     50|            )
  786|       |        }
  787|      4|        CallTarget::Indirect(func_operand) => {
  788|      4|            let func_ptr_type_id = lower_operand_type_id(func_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
  789|      4|            let func_ptr_type = ctx.mir.get_type(func_ptr_type_id);
  790|       |
  791|      4|            let ((return_type_id, param_types), is_function_type, is_variadic_call) = match func_ptr_type {
  792|      3|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => match ctx.mir.get_type(*pointee) {
  793|       |                    MirType::Function {
  794|      3|                        return_type,
  795|      3|                        params,
  796|      3|                        is_variadic,
  797|      3|                    } => ((*return_type, params.clone()), false, *is_variadic),
  798|      0|                    _ => panic!("Indirect call operand points to non-function type"),
  799|       |                },
  800|       |                MirType::Function {
  801|      1|                    return_type,
  802|      1|                    params,
  803|      1|                    is_variadic,
  804|      1|                } => ((*return_type, params.clone()), true, *is_variadic),
  805|      0|                _ => panic!("Indirect call operand is not a pointer"),
  806|       |            };
  807|       |
  808|      4|            let callee_val = if is_function_type {
  809|      1|                match func_operand {
  810|      1|                    Operand::Copy(place) => emit_place_addr(place, ctx),
  811|      0|                    _ => emit_operand(func_operand, ctx, types::I64),
  812|       |                }
  813|       |            } else {
  814|      3|                emit_operand(func_operand, ctx, types::I64)
  815|       |            };
  816|       |
  817|       |            // Assuming function pointers point to internal functions requiring the hack.
  818|       |            // TODO: Distinguish between internal and external function pointers if possible.
  819|      4|            (
  820|      4|                return_type_id,
  821|      4|                param_types,
  822|      4|                is_variadic_call,
  823|      4|                None,
  824|      4|                Some(callee_val),
  825|      4|                true,
  826|      4|            )
  827|       |        }
  828|       |    };
  829|       |
  830|       |    // 2. Prepare call site signature and resolve arguments
  831|     54|    let lower_ctx = SignatureLoweringContext {
  832|     54|        mir: ctx.mir,
  833|     54|        triple: ctx.triple,
  834|     54|        pointee_to_pointer: ctx.pointee_to_pointer,
  835|     54|    };
  836|       |
  837|     54|    let sig = lower_call_signature(
  838|     54|        ctx.builder.func.signature.call_conv,
  839|     54|        return_type_id,
  840|     54|        &param_types,
  841|     54|        args,
  842|     54|        is_variadic,
  843|     54|        use_variadic_hack,
  844|     54|        &lower_ctx,
  845|       |    );
  846|       |
  847|     54|    let split_f128 = use_variadic_hack;
  848|     54|    let arg_values = emit_call_args(args, param_types.len(), &sig, ctx, is_variadic, split_f128, ctx.triple);
  849|       |
  850|       |    // 3. Emit the call
  851|     54|    let call_inst = if is_variadic {
  852|     40|        if let Some((name, linkage)) = name_linkage {
                                   ^39   ^39
  853|       |            // Variadic direct calls must be indirect to use the custom signature
  854|     39|            let canonical_sig = lower_call_signature(
  855|     39|                ctx.builder.func.signature.call_conv,
  856|     39|                return_type_id,
  857|     39|                &param_types,
  858|     39|                &[],
  859|     39|                is_variadic,
  860|     39|                use_variadic_hack,
  861|     39|                &lower_ctx,
  862|       |            );
  863|     39|            let decl = ctx
  864|     39|                .module
  865|     39|                .declare_function(name, linkage, &canonical_sig)
  866|     39|                .expect("Failed to declare variadic function");
  867|     39|            let func_ref = ctx.module.declare_func_in_func(decl, ctx.builder.func);
  868|     39|            let addr = ctx.builder.ins().func_addr(types::I64, func_ref);
  869|     39|            let sig_ref = ctx.builder.import_signature(sig);
  870|       |
  871|     39|            let addr = emit_al_count_and_pass_addr(args, &param_types, addr, ctx);
  872|       |
  873|     39|            ctx.builder.ins().call_indirect(sig_ref, addr, &arg_values)
  874|      1|        } else if let Some(addr) = target_addr {

  978|  1.33k|fn emit_type_conversion(val: Value, from: Type, to: Type, is_signed: bool, builder: &mut FunctionBuilder) -> Value {
  979|  1.33k|    if from == to {
  980|  1.26k|        return val;
  981|     70|    }
  982|       |
  983|       |    // Float to Float
  984|     70|    if from.is_float() && to.is_float() {
                                        ^4 ^4
  985|      2|        let from_width = from.bits();
  986|      2|        let to_width = to.bits();
  987|      2|        if from_width < to_width {
  988|      2|            return builder.ins().fpromote(to, val);
  989|      0|        } else if from_width > to_width {
  990|      0|            return builder.ins().fdemote(to, val);
  991|       |        } else {
  992|      0|            return val;
  993|       |        }

  995|       |
  996|       |    // Integer to Float

                                      ^66^66

 1000|       |        } else {
 1001|      0|            builder.ins().fcvt_from_uint(to, val)
 1002|       |        };
 1003|     68|    }
 1004|       |
 1005|       |    // Float to Integer
 1006|     68|    if from.is_float() && to.is_int() {
                                        ^2 ^2
 1007|      2|        let to_width = to.bits();
 1008|      2|        if to_width < 32 {
 1009|       |            // Cranelift x64 backend doesn't support fcvt_to_sint/uint for < 32-bit targets
 1010|       |            // Convert to I32 first, then reduce
 1011|      2|            let intermediate = if is_signed {
 1012|      0|                builder.ins().fcvt_to_sint(types::I32, val)
 1013|       |            } else {
 1014|      2|                builder.ins().fcvt_to_uint(types::I32, val)
 1015|       |            };

 1017|      0|        }

 1019|      0|        return if is_signed {
 1020|      0|            builder.ins().fcvt_to_sint(to, val)
 1021|       |        } else {
 1022|      0|            builder.ins().fcvt_to_uint(to, val)
 1023|       |        };
 1024|     66|    }

 1028|     66|    let to_width = to.bits();

 1036|       |        }

 1270|       |/// Helper function to get the Cranelift Type of an operand
 1271|    476|fn lower_operand_type(operand: &Operand, mir: &MirProgram, pointee_to_pointer: &HashMap<TypeId, TypeId>) -> Type {
 1272|    476|    match operand {
 1273|    145|        Operand::Constant(const_id) => {
 1274|    145|            let const_value = mir.constants.get(const_id).expect("constant id not found");
 1275|    145|            let mir_type = mir.get_type(const_value.ty);
 1276|       |
 1277|    145|            if let Some(clif_type) = lower_type(mir_type) {
                                      ^110
 1278|    110|                return clif_type;
 1279|     35|            }
 1280|       |
 1281|     35|            match &const_value.kind {
 1282|      0|                ConstValueKind::Int(_) => types::I32,
 1283|      0|                ConstValueKind::Float(_) => types::F64,
 1284|      0|                ConstValueKind::Bool(_) => types::I32,
 1285|     35|                ConstValueKind::Null | ConstValueKind::Zero | ConstValueKind::GlobalAddress(_) => types::I64,
 1286|      0|                ConstValueKind::FunctionAddress(_) => types::I64,
 1287|      0|                ConstValueKind::StructLiteral(_) => types::I32,
 1288|      0|                ConstValueKind::ArrayLiteral(_) => types::I32,
 1289|       |            }
 1290|       |        }
 1291|    129|        Operand::Copy(place) => {
 1292|    129|            let place_type_id = lower_place_type_id(place, mir, pointee_to_pointer);
 1293|    129|            let place_type = mir.get_type(place_type_id);
 1294|    129|            if place_type.is_aggregate() {

 1348|       |/// Helper function to get the TypeId of a place
 1349|  1.27k|fn lower_place_type_id(place: &Place, mir: &MirProgram, pointee_to_pointer: &HashMap<TypeId, TypeId>) -> TypeId {
 1350|  1.27k|    match place {
 1351|    808|        Place::Local(local_id) => mir.get_local(*local_id).type_id,
 1352|    107|        Place::Global(global_id) => mir.get_global(*global_id).type_id,
 1353|     12|        Place::Deref(operand) => {
 1354|       |            // To get the type of a dereference, we need the type of the operand,
 1355|       |            // which should be a pointer. The resulting type is the pointee.
 1356|     12|            let operand_type_id = lower_operand_type_id(operand, mir, pointee_to_pointer);
 1357|     12|            let operand_type = mir.get_type(operand_type_id);
 1358|     12|            match operand_type {
 1359|     12|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => *pointee,
 1360|      0|                _ => panic!("Cannot determine type for deref operand"),
 1361|       |            }
 1362|       |        }
 1363|    273|        Place::StructField(base_place, field_index) => {
 1364|    273|            let base_type_id = lower_place_type_id(base_place, mir, pointee_to_pointer);
 1365|    273|            let base_type = mir.get_type(base_type_id);
 1366|    273|            match base_type {
 1367|    273|                MirType::Record { field_types, .. } => field_types.get(*field_index).copied().unwrap(),
 1368|      0|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => {
 1369|      0|                    let pointee_type = mir.get_type(*pointee);
 1370|      0|                    if let MirType::Record { field_types, .. } = pointee_type {
 1371|      0|                        field_types.get(*field_index).copied().unwrap()
 1372|       |                    } else {
 1373|      0|                        panic!("Base of StructField is not a struct type")
 1374|       |                    }
 1375|       |                }
 1376|      0|                _ => panic!("Base of StructField is not a struct type"),
 1377|       |            }
 1378|       |        }
 1379|     78|        Place::ArrayIndex(base_place, _) => {
 1380|     78|            let base_type_id = lower_place_type_id(base_place, mir, pointee_to_pointer);
 1381|     78|            let base_type = mir.get_type(base_type_id);
 1382|     78|            match base_type {
 1383|     78|                MirType::Array { element, .. } => *element,
 1384|      0|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => *pointee,
 1385|      0|                _ => panic!("Base of ArrayIndex is not an array or pointer"),
 1386|       |            }
 1387|       |        }
 1388|       |    }

 1391|       |/// Helper function to resolve a MIR place to a memory address
 1392|    417|fn emit_place_addr(place: &Place, ctx: &mut BodyEmitContext) -> Value {
 1393|    417|    match place {
 1394|    119|        Place::Local(local_id) => {
 1395|    119|            if let Some(stack_slot) = ctx.stack_slots.get(local_id) {
 1396|    119|                ctx.builder.ins().stack_addr(types::I64, *stack_slot, 0)
 1397|       |            } else {
 1398|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, 0)
 1399|       |            }
 1400|       |        }
 1401|     30|        Place::Global(global_id) => {
 1402|     30|            let global = ctx.mir.get_global(*global_id);
 1403|     30|            let linkage = if global.name.as_str().starts_with(".L.str") {
 1404|      0|                Linkage::Local
 1405|     30|            } else if global.initial_value.is_some() {
 1406|     30|                Linkage::Export
 1407|       |            } else {
 1408|      0|                Linkage::Import
 1409|       |            };
 1410|       |
 1411|     30|            let global_val = ctx
 1412|     30|                .module
 1413|     30|                .declare_data(global.name.as_str(), linkage, true, false)
 1414|     30|                .expect("Failed to declare global data");
 1415|     30|            let local_id = ctx.module.declare_data_in_func(global_val, ctx.builder.func);
 1416|     30|            ctx.builder.ins().global_value(types::I64, local_id)
 1417|       |        }
 1418|    160|        Place::Deref(operand) => emit_operand(operand, ctx, types::I64),
 1419|     75|        Place::StructField(base_place, field_index) => {
 1420|     75|            let base_addr = emit_place_addr(base_place, ctx);
 1421|       |
 1422|     75|            let base_place_type_id = lower_place_type_id(base_place, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1423|     75|            let base_type = ctx.mir.get_type(base_place_type_id);
 1424|       |
 1425|     75|            let (field_offset, is_pointer) = match base_type {
 1426|     75|                MirType::Record { layout, .. } => {
 1427|     75|                    let offset = layout.field_offsets[*field_index];
 1428|     75|                    (offset, false)
 1429|       |                }
 1430|      0|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => {
 1431|      0|                    let pointee_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*pointee);
 1432|      0|                    if let MirType::Record { layout, .. } = pointee_type {
 1433|      0|                        let offset = layout.field_offsets[*field_index];
 1434|      0|                        (offset, true)
 1435|       |                    } else {
 1436|      0|                        panic!("Base of StructField is not a struct type");
 1437|       |                    }
 1438|       |                }
 1439|      0|                _ => panic!("Base of StructField is not a struct type"),
 1440|       |            };
 1441|       |
 1442|     75|            let final_addr = if is_pointer {
 1443|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), base_addr, 0)
 1444|       |            } else {
 1445|     75|                base_addr
 1446|       |            };
 1447|       |
 1448|     75|            let offset_val = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, field_offset as i64);
 1449|     75|            ctx.builder.ins().iadd(final_addr, offset_val)
 1450|       |        }
 1451|     33|        Place::ArrayIndex(base_place, index_operand) => {
 1452|     33|            let base_addr = emit_place_addr(base_place, ctx);
 1453|     33|            let index_val = emit_operand(index_operand, ctx, types::I64);
 1454|       |
 1455|     33|            let base_place_type_id = lower_place_type_id(base_place, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1456|     33|            let base_type = ctx.mir.get_type(base_place_type_id);
 1457|       |
 1458|     33|            let (element_size, final_base_addr) = match base_type {
 1459|     33|                MirType::Array { layout, .. } => (layout.stride as u32, base_addr),
 1460|      0|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => {
 1461|      0|                    let pointee_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*pointee);
 1462|      0|                    let size = lower_type_size(pointee_type, ctx.mir);
 1463|      0|                    let loaded_ptr = ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), base_addr, 0);
 1464|      0|                    (size, loaded_ptr)
 1465|       |                }
 1466|      0|                _ => panic!("Base of ArrayIndex is not an array or pointer"),
 1467|       |            };
 1468|       |
 1469|     33|            let element_size_val = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, element_size as i64);
 1470|     33|            let offset = ctx.builder.ins().imul(index_val, element_size_val);
 1471|       |
 1472|     33|            ctx.builder.ins().iadd(final_base_addr, offset)
 1473|       |        }
 1474|       |    }

 1695|       |                        BinaryIntOp::Le => {
 1696|      0|                            let cond = if is_operand_signed(left_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer) {
 1697|      0|                                ctx.builder
 1698|      0|                                    .ins()
 1699|      0|                                    .icmp(IntCC::SignedLessThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val)
 1700|       |                            } else {
 1701|      0|                                ctx.builder
 1702|      0|                                    .ins()
 1703|      0|                                    .icmp(IntCC::UnsignedLessThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val)
 1704|       |                            };
 1705|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1706|       |                        }
 1707|       |                        BinaryIntOp::Gt => {
 1708|      0|                            let cond = if is_operand_signed(left_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer) {
 1709|      0|                                ctx.builder.ins().icmp(IntCC::SignedGreaterThan, left_val, right_val)
 1710|       |                            } else {
 1711|      0|                                ctx.builder.ins().icmp(IntCC::UnsignedGreaterThan, left_val, right_val)
 1712|       |                            };
 1713|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1714|       |                        }
 1715|       |                        BinaryIntOp::Ge => {
 1716|      0|                            let cond = if is_operand_signed(left_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer) {
 1717|      0|                                ctx.builder
 1718|      0|                                    .ins()
 1719|      0|                                    .icmp(IntCC::SignedGreaterThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val)
 1720|       |                            } else {
 1721|      0|                                ctx.builder
 1722|      0|                                    .ins()
 1723|      0|                                    .icmp(IntCC::UnsignedGreaterThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val)
 1724|       |                            };
 1725|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1726|       |                        }
 1727|       |                    }
 1728|       |                }
 1729|      5|                Rvalue::BinaryFloatOp(op, left_operand, right_operand) => {
 1730|      5|                    let left_cranelift_type = lower_operand_type(left_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1731|      5|                    let right_cranelift_type = lower_operand_type(right_operand, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1732|       |
 1733|      5|                    let left_val = emit_operand(left_operand, ctx, left_cranelift_type);
 1734|      5|                    let right_val = emit_operand(right_operand, ctx, right_cranelift_type);
 1735|       |
 1736|      5|                    match op {
 1737|      0|                        BinaryFloatOp::Add => ctx.builder.ins().fadd(left_val, right_val),
 1738|      0|                        BinaryFloatOp::Sub => ctx.builder.ins().fsub(left_val, right_val),
 1739|      0|                        BinaryFloatOp::Mul => ctx.builder.ins().fmul(left_val, right_val),
 1740|      0|                        BinaryFloatOp::Div => ctx.builder.ins().fdiv(left_val, right_val),
 1741|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Eq => {
 1742|      1|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::Equal, left_val, right_val);
 1743|      1|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1744|       |                        }
 1745|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Ne => {
 1746|      4|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::NotEqual, left_val, right_val);
 1747|      4|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1748|       |                        }
 1749|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Lt => {
 1750|      0|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::LessThan, left_val, right_val);
 1751|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1752|       |                        }
 1753|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Le => {
 1754|      0|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::LessThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val);
 1755|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1756|       |                        }
 1757|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Gt => {
 1758|      0|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::GreaterThan, left_val, right_val);
 1759|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1760|       |                        }
 1761|       |                        BinaryFloatOp::Ge => {
 1762|      0|                            let cond = ctx.builder.ins().fcmp(FloatCC::GreaterThanOrEqual, left_val, right_val);
 1763|      0|                            emit_bool_to_int(cond, expected_type, ctx.builder)
 1764|       |                        }
 1765|       |                    }
 1766|       |                }
 1767|     11|                Rvalue::BuiltinVaArg(ap, type_id) => {
 1768|       |                    // X86_64 SysV va_arg implementation
 1769|       |                    // va_list is struct { gp_offset, fp_offset, overflow_arg_area, reg_save_area }
 1770|       |                    // For GP types: if gp_offset < 48, fetch from reg_save_area + gp_offset
 1771|       |                    //               else fetch from overflow_arg_area
 1772|       |
 1773|     11|                    let ap_addr = emit_place_addr(ap, ctx);
 1774|       |
 1775|       |                    // Load fields from va_list
 1776|     11|                    let gp_offset = ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I32, MemFlags::new(), ap_addr, 0);
 1777|     11|                    let _fp_offset = ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I32, MemFlags::new(), ap_addr, 4);
 1778|     11|                    let _overflow_area = ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), ap_addr, 8);
 1779|     11|                    let reg_save_area = ctx.builder.ins().load(types::I64, MemFlags::new(), ap_addr, 16);
 1780|       |
 1781|     11|                    let mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*type_id);
 1782|     11|                    let cl_type = lower_type(mir_type).unwrap_or(types::I64);
 1783|       |
 1784|       |                    // Unified va_arg implementation for "Cendol" ABI (all args in spill_slot/reg_save_area)
 1785|       |                    // We ignore is_float and standard SystemV ABI register separation because our
 1786|       |                    // implementation flattens everything into a sequential spill slot pointed to by reg_save_area.
 1787|       |
 1788|     11|                    let size = lower_type_size(mir_type, ctx.mir);
 1789|     11|                    let size = size.max(8);
 1790|       |
 1791|     11|                    let needed_slots = size.div_ceil(8);
 1792|     11|                    let needed_gp = needed_slots * 8;
 1793|       |
 1794|       |                    // Address is always reg_save_area + gp_offset
 1795|     11|                    let offset_64 = ctx.builder.ins().uextend(types::I64, gp_offset);
 1796|     11|                    let addr = ctx.builder.ins().iadd(reg_save_area, offset_64);
 1797|       |
 1798|     11|                    let result = if mir_type.is_aggregate() {
 1799|      5|                        addr // Return address for aggregates
 1800|       |                    } else {
 1801|      6|                        ctx.builder.ins().load(cl_type, MemFlags::new(), addr, 0)
 1802|       |                    };
 1803|       |
 1804|       |                    // Increment gp_offset
 1805|     11|                    let next_gp_increment = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I32, needed_gp as i64);
 1806|     11|                    let next_gp = ctx.builder.ins().iadd(gp_offset, next_gp_increment);
 1807|     11|                    ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), next_gp, ap_addr, 0);
 1808|       |
 1809|       |                    // Sync overflow_area to point to the next slot (needed if we pass ap to standard functions like vprintf)
 1810|       |                    // overflow_area = reg_save_area + next_gp
 1811|     11|                    let next_gp_64 = ctx.builder.ins().uextend(types::I64, next_gp);
 1812|     11|                    let next_overflow = ctx.builder.ins().iadd(reg_save_area, next_gp_64);
 1813|     11|                    ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), next_overflow, ap_addr, 8);
 1814|     11|                    result
 1815|       |                }
 1816|      6|                Rvalue::ArrayLiteral(elements) => {
 1817|      6|                    let dest_addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 1818|      6|                    let MirType::Array { element, layout, .. } = place_mir_type else {
 1819|      0|                        panic!("ArrayLiteral with non-array type");
 1820|       |                    };
 1821|      6|                    let element_mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*element);
 1822|      6|                    let element_clif_type = lower_type(element_mir_type);
 1823|      6|                    let stride = layout.stride as i64;
 1824|       |
 1825|     11|                    for (i, element_op) in elements.iter().enumerate() {
                                                         ^6              ^6
 1826|     11|                        let offset = i as i64 * stride;
 1827|     11|                        let element_dest_addr = if offset == 0 {
 1828|      6|                            dest_addr
 1829|       |                        } else {
 1830|      5|                            ctx.builder.ins().iadd_imm(dest_addr, offset)
 1831|       |                        };
 1832|       |
 1833|     11|                        if element_mir_type.is_aggregate() {
 1834|      2|                            let src_addr = emit_operand(element_op, ctx, types::I64);
 1835|      2|                            let size = lower_type_size(element_mir_type, ctx.mir) as i64;
 1836|      2|                            emit_memcpy(element_dest_addr, src_addr, size, ctx.builder, ctx.module);
 1837|      9|                        } else {
 1838|      9|                            let val = emit_operand(element_op, ctx, element_clif_type.unwrap());
 1839|      9|                            ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), val, element_dest_addr, 0);
 1840|      9|                        }
 1841|       |                    }
 1842|      6|                    return;
 1843|       |                }
 1844|      7|                Rvalue::StructLiteral(fields) => {
 1845|      7|                    let dest_addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 1846|       |                    let MirType::Record {
 1847|      7|                        layout, field_types, ..
 1848|      7|                    } = place_mir_type
 1849|       |                    else {
 1850|      0|                        panic!("StructLiteral with non-record type");
 1851|       |                    };
 1852|       |
 1853|       |                    // Zero-initialize the entire struct first to ensure uninitialized members are zero
 1854|      7|                    let struct_size = layout.size as i64;
 1855|      7|                    emit_memset(dest_addr, 0, struct_size, ctx.builder, ctx.module);
 1856|       |
 1857|     14|                    for (field_idx, element_op) in fields.iter() {
                                                                 ^7     ^7
 1858|     14|                        let offset = layout.field_offsets[*field_idx] as i64;
 1859|     14|                        let field_dest_addr = if offset == 0 {
 1860|      7|                            dest_addr
 1861|       |                        } else {
 1862|      7|                            ctx.builder.ins().iadd_imm(dest_addr, offset)
 1863|       |                        };
 1864|       |
 1865|     14|                        let field_mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(field_types[*field_idx]);
 1866|     14|                        if field_mir_type.is_aggregate() {
 1867|      4|                            let src_addr = emit_operand(element_op, ctx, types::I64);
 1868|      4|                            let size = lower_type_size(field_mir_type, ctx.mir) as i64;
 1869|      4|                            emit_memcpy(field_dest_addr, src_addr, size, ctx.builder, ctx.module);
 1870|     10|                        } else {
 1871|     10|                            let field_clif_type = lower_type(field_mir_type).unwrap();
 1872|     10|                            let val = emit_operand(element_op, ctx, field_clif_type);
 1873|     10|                            ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), val, field_dest_addr, 0);
 1874|     10|                        }
 1875|       |                    }
 1876|      7|                    return;
 1877|       |                }
 1878|      0|                Rvalue::AtomicLoad(ptr, _order) => {

 1882|      0|                Rvalue::AtomicExchange(ptr, val, _order) => {

 1885|      0|                    let val_op = emit_operand(val, ctx, val_type);

 1887|      0|                        .ins()

 1889|       |                }
 1890|      0|                Rvalue::AtomicCompareExchange(ptr, expected, desired, _, _, _) => {

 1894|       |

 1896|      0|                        .ins()

 1898|       |                }
 1899|      0|                Rvalue::AtomicFetchOp(op, ptr, val, _order) => {
 1900|      0|                    let ptr_val = emit_operand(ptr, ctx, types::I64);
 1901|      0|                    let val_type = lower_operand_type(val, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1902|      0|                    let val_op = emit_operand(val, ctx, val_type);
 1903|       |
 1904|      0|                    let rmw_op = match op {
 1905|      0|                        BinaryIntOp::Add => AtomicRmwOp::Add,
 1906|      0|                        BinaryIntOp::Sub => AtomicRmwOp::Sub,
 1907|      0|                        BinaryIntOp::BitAnd => AtomicRmwOp::And,
 1908|      0|                        BinaryIntOp::BitOr => AtomicRmwOp::Or,
 1909|      0|                        BinaryIntOp::BitXor => AtomicRmwOp::Xor,
 1910|      0|                        _ => panic!("Unsupported atomic fetch op: {:?}", op),
 1911|       |                    };
 1912|       |
 1913|      0|                    ctx.builder
 1914|      0|                        .ins()
 1915|      0|                        .atomic_rmw(expected_type, MemFlags::new(), rmw_op, ptr_val, val_op)
 1916|       |                }
 1917|       |            };
 1918|       |
 1919|       |            // Now, assign the resolved value to the place
 1920|    207|            let value_type = ctx.builder.func.dfg.value_type(rvalue_result);
 1921|       |            // Ensure value matches expected type (truncating if necessary)
 1922|    207|            let value = emit_type_conversion(
 1923|    207|                rvalue_result,
 1924|    207|                value_type,
 1925|    207|                expected_type,
 1926|    207|                place_mir_type.is_signed(),
 1927|    207|                ctx.builder,
 1928|       |            );
 1929|       |            {
 1930|    207|                let mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(place_type_id);
 1931|       |
 1932|    207|                if mir_type.is_aggregate() {
 1933|     12|                    let dest_addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 1934|     12|                    let size = lower_type_size(mir_type, ctx.mir) as i64;
 1935|     12|                    emit_memcpy(dest_addr, value, size, ctx.builder, ctx.module);
 1936|     12|                } else {
 1937|    195|                    match place {
 1938|    186|                        Place::Local(local_id) => {
 1939|       |                            // Check if this local has a stack slot (non-void types)
 1940|    186|                            if let Some(stack_slot) = ctx.stack_slots.get(local_id) {
 1941|    186|                                ctx.builder.ins().stack_store(value, *stack_slot, 0);
 1942|    186|                            }
                                          ^0
 1943|       |                            // Else: This local doesn't have a stack slot (likely a void type)
 1944|       |                            // or it's optimized out. In MIR, we assume it's valid to ignore.
 1945|       |                        }
 1946|       |                        _ => {
 1947|       |                            // This covers StructField, ArrayIndex, Deref, and Global assignments
 1948|      9|                            {
 1949|      9|                                let addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 1950|      9|                                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), value, addr, 0);
 1951|      9|                            }
 1952|       |                        }
 1953|       |                    }
 1954|       |                }
 1955|       |            }
 1956|       |        }
 1957|       |
 1958|      3|        MirStmt::Store(operand, place) => {
 1959|       |            // We need to determine the correct type for the operand
 1960|      3|            let place_type_id = lower_place_type_id(place, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1961|      3|            let place_type = ctx.mir.get_type(place_type_id);
 1962|      3|            let cranelift_type = lower_type(place_type).expect("Cannot store to a void type");
 1963|       |
 1964|      3|            let value = emit_operand(operand, ctx, cranelift_type);
 1965|       |
 1966|       |            // Now, store the value into the place
 1967|      3|            match place {
 1968|      2|                Place::Local(local_id) => {
 1969|      2|                    let stack_slot = ctx
 1970|      2|                        .stack_slots
 1971|      2|                        .get(local_id)
 1972|      2|                        .ok_or_else(|| {
                                                     ^0
 1973|      0|                            format!(
 1974|      0|                                "Stack slot not found for local {} in function {}",
 1975|      0|                                local_id.get(),
 1976|       |                                ctx.func.name
 1977|       |                            )
 1978|      0|                        })
 1979|      2|                        .expect("Failed to declare data");
 1980|      2|                    ctx.builder.ins().stack_store(value, *stack_slot, 0);
 1981|       |                }
 1982|      1|                _ => {
 1983|      1|                    // For other places, resolve to an address and store
 1984|      1|                    let addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 1985|      1|                    ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), value, addr, 0);
 1986|      1|                }
 1987|       |            }
 1988|       |        }
 1989|     54|        MirStmt::Call { target, args, dest } => {
 1990|     54|            if let Some(dest_place) = dest {
                                      ^5
 1991|       |                // Call with destination - need to store the result
 1992|      5|                let dest_type_id = lower_place_type_id(dest_place, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 1993|      5|                let dest_mir_type = ctx.mir.get_type(dest_type_id);

 1995|      5|                    Some(t) => t,
 1996|      0|                    None if dest_mir_type.is_aggregate() => types::I64,
 1997|      0|                    None => panic!("Cannot assign to void type"),
 1998|       |                };

 2000|       |

 2003|      5|                if dest_mir_type.is_aggregate() {
 2004|      0|                    // For aggregate types, result is an address, memcpy to dest
 2005|      0|                    let dest_addr = emit_place_addr(dest_place, ctx);

 2012|      5|                                ctx.builder.ins().stack_store(result, *stack_slot, 0);
 2013|      5|                            }
                                          ^0

 2016|      0|                            let addr = emit_place_addr(dest_place, ctx);
 2017|      0|                            ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), result, addr, 0);
 2018|      0|                        }
 2019|       |                    }

 2023|     49|                let _ = emit_function_call(target, args, types::I32, ctx);

 2027|      1|        MirStmt::Alloc(place, type_id) => {
 2028|       |            // Get the size of the type to be allocated
 2029|      1|            let alloc_type = ctx.mir.get_type(*type_id);
 2030|      1|            let size = lower_type_size(alloc_type, ctx.mir);
 2031|       |
 2032|       |            // Define the `malloc` function signature (size_t -> void*)
 2033|       |            // In Cranelift, this would be (i64) -> i64 for a 64-bit target
 2034|      1|            let mut malloc_sig = Signature::new(ctx.builder.func.signature.call_conv);
 2035|      1|            malloc_sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2036|      1|            malloc_sig.returns.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2037|       |
 2038|       |            // Declare `malloc` if not already declared
 2039|      1|            let malloc_func = ctx
 2040|      1|                .module
 2041|      1|                .declare_function("malloc", Linkage::Import, &malloc_sig)
 2042|      1|                .expect("module operation failed");

 2044|       |

 2054|      1|                        ctx.builder.ins().stack_store(alloc_ptr, *stack_slot, 0);
 2055|      1|                    }

 2082|      7|        MirStmt::BuiltinVaStart(place, _operand) => {
 2083|      7|            let ap_addr = emit_place_addr(place, ctx);
 2084|       |
 2085|      7|            if let Some(spill_slot) = ctx.va_spill_slot {
 2086|      7|                let spill_addr = ctx.builder.ins().stack_addr(types::I64, spill_slot, 0);
 2087|      7|
 2088|      7|                // 1. gp_offset
 2089|      7|                // Calculate how many bytes are consumed by fixed parameters
 2090|      7|                let fixed_count = ctx.func.params.len();
 2091|      7|                let gp_val = fixed_count * 8;
 2092|      7|
 2093|      7|                // Clamp to 48 (max GPR registers)
 2094|      7|                let effective_gp = std::cmp::min(gp_val, 48);
 2095|      7|                let gp_const = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I32, effective_gp as i64);
 2096|      7|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), gp_const, ap_addr, 0);
 2097|      7|
 2098|      7|                // 2. fp_offset = 176 (unused as we map everything to I64 GPRs in current hack)
 2099|      7|                let fp_val = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I32, 176);
 2100|      7|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), fp_val, ap_addr, 4);
 2101|      7|
 2102|      7|                // 3. overflow_arg_area
 2103|      7|                // If gp < 48, overflow starts at 48.
 2104|      7|                // If gp >= 48, overflow starts at gp.
 2105|      7|                let overflow_offset = std::cmp::max(gp_val, 48) as i64;
 2106|      7|                let overflow_ptr = ctx.builder.ins().iadd_imm(spill_addr, overflow_offset);
 2107|      7|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), overflow_ptr, ap_addr, 8);
 2108|      7|
 2109|      7|                // 4. reg_save_area
 2110|      7|                // Points to the start of spill slot where we saved all params
 2111|      7|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), spill_addr, ap_addr, 16);
 2112|      7|            } else {
 2113|      0|                // Fallback (should not happen for variadic functions)
 2114|      0|                let zero = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(types::I64, 0);
 2115|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), zero, ap_addr, 8); // overflow
 2116|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().store(MemFlags::new(), zero, ap_addr, 16); // reg_save
 2117|      0|            }
 2118|       |        }
 2119|      0|        MirStmt::AtomicStore(ptr, val, _order) => {
 2120|      0|            let ptr_val = emit_operand(ptr, ctx, types::I64);
 2121|      0|            let val_type = lower_operand_type(val, ctx.mir, ctx.pointee_to_pointer);
 2122|      0|            let val_op = emit_operand(val, ctx, val_type);
 2123|      0|
 2124|      0|            ctx.builder.ins().atomic_store(MemFlags::new(), val_op, ptr_val);
 2125|      0|        }
 2126|      6|        MirStmt::BuiltinVaEnd(_place) => {
 2127|      6|            // No-op for x86_64
 2128|      6|        }
 2129|      1|        MirStmt::BuiltinVaCopy(dest, src) => {
 2130|      1|            let dest_addr = emit_place_addr(dest, ctx);
 2131|      1|            let src_addr = emit_place_addr(src, ctx);
 2132|      1|            // va_list is 24 bytes on x86_64
 2133|      1|            emit_memcpy(dest_addr, src_addr, 24, ctx.builder, ctx.module);
 2134|      1|        }
 2135|       |    }

 2139|    365|fn visit_terminator(terminator: &Terminator, ctx: &mut BodyEmitContext) {
 2140|    365|    match terminator {
 2141|    111|        Terminator::Goto(target) => {
 2142|    111|            let target_cl_block = ctx.clif_blocks.get(target).expect("Target block not found");
 2143|    111|            ctx.builder.ins().jump(*target_cl_block, &[]);
 2144|    111|            ctx.worklist.push(*target);
 2145|    111|        }
 2146|       |
 2147|     87|        Terminator::If(cond, then_bb, else_bb) => {
 2148|     87|            let cond_val = emit_operand(cond, ctx, types::I32);
 2149|     87|
 2150|     87|            let then_cl_block = ctx.clif_blocks.get(then_bb).expect("Then block not found");
 2151|     87|            let else_cl_block = ctx.clif_blocks.get(else_bb).expect("Else block not found");
 2152|     87|
 2153|     87|            ctx.builder
 2154|     87|                .ins()
 2155|     87|                .brif(cond_val, *then_cl_block, &[], *else_cl_block, &[]);
 2156|     87|

 2158|     87|            ctx.worklist.push(*else_bb);
 2159|     87|        }
 2160|       |

 2162|    167|            if let Some(operand) = opt {
                                      ^146

 2164|    146|                    let return_value = emit_operand(operand, ctx, ret_type);
 2165|    146|                    ctx.builder.ins().return_(&[return_value]);
 2166|    146|                } else {
 2167|      0|                    panic!("Returning a value from a void function");
 2168|       |                }
 2169|     21|            } else {
 2170|     21|                ctx.builder.ins().return_(&[]);
 2171|     21|            }
 2172|       |        }
 2173|       |

 2175|       |            // For unreachable, default to appropriate return based on function type
 2176|      0|            if let Some(ret_type) = ctx.return_type {
 2177|      0|                let return_value = ctx.builder.ins().iconst(ret_type, 0i64);
 2178|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().return_(&[return_value]);
 2179|      0|            } else {

 2181|      0|                ctx.builder.ins().return_(&[]);
 2182|      0|            }

 2187|    253|fn lower_function_signature(
 2188|    253|    func: &MirFunction,
 2189|    253|    mir: &MirProgram,
 2190|    253|    func_ctx: &mut Signature,
 2191|    253|) -> (Option<Type>, Vec<Type>) {
 2192|       |    // Set up function signature using the actual return type from MIR
 2193|    253|    func_ctx.params.clear();
 2194|       |
 2195|       |    // Get the return type from MIR and convert to Cranelift type
 2196|    253|    let return_mir_type = mir.get_type(func.return_type);
 2197|    253|    let return_type_opt = match lower_type(return_mir_type) {
 2198|    210|        Some(t) => Some(t),
 2199|     43|        None if return_mir_type.is_aggregate() => Some(types::I64),
                                                           ^0   ^0
 2200|     43|        None => None, // Void
 2201|       |    };
 2202|       |
 2203|       |    // Add parameters from MIR function signature
 2204|    253|    let mut param_types = Vec::new();
 2205|    331|    for &param_id in &func.params {
                       ^78
 2206|     78|        let param_local = mir.get_local(param_id);
 2207|     78|        let mir_type = mir.get_type(param_local.type_id);
 2208|       |
 2209|     78|        if let Some(count) = get_struct_packing(mir_type, mir) {
                                  ^8
 2210|      8|            for _ in 0..count {
 2211|      8|                func_ctx.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2212|      8|                param_types.push(types::I64);
 2213|      8|            }
 2214|      8|            continue;
 2215|     70|        }
 2216|       |
 2217|     70|        if matches!(mir_type, MirType::F80 | MirType::F128) {
 2218|       |            // Split F128/F80 into 2 I64s for internal ABI
 2219|      0|            func_ctx.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2220|      0|            func_ctx.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2221|       |            // Track them as F128 in param_types for visit_function to know,
 2222|       |            // OR track split types? visit_function iterates MIR params.
 2223|       |            // It needs to know it consumes 2 slots.
 2224|       |            // We push F128 to param_types so visit_function knows the logic type.
 2225|       |            // BUT lower_function_signature return type `Vec<Type>` is used by visit_function
 2226|       |            // to append_block_param.
 2227|       |            // So we must push I64, I64 to param_types.
 2228|      0|            param_types.push(types::I64);
 2229|      0|            param_types.push(types::I64);
 2230|      0|            continue;
 2231|     70|        }
 2232|       |
 2233|     70|        let param_type = match lower_type(mir_type) {
 2234|     68|            Some(t) => t,
 2235|      2|            None if mir_type.is_aggregate() => types::I64,
 2236|      0|            None => panic!("Unsupported parameter type for local {}", param_id.get()),
 2237|       |        };
 2238|     70|        func_ctx.params.push(AbiParam::new(param_type));
 2239|     70|        param_types.push(param_type);
 2240|       |    }
 2241|       |
 2242|    253|    if func.is_variadic && matches!(func.kind, MirFunctionKind::Defined) {
                                         ^24      ^40
 2243|       |        // Add 32 total I64 parameters to capture variadic arguments (6 GPRs + 26 stack slots)
 2244|       |        // This allows variadic functions to receive many struct args that expand to multiple I64s
 2245|     16|        let fixed_params_count = func.params.len();
 2246|     16|        let total_variadic_slots = 128; // Support up to 128 I64 slots for variadic args
 2247|     16|        if fixed_params_count < total_variadic_slots {
 2248|  2.03k|            for _ in 0..(total_variadic_slots - fixed_params_count) {
                                      ^16
 2249|  2.03k|                func_ctx.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64));
 2250|  2.03k|            }
 2251|      0|        }
 2252|    237|    }
 2253|       |
 2254|       |    // Only add return parameter if the function has a non-void return type
 2255|    253|    if let Some(return_type) = return_type_opt {
                              ^210
 2256|    210|        func_ctx.returns.push(AbiParam::new(return_type));
 2257|    210|    }
                  ^43
 2258|       |
 2259|    253|    (return_type_opt, param_types)

 2262|    114|fn emit_stack_slots(
 2263|    114|    func: &MirFunction,
 2264|    114|    mir: &MirProgram,
 2265|    114|    builder: &mut FunctionBuilder,
 2266|    114|    clif_stack_slots: &mut HashMap<LocalId, StackSlot>,
 2267|    114|) {

                       ^222

 2276|    222|        let size = lower_type_size(local_type, mir);
 2277|       |
 2278|       |        // Don't allocate space for zero-sized types
 2279|    222|        if size > 0 {

 2281|    222|            clif_stack_slots.insert(local_id, slot);
 2282|    222|        }

                           ^72

 2387|      0|                continue;

 2390|       |            // Use local linkage for string literals to avoid multiple definition errors

 2392|     39|                Linkage::Local

 2394|     33|                Linkage::Export

 2506|       |    /// Lower a MIR function to Cranelift IR using 3-phase algorithm
 2507|    114|    fn visit_function(&mut self, func_id: MirFunctionId) {
 2508|    114|        let func = self.mir.get_function(func_id);
 2509|       |        // Create a fresh context for this function
 2510|    114|        let mut func_ctx = self.module.make_context();
 2511|       |
 2512|    114|        let (return_type_opt, param_types) = lower_function_signature(func, &self.mir, &mut func_ctx.func.signature);
 2513|       |
 2514|       |        // Create a function builder with the fresh context
 2515|    114|        let mut builder = FunctionBuilder::new(&mut func_ctx.func, &mut self.builder_context);
 2516|       |
 2517|    114|        emit_stack_slots(func, &self.mir, &mut builder, &mut self.clif_stack_slots);
 2518|       |
 2519|       |        // PHASE 1️⃣ — Create all Cranelift blocks first (no instructions)
 2520|    114|        let mut clif_blocks = HashMap::new();
 2521|       |
 2522|    480|        for &block_id in &func.blocks {
                           ^366
 2523|    366|            clif_blocks.insert(block_id, builder.create_block());
 2524|    366|        }
 2525|       |
 2526|       |        // PHASE 2️⃣ — Lower block content (without sealing)
 2527|    114|        let mut va_spill_slot = None;
 2528|       |
 2529|       |        // Use worklist algorithm for proper traversal
 2530|    114|        let mut worklist = vec![func.entry_block.expect("Defined function must have entry block")];
 2531|    114|        let mut visited = HashSet::new();
 2532|       |
 2533|    513|        while let Some(current_block_id) = worklist.pop() {
                                     ^399
 2534|    399|            if visited.contains(&current_block_id) {
 2535|     34|                continue;
 2536|    365|            }
 2537|    365|            visited.insert(current_block_id);
 2538|       |
 2539|    365|            let clif_block = clif_blocks.get(&current_block_id).expect("Block not found in mapping");
 2540|    365|            builder.switch_to_block(*clif_block);
 2541|       |
 2542|       |            // Setup entry block parameters
 2543|    365|            if Some(current_block_id) == func.entry_block {
 2544|       |                // Step 1: Add ALL block parameters first (fixed params)
 2545|    140|                for &param_type in &param_types {
                                   ^26
 2546|     26|                    builder.append_block_param(*clif_block, param_type);
 2547|     26|                }
 2548|       |
 2549|       |                // Step 2: Add variadic block parameters if needed (still before any instructions)

 3252|       |/// Internal helper for variadic calls on x86_64 SysV
 3253|     25|fn emit_cendol_set_al(module: &mut ObjectModule) -> FuncId {
 3254|     25|    let mut sig = Signature::new(cranelift::codegen::isa::CallConv::SystemV);
 3255|     25|    sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64)); // count
 3256|     25|    sig.params.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64)); // addr
 3257|     25|    sig.returns.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64)); // count (RAX)
 3258|     25|    sig.returns.push(AbiParam::new(types::I64)); // addr (RDX)
 3259|       |
 3260|     25|    let func_id = module
 3261|     25|        .declare_function("__cendol_set_al", Linkage::Local, &sig)
 3262|     25|        .expect("Failed to declare __cendol_set_al");
 3263|       |
 3264|     25|    let mut ctx = cranelift::codegen::Context::new();
 3265|     25|    ctx.func.signature = sig;
 3266|       |
 3267|     25|    let mut func_ctx = FunctionBuilderContext::new();
 3268|     25|    let mut builder = FunctionBuilder::new(&mut ctx.func, &mut func_ctx);
 3269|       |
 3270|     25|    let block = builder.create_block();
 3271|     25|    builder.append_block_params_for_function_params(block);
 3272|     25|    builder.switch_to_block(block);
 3273|     25|    builder.seal_block(block);
 3274|       |
 3275|     25|    let count = builder.block_params(block)[0];
 3276|     25|    let addr = builder.block_params(block)[1];
 3277|     25|    builder.ins().return_(&[count, addr]);
 3278|       |
 3279|     25|    builder.finalize();
 3280|       |
 3281|     25|    module
 3282|     25|        .define_function(func_id, &mut ctx)
 3283|     25|        .expect("Failed to define __cendol_set_al");
 3284|       |
 3285|     25|    func_id
 3286|     25|}

/app/src/codegen/link_gen.rs:
    1|       |//! Linking executable/library from object files.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides functionality for linking compiled object files
    4|       |//! into final executables or libraries using an external linker (clang).
    5|       |
    6|       |use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    7|       |use std::process::Command;
    8|       |
    9|       |/// Configuration for the linker.
   10|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   11|       |pub(crate) struct LinkConfig {
   12|       |    /// Output file path
   13|       |    pub output_path: PathBuf,
   14|       |    /// Library search paths (-L)
   15|       |    pub library_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
   16|       |    /// Libraries to link (-l)
   17|       |    pub libraries: Vec<String>,
   18|       |    /// Optimization level (e.g., "0", "1", "2", "3", "s")
   19|       |    pub optimization: Option<String>,
   20|       |    /// Include debug info (-g)
   21|       |    pub debug_info: bool,
   22|       |    /// Verbose output
   23|       |    pub verbose: bool,
   24|       |    /// Use a specific linker
   25|       |    pub fuse_ld: Option<String>,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Error type for linking operations.
   29|       |#[derive(Debug)]
   30|       |pub(crate) enum LinkError {
   31|       |    /// IO error during linking
   32|       |    IoError(String),
   33|       |    /// Linker returned non-zero exit code
   34|       |    LinkFailed,
   35|       |}
   36|       |
   37|       |impl std::fmt::Display for LinkError {
   38|      2|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   39|      2|        match self {
   40|      1|            LinkError::IoError(msg) => write!(f, "IO error: {}", msg),
   41|      1|            LinkError::LinkFailed => write!(f, "Linking failed"),
   42|       |        }
   43|      2|    }
   44|       |}
   45|       |
   46|       |impl std::error::Error for LinkError {}
   47|       |
   48|       |/// Links object files into an executable or library.
   49|       |pub(crate) struct LinkGen;
   50|       |
   51|       |impl LinkGen {
   52|       |    /// Link object files into an executable using clang.
   53|       |    ///
   54|       |    /// # Arguments
   55|       |    /// * `object_files` - Paths to object files to link
   56|       |    /// * `config` - Linking configuration
   57|       |    ///
   58|       |    /// # Returns
   59|       |    /// * `Ok(())` on success
   60|       |    /// * `Err(LinkError)` on failure
   61|     49|    pub(crate) fn link<P: AsRef<Path>>(object_files: &[P], config: &LinkConfig) -> Result<(), LinkError> {
   62|     49|        if object_files.is_empty() {
   63|      0|            return Ok(());
   64|     49|        }
   65|       |
   66|     49|        let mut clang_cmd = Command::new("clang");
   67|       |
   68|       |        // Add object files
   69|     98|        for obj in object_files {
                          ^49
   70|     49|            clang_cmd.arg(obj.as_ref());
   71|     49|        }
   72|       |
   73|       |        // Add library paths
   74|     49|        for path in &config.library_paths {
                          ^0
   75|      0|            clang_cmd.arg("-L").arg(path);
   76|      0|        }
   77|       |
   78|       |        // Add libraries
   79|     49|        for lib in &config.libraries {
                          ^0
   80|      0|            clang_cmd.arg(format!("-l{}", lib));
   81|      0|        }
   82|       |
   83|       |        // Add optimization flags if present
   84|     49|        if let Some(opt) = &config.optimization {
                                  ^0
   85|      0|            clang_cmd.arg(format!("-O{}", opt));
   86|     49|        }
   87|       |
   88|       |        // Add debug info if requested
   89|     49|        if config.debug_info {
   90|      0|            clang_cmd.arg("-g");
   91|     49|        }
   92|       |
   93|     49|        if let Some(linker) = &config.fuse_ld {
                                  ^0
   94|      0|            clang_cmd.arg(format!("-fuse-ld={}", linker));
   95|     49|        }
   96|       |
   97|       |        // Default to linking math library
   98|     49|        if !config.libraries.contains(&"m".to_string()) {
   99|     49|            clang_cmd.arg("-lm");
  100|     49|        }
                      ^0
  101|       |
  102|       |        // We no longer need to suppress linker warnings since we now use reachability
  103|       |        // analysis to only emit used symbols. This also ensures that "missing main"
  104|       |        // and other linker errors are correctly reported.
  105|       |
  106|       |        // Set output path
  107|     49|        clang_cmd.arg("-o").arg(&config.output_path);
  108|       |
  109|     49|        if config.verbose {
  110|      0|            println!("Executing linker: {:?}", clang_cmd);
  111|     49|        }
  112|       |
  113|     49|        let status = clang_cmd
  114|     49|            .status()
  115|     49|            .map_err(|e| LinkError::IoError(format!("Failed to execute clang for linking: {}", e)))?;
                                                          ^0      ^0                                             ^0
  116|       |
  117|     49|        if config.verbose {
  118|      0|            println!("Linker status: {:?}", status);
  119|     49|        }
  120|       |
  121|     49|        if !status.success() {
  122|      0|            eprintln!("cendol: error: linker exited with status {}", status);
  123|      0|            return Err(LinkError::LinkFailed);
  124|     49|        }
  125|       |
  126|       |        // Set executable permissions on the output file (Unix only)
  127|       |        #[cfg(unix)]
  128|       |        {
  129|       |            use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
  130|     49|            if let Ok(metadata) = std::fs::metadata(&config.output_path) {
  131|     49|                let mut permissions = metadata.permissions();
  132|     49|                permissions.set_mode(0o755); // rwxr-xr-x
  133|     49|                if let Err(e) = std::fs::set_permissions(&config.output_path, permissions) {
                                         ^0
  134|      0|                    eprintln!("Warning: Failed to set executable permissions: {}", e);
  135|     49|                }
  136|      0|            }
  137|       |        }
  138|       |
  139|     49|        Ok(())
  140|     49|    }
  141|       |}
  142|       |
  143|       |#[cfg(test)]
  144|       |mod tests {
  145|       |    use super::*;
  146|       |

  149|      1|        let err = LinkError::IoError("test error".to_string());
  150|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{}", err), "IO error: test error");
  151|       |
  152|      1|        let err = LinkError::LinkFailed;
  153|      1|        assert_eq!(format!("{}", err), "Linking failed");

   83|    360|    pub(crate) fn visit_module(&mut self) -> MirProgram {
   84|    360|        debug!("Starting semantic analysis and MIR construction (complete)");
                             ^0
   85|    360|        let root = self.ast.get_root();

   90|    360|        let builder = std::mem::replace(

   93|       |        );

   95|       |

  109|  2.54k|    pub(crate) fn visit_node(&mut self, node_ref: NodeRef) {
  110|  2.54k|        let old_scope = self.current_scope_id;

  203|    360|    fn predeclare_global_functions(&mut self) {
  204|       |        // Ensure all global functions (including declarations) have a MIR representation.
  205|       |        // This is done before traversing the AST to ensure that function calls
  206|       |        // can be resolved even if the function is defined later in the file or is external.

  209|       |
  210|       |        // Sort by symbol name to ensure deterministic order for snapshot tests

                      ^360           ^360
  212|       |
  213|    944|        for sym_ref in global_symbols {
                          ^584

  216|       |                (
  217|    584|                    symbol.name,
  218|    584|                    symbol.type_info,
  219|    584|                    matches!(symbol.kind, SymbolKind::Function { .. }),
                                  ^167
  220|    584|                    symbol.def_state == DefinitionState::Defined,
  221|       |                )
  222|       |            };
  223|       |
  224|    584|            if is_function {

  226|    417|                    .mir_builder
  227|    417|                    .get_functions()

  229|    417|                    .any(|(_, f)| f.name == symbol_name)

  230|       |                {

  236|    417|                    return_type,

  283|    121|    pub(crate) fn lower_condition(&mut self, condition: NodeRef) -> Operand {

  288|    121|        self.apply_conversions(cond_operand, condition, cond_mir_ty)
  289|    121|    }

  300|    367|    fn visit_function(&mut self, function_data: &FunctionData) {

  483|     78|    fn visit_if_stmt(&mut self, if_stmt: &IfStmt) {

  491|       |

  510|     16|    fn emit_loop_generic<I, C, B, Inc>(
  511|     16|        &mut self,
  512|     16|        init_fn: Option<I>,
  513|     16|        cond_fn: Option<C>,
  514|     16|        body_fn: B,
  515|     16|        inc_fn: Option<Inc>,
  516|     16|        is_do_while: bool,
  517|     16|    ) where
  518|     16|        I: FnOnce(&mut Self),
  519|     16|        C: FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Operand,
  520|     16|        B: FnOnce(&mut Self),
  521|     16|        Inc: FnOnce(&mut Self),
  522|       |    {
  523|     16|        let cond_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  524|     16|        let body_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  525|     16|        let increment_block = if inc_fn.is_some() {
  526|      6|            self.mir_builder.create_block()
  527|       |        } else {
  528|       |            // If there's no increment block (e.g. while/do-while), "continue" goes to condition
  529|     10|            cond_block
  530|       |        };
  531|     16|        let exit_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  532|       |
  533|       |        // Continue target depends on whether we have an increment step
  534|     16|        let continue_target = increment_block;
  535|       |
  536|     16|        self.with_loop_targets(exit_block, continue_target, |this| {
  537|     16|            if let Some(init) = init_fn {
                                      ^5
  538|      5|                init(this);
  539|     11|            }
  540|       |
  541|     16|            if is_do_while {
  542|      3|                // do-while: jump straight to body
  543|      3|                this.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(body_block));
  544|     13|            } else {
  545|     13|                // for/while: jump to condition
  546|     13|                this.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(cond_block));
  547|     13|            }
  548|       |
  549|       |            // Condition block
  550|     16|            this.mir_builder.set_current_block(cond_block);
  551|     16|            if let Some(cond) = cond_fn {
                                      ^15
  552|     15|                let cond_val = cond(this);
  553|     15|                this.mir_builder
  554|     15|                    .set_terminator(Terminator::If(cond_val, body_block, exit_block));
  555|     15|            } else {
  556|      1|                // No condition (e.g. for(;;)) -> infinite loop
  557|      1|                this.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(body_block));
  558|      1|            }
  559|       |
  560|       |            // Body block
  561|     16|            this.mir_builder.set_current_block(body_block);
  562|     16|            body_fn(this);
  563|       |
  564|       |            // After body, jump to increment (or condition if no increment)
  565|     16|            if !this.mir_builder.current_block_has_terminator() {
  566|     16|                this.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(increment_block));
  567|     16|            }
                          ^0
  568|       |
  569|       |            // Increment block (only if it exists and is distinct from cond_block)
  570|     16|            if let Some(inc) = inc_fn {
                                      ^6
  571|      6|                this.mir_builder.set_current_block(increment_block);
  572|      6|                inc(this);
  573|      6|                this.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(cond_block));
  574|     10|            }
  575|     16|        });
  576|       |
  577|     16|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(exit_block);
  578|     16|        self.current_block = Some(exit_block);
  579|     16|    }
  580|       |
  581|      5|    fn visit_while_stmt(&mut self, while_stmt: &WhileStmt) {
  582|      5|        self.emit_loop_generic(
  583|      5|            None::<fn(&mut Self)>,
  584|      5|            Some(|this: &mut Self| this.lower_condition(while_stmt.condition)),
  585|      5|            |this| this.visit_node(while_stmt.body),
  586|      5|            None::<fn(&mut Self)>,
  587|       |            false,
  588|       |        );
  589|      5|    }
