  591|      3|    fn visit_do_while_stmt(&mut self, body: NodeRef, condition: NodeRef) {
  592|      3|        self.emit_loop_generic(
  593|      3|            None::<fn(&mut Self)>,
  594|      3|            Some(|this: &mut Self| this.lower_condition(condition)),
  595|      3|            |this| this.visit_node(body),
  596|      3|            None::<fn(&mut Self)>,
  597|       |            true,
  598|       |        );
  599|      3|    }
  600|       |
  601|      8|    fn visit_for_stmt(&mut self, for_stmt: &ForStmt) {
  602|      8|        let init_fn = for_stmt.init.map(|init| move |this: &mut Self| this.visit_node(init));
                                                                                    ^5   ^5         ^5
  603|       |
  604|      8|        let cond_fn = for_stmt
  605|      8|            .condition
  606|      8|            .map(|cond| move |this: &mut Self| this.lower_condition(cond));
                                                             ^7   ^7              ^7
  607|       |
  608|      8|        let inc_fn = for_stmt.increment.map(|inc| {
                                                                ^6
  609|      6|            move |this: &mut Self| {
  610|      6|                this.visit_expression(inc, false);
  611|      6|            }
  612|      6|        });
  613|       |
  614|      8|        self.emit_loop_generic(init_fn, cond_fn, |this| this.visit_node(for_stmt.body), inc_fn, false);
  615|      8|    }

  617|      4|    fn visit_switch_stmt(&mut self, cond: NodeRef, body: NodeRef) {
  618|      4|        let cond_op = self.visit_expression(cond, true);
  619|      4|        let cond_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(cond).unwrap();
  620|      4|        let mir_ty = self.lower_qual_type(cond_ty);
  621|      4|        let cond_op = self.apply_conversions(cond_op, cond, mir_ty);
  622|       |
  623|      4|        let cond_ty_id = self.get_operand_type(&cond_op);
  624|       |
  625|      4|        let merge_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  626|       |
  627|      4|        let saved_break = self.break_target;
  628|      4|        self.break_target = Some(merge_block);
  629|       |
  630|       |        // Collect cases
  631|      4|        let cases = self.collect_switch_cases(body);
  632|       |
  633|       |        // Create blocks for cases
  634|      4|        let mut case_blocks = Vec::new();
  635|      4|        let mut default_block = None;
  636|       |
  637|     15|        for (node, start_val, end_val) in cases {
                           ^11   ^11        ^11
  638|     11|            let block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  639|     11|            self.switch_case_map.insert(node, block);
  640|       |
  641|     11|            if let Some(start) = start_val {
                                      ^8
  642|      8|                case_blocks.push((start, end_val, block));
  643|      8|            } else {
  644|      3|                default_block = Some(block);
  645|      3|            }
  646|       |        }
  647|       |
  648|       |        // Generate dispatch
  649|      4|        let fallback_block = default_block.unwrap_or(merge_block);
  650|      4|        let bool_type_id = self.lower_type(self.registry.type_bool);
  651|       |
  652|     12|        for (start_id, end_id_opt, target_block) in case_blocks {
                           ^8        ^8          ^8
  653|      8|            let next_test_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  654|      8|            let start_const = self.mir_builder.get_constants().get(&start_id).unwrap().clone();
  655|       |
  656|       |            // Re-create constant with the same type as condition to ensure safe comparison
  657|      8|            let start_op = Operand::Constant(start_id);
  658|      8|            let cast_start_op = if start_const.ty != cond_ty_id {
  659|      0|                Operand::Cast(cond_ty_id, Box::new(start_op))
  660|       |            } else {
  661|      8|                start_op
  662|       |            };
  663|       |
  664|      8|            let cmp_op = if let Some(end_id) = end_id_opt {
                                                   ^2
  665|       |                // Range check: x >= start && x <= end
  666|      2|                let end_const = self.mir_builder.get_constants().get(&end_id).unwrap().clone();
  667|      2|                let end_op = Operand::Constant(end_id);
  668|      2|                let cast_end_op = if end_const.ty != cond_ty_id {
  669|      0|                    Operand::Cast(cond_ty_id, Box::new(end_op))
  670|       |                } else {
  671|      2|                    end_op
  672|       |                };
  673|       |
  674|       |                // GE: x >= start
  675|      2|                let ge_rvalue = Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::Ge, cond_op.clone(), cast_start_op);
  676|      2|                let (_, ge_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(ge_rvalue, bool_type_id);
  677|      2|                let ge_op = Operand::Copy(Box::new(ge_place));
  678|       |
  679|       |                // LE: x <= end
  680|      2|                let le_rvalue = Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::Le, cond_op.clone(), cast_end_op);
  681|      2|                let (_, le_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(le_rvalue, bool_type_id);
  682|      2|                let le_op = Operand::Copy(Box::new(le_place));
  683|       |
  684|       |                // AND: (x >= start) && (x <= end)
  685|       |                // Use BitAnd which works for boolean types (conceptually i1/i8 0 or 1)
  686|      2|                let and_rvalue = Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::BitAnd, ge_op, le_op);
  687|      2|                let (_, and_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(and_rvalue, bool_type_id);
  688|      2|                Operand::Copy(Box::new(and_place))
  689|       |            } else {
  690|       |                // Single value check: x == val
  691|      6|                let eq_rvalue = Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::Eq, cond_op.clone(), cast_start_op);
  692|      6|                let (_, eq_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(eq_rvalue, bool_type_id);
  693|      6|                Operand::Copy(Box::new(eq_place))
  694|       |            };
  695|       |
  696|      8|            self.mir_builder
  697|      8|                .set_terminator(Terminator::If(cmp_op, target_block, next_test_block));
  698|       |
  699|      8|            self.mir_builder.set_current_block(next_test_block);
  700|      8|            self.current_block = Some(next_test_block);
  701|       |        }
  702|       |
  703|       |        // Final jump to default or merge
  704|      4|        self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(fallback_block));
  705|       |
  706|       |        // Lower body
  707|       |        // Start a dummy unreachable block for the body entry (to catch unreachable statements)
  708|      4|        let body_entry_dummy = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  709|       |        // Do not jump to it. It is unreachable.
  710|       |
  711|      4|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(body_entry_dummy);
  712|      4|        self.current_block = Some(body_entry_dummy);
  713|       |
  714|      4|        self.visit_node(body);
  715|       |
  716|       |        // If body falls through, go to merge
  717|      4|        if self.mir_builder.current_block_has_terminator() == false {
  718|      0|            // Check if terminator is Unreachable (default) - if so, we can replace it or just leave it?
  719|      0|            // `current_block_has_terminator` returns false if it is Unreachable.
  720|      0|            // But if we are in body_entry_dummy and it's empty, we shouldn't jump to merge.
  721|      0|            // Actually, if execution falls through the body, it should hit merge.
  722|      0|            // But valid C code falling through end of switch goes to next stmt (merge).
  723|      0|            self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(merge_block));
  724|      4|        }
  725|       |
  726|      4|        self.break_target = saved_break;
  727|      4|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(merge_block);
  728|      4|        self.current_block = Some(merge_block);
  729|      4|    }
  730|       |
  731|     11|    fn visit_case_default_stmt(&mut self, node: NodeRef, stmt: NodeRef) {
  732|     11|        let target_block = *self.switch_case_map.get(&node).expect("Case/Default not mapped");
  733|       |
  734|       |        // Fallthrough from previous block
  735|     11|        if !self.mir_builder.current_block_has_terminator() {
  736|      4|            self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(target_block));
  737|      7|        }
  738|       |
  739|     11|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(target_block);
  740|     11|        self.current_block = Some(target_block);
  741|       |
  742|     11|        self.visit_node(stmt);
  743|     11|    }

  745|      4|    fn collect_switch_cases(&mut self, node: NodeRef) -> Vec<(NodeRef, Option<ConstValueId>, Option<ConstValueId>)> {
  746|      4|        let mut cases = Vec::new();
  747|      4|        self.collect_switch_cases_recursive(node, &mut cases);
  748|      4|        cases
  749|      4|    }
  750|       |
  751|     53|    fn collect_switch_cases_recursive(
  752|     53|        &mut self,
  753|     53|        node: NodeRef,
  754|     53|        cases: &mut Vec<(NodeRef, Option<ConstValueId>, Option<ConstValueId>)>,
  755|     53|    ) {
  756|     53|        let kind = *self.ast.get_kind(node);
  757|     53|        match kind {
  758|      6|            NodeKind::Case(expr, stmt) => {
  759|      6|                let op = self.visit_expression(expr, true);
  760|      6|                let val = self.operand_to_const_id_strict(op, "Case label must be constant");
  761|      6|                cases.push((node, Some(val), None));
  762|      6|                self.collect_switch_cases_recursive(stmt, cases);
  763|      6|            }
  764|      2|            NodeKind::CaseRange(start, end, stmt) => {
  765|      2|                let start_op = self.visit_expression(start, true);
  766|      2|                let start_val = self.operand_to_const_id_strict(start_op, "Case range start must be constant");
  767|      2|
  768|      2|                let end_op = self.visit_expression(end, true);
  769|      2|                let end_val = self.operand_to_const_id_strict(end_op, "Case range end must be constant");
  770|      2|
  771|      2|                cases.push((node, Some(start_val), Some(end_val)));
  772|      2|                self.collect_switch_cases_recursive(stmt, cases);
  773|      2|            }
  774|      3|            NodeKind::Default(stmt) => {
  775|      3|                cases.push((node, None, None));
  776|      3|                self.collect_switch_cases_recursive(stmt, cases);
  777|      3|            }
  778|      0|            NodeKind::Switch(..) => {
  779|      0|                // Do not recurse into nested switch
  780|      0|            }
  781|       |            _ => {
  782|     42|                kind.visit_children(|child| self.collect_switch_cases_recursive(child, cases));
                                                          ^38  ^38                            ^38    ^38
  783|       |            }
  784|       |        }
  785|     53|    }

  787|    340|    pub(crate) fn emit_assignment(&mut self, place: Place, operand: Operand) {
  788|    340|        if self.mir_builder.current_block_has_terminator() {
  789|      0|            return;
  790|    340|        }
  791|       |
  792|       |        // Avoid identity assignments like %x = %x
  793|    340|        if let Operand::Copy(box_place) = &operand
                                           ^122
  794|    122|            && **box_place == place
  795|       |        {
  796|     20|            return;
  797|    320|        }
  798|       |
  799|    320|        let rvalue = Rvalue::Use(operand);
  800|    320|        let stmt = MirStmt::Assign(place, rvalue);
  801|    320|        self.mir_builder.add_statement(stmt);
  802|    340|    }

  808|  7.85k|    pub(crate) fn lower_type(&mut self, type_ref: TypeRef) -> TypeId {

  866|     69|    fn lower_recursive_record_pattern(
  867|     69|        &mut self,
  868|     69|        type_ref: TypeRef,
  869|     69|        tag: Option<NameId>,
  870|     69|        is_union: bool,
  871|     69|        is_complete: bool,
  872|     69|    ) -> TypeId {
  873|       |        // Begin conversion: reserve a placeholder TypeId so recursive references can point to it.
  874|     69|        self.type_conversion_in_progress.insert(type_ref);
  875|     69|        let placeholder_name = NameId::new(format!("__recursive_placeholder_{}", type_ref.get()));
  876|     69|        let placeholder_type = MirType::Record {
  877|     69|            name: placeholder_name,
  878|     69|            field_types: Vec::new(),
  879|     69|            field_names: Vec::new(),
  880|     69|            is_union: false,
  881|     69|            layout: MirRecordLayout {
  882|     69|                size: 0,
  883|     69|                alignment: 0,
  884|     69|                field_offsets: Vec::new(),
  885|     69|            },
  886|     69|        };
  887|     69|        let placeholder_id = self.mir_builder.add_type(placeholder_type);
  888|     69|        self.type_cache.insert(type_ref, placeholder_id);
  889|       |
  890|     69|        let mir_type = self.lower_record_type(type_ref, &tag, is_union, is_complete);
  891|       |
  892|       |        // Remove from in-progress set
  893|     69|        self.type_conversion_in_progress.remove(&type_ref);
  894|       |
  895|       |        // Replace the placeholder entry with the real type
  896|     69|        self.mir_builder.update_type(placeholder_id, mir_type);
  897|     69|        placeholder_id
  898|     69|    }

  904|  1.02k|    }

  945|    673|    fn lower_builtin_type(&self, b: &BuiltinType) -> MirType {
  946|    673|        match b {
  947|     73|            BuiltinType::Void => MirType::Void,
  948|     12|            BuiltinType::Bool => MirType::Bool,
  949|     59|            BuiltinType::Char | BuiltinType::SChar => MirType::I8,
  950|     11|            BuiltinType::UChar => MirType::U8,
  951|      5|            BuiltinType::Short => MirType::I16,
  952|      6|            BuiltinType::UShort => MirType::U16,
  953|    332|            BuiltinType::Int => MirType::I32,
  954|     20|            BuiltinType::UInt => MirType::U32,
  955|     22|            BuiltinType::Long | BuiltinType::LongLong => MirType::I64,
  956|     72|            BuiltinType::ULong | BuiltinType::ULongLong => MirType::U64,
  957|     23|            BuiltinType::Float => MirType::F32,

  978|    112|    fn lower_array_type(&mut self, type_ref: TypeRef, element_type: TypeRef, size: &ArraySizeType) -> MirType {
  979|    112|        let element = self.lower_type(element_type);
  980|       |
  981|    112|        match size {
  982|    104|            ArraySizeType::Constant(s) => {

  984|    104|                let element_layout = self.registry.get_layout(element_ref);
  985|       |
  986|    104|                MirType::Array {
  987|    104|                    element,
  988|    104|                    size: *s,

  990|    104|                        size: layout_size,
  991|    104|                        align: layout_align,
  992|    104|                        stride: element_layout.size,
  993|    104|                    },
  994|    104|                }
  995|       |            }
  996|       |            _ => {

  998|       |                // We use dummy layout (size 0) but try to preserve alignment/stride from element type.

 1004|      8|                    (layout.alignment, layout.size)

 1007|      0|                    (1, 0)
 1008|       |                };

 1011|      8|                    element,
 1012|      8|                    size: 0,

 1014|      8|                }

 1131|    980|    fn emit_conversion(&mut self, operand: Operand, conv: &Conversion, target_type_id: TypeId) -> Operand {
 1132|    980|        let to_ty_ref = match conv {
 1133|    633|            Conversion::IntegerCast { to, .. }
 1134|     54|            | Conversion::IntegerPromotion { to, .. }
 1135|    749|            | Conversion::PointerCast { to, .. } => Some(*to),
                                                      ^62
 1136|    218|            Conversion::PointerDecay { to } => Some(*to),
 1137|     13|            _ => None,
 1138|       |        };
 1139|       |
 1140|    980|        let from_ty_ref = match conv {
 1141|    633|            Conversion::IntegerCast { from, .. }
 1142|     54|            | Conversion::IntegerPromotion { from, .. }
 1143|    749|            | Conversion::PointerCast { from, .. } => Some(*from),
                                                      ^62
 1144|    231|            _ => None,
 1145|       |        };
 1146|       |
 1147|    980|        if let Some(to) = to_ty_ref
                                  ^967
 1148|    967|            && (to.is_complex() || from_ty_ref.is_some_and(|f| f.is_complex()))
                                                 ^961        ^961            ^743^743
 1149|       |        {

 1151|    974|        }
 1152|       |
 1153|    974|        let to_mir_type = match conv {
 1154|    627|            Conversion::IntegerCast { to, .. }
 1155|     54|            | Conversion::IntegerPromotion { to, .. }
 1156|    743|            | Conversion::PointerCast { to, .. } => self.lower_type(*to),
                                                      ^62
 1157|       |            Conversion::NullPointerConstant => {
 1158|       |                // Null pointer constant usually converts to void* first.
 1159|       |                // However, we can use target_type_id if it's already a pointer.
 1160|     12|                let void_ptr_mir = self.lower_type(self.registry.type_void_ptr);
 1161|     12|                if self.mir_builder.get_type(target_type_id).is_pointer() {
 1162|     12|                    target_type_id
 1163|       |                } else {
 1164|      0|                    void_ptr_mir
 1165|       |                }

 1168|      0|            Conversion::LValueToRValue => target_type_id,
 1169|      1|            Conversion::QualifierAdjust { .. } => target_type_id,
 1170|       |        };
 1171|       |
 1172|       |        // Optimization: skip if already same type
 1173|    974|        let current_ty = self.get_operand_type(&operand);
 1174|    974|        if current_ty == to_mir_type && !matches!(conv, Conversion::PointerDecay { .. }) {
                                                       ^534     ^534
 1175|    534|            return operand;
 1176|    440|        }
 1177|       |
 1178|    440|        match conv {
 1179|       |            Conversion::IntegerCast { .. } | Conversion::IntegerPromotion { .. } | Conversion::PointerCast { .. } => {
 1180|       |                // Fold constant casts if types are compatible
 1181|    212|                if let Some(const_id) = self.operand_to_const_id(&operand) {
                                          ^110

 1184|       |
 1185|    110|                    let is_compatible = match (&const_val.kind, mir_type) {
 1186|    101|                        (ConstValueKind::Int(_), t) => t.is_int() || t.is_pointer(),
                                                                                   ^8^8

 1188|      0|                        (ConstValueKind::GlobalAddress(_), t) => t.is_pointer() || t.is_int(),

                                                                                                   ^0^0
 1190|      0|                        _ => false,
 1191|       |                    };
 1192|       |
 1193|    110|                    if is_compatible {
 1194|    100|                        let truncated_kind = match const_val.kind {
 1195|     96|                            ConstValueKind::Int(val) => ConstValueKind::Int(mir_type.truncate_int(val)),
 1196|      4|                            kind => kind,
 1197|       |                        };

  249|     25|    fn visit_cast(&mut self, operand_ref: NodeRef, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
  250|     25|        let is_void = self.mir_builder.get_type(mir_ty).is_void();
  251|     25|        if is_void {
  252|      1|            self.visit_expression(operand_ref, false);
  253|      1|            return self.create_dummy_operand();

  257|      1|            return operand;

  259|       |
  260|       |        // Fold constant casts if types are compatible

  264|       |
  265|     20|            let is_compatible = match (&const_val.kind, mir_type) {
  266|     18|                (ConstValueKind::Int(_), t) => t.is_int() || t.is_pointer(),

  270|      1|                _ => false,
  271|       |            };
  272|       |
  273|     20|            if is_compatible {
  274|     18|                let truncated_kind = match const_val.kind {
  275|     18|                    ConstValueKind::Int(val) => ConstValueKind::Int(mir_type.truncate_int(val)),
  276|      0|                    kind => kind,
  277|       |                };

  279|      2|            }
  280|      3|        }
  281|       |
  282|      5|        Operand::Cast(mir_ty, Box::new(operand))
  283|     25|    }

  304|    207|    fn visit_unary_op(&mut self, op: &UnaryOp, operand_ref: NodeRef, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
  305|    207|        let ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(operand_ref).unwrap();
  306|    207|        if ty.is_complex() && !matches!(op, UnaryOp::AddrOf | UnaryOp::Deref) {

  319|      1|                self.apply_conversions(operand, operand_ref, mir_ty)
  320|       |            }

  324|      1|                    ConstValueKind::Float(0.0)
  325|       |                } else {
  326|      0|                    ConstValueKind::Int(0)
  327|       |                };
  328|      1|                Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(mir_ty, kind))
  329|       |            }

  342|     22|    fn visit_unary_addrof(&mut self, operand_ref: NodeRef) -> Operand {

                                           ^20

  347|      2|            && self.ast.get_value_category(operand_ref) == Some(ValueCategory::LValue)

  351|       |                    kind: ConstValueKind::FunctionAddress(_),
  352|       |                    ..

  377|    604|    fn visit_ident(&mut self, resolved_ref: SymbolRef) -> Operand {

  379|       |

  388|      1|                        self.visit_variable(resolved_ref, mir_type_id);
  389|     50|                    }

  392|     51|                        Some(id) => id,
  393|       |                        None => {

  395|      0|                                "Global variable '{}' not found in MIR map even after lazy lowering attempt. Visited? {:?}",
  396|       |                                entry.name,
  397|      0|                                self.global_map.keys()

  399|       |                        }
  400|       |                    };

  402|       |                } else {

  423|    115|    fn unify_binary_operands(
  424|    115|        &mut self,
  425|    115|        mut lhs: Operand,
  426|    115|        mut rhs: Operand,
  427|    115|        lhs_mir_ty: TypeId,
  428|    115|        rhs_mir_ty: TypeId,
  429|    115|    ) -> (Operand, Operand) {
  430|    115|        if lhs_mir_ty != rhs_mir_ty {
  431|      0|            let lhs_mir = self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_mir_ty);
  432|      0|            let rhs_mir = self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_mir_ty);
  433|       |
  434|      0|            if lhs_mir.is_int() && rhs_mir.is_int() {
  435|      0|                let w1 = lhs_mir.width();
  436|      0|                let w2 = rhs_mir.width();
  437|      0|                if w1 > w2 {
  438|      0|                    rhs = Operand::Cast(lhs_mir_ty, Box::new(rhs));
  439|      0|                } else if w2 > w1 {
  440|      0|                    lhs = Operand::Cast(rhs_mir_ty, Box::new(lhs));
  441|      0|                }
  442|      0|            } else if lhs_mir.is_pointer() && rhs_mir.is_int() {
  443|      0|                rhs = Operand::Cast(lhs_mir_ty, Box::new(rhs));
  444|      0|            } else if lhs_mir.is_int() && rhs_mir.is_pointer() {
  445|      0|                lhs = Operand::Cast(rhs_mir_ty, Box::new(lhs));
  446|      0|            } else if lhs_mir.is_float() && rhs_mir.is_float() {
  447|      0|                let w1 = lhs_mir.width();
  448|      0|                let w2 = rhs_mir.width();
  449|      0|                if w1 > w2 {
  450|      0|                    rhs = Operand::Cast(lhs_mir_ty, Box::new(rhs));
  451|      0|                } else if w2 > w1 {
  452|      0|                    lhs = Operand::Cast(rhs_mir_ty, Box::new(lhs));
  453|      0|                }
  454|      0|            } else if lhs_mir.is_float() && rhs_mir.is_int() {
  455|      0|                rhs = Operand::Cast(lhs_mir_ty, Box::new(rhs));
  456|      0|            } else if lhs_mir.is_int() && rhs_mir.is_float() {
  457|      0|                lhs = Operand::Cast(rhs_mir_ty, Box::new(lhs));
  458|      0|            }
  459|    115|        }
  460|    115|        (lhs, rhs)
  461|    115|    }
  462|       |
  463|    131|    fn visit_binary_op(&mut self, op: &BinaryOp, left_ref: NodeRef, right_ref: NodeRef, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
  464|    131|        debug_assert!(
  465|    131|            !op.is_assignment(),
  466|      0|            "visit_binary_op called with assignment operator: {:?}",
  467|       |            op
  468|       |        );
  469|    131|        if matches!(op, BinaryOp::LogicAnd | BinaryOp::LogicOr) {
                         ^117
  470|     14|            return self.visit_logical_op(op, left_ref, right_ref, mir_ty);
  471|    117|        }
  472|       |
  473|    117|        if matches!(op, BinaryOp::Comma) {
                         ^114
  474|      3|            self.visit_expression(left_ref, false);
  475|      3|            let rhs = self.visit_expression(right_ref, true);
  476|       |            // Apply conversions for RHS to match result type (comma result type is RHS type)
  477|      3|            return self.apply_conversions(rhs, right_ref, mir_ty);
  478|    114|        }
  479|       |
  480|    114|        let lhs = self.visit_expression(left_ref, true);
  481|    114|        let rhs = self.visit_expression(right_ref, true);
  482|       |
  483|    114|        let (rval, _op_ty) = self.visit_binary_arithmetic_logic(op, lhs, rhs, left_ref, right_ref, mir_ty);
  484|    114|        self.emit_rvalue_to_operand(rval, mir_ty)
  485|    131|    }

  487|    137|    fn visit_binary_arithmetic_logic(
  488|    137|        &mut self,
  489|    137|        op: &BinaryOp,
  490|    137|        lhs: Operand,
  491|    137|        rhs: Operand,
  492|    137|        left_ref: NodeRef,
  493|    137|        right_ref: NodeRef,
  494|    137|        context_ty: TypeId,
  495|    137|    ) -> (Rvalue, TypeId) {
  496|       |        // Handle pointer arithmetic
  497|    137|        if let Some(rval) = self.visit_pointer_arithmetic(op, lhs.clone(), rhs.clone(), left_ref, right_ref) {
                                  ^3
  498|      3|            let res_ty = match &rval {
  499|      3|                Rvalue::PtrAdd(base, _) | Rvalue::PtrSub(base, _) => self.get_operand_type(base),
                                             ^2                        ^1
  500|      0|                Rvalue::PtrDiff(..) => self.lower_type(self.registry.type_long),
  501|      0|                _ => unreachable!(),
  502|       |            };
  503|      3|            return (rval, res_ty);
  504|    134|        }
  505|       |
  506|       |        // Apply implicit conversions from semantic info first to match AST
  507|    134|        let lhs_converted = self.apply_conversions(lhs, left_ref, context_ty);
  508|    134|        let rhs_converted = self.apply_conversions(rhs, right_ref, context_ty);
  509|       |
  510|    134|        let lhs_mir_ty = self.get_operand_type(&lhs_converted);
  511|    134|        let rhs_mir_ty = self.get_operand_type(&rhs_converted);
  512|       |
  513|    134|        let lhs_is_complex = self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_mir_ty).is_aggregate()
  514|     19|            && self.ast.get_resolved_type(left_ref).is_some_and(|t| t.is_complex());
  515|    134|        let rhs_is_complex = self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_mir_ty).is_aggregate()
  516|     19|            && self.ast.get_resolved_type(right_ref).is_some_and(|t| t.is_complex());
  517|       |
  518|    134|        if lhs_is_complex || rhs_is_complex {
                                           ^116
  519|     19|            let op = self.visit_complex_binary_op(op, lhs_converted, rhs_converted, context_ty);
  520|     19|            return (Rvalue::Use(op), context_ty);
  521|    115|        }
  522|       |
  523|       |        // Ensure both operands have the same type for MIR operations.
  524|    115|        let (lhs_unified, rhs_unified) =
  525|    115|            self.unify_binary_operands(lhs_converted, rhs_converted, lhs_mir_ty, rhs_mir_ty);
  526|       |
  527|    115|        let lhs_final = lhs_unified;
  528|    115|        let rhs_final = rhs_unified;
  529|       |
  530|    115|        let common_ty = self.get_operand_type(&lhs_final);
  531|       |
  532|    115|        let common_mir_type = self.mir_builder.get_type(common_ty);
  533|    115|        let rval = mir_gen_ops::emit_binary_rvalue(op, lhs_final, rhs_final, common_mir_type.is_float());
  534|       |
  535|    115|        let result_ty = match op {
  536|       |            BinaryOp::Equal
  537|       |            | BinaryOp::NotEqual
  538|       |            | BinaryOp::Less
  539|       |            | BinaryOp::LessEqual
  540|       |            | BinaryOp::Greater
  541|     69|            | BinaryOp::GreaterEqual => self.get_int_type(),
  542|     46|            _ => common_ty,
  543|       |        };
  544|       |
  545|    115|        (rval, result_ty)
  546|    137|    }
  547|       |
  548|     14|    fn visit_bool_normalization(
  549|     14|        &mut self,
  550|     14|        value_op: Operand,
  551|     14|        result_place: Place,
  552|     14|        merge_block: crate::mir::MirBlockId,
  553|     14|    ) {
  554|     14|        let true_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  555|     14|        let false_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  556|       |
  557|     14|        self.mir_builder
  558|     14|            .set_terminator(Terminator::If(value_op, true_block, false_block));
  559|       |
  560|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(true_block);
  561|     14|        let one_op = self.create_int_operand(1);
  562|     14|        self.mir_builder
  563|     14|            .add_statement(MirStmt::Assign(result_place.clone(), Rvalue::Use(one_op)));
  564|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(merge_block));
  565|       |
  566|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(false_block);
  567|     14|        let zero_op = self.create_int_operand(0);
  568|     14|        self.mir_builder
  569|     14|            .add_statement(MirStmt::Assign(result_place.clone(), Rvalue::Use(zero_op)));
  570|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(merge_block));
  571|     14|    }
  572|       |
  573|     14|    fn visit_logical_op(&mut self, op: &BinaryOp, left_ref: NodeRef, right_ref: NodeRef, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
  574|       |        // Short-circuiting logic for && and ||
  575|     14|        let (_res_local, res_place) = self.create_temp_local(mir_ty);
  576|       |
  577|     14|        let eval_rhs_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  578|     14|        let merge_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  579|     14|        let short_circuit_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
  580|       |
  581|       |        // 1. Evaluate LHS
  582|     14|        let lhs_op = self.lower_condition(left_ref);
  583|       |
  584|       |        // Pre-create constants to avoid double borrow
  585|     14|        let zero_op = self.create_int_operand(0);
  586|     14|        let one_op = self.create_int_operand(1);
  587|       |
  588|     14|        let (short_circuit_val, true_target, false_target) = match op {
  589|      7|            BinaryOp::LogicAnd => (zero_op.clone(), eval_rhs_block, short_circuit_block),
  590|      7|            BinaryOp::LogicOr => (one_op.clone(), short_circuit_block, eval_rhs_block),
  591|      0|            _ => unreachable!(),
  592|       |        };
  593|       |
  594|       |        // if lhs { goto true_target } else { goto false_target }
  595|     14|        self.mir_builder
  596|     14|            .set_terminator(Terminator::If(lhs_op, true_target, false_target));
  597|       |
  598|       |        // Short circuit case
  599|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(short_circuit_block);
  600|     14|        self.mir_builder
  601|     14|            .add_statement(MirStmt::Assign(res_place.clone(), Rvalue::Use(short_circuit_val)));
  602|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(merge_block));
  603|       |
  604|       |        // 2. Evaluate RHS
  605|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(eval_rhs_block);
  606|     14|        let rhs_val = self.lower_condition(right_ref);
  607|       |
  608|     14|        self.visit_bool_normalization(rhs_val, res_place.clone(), merge_block);
  609|       |
  610|       |        // Merge
  611|     14|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(merge_block);
  612|     14|        self.current_block = Some(merge_block);
  613|       |
  614|     14|        Operand::Copy(Box::new(res_place))
  615|     14|    }
  616|       |
  617|      6|    fn unwrap_cast_if_int(&mut self, op: Operand) -> Operand {
  618|      6|        match op {
  619|      1|            Operand::Cast(ty, inner) => {
  620|      1|                let inner_ty = self.get_operand_type(&inner);
  621|      1|                if self.mir_builder.get_type(inner_ty).is_int() {
  622|      0|                    return *inner;
  623|      1|                }
  624|       |
  625|      1|                let unwrapped = self.unwrap_cast_if_int(*inner.clone());
  626|      1|                let unwrapped_ty = self.get_operand_type(&unwrapped);
  627|      1|                if self.mir_builder.get_type(unwrapped_ty).is_int() {
  628|      0|                    return unwrapped;
  629|      1|                }
  630|       |
  631|      1|                Operand::Cast(ty, inner)
  632|       |            }
  633|      3|            Operand::Constant(const_id) => {
  634|      3|                let val_opt = {
  635|      3|                    let const_val = self.mir_builder.get_constants().get(&const_id).unwrap();
  636|      3|                    if let ConstValueKind::Int(val) = const_val.kind {
  637|      3|                        Some(val)
  638|       |                    } else {
  639|      0|                        None
  640|       |                    }
  641|       |                };
  642|       |
  643|      3|                if let Some(val) = val_opt {
  644|      3|                    return self.create_int_operand(val);
  645|      0|                }
  646|      0|                Operand::Constant(const_id)
  647|       |            }
  648|      2|            _ => op,
  649|       |        }
  650|      6|    }
  651|       |
  652|    137|    fn visit_pointer_arithmetic(
  653|    137|        &mut self,
  654|    137|        op: &BinaryOp,
  655|    137|        lhs: Operand,
  656|    137|        rhs: Operand,
  657|    137|        left_ref: NodeRef,
  658|    137|        right_ref: NodeRef,
  659|    137|    ) -> Option<Rvalue> {
  660|       |        // Lower types and apply conversions locally to check for pointer arithmetic
  661|       |        // We use the operand's own type as the target for conversion to avoid forcing
  662|       |        // implicit casts to the result type (which causes issues for Ptr + Int -> Ptr)
  663|    137|        let lhs_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(left_ref).unwrap();
  664|    137|        let rhs_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(right_ref).unwrap();
  665|       |
  666|    137|        let lhs_mir_target = self.lower_qual_type(lhs_ty);
  667|    137|        let rhs_mir_target = self.lower_qual_type(rhs_ty);
  668|       |
  669|    137|        let lhs_converted = self.apply_conversions(lhs, left_ref, lhs_mir_target);
  670|    137|        let rhs_converted = self.apply_conversions(rhs, right_ref, rhs_mir_target);
  671|       |
  672|    137|        let lhs_mir_ty = self.get_operand_type(&lhs_converted);
  673|    137|        let rhs_mir_ty = self.get_operand_type(&rhs_converted);
  674|       |
  675|    137|        let lhs_type_info = self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_mir_ty);
  676|    137|        let rhs_type_info = self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_mir_ty);
  677|       |
  678|    137|        match op {
  679|       |            BinaryOp::Add => {
  680|     28|                if lhs_type_info.is_pointer() && rhs_type_info.is_int() {
                                                               ^2            ^2
  681|      0|                    Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(lhs_converted, rhs_converted, BinaryOp::Add))
  682|     28|                } else if rhs_type_info.is_pointer() && lhs_type_info.is_int() {
                                                                      ^2            ^2
  683|      0|                    Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(rhs_converted, lhs_converted, BinaryOp::Add))
  684|     28|                } else if lhs_type_info.is_pointer() && rhs_type_info.is_pointer() {
                                                                      ^2            ^2
  685|       |                    // Try unwrapping implicit casts (Analyzer might have casted Int to Ptr)
  686|      2|                    let lhs_unwrapped = self.unwrap_cast_if_int(lhs_converted.clone());
  687|      2|                    let rhs_unwrapped = self.unwrap_cast_if_int(rhs_converted.clone());
  688|       |
  689|      2|                    let lhs_u_ty = self.get_operand_type(&lhs_unwrapped);
  690|      2|                    let rhs_u_ty = self.get_operand_type(&rhs_unwrapped);
  691|       |
  692|      2|                    if self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_u_ty).is_pointer() && self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_u_ty).is_int()
  693|       |                    {
  694|      2|                        Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(lhs_unwrapped, rhs_unwrapped, BinaryOp::Add))
  695|      0|                    } else if self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_u_ty).is_pointer()
  696|      0|                        && self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_u_ty).is_int()
  697|       |                    {
  698|      0|                        Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(rhs_unwrapped, lhs_unwrapped, BinaryOp::Add))
  699|       |                    } else {
  700|      0|                        None
  701|       |                    }
  702|       |                } else {
  703|     26|                    None
  704|       |                }
  705|       |            }
  706|       |            BinaryOp::Sub => {
  707|     16|                if lhs_type_info.is_pointer() {
  708|      1|                    if rhs_type_info.is_pointer() {
  709|       |                        // Check if rhs is casted from int (Analyzer might have casted Int to Ptr)
  710|      1|                        let rhs_unwrapped = self.unwrap_cast_if_int(rhs_converted.clone());
  711|      1|                        let rhs_u_ty_id = self.get_operand_type(&rhs_unwrapped);
  712|       |
  713|      1|                        if self.mir_builder.get_type(rhs_u_ty_id).is_int() {
  714|      1|                            Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(lhs_converted, rhs_unwrapped, BinaryOp::Sub))
  715|       |                        } else {
  716|      0|                            Some(Rvalue::PtrDiff(lhs_converted, rhs_converted))
  717|       |                        }
  718|      0|                    } else if rhs_type_info.is_int() {
  719|      0|                        Some(self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(lhs_converted, rhs_converted, BinaryOp::Sub))
  720|       |                    } else {
  721|      0|                        None
  722|       |                    }
  723|       |                } else {
  724|     15|                    None
  725|       |                }
  726|       |            }
  727|     93|            _ => None,
  728|       |        }
  729|    137|    }

  731|    116|    fn visit_assignment_expr(
  732|    116|        &mut self,
  733|    116|        node_ref: NodeRef,
  734|    116|        op: &BinaryOp,
  735|    116|        left_ref: NodeRef,
  736|    116|        right_ref: NodeRef,
  737|    116|        mir_ty: TypeId,
  738|    116|    ) -> Operand {
  739|    116|        debug_assert!(
  740|    116|            op.is_assignment(),
  741|      0|            "visit_assignment_expr called with non-assignment operator: {:?}",
  742|       |            op
  743|       |        );
  744|       |
  745|       |        // Ensure the LHS is a place. If not, this is a semantic error.
  746|    116|        if self.ast.get_value_category(left_ref) != Some(ValueCategory::LValue) {
  747|      0|            panic!("LHS of assignment must be an lvalue");
  748|    116|        }
  749|       |
  750|       |        // Use visit_expression_as_place to properly resolve the destination place
  751|    116|        let place = self.visit_expression_as_place(left_ref);
  752|       |
  753|    116|        let rhs_op = self.visit_expression(right_ref, true);
  754|       |
  755|    116|        if let Some(compound_op) = op.without_assignment() {
                                  ^23
  756|     23|            self.visit_compound_assignment(node_ref, compound_op, place, rhs_op, left_ref, right_ref, mir_ty)
  757|       |        } else {
  758|       |            // Simple assignment, just use the RHS
  759|     93|            let final_rhs = self.apply_conversions(rhs_op, right_ref, mir_ty);
  760|     93|            self.emit_assignment(place, final_rhs.clone());
  761|     93|            final_rhs // C assignment expressions evaluate to the assigned value
  762|       |        }
  763|    116|    }

  765|     23|    fn visit_compound_assignment(
  766|     23|        &mut self,
  767|     23|        node_ref: NodeRef,
  768|     23|        compound_op: BinaryOp,
  769|     23|        place: Place,
  770|     23|        rhs_op: Operand,
  771|     23|        left_ref: NodeRef,
  772|     23|        right_ref: NodeRef,
  773|     23|        mir_ty: TypeId,
  774|     23|    ) -> Operand {
  775|       |        // This is a compound assignment, e.g., a += b
  776|       |        // Use the already-evaluated place to read the current value.
  777|     23|        let lhs_copy = Operand::Copy(Box::new(place.clone()));
  778|       |
  779|     23|        let (rval, op_ty) =
  780|     23|            self.visit_binary_arithmetic_logic(&compound_op, lhs_copy, rhs_op, left_ref, right_ref, mir_ty);
  781|     23|        let result_op = self.emit_rvalue_to_operand(rval, op_ty);
  782|       |
  783|     23|        let truncated_op = self.emit_cast(result_op, mir_ty);
  784|       |
  785|     23|        self.emit_assignment(place, truncated_op.clone());
  786|       |
  787|     23|        self.apply_conversions(truncated_op, node_ref, mir_ty)
  788|     23|    }
  789|       |
  790|     23|    fn emit_cast(&mut self, operand: Operand, target_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
  791|     23|        if self.get_operand_type(&operand) == target_ty {
  792|     18|            return operand;
  793|      5|        }
  794|       |
  795|       |        // Fold constant casts if types are compatible
  796|      5|        if let Some(const_id) = self.operand_to_const_id(&operand) {
                                  ^0
  797|      0|            let const_val = self.mir_builder.get_constants().get(&const_id).unwrap().clone();
  798|      0|            let mir_type = self.mir_builder.get_type(target_ty);
  799|       |
  800|      0|            let is_compatible = match (&const_val.kind, mir_type) {
  801|      0|                (ConstValueKind::Int(_), t) => t.is_int() || t.is_pointer(),
  802|      0|                (ConstValueKind::Float(_), t) => t.is_float(),
  803|      0|                (ConstValueKind::GlobalAddress(_), t) => t.is_pointer() || t.is_int(),
  804|      0|                (ConstValueKind::FunctionAddress(_), t) => t.is_pointer() || t.is_int(),
  805|      0|                _ => false,
  806|       |            };
  807|       |
  808|      0|            if is_compatible {
  809|      0|                let truncated_kind = match const_val.kind {
  810|      0|                    ConstValueKind::Int(val) => ConstValueKind::Int(mir_type.truncate_int(val)),
  811|      0|                    kind => kind,
  812|       |                };
  813|      0|                return Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(target_ty, truncated_kind));
  814|      0|            }
  815|      5|        }
  816|       |
  817|      5|        Operand::Cast(target_ty, Box::new(operand))
  818|     23|    }

  820|     93|    fn visit_function_call(&mut self, call_expr: &ast::nodes::CallExpr, dest_place: Option<Place>) {
  821|     93|        let callee = self.visit_expression(call_expr.callee, true);
  822|       |
  823|     93|        let arg_operands = self.visit_function_call_args(call_expr);
  824|       |
  825|     93|        let call_target = if let Operand::Constant(const_id) = callee {
                                                                 ^89
  826|       |            if let ConstValue {
  827|     88|                kind: ConstValueKind::FunctionAddress(func_id),
  828|       |                ..
  829|     89|            } = self.mir_builder.get_constants().get(&const_id).unwrap()
  830|       |            {
  831|     88|                CallTarget::Direct(*func_id)
  832|       |            } else {
  833|       |                // Handle non-function-address constants (e.g. casted integers) as indirect calls
  834|      1|                CallTarget::Indirect(Operand::Constant(const_id))
  835|       |            }
  836|       |        } else {
  837|      4|            CallTarget::Indirect(callee)
  838|       |        };
  839|       |
  840|     93|        let stmt = MirStmt::Call {
  841|     93|            target: call_target,
  842|     93|            args: arg_operands,
  843|     93|            dest: dest_place,
  844|     93|        };
  845|     93|        self.mir_builder.add_statement(stmt);
  846|     93|    }

  848|     93|    fn visit_function_call_args(&mut self, call_expr: &ast::nodes::CallExpr) -> Vec<Operand> {
  849|     93|        let mut arg_operands = Vec::new();
  850|       |
  851|       |        // Get the function type to determine parameter types for conversions
  852|     93|        let func_node_kind = self.ast.get_kind(call_expr.callee);

                                                                     ^90

  856|     90|            Some(self.registry.get(func_entry.type_info.ty()).kind.clone())
  857|       |        } else {
  858|      3|            None
  859|       |        };

                                                                         ^88
  862|       |            Some(

  864|     88|                    .iter()

                                               ^78  ^78             ^78
  866|     88|                    .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
  867|       |            )
  868|       |        } else {
  869|      5|            None
  870|       |        };

                                          ^93                 ^93   ^93                ^93

  874|       |            // But we use arg_ref (the CallArg node) for implicit conversion lookup.
  875|    152|            let arg_operand = self.visit_expression(arg_ref, true);
  876|       |

                                                          ^149

  888|       |                    // We just need a placeholder type, or the promoted type if we could calculate it.
  889|       |                    // Since semantic analysis has inserted promoted type casts/conversions, we can start with the argument's own type.
  890|     71|                    arg_mir_ty
  891|       |                }

  893|       |                // Unprototyped function: Logic handled by implicit conversions
  894|      3|                arg_mir_ty

  898|       |
  899|    152|            arg_operands.push(converted_arg);
  900|       |        }
  901|     93|        arg_operands
  902|     93|    }

  904|    121|    fn find_member_path(&self, record_ty: semantic::TypeRef, field_name: ast::NameId) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {

                                  ^121   ^121                ^121
  910|    121|            return Some(vec![idx]);

  912|      0|        None
  913|    121|    }

  915|    121|    fn visit_member_access(&mut self, obj_ref: NodeRef, field_name: &ast::NameId, is_arrow: bool) -> Operand {
  916|    121|        let obj_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(obj_ref).unwrap();

  918|     16|            self.registry
  919|     16|                .get_pointee(obj_ty.ty())
  920|     16|                .expect("Arrow access on non-pointer type")
  921|     16|                .ty()
  922|       |        } else {
  923|    105|            obj_ty.ty()
  924|       |        };
  925|       |
  926|    121|        if record_ty.is_record() {
  927|       |            // Validate that the field exists and get its layout information
  928|    121|            let path = self
  929|    121|                .find_member_path(record_ty, *field_name)
  930|    121|                .expect("Field not found - should be caught by semantic analysis");
  931|       |
  932|       |            // Apply the chain of field accesses
  933|       |
  934|       |            // Resolve base place
  935|    121|            let mut current_place = self.visit_expression_as_place(obj_ref);
  936|       |
  937|    121|            if is_arrow {
  938|     16|                // Dereference: *ptr
  939|     16|                current_place = self.deref_place(current_place);
  940|    105|            }
  941|       |
  942|    242|            for field_idx in path {

  943|    121|                current_place = Place::StructField(Box::new(current_place), field_idx);
  944|    121|            }
  945|       |
  946|    121|            Operand::Copy(Box::new(current_place))
  947|       |        } else {
  948|      0|            panic!("Member access on non-record type");
  949|       |        }
  950|    121|    }

  952|     42|    fn visit_index_access(&mut self, arr_ref: NodeRef, idx_ref: NodeRef) -> Operand {
  953|     42|        let arr_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(arr_ref).unwrap();
  954|     42|        let idx_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(idx_ref).unwrap();
  955|       |
  956|       |        // Handle both arr[idx] and idx[arr] (subscripting is commutative in C)
  957|       |        // One must be a pointer/array, the other must be an integer
  958|     42|        let (sequence_ref, index_ref) = if arr_ty.is_array() || arr_ty.is_pointer() {
                                                                              ^3     ^3
  959|     40|            (arr_ref, idx_ref)
  960|      2|        } else if idx_ty.is_array() || idx_ty.is_pointer() {
                                                     ^1     ^1
  961|      2|            (idx_ref, arr_ref)
  962|       |        } else {
  963|      0|            panic!("Index access: neither operand is an array or pointer type");
  964|       |        };
  965|       |
  966|       |        // In C, arr[idx] is equivalent to *(arr + idx)
  967|     42|        let arr_place = self.visit_expression_as_place(sequence_ref);
  968|     42|        let idx_operand = self.visit_expression(index_ref, true);
  969|       |
  970|     42|        Operand::Copy(Box::new(Place::ArrayIndex(Box::new(arr_place), Box::new(idx_operand))))
  971|     42|    }

  973|     19|    fn visit_inc_dec_expression(
  974|     19|        &mut self,
  975|     19|        operand_ref: NodeRef,
  976|     19|        is_inc: bool,
  977|     19|        is_post: bool,
  978|     19|        need_value: bool,
  979|     19|    ) -> Operand {
  980|     19|        let operand = self.visit_expression(operand_ref, true);
  981|     19|        let operand_ty = self.ast.get_resolved_type(operand_ref).unwrap();
  982|     19|        let mir_ty = self.lower_qual_type(operand_ty);
  983|       |
  984|     19|        if self.ast.get_value_category(operand_ref) != Some(ValueCategory::LValue) {
  985|      0|            panic!("Inc/Dec operand must be an lvalue");
  986|     19|        }
  987|       |
  988|     19|        let place = if let Operand::Copy(place) = operand.clone() {
  989|     19|            place
  990|       |        } else {
  991|      0|            panic!("Inc/Dec operand is not a place");
  992|       |        };
  993|       |
  994|       |        // If it's post-inc/dec and we need the value, save the old value
  995|     19|        let old_value = if is_post && need_value {
                                                    ^17
  996|      6|            let rval = Rvalue::Use(operand.clone());
  997|      6|            let (_, temp_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(rval, mir_ty);
  998|      6|            Some(Operand::Copy(Box::new(temp_place)))
  999|       |        } else {
 1000|     13|            None
 1001|       |        };
 1002|       |
 1003|       |        // Determine MIR operation and Rvalue
 1004|     19|        let rval = self.create_inc_dec_rvalue(operand.clone(), operand_ty, is_inc);
 1005|       |
 1006|       |        // Perform the assignment
 1007|     19|        if is_post && !need_value {
                                    ^17
 1008|     11|            // Optimization: assign directly to place if old value not needed
 1009|     11|            self.mir_builder.add_statement(MirStmt::Assign(*place.clone(), rval));
 1010|     11|        } else {
 1011|       |            // If we needed old value (is_post), or if it is pre-inc (need new value),
 1012|       |            // we compute to a temp first to ensure correctness and return the right value.
 1013|      8|            let (_, new_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(rval, mir_ty);
 1014|      8|            self.emit_assignment(*place.clone(), Operand::Copy(Box::new(new_place.clone())));
 1015|       |
 1016|      8|            if !is_post {
 1017|       |                // Pre-inc: return the new value
 1018|      2|                return Operand::Copy(Box::new(new_place));
 1019|      6|            }
 1020|       |        }
 1021|       |
 1022|     17|        if is_post {
 1023|     17|            if need_value {
 1024|      6|                old_value.unwrap()
 1025|       |            } else {
 1026|     11|                self.create_int_operand(0)
 1027|       |            }
 1028|       |        } else {
 1029|       |            // Pre-inc: we already returned inside the block above.
 1030|       |            // RE-FETCH from place as a fallback (should not be reached)
 1031|      0|            Operand::Copy(place)
 1032|       |        }
 1033|     19|    }
 1034|       |
 1035|     19|    fn create_inc_dec_rvalue(&mut self, operand: Operand, operand_ty: QualType, is_inc: bool) -> Rvalue {
 1036|     19|        let one_const = self.create_int_operand(1);
 1037|     19|        let minus_one_const = self.create_int_operand(-1);
 1038|       |
 1039|     19|        if operand_ty.is_pointer() {
 1040|      4|            let op = if is_inc { BinaryOp::Add } else { BinaryOp::Sub };
                                                                      ^0
 1041|      4|            self.create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(operand, one_const, op)
 1042|       |        } else {
 1043|     15|            let mir_ty_id = self.lower_qual_type(operand_ty);
 1044|     15|            let mir_ty = self.mir_builder.get_type(mir_ty_id);
 1045|       |
 1046|     15|            if mir_ty.is_float() {
 1047|      0|                let val = if is_inc { 1.0 } else { -1.0 };
 1048|      0|                let const_val = self.create_constant(mir_ty_id, ConstValueKind::Float(val));
 1049|      0|                let rhs = Operand::Constant(const_val);
 1050|      0|                Rvalue::BinaryFloatOp(crate::mir::BinaryFloatOp::Add, operand, rhs)
 1051|       |            } else {
 1052|       |                // For Integers: Add(delta) (Note: we use Add with negative delta for decrement
 1053|       |                // to support proper wrapping arithmetic and fix previous bugs)
 1054|     15|                let rhs = if is_inc { one_const } else { minus_one_const };
                                                    ^10                ^5
 1055|     15|                Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::Add, operand, rhs)
 1056|       |            }
 1057|       |        }
 1058|     19|    }
 1059|       |
 1060|      7|    fn create_pointer_arithmetic_rvalue(&mut self, lhs: Operand, rhs: Operand, op: BinaryOp) -> Rvalue {
 1061|      7|        match op {
 1062|      6|            BinaryOp::Add => Rvalue::PtrAdd(lhs, rhs),
 1063|      1|            BinaryOp::Sub => Rvalue::PtrSub(lhs, rhs),
 1064|      0|            _ => panic!("Invalid pointer arithmetic op"),

 1113|     10|    fn visit_atomic_op(&mut self, op: AtomicOp, args_start: NodeRef, args_len: u16, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
 1114|     10|        let args = self.get_atomic_args(args_start, args_len);
 1115|     10|        let order = AtomicMemOrder::SeqCst; // Default to SeqCst for now
 1116|       |
 1117|     10|        match op {
 1118|       |            AtomicOp::LoadN => {
 1119|      1|                let ptr = args[0].clone();
 1120|       |                // args[1] is memorder, ignored
 1121|      1|                let rval = Rvalue::AtomicLoad(ptr, order);
 1122|      1|                self.emit_rvalue_to_operand(rval, mir_ty)
 1123|       |            }
 1124|       |            AtomicOp::StoreN => {
 1125|      1|                let ptr = args[0].clone();
 1126|      1|                let val = args[1].clone();
 1127|       |                // args[2] is memorder, ignored
 1128|      1|                self.mir_builder.add_statement(MirStmt::AtomicStore(ptr, val, order));
 1129|      1|                self.create_dummy_operand()
 1130|       |            }
 1131|       |            AtomicOp::ExchangeN => {
 1132|      1|                let ptr = args[0].clone();
 1133|      1|                let val = args[1].clone();
 1134|       |                // args[2] is memorder, ignored
 1135|      1|                let rval = Rvalue::AtomicExchange(ptr, val, order);
 1136|      1|                self.emit_rvalue_to_operand(rval, mir_ty)
 1137|       |            }
 1138|      2|            AtomicOp::CompareExchangeN => self.visit_atomic_cmpxchg(&args, order, mir_ty),
 1139|      1|            AtomicOp::FetchAdd => self.visit_atomic_fetch_op(BinaryIntOp::Add, &args, order, mir_ty),
 1140|      1|            AtomicOp::FetchSub => self.visit_atomic_fetch_op(BinaryIntOp::Sub, &args, order, mir_ty),
 1141|      1|            AtomicOp::FetchAnd => self.visit_atomic_fetch_op(BinaryIntOp::BitAnd, &args, order, mir_ty),
 1142|      1|            AtomicOp::FetchOr => self.visit_atomic_fetch_op(BinaryIntOp::BitOr, &args, order, mir_ty),
 1143|      1|            AtomicOp::FetchXor => self.visit_atomic_fetch_op(BinaryIntOp::BitXor, &args, order, mir_ty),
 1144|       |        }
 1145|     10|    }

 1154|      2|    fn visit_atomic_cmpxchg(&mut self, args: &[Operand], order: AtomicMemOrder, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
 1155|      2|        let ptr = args[0].clone();
 1156|      2|        let expected_ptr = args[1].clone();
 1157|      2|        let desired = args[2].clone();
 1158|       |        // args[3] weak, args[4] success, args[5] failure (ignored side effects were handled by lowering args)
 1159|       |
 1160|      2|        let expected_place = match expected_ptr {
 1161|      1|            Operand::Copy(p) => Place::Deref(Box::new(Operand::Copy(p))),
 1162|      1|            _ => Place::Deref(Box::new(expected_ptr)),
 1163|       |        };
 1164|      2|        let expected_val_op = Operand::Copy(Box::new(expected_place.clone()));
 1165|       |
 1166|      2|        let weak = false;
 1167|      2|        let cas_rval = Rvalue::AtomicCompareExchange(ptr, expected_val_op.clone(), desired.clone(), weak, order, order);
 1168|       |
 1169|      2|        let val_ty = self.get_operand_type(&desired);
 1170|      2|        let (_, old_val_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(cas_rval, val_ty);
 1171|      2|        let old_val = Operand::Copy(Box::new(old_val_place));
 1172|       |
 1173|      2|        let mir_type_info = self.mir_builder.get_type(val_ty);
 1174|      2|        let cmp_rval = if mir_type_info.is_float() {
 1175|      0|            Rvalue::BinaryFloatOp(crate::mir::BinaryFloatOp::Eq, old_val.clone(), expected_val_op)
 1176|       |        } else {
 1177|      2|            Rvalue::BinaryIntOp(BinaryIntOp::Eq, old_val.clone(), expected_val_op)
 1178|       |        };
 1179|       |
 1180|      2|        let (_, success_place) = self.create_temp_local_with_assignment(cmp_rval, mir_ty);
 1181|      2|        let success_op = Operand::Copy(Box::new(success_place));
 1182|       |
 1183|      2|        let update_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
 1184|      2|        let end_block = self.mir_builder.create_block();
 1185|       |
 1186|      2|        self.mir_builder
 1187|      2|            .set_terminator(Terminator::If(success_op.clone(), end_block, update_block));
 1188|       |
 1189|      2|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(update_block);
 1190|      2|        self.mir_builder
 1191|      2|            .add_statement(MirStmt::Assign(expected_place, Rvalue::Use(old_val)));
 1192|      2|        self.mir_builder.set_terminator(Terminator::Goto(end_block));
 1193|       |
 1194|      2|        self.mir_builder.set_current_block(end_block);
 1195|      2|        success_op
 1196|      2|    }

 1213|     19|    fn visit_complex_binary_op(&mut self, op: &BinaryOp, lhs: Operand, rhs: Operand, mir_ty: TypeId) -> Operand {
 1214|     19|        let lhs_ty = self.get_operand_type(&lhs);
 1215|     19|        let rhs_ty = self.get_operand_type(&rhs);
 1216|       |

 1218|     19|        let (rhs_real, rhs_imag) = self.get_complex_components(rhs, rhs_ty);
 1219|       |
 1220|     19|        let element_ty = match self.mir_builder.get_type(lhs_ty) {
 1221|     19|            crate::mir::MirType::Record { field_types, .. } => field_types[0],

 1223|       |        };
 1224|       |

 1226|       |
 1227|     19|        match op {
 1228|       |            BinaryOp::Add | BinaryOp::Sub => {

                                                                     ^5           ^3
 1230|      8|                let res_real = self.emit_float_binop(mir_op, lhs_real, rhs_real, element_ty);
 1231|      8|                let res_imag = self.emit_float_binop(mir_op, lhs_imag, rhs_imag, element_ty);
 1232|      8|                self.emit_complex_struct(res_real, res_imag, mir_ty)
 1233|       |            }
 1234|       |            BinaryOp::Mul => {
 1235|      4|                let ac = self.emit_float_binop(Mul, lhs_real.clone(), rhs_real.clone(), element_ty);
 1236|      4|                let bd = self.emit_float_binop(Mul, lhs_imag.clone(), rhs_imag.clone(), element_ty);
 1237|      4|                let real = self.emit_float_binop(Sub, ac, bd, element_ty);
 1238|       |

 1242|       |
 1243|      4|                self.emit_complex_struct(real, imag, mir_ty)
 1244|       |            }
 1245|       |            BinaryOp::Div => {
 1246|      3|                let cc = self.emit_float_binop(Mul, rhs_real.clone(), rhs_real.clone(), element_ty);
 1247|      3|                let dd = self.emit_float_binop(Mul, rhs_imag.clone(), rhs_imag.clone(), element_ty);

 1249|       |

 1253|      3|                let real = self.emit_float_binop(Div, num_real, denom.clone(), element_ty);
 1254|       |

 1259|       |
 1260|      3|                self.emit_complex_struct(real, imag, mir_ty)
 1261|       |            }
 1262|       |            BinaryOp::Equal | BinaryOp::NotEqual => {
 1263|      4|                let bool_ty = self.lower_type(self.registry.type_bool);
 1264|      4|                if *op == BinaryOp::Equal {
 1265|      2|                    let real_eq = self.emit_float_binop(Eq, lhs_real, rhs_real, bool_ty);

 1274|      0|            _ => panic!("Unsupported complex binary operator: {:?}", op),
 1275|       |        }

 1296|       |            }

 1332|      2|                Some(self.create_float_operand(val, mir_ty))
 1333|       |            }

  166|    136|    fn should_recurse_aggregate(&self, target_ty: QualType, initializer: NodeRef) -> bool {

  192|      5|    fn visit_initializer_with_path_recursive(
  193|      5|        &mut self,
  194|      5|        path: &[usize],
  195|      5|        extra_designators: Option<&mut dyn Iterator<Item = NodeRef>>,
  196|      5|        initializer: NodeRef,
  197|      5|        target_ty: QualType,
  198|      5|    ) -> Vec<(usize, Operand)> {
  199|      5|        if path.is_empty() {
  200|      3|            let op = if let Some(designators) = extra_designators {
                                               ^1

  202|       |            } else {
  203|      2|                self.visit_initializer(initializer, target_ty, None)
  204|       |            };
  205|      3|            return vec![(0, op)];
  206|      2|        }
  207|       |
  208|      2|        let target_type = self.registry.get(target_ty.ty()).into_owned();
  209|      2|        let members = match &target_type.kind {
  210|      2|            TypeKind::Record { members, .. } => members.clone(),
  211|      0|            _ => panic!("Expected record type for designator path"),
  212|       |        };
  213|       |
  214|      2|        let field_idx = path[0];
  215|      2|        let mut flat_offset = 0;
  216|      2|        for member in members.iter().take(field_idx) {
                          ^1
  217|      1|            flat_offset += self.count_flattened_members(member);
  218|      1|        }
  219|       |
  220|      2|        let sub_fields = self.visit_initializer_with_path_recursive(
  221|      2|            &path[1..],
  222|      2|            extra_designators,
  223|      2|            initializer,
  224|      2|            members[field_idx].member_type,
  225|       |        );
  226|       |
  227|      2|        sub_fields
  228|      2|            .into_iter()
  229|      2|            .map(|(idx, op)| (flat_offset + idx, op))
  230|      2|            .collect()
  231|      5|    }

  233|     20|    fn resolve_designator_range(&mut self, designator_ref: NodeRef) -> (usize, usize) {
  234|     20|        match self.ast.get_kind(designator_ref) {
  235|     18|            NodeKind::Designator(Designator::ArrayIndex(idx_expr)) => {
  236|     18|                let idx = self.evaluate_constant_usize(*idx_expr, "Array designator must be constant");
  237|     18|                (idx, idx)
  238|       |            }
  239|      2|            NodeKind::Designator(Designator::GnuArrayRange(start, end)) => {
  240|      2|                let s = self.evaluate_constant_usize(*start, "Range start must be constant");
  241|      2|                let e = self.evaluate_constant_usize(*end, "Range end must be constant");
  242|      2|                if e < s {
  243|      0|                    panic!("Array range end < start");
  244|      2|                }
  245|      2|                (s, e)
  246|       |            }
  247|      0|            _ => panic!("Invalid designator for array"),
  248|       |        }
  249|     20|    }

  385|     73|    fn finalize_initializer_generic<T, C, R>(

  465|    406|    pub(crate) fn visit_initializer(
  466|    406|        &mut self,
  467|    406|        init_ref: NodeRef,
  468|    406|        target_ty: QualType,
  469|    406|        destination: Option<Place>,
  470|    406|    ) -> Operand {
  471|    406|        let kind = *self.ast.get_kind(init_ref);
  472|    406|        let target_type = self.registry.get(target_ty.ty()).into_owned();
  473|       |
  474|    406|        match (&kind, &target_type.kind) {
  475|     36|            (NodeKind::InitializerList(list), TypeKind::Record { .. }) => {
  476|     36|                self.visit_initializer_list(list, target_ty, destination)
  477|       |            }
  478|     26|            (NodeKind::InitializerList(list), TypeKind::Array { element_type, size }) => {
  479|     26|                let array_size = if let ArraySizeType::Constant(s) = size { *s } else { 0 };
                                                                              ^25         ^25         ^1
  480|     26|                self.visit_array_initializer(
  481|     26|                    list,
  482|     26|                    QualType::unqualified(*element_type),
  483|     26|                    array_size,
  484|     26|                    target_ty,
  485|     26|                    destination,
  486|       |                )
  487|       |            }
  488|       |            // ...
  489|     14|            (NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::String(val)), TypeKind::Array { element_type, size }) => {
  490|     14|                let fixed_size = if let ArraySizeType::Constant(s) = size {
  491|     14|                    Some(*s)
  492|       |                } else {
  493|      0|                    None
  494|       |                };
  495|     14|                Operand::Constant(self.create_array_const_from_string(
  496|     14|                    val,
  497|     14|                    fixed_size,
  498|     14|                    Some(QualType::unqualified(*element_type)),
  499|     14|                ))
  500|       |            }
  501|      3|            (NodeKind::InitializerList(list), _) => {
  502|      3|                if list.init_len == 0 {
  503|      1|                    let mir_ty = self.lower_qual_type(target_ty);
  504|      1|                    return Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(mir_ty, ConstValueKind::Zero));
  505|      2|                }
  506|      2|                let NodeKind::InitializerItem(item) = self.ast.get_kind(list.init_start) else {
  507|      0|                    unreachable!()
  508|       |                };
  509|      2|                self.visit_initializer(item.initializer, target_ty, destination)
  510|       |            }
  511|       |            _ => {
  512|    327|                let operand = self.visit_expression(init_ref, true);
  513|    327|                let mir_target_ty = self.lower_qual_type(target_ty);
  514|       |
  515|    327|                if self.get_operand_type(&operand) == mir_target_ty {
  516|    212|                    return operand;
  517|    115|                }
  518|       |
  519|       |                // Brace elision: scalar -> aggregate
  520|    115|                self.visit_brace_elision(operand, init_ref, target_ty, destination)
  521|       |            }
  522|       |        }
  523|    406|    }

  525|     68|    fn create_array_const_from_string(
  526|     68|        &mut self,
  527|     68|        val: &ast::NameId,
  528|     68|        fixed_size: Option<usize>,

  530|     68|    ) -> crate::mir::ConstValueId {

  532|     68|        let size = fixed_size.unwrap_or(parsed.size);
  533|       |

  535|     68|            (self.lower_qual_type(qt), self.registry.get_layout(qt.ty()).into_owned())

  537|      0|            let ty_ref = self.registry.get_builtin_type(parsed.builtin_type);

  540|      0|                self.registry.get_layout(ty_ref).into_owned(),

  548|    621|            })

  550|       |

  552|     68|            element: mir_elem_ty,

  555|     68|                size: 0,

  558|     68|            },
  559|     68|        });

  562|     68|    }

  564|     54|    pub(crate) fn visit_literal_string(&mut self, val: &ast::NameId, ty: QualType) -> Operand {
  565|     54|        let mir_ty = self.lower_qual_type(ty);
  566|     54|        let elem_ty = match &self.registry.get(ty.ty()).kind {
  567|     54|            TypeKind::Array { element_type, .. } => *element_type,
  568|      0|            _ => self.registry.type_char,
  569|       |        };
  570|       |
  571|     54|        let array_const = self.create_array_const_from_string(val, None, Some(QualType::unqualified(elem_ty)));
  572|     54|        let name = self.mir_builder.get_next_anonymous_global_name();
  573|     54|        let global = self
  574|     54|            .mir_builder
  575|     54|            .create_global_with_init(name, mir_ty, true, Some(array_const));
  576|       |
  577|     54|        Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(mir_ty, ConstValueKind::GlobalAddress(global)))
  578|     54|    }
  579|       |
  580|      5|    pub(crate) fn visit_compound_literal(&mut self, ty: QualType, init_ref: NodeRef) -> Operand {
  581|      5|        let mir_ty = self.lower_qual_type(ty);
  582|       |
  583|      5|        if self.current_function.is_none() {
  584|      3|            let init_const = self
  585|      3|                .eval_initializer_to_const(init_ref, ty)
  586|      3|                .expect("Global compound literal init must be const");
  587|      3|            let name = self.mir_builder.get_next_anonymous_global_name();
  588|      3|            let global = self
  589|      3|                .mir_builder
  590|      3|                .create_global_with_init(name, mir_ty, false, Some(init_const));
  591|      3|            Operand::Copy(Box::new(Place::Global(global)))
  592|       |        } else {
  593|      2|            let (_, place) = self.create_temp_local(mir_ty);
  594|      2|            let init_op = self.visit_initializer(init_ref, ty, Some(place.clone()));
  595|      2|            self.emit_assignment(place.clone(), init_op);
  596|      2|            Operand::Copy(Box::new(place))
  597|       |        }
  598|      5|    }

  600|     70|    pub(crate) fn eval_initializer_to_const(
  601|     70|        &mut self,
  602|     70|        init_ref: NodeRef,
  603|     70|        ty: QualType,
  604|     70|    ) -> Option<crate::mir::ConstValueId> {

  606|     70|        self.operand_to_const_id(&operand)
