  609|      2|    fn visit_initializer_with_designators(
  610|      2|        &mut self,
  611|      2|        mut designators: impl Iterator<Item = NodeRef>,
  612|      2|        initializer: NodeRef,
  613|      2|        target_ty: QualType,
  614|      2|        destination: Option<Place>,
  615|      2|    ) -> Operand {
  616|      2|        let designator_ref = if let Some(d) = designators.next() {
                          ^1                           ^1
  617|      1|            d
  618|       |        } else {
  619|      1|            return self.visit_initializer(initializer, target_ty, destination);
  620|       |        };
  621|       |
  622|      1|        let target_type = self.registry.get(target_ty.ty()).into_owned();
  623|       |
  624|      1|        match &target_type.kind {
  625|      0|            TypeKind::Array { element_type, size } => {
  626|      0|                let (start, end) = self.resolve_designator_range(designator_ref);
  627|      0|                let array_size = if let ArraySizeType::Constant(s) = size { *s } else { 0 };
  628|       |
  629|      0|                let sub_op = self.visit_initializer_with_designators(
  630|      0|                    designators,
  631|      0|                    initializer,
  632|      0|                    QualType::unqualified(*element_type),
  633|      0|                    None,
  634|       |                );
  635|       |
  636|      0|                let mut elements = vec![None; array_size];
  637|      0|                for (i, element) in elements.iter_mut().enumerate().take(end + 1).skip(start) {
  638|      0|                    if i < array_size {
  639|      0|                        *element = Some(sub_op.clone());
  640|      0|                    }
  641|       |                }
  642|       |
  643|      0|                let mir_elem_ty = self.lower_qual_type(QualType::unqualified(*element_type));
  644|      0|                let zero_op = Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(mir_elem_ty, ConstValueKind::Zero));
  645|       |
  646|      0|                self.finalize_array_initializer(
  647|      0|                    elements.into_iter().map(|e| e.unwrap_or(zero_op.clone())).collect(),
  648|      0|                    target_ty,
  649|      0|                    destination,
  650|       |                )
  651|       |            }
  652|       |            TypeKind::Record { .. } => {
  653|      1|                let (flat_members, _) = self.get_flattened_type_info(target_ty.ty());
  654|      1|                let NodeKind::Designator(Designator::FieldName(name)) = self.ast.get_kind(designator_ref) else {
  655|      0|                    panic!("Expected field name designator for record");
  656|       |                };
  657|       |
  658|      1|                let field_idx = flat_members
  659|      1|                    .iter()
  660|      2|                    .position(|m| m.name == Some(*name))
                                   ^1
  661|      1|                    .expect("Unknown field in designator");
  662|       |
  663|      1|                let value = self.visit_initializer_with_designators(
  664|      1|                    designators,
  665|      1|                    initializer,
  666|      1|                    flat_members[field_idx].member_type,
  667|      1|                    None,
  668|       |                );
  669|       |
  670|      1|                self.finalize_struct_initializer(vec![(field_idx, value)], target_ty, destination)
  671|       |            }
  672|      0|            _ => panic!("Designator on non-aggregate type"),
  673|       |        }
  674|      2|    }
  675|    115|    fn visit_brace_elision(
  676|    115|        &mut self,
  677|    115|        operand: Operand,
  678|    115|        init_ref: NodeRef,
  679|    115|        target_ty: QualType,
  680|    115|        destination: Option<Place>,
  681|    115|    ) -> Operand {
  682|    115|        let target_type = self.registry.get(target_ty.ty()).into_owned();
  683|      0|        match &target_type.kind {
  684|      0|            TypeKind::Array { element_type, size } => {
  685|      0|                let elem_ty = QualType::unqualified(*element_type);
  686|      0|                let final_op = self.visit_brace_elision(operand, init_ref, elem_ty, None);
  687|      0|                let mir_elem_ty = self.lower_qual_type(elem_ty);
  688|      0|                let len = if let ArraySizeType::Constant(l) = size { *l } else { 1 };
  689|      0|                let mut elements = vec![final_op];
  690|      0|                while elements.len() < len {
  691|      0|                    elements.push(Operand::Constant(
  692|      0|                        self.create_constant(mir_elem_ty, ConstValueKind::Zero),
  693|      0|                    ));
  694|      0|                }
  695|      0|                self.finalize_array_initializer(elements, target_ty, destination)
  696|       |            }
  697|      0|            TypeKind::Record { .. } if !target_type.is_record_empty() => {
  698|      0|                let (flat_members, _) = self.get_flattened_type_info(target_ty.ty());
  699|      0|                if flat_members.is_empty() {
  700|      0|                    let mir_target_ty = self.lower_qual_type(target_ty);
  701|      0|                    return Operand::Constant(self.create_constant(mir_target_ty, ConstValueKind::Zero));
  702|      0|                }
  703|      0|                let member_ty = flat_members[0].member_type;
  704|      0|                let final_op = self.visit_brace_elision(operand, init_ref, member_ty, None);
  705|      0|                self.finalize_struct_initializer(vec![(0, final_op)], target_ty, destination)
  706|       |            }
  707|       |            _ => {
  708|    115|                let mir_target_ty = self.lower_qual_type(target_ty);
  709|    115|                let conv_op = self.apply_conversions(operand, init_ref, mir_target_ty);
  710|    115|                if self.get_operand_type(&conv_op) != mir_target_ty {
  711|     38|                    Operand::Cast(mir_target_ty, Box::new(conv_op))
  712|       |                } else {
  713|     77|                    conv_op
  714|       |                }
  715|       |            }
  716|       |        }
  717|    115|    }
  718|       |
  719|     80|    fn count_flattened_members(&self, m: &StructMember) -> usize {
  720|     80|        if m.name.is_some() {
  721|     79|            return 1;
  722|      1|        }
  723|      1|        let ty_ref = m.member_type.ty();
  724|      1|        if !ty_ref.is_record() {
  725|      0|            return 1;
  726|      1|        }
  727|      1|        let mut flat_members = Vec::new();
  728|      1|        let mut flat_offsets = Vec::new();
  729|      1|        let type_obj = self.registry.get(ty_ref);
  730|      1|        type_obj.flatten_members_with_layouts(self.registry, &mut flat_members, &mut flat_offsets, 0);
  731|      1|        flat_members.len()
  732|     80|    }

  734|     22|    fn find_member_recursive(&self, members: &[StructMember], name: crate::ast::NameId) -> Option<(usize, Vec<usize>)> {
  735|     32|        for (i, m) in members.iter().enumerate() {
                                    ^22     ^22    ^22
  736|     32|            if m.name == Some(name) {
  737|     19|                return Some((i, Vec::new()));
  738|     13|            }
  739|     13|            if m.name.is_none() {
  740|       |                // Anonymous member: look inside
  741|      2|                let sub_ty = self.registry.get(m.member_type.ty());
  742|       |                if let TypeKind::Record {
  743|      2|                    members: sub_members, ..
  744|      2|                } = &sub_ty.kind
  745|      2|                    && let Some((sub_idx, mut path)) = self.find_member_recursive(sub_members, name)
  746|       |                {
  747|      2|                    path.insert(0, sub_idx);
  748|      2|                    return Some((i, path));
  749|      0|                }
  750|     11|            }
  751|       |        }
  752|      1|        None
  753|     22|    }

   14|     18|pub(crate) fn emit_unary_rvalue(op: &UnaryOp, operand: Operand, is_float: bool) -> Rvalue {
   15|       |    // Unary plus is a no-op in MIR (conversions handled by caller)
   16|     18|    if matches!(op, UnaryOp::Plus) {
   17|      0|        return Rvalue::Use(operand);
   18|     18|    }
   19|       |
   20|     18|    if is_float {
   21|      2|        let mir_op = map_ast_unary_op_to_mir_float(op);
   22|      2|        Rvalue::UnaryFloatOp(mir_op, operand)
   23|       |    } else {
   24|     16|        let mir_op = map_ast_unary_op_to_mir_int(op);
   25|     16|        Rvalue::UnaryIntOp(mir_op, operand)
   26|       |    }

   28|       |
   29|    128|fn map_ast_binary_op_to_mir_int(ast_op: &BinaryOp) -> BinaryIntOp {
   30|    128|    let op = ast_op.without_assignment().unwrap_or(*ast_op);
   31|    128|    match op {
   32|     23|        BinaryOp::Add => BinaryIntOp::Add,
   33|     14|        BinaryOp::Sub => BinaryIntOp::Sub,
   34|      3|        BinaryOp::Mul => BinaryIntOp::Mul,
   35|      3|        BinaryOp::Div => BinaryIntOp::Div,
   36|      4|        BinaryOp::Mod => BinaryIntOp::Mod,
   37|      3|        BinaryOp::BitAnd => BinaryIntOp::BitAnd,
   38|      2|        BinaryOp::BitOr => BinaryIntOp::BitOr,
   39|      2|        BinaryOp::BitXor => BinaryIntOp::BitXor,
   40|      2|        BinaryOp::LShift => BinaryIntOp::LShift,
   41|      2|        BinaryOp::RShift => BinaryIntOp::RShift,
   42|     15|        BinaryOp::Equal => BinaryIntOp::Eq,
   43|     44|        BinaryOp::NotEqual => BinaryIntOp::Ne,
   44|      6|        BinaryOp::Less => BinaryIntOp::Lt,
   45|      1|        BinaryOp::LessEqual => BinaryIntOp::Le,
   46|      3|        BinaryOp::Greater => BinaryIntOp::Gt,
   47|      1|        BinaryOp::GreaterEqual => BinaryIntOp::Ge,
   48|       |        // Logic ops are handled separately (short-circuit)
   49|      0|        BinaryOp::LogicAnd | BinaryOp::LogicOr => panic!("Logic ops should be handled separately"),
   50|      0|        BinaryOp::Comma => panic!("Comma op should be handled separately"), // Comma usually handled in expression lowering
   51|      0|        _ => panic!("Unsupported integer binary operator: {:?}", ast_op),
   52|       |    }
   53|    128|}
   54|       |
   55|     17|fn map_ast_binary_op_to_mir_float(ast_op: &BinaryOp) -> BinaryFloatOp {

   57|     17|    match op {
   58|      3|        BinaryOp::Add => BinaryFloatOp::Add,
   59|      1|        BinaryOp::Sub => BinaryFloatOp::Sub,
   60|      1|        BinaryOp::Mul => BinaryFloatOp::Mul,
   61|      1|        BinaryOp::Div => BinaryFloatOp::Div,
   62|      2|        BinaryOp::Equal => BinaryFloatOp::Eq,
   63|      5|        BinaryOp::NotEqual => BinaryFloatOp::Ne,
   64|      1|        BinaryOp::Less => BinaryFloatOp::Lt,
   65|      1|        BinaryOp::LessEqual => BinaryFloatOp::Le,
   66|      1|        BinaryOp::Greater => BinaryFloatOp::Gt,
   67|      1|        BinaryOp::GreaterEqual => BinaryFloatOp::Ge,
   68|      0|        _ => panic!("Unsupported float binary operator: {:?}", ast_op),
   69|       |    }

   76|     15|        UnaryOp::LogicNot => UnaryIntOp::LogicalNot,

   78|       |    }

  140|      1|    fn create_snippet<'a>(

  166|       |    /// Format a single diagnostic with rich source code context
  167|      1|    fn format_diagnostic(&self, diag: &Diagnostic, source_manager: &SourceManager) -> String {
  168|      1|        let renderer = if self.use_colors {
  169|      1|            Renderer::styled().decor_style(DecorStyle::Unicode)
  170|       |        } else {
  171|      0|            Renderer::plain()
  172|       |        };
  173|       |
  174|       |        // If it's a built-in source ID (e.g. command line define), simple print
  175|      1|        if diag.span.is_source_id_builtin() {
  176|      0|            return format!("{}: {}", self.format_location(diag, source_manager), diag.message);
  177|      1|        }
  178|       |
  179|       |        // Primary error snippet
  180|      1|        let snippet = self.create_snippet(diag.span, &diag.message, source_manager);
  181|       |        // Use primary_title instead of title
  182|      1|        let mut group = self.level(diag).primary_title(&diag.message).element(snippet);
  183|       |
  184|      1|        for hint in &diag.hints {
                          ^0
  185|      0|            group = group.element(Level::HELP.message(hint));
  186|      0|        }
  187|       |
  188|       |        // Handle macro expansion history
  189|       |        // We must collect strings first to ensure they live long enough for the snippets
  190|      1|        let mut expansion_history = Vec::new();
  191|      1|        let mut current_id = diag.span.source_id();
  192|       |
  193|      1|        while let Some(file_info) = source_manager.get_file_info(current_id) {
  194|      1|            if let Some(include_loc) = file_info.include_loc {
                                      ^0
  195|       |                // Determine if this is a macro expansion (virtual file) or an include
  196|      0|                let is_macro = file_info.path.to_str().is_some_and(|s| s.starts_with("<macro_"));
  197|      0|                let note_msg = if is_macro {
  198|      0|                    let macro_name = file_info
  199|      0|                        .path
  200|      0|                        .to_str()
  201|      0|                        .unwrap()
  202|      0|                        .trim_start_matches("<macro_")
  203|      0|                        .trim_end_matches('>');
  204|      0|                    format!("expanded from macro '{}'", macro_name)
  205|       |                } else {
  206|      0|                    "included from here".to_string()
  207|       |                };
  208|       |
  209|       |                // For visualization, use a 1-char span at the include/expansion location
  210|      0|                let exp_span = SourceSpan::new_with_length(include_loc.source_id(), include_loc.offset(), 1);
  211|      0|                expansion_history.push((exp_span, note_msg));
  212|       |
  213|      0|                current_id = include_loc.source_id();
  214|       |            } else {
  215|      1|                break;
  216|       |            }
  217|       |        }
  218|       |
  219|      1|        for (span, msg) in &expansion_history {
                           ^0    ^0
  220|      0|            let exp_snippet = self.create_snippet(*span, msg, source_manager);
  221|      0|            group = group.element(exp_snippet);
  222|      0|        }
  223|       |
  224|      1|        let report = &[group];
  225|      1|        renderer.render(report).to_string()
  226|      1|    }

    1|       |//! CLI parsing and configuration module
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles command-line argument parsing using clap and
    4|       |//! provides configuration structures for the compiler driver.
    5|       |
    6|       |use clap::{Args, Parser as CliParser};
    7|       |use std::path::PathBuf;
    8|       |use target_lexicon::Triple;
    9|       |
   10|       |use crate::{driver::artifact::CompilePhase, lang_options::CStandard};
   11|       |
   12|       |/// CLI interface using clap
   13|       |#[derive(CliParser, Debug)]
   14|       |#[clap(name = "cendol", about = "C11 Compiler written in Rust")]
   15|       |pub struct Cli {
   16|       |    /// Input C source files
   17|       |    #[clap(value_parser, required = true)]
   18|       |    pub input_files: Vec<PathBuf>,
   19|       |
   20|       |    /// Output file for AST dump
   21|       |    #[clap(short, long, value_name = "FILE")]
   22|       |    pub output: Option<PathBuf>,
   23|       |
   24|       |    /// Enable verbose diagnostic output
   25|       |    #[clap(short, long)]
   26|       |    pub verbose: bool,
   27|       |
   28|       |    /// Dump AST after parser phase
   29|       |    #[clap(long)]
   30|       |    pub dump_ast_after_parser: bool,
   31|       |
   32|       |    /// Dump AST after semantic lowering phase
   33|       |    #[clap(long)]
   34|       |    pub dump_ast_after_semantic_lowering: bool,
   35|       |
   36|       |    /// Dump MIR (Mid-level Intermediate Representation) to console
   37|       |    #[clap(long)]
   38|       |    pub dump_mir: bool,
   39|       |
   40|       |    /// Dump Cranelift IR (Intermediate Representation) to console
   41|       |    #[clap(long)]
   42|       |    pub dump_cranelift: bool,
   43|       |
   44|       |    /// Preprocess only, output preprocessed source to stdout
   45|       |    #[clap(short = 'E')]
   46|       |    pub preprocess_only: bool,
   47|       |
   48|       |    /// Preprocessor options
   49|       |    #[clap(flatten)]
   50|       |    pub preprocessor: PreprocessorOptions,
   51|       |
   52|       |    /// Suppress line markers in preprocessor output
   53|       |    #[clap(short = 'P')]
   54|       |    pub suppress_line_markers: bool,
   55|       |
   56|       |    /// Include search paths
   57|       |    #[clap(short = 'I', long = "include-path", value_name = "DIR", action = clap::ArgAction::Append)]
   58|       |    pub include_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
   59|       |
   60|       |    /// Preprocessor macro definitions
   61|       |    #[clap(short = 'D', long = "define", value_name = "NAME[=VALUE]", action = clap::ArgAction::Append)]
   62|       |    pub defines: Vec<String>,
   63|       |
   64|       |    /// Compiler warnings (e.g., -Wall, -Wshadow)
   65|       |    #[clap(short = 'W', action = clap::ArgAction::Append)]
   66|       |    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
   67|       |
   68|       |    /// Issue all the warnings demanded by strict ISO C
   69|       |    #[clap(long)]
   70|       |    pub pedantic: bool,
   71|       |
   72|       |    /// Issue all the warnings demanded by strict ISO C as errors
   73|       |    #[clap(long)]
   74|       |    pub pedantic_errors: bool,
   75|       |
   76|       |    /// Set C language standard (e.g., c99, c11)
   77|       |    #[clap(long = "std", value_name = "STANDARD")]
   78|       |    pub c_standard: Option<CStandard>,
   79|       |
   80|       |    /// Target triple (e.g. x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)
   81|       |    #[clap(long = "target", value_name = "TRIPLE")]
   82|       |    pub target: Option<String>,
   83|       |
   84|       |    /// Optimization level (e.g., -O0, -O1, -O2, -O3, -Os, -Oz)
   85|       |    #[clap(short = 'O', action = clap::ArgAction::Set)]
   86|       |    pub optimization: Option<String>,
   87|       |
   88|       |    /// Linked libraries (e.g., -lm)
   89|       |    #[clap(short = 'l', action = clap::ArgAction::Append)]
   90|       |    pub libraries: Vec<String>,
   91|       |
   92|       |    /// Library search paths (e.g., -L/usr/local/lib)
   93|       |    #[clap(short = 'L', action = clap::ArgAction::Append)]
   94|       |    pub library_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
   95|       |
   96|       |    /// Compile only, do not link
   97|       |    #[clap(short = 'c')]
   98|       |    pub compile_only: bool,
   99|       |
  100|       |    /// Generate debug information
  101|       |    #[clap(short = 'g')]
  102|       |    pub debug_info: bool,
  103|       |
  104|       |    /// Use a specific linker
  105|       |    #[clap(long = "fuse-ld", value_name = "LINKER")]
  106|       |    pub fuse_ld: Option<String>,
  107|       |
  108|       |    /// Maximum number of errors to report (default: 1)
  109|       |    #[clap(long = "fmax-errors", value_name = "N")]
  110|       |    pub fmax_errors: Option<usize>,
  111|       |}
  112|       |
  113|       |#[derive(Args, Debug)]
  114|       |pub struct PreprocessorOptions {
  115|       |    /// Maximum include depth
  116|       |    #[clap(long, default_value = "100")]
  117|       |    pub max_include_depth: usize,
  118|       |}
  119|       |
  120|       |/// input file can be path to file or string buffer pair (filename, buffer)

  124|       |    Buffer(String, Vec<u8>),

  126|       |
  127|       |/// Configuration for compilation

  141|       |
  142|       |    pub optimization: Option<String>,

  192|       |impl Cli {
  193|       |    /// Validate input files and check for option-like filenames
  194|      3|    fn validate_input_files(&self) -> Result<(), String> {
  195|      5|        for input_file in &self.input_files {
                          ^3
  196|      3|            let file_name = input_file.to_string_lossy();
  197|       |            // Allow files starting with '-' if they exist, or if they have an extension that looks like a C file
  198|      3|            if file_name.starts_with('-') && !input_file.exists() {
                                                           ^1
  199|       |                // If it starts with - and doesn't exist, it might be an unrecognized flag
  200|       |                // but clap should have handled it if it was a valid flag.
  201|       |                // However, some flags like -O2 or -Wshadow might not be explicitly defined in clap yet.
  202|       |                // So we should only error if it's definitely NOT meant to be a flag.
  203|       |
  204|       |                // For now, let's keep it but maybe we should allow it if we want to ignore unknown flags?
  205|       |                // Actually, the user wants us to support these flags.
  206|      1|                return Err(format!("File '{}' not found: No such file or directory", file_name)
  207|      1|                    + "\n"
  208|      1|                    + "If this is meant to be a command-line option, place it before the filename.\n"
  209|      1|                    + &format!(
  210|      1|                        "Example: {} -o output_file",
  211|      1|                        std::env::args().next().unwrap_or("cendol".to_string())
  212|      1|                    ));
  213|      2|            }
  214|       |        }
  215|      2|        Ok(())
  216|      3|    }
  217|       |
  218|       |    /// Convert CLI arguments into compilation configuration
  219|      3|    pub(crate) fn into_config(self) -> Result<CompileConfig, String> {
  220|       |        // Validate input files first
  221|      3|        self.validate_input_files()?;
                                                 ^1
  222|       |
  223|       |        // Parse defines
  224|      2|        let defines = self
  225|      2|            .defines
  226|      2|            .iter()
  227|      2|            .map(|def| {
  228|      2|                if let Some(eq_pos) = def.find('=') {
                                          ^1
  229|      1|                    let name = def[..eq_pos].to_string();
  230|      1|                    let value = Some(def[eq_pos + 1..].to_string());
  231|      1|                    (name, value)
  232|       |                } else {
  233|      1|                    (def.clone(), None)
  234|       |                }
  235|      2|            })
  236|      2|            .collect();
  237|       |
  238|       |        // Handle -Wall flag by adding "all" to warnings if -Wall is specified
  239|       |        // Actually, with short='W' and append, -Wall will result in "all" in warnings.
  240|      2|        let mut warnings = self.warnings;
  241|       |
  242|      2|        if self.pedantic {
  243|      1|            warnings.push("pedantic".to_string());
  244|      1|        }
  245|      2|        if self.pedantic_errors {
  246|      1|            warnings.push("pedantic-errors".to_string());
  247|      1|        }
  248|       |
  249|       |        // Build language options
  250|       |
  251|      2|        let c_standard = self.c_standard.unwrap_or_default();
  252|       |
  253|       |        // Build preprocessor configuration with include paths
  254|      2|        let mut system_include_paths = Vec::new();
  255|       |
  256|       |        // Add user-specified include paths as system include paths
  257|      3|        for path in &self.include_paths {
                          ^1
  258|      1|            system_include_paths.push(path.clone());
  259|      1|        }
  260|       |
  261|       |        // Add default system include paths
  262|      2|        if std::path::Path::new("custom-include").exists() {
  263|      2|            system_include_paths.push(PathBuf::from("custom-include"));
  264|      2|        }
                      ^0
  265|      2|        system_include_paths.push(PathBuf::from("/usr/include"));
  266|       |
  267|       |        // Add architecture-specific include paths
  268|      2|        let arch_paths = ["/usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu"];
  269|       |
  270|      4|        for arch_path in &arch_paths {
                          ^2
  271|      2|            let path = PathBuf::from(arch_path);
  272|      2|            if path.exists() {
  273|      2|                system_include_paths.push(path);
  274|      2|            }
                          ^0
  275|       |        }
  276|       |
  277|      2|        let stop_after = if self.preprocess_only {
  278|      1|            CompilePhase::Preprocess
  279|      1|        } else if self.dump_ast_after_parser {
  280|      0|            CompilePhase::Parse
  281|      1|        } else if self.dump_ast_after_semantic_lowering {
  282|      0|            CompilePhase::SemanticLowering
  283|      1|        } else if self.dump_mir {
  284|      1|            CompilePhase::Mir
  285|      0|        } else if self.dump_cranelift {
  286|      0|            CompilePhase::Cranelift
  287|       |        } else {
  288|      0|            CompilePhase::EmitObject
  289|       |        };
  290|       |
  291|      2|        let target_triple = if let Some(t) = self.target {
                                                      ^1
  292|      1|            t.parse::<Triple>()
  293|      1|                .map_err(|e| format!("Invalid target triple: {}", e))?
                                           ^0      ^0                              ^0
  294|       |        } else {
  295|      1|            Triple::host()
  296|       |        };
  297|       |
  298|      2|        Ok(CompileConfig {
  299|      2|            input_files: self.input_files.into_iter().map(PathOrBuffer::Path).collect(),
  300|      2|            output_path: self.output,
  301|      2|            stop_after,
  302|      2|            verbose: self.verbose,
  303|      2|            preprocessor: crate::pp::PPConfig {
  304|      2|                max_include_depth: self.preprocessor.max_include_depth,
  305|      2|                system_include_paths,
  306|      2|                target: target_triple.clone(),
  307|      2|                c_standard,
  308|      2|                ..Default::default()
  309|      2|            },
  310|      2|            suppress_line_markers: self.suppress_line_markers,
  311|      2|            defines,
  312|      2|            warnings,
  313|      2|            c_standard,
  314|      2|            target: target_triple,
  315|      2|            optimization: self.optimization,
  316|      2|            libraries: self.libraries,
  317|      2|            library_paths: self.library_paths,
  318|      2|            compile_only: self.compile_only,
  319|      2|            debug_info: self.debug_info,
  320|      2|            fuse_ld: self.fuse_ld,
  321|      2|            fmax_errors: self.fmax_errors,
  322|      2|        })
  323|      3|    }

  408|       |    #[test]
  409|      1|    fn test_cli_config_features() {
  410|      1|        let cli = Cli {
  411|      1|            input_files: vec![PathBuf::from("dummy.c")],
  412|      1|            output: None,
  413|      1|            verbose: false,
  414|      1|            dump_ast_after_parser: true,
  415|      1|            dump_ast_after_semantic_lowering: true,

  419|      1|            preprocessor: PreprocessorOptions { max_include_depth: 100 },

/app/src/main.rs:
    1|       |use cendol::driver::{Cli, CompilerDriver, DriverError};
    2|       |use clap::Parser;
    3|       |
    4|      0|fn main() {
    5|      0|    env_logger::init();
    6|       |
    7|       |    // Preprocess arguments to support single-dash long options specific to GCC/Clang compatibility.
    8|       |    // Clap does not strictly support single-dash long options (e.g. -std=c99), so we manually
    9|       |    // map them to double-dash options (e.g. --std=c99) before parsing.
   10|      0|    let args = std::env::args().map(|arg| {
   11|      0|        if arg.starts_with("-std=") || arg.starts_with("-target=") || arg.starts_with("-fuse-ld=") {
   12|      0|            format!("-{}", arg)
   13|      0|        } else if arg == "-pedantic" {
   14|      0|            "--pedantic".to_string()
   15|      0|        } else if arg == "-pedantic-errors" {
   16|      0|            "--pedantic-errors".to_string()
   17|       |        } else {
   18|      0|            arg
   19|       |        }
   20|      0|    });
   21|       |
   22|      0|    let cli = Cli::parse_from(args);
   23|       |
   24|      0|    match CompilerDriver::new(cli) {
   25|      0|        Ok(mut driver) => {
   26|      0|            match driver.run() {
   27|      0|                Ok(_) => std::process::exit(0),
   28|      0|                Err(e) => {
   29|      0|                    match e {
   30|      0|                        DriverError::IoError(e) => eprintln!("I/O error: {:?}", e),
   31|      0|                        DriverError::CompilationFailed => (), // diagnostic already printed by driver.run()
   32|      0|                        DriverError::LinkingFailed => (),     // error already printed by LinkGen
   33|       |                    };
   34|      0|                    std::process::exit(1);
   35|       |                }
   36|       |            }
   37|       |        }
   38|      0|        Err(e) => {
   39|      0|            eprintln!("Failed to initialize compiler: {}", e);
   40|      0|            std::process::exit(1);
   41|       |        }
   42|       |    }
   43|       |}

/app/src/mir.rs:
    1|       |//! Mid-level Intermediate Representation (MIR) for C11 compiler.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the MIR data structures and APIs for representing
    4|       |//! C11 programs after semantic analysis. The MIR is designed to be:
    5|       |//! - Typed: All entities have explicit types
    6|       |//! - Explicit: All C semantics are made explicit
    7|       |//! - Cranelift-friendly: Easy to lower to Cranelift IR
    8|       |//! - Non-SSA: Uses basic blocks with explicit control flow
    9|       |
   10|       |use hashbrown::HashMap;
   11|       |use serde::Serialize;
   12|       |use std::num::NonZeroU32;
   13|       |
   14|       |use crate::ast::NameId;
   15|       |
   16|       |pub mod dumper;
   17|       |pub mod validation;
   18|       |
   19|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR global variables
   20|       |pub type GlobalId = NonZeroU32;
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR modules
   23|       |pub type MirModuleId = NonZeroU32;
   24|       |
   25|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR functions
   26|       |pub type MirFunctionId = NonZeroU32;
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR blocks
   29|       |pub type MirBlockId = NonZeroU32;
   30|       |
   31|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR statements
   32|       |pub type MirStmtId = NonZeroU32;
   33|       |
   34|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR locals
   35|       |pub type LocalId = NonZeroU32;
   36|       |
   37|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR types
   38|       |pub type TypeId = NonZeroU32;
   39|       |
   40|       |/// Unique identifier for MIR constant values
   41|       |pub type ConstValueId = NonZeroU32;
   42|       |
   43|       |/// Function kind - distinguishes between defined and extern functions
   44|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Serialize)]
   45|       |pub enum MirFunctionKind {
   46|       |    Defined,
   47|       |    Extern,
   48|       |}
   49|       |
   50|       |/// MIR Module - Top-level container for MIR
   51|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize)]

  177|       |    Deref(Box<Operand>),

  182|       |}

  363|       |                | MirType::I16

  374|    182|    pub(crate) fn width(&self) -> u32 {
  375|    182|        match self {
  376|     29|            MirType::I8 | MirType::U8 | MirType::Bool => 8,
  377|      7|            MirType::I16 | MirType::U16 => 16,
  378|     93|            MirType::I32 | MirType::U32 | MirType::F32 => 32,
  379|     41|            MirType::I64 | MirType::U64 | MirType::F64 => 64,
  380|      2|            MirType::F80 | MirType::F128 => 128,
  381|      8|            MirType::Pointer { .. } => 64, // Assume 64-bit pointers
  382|      2|            _ => 0,                        // Others have no intrinsic "width" in this context
  383|       |        }
  384|    182|    }
  385|       |
  386|       |    /// Truncate an integer value to the width of this type.
  387|       |    /// Handles sign extension if this is a signed type.
  388|    166|    pub(crate) fn truncate_int(&self, val: i64) -> i64 {
  389|    166|        if !self.is_int() && !self.is_pointer() {
                                           ^7
  390|      0|            return val;
  391|    166|        }
  392|       |
  393|    166|        let width = self.width();
  394|    166|        if width >= 64 {
  395|     45|            return val;
  396|    121|        }
  397|       |
  398|       |        // Apply bitmask for the width
  399|    121|        let mask = if width > 0 { (1u64 << width) - 1 } else { 0 };
                                                                             ^0
  400|    121|        let truncated = (val as u64) & mask;
  401|       |
  402|    121|        if self.is_signed() {
  403|       |            // Sign-extend if the MSB of the truncated value is set
  404|     99|            let sign_bit = 1u64 << (width - 1);

/app/src/mir/dumper.rs:
    1|       |//! MIR (Mid-level Intermediate Representation) Dumper
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides functionality to dump MIR in a human-readable format
    4|       |//! following the C11 compiler's specific conventions:
    5|       |//! - Local variables: %0, %1, %tmp3
    6|       |//! - Global variables: @g_var
    7|       |//! - Basic blocks: bb0, bb1
    8|       |//! - Constants: const 42, const null
    9|       |//! - Types: i8, i16, i32, i64, u8, u16, u32, u64, f32, f64, ptr<T>
   10|       |
   11|       |use std::fmt::Write;
   12|       |
   13|       |use super::{
   14|       |    BinaryFloatOp, CallTarget, ConstValueId, ConstValueKind, Global, GlobalId, LocalId, MirBlock, MirBlockId,
   15|       |    MirFunction, MirFunctionId, MirFunctionKind, MirStmt, MirType, Operand, Place, Rvalue, Terminator, TypeId,
   16|       |    UnaryFloatOp,
   17|       |};

   47|       |

   50|      1|            writeln!(output, "; MIR Dump for Module {}", self.mir.module.id.get())?;
                                                                                                ^0

                                                                                ^0
   52|      1|            writeln!(output)?;

   54|       |

   65|      0|                }

   70|       |        // Dump all functions

   73|     95|                self.dump_function(&mut output, func)?;

                                              ^0
   75|      0|            }
   76|       |        }
   77|       |
   78|     89|        Ok(output)
   79|     89|    }
   80|       |
   81|       |    /// Dump a single function
   82|     95|    fn dump_function(&self, output: &mut String, func: &MirFunction) -> Result<(), std::fmt::Error> {
   83|       |        // Function signature
   84|     95|        let return_type = self.type_to_string(func.return_type);
   85|     95|        let fn_keyword = match func.kind {
   86|      7|            MirFunctionKind::Extern => "extern fn",
   87|     88|            MirFunctionKind::Defined => "fn",
   88|       |        };
   89|     95|        write!(output, "{} {}(", fn_keyword, func.name)?;
                                                                     ^0
   90|       |
   91|       |        // Dump parameters
   92|     95|        for (i, &param_id) in func.params.iter().enumerate() {
                           ^29 ^29
   93|     29|            if i > 0 {
   94|      9|                write!(output, ", ")?;
                                                  ^0
   95|     20|            }

   97|     29|                let param_type = self.type_to_string(param.type_id);
   98|     29|                let param_name = param.name.as_ref().map_or("%unnamed".to_string(), |s| s.to_string());
   99|     29|                write!(output, "%{}: {}", param_name, param_type)?;
                                                                               ^0
  100|       |            } else {
  101|      0|                write!(output, "%param{}: ?", param_id.get())?;
  102|       |            }
  103|       |        }
  104|     95|        if func.is_variadic {
  105|      4|            if !func.params.is_empty() {
  106|      4|                write!(output, ", ")?;
                                                  ^0
  107|      0|            }
  108|      4|            write!(output, "...")?;
                                               ^0
  109|     91|        }
  110|       |
  111|     95|        write!(output, ") -> {}", return_type)?;
                                                            ^0
  112|       |
  113|       |        // For extern functions, don't output any body
  114|     95|        if matches!(func.kind, MirFunctionKind::Extern) {
                         ^88
  115|      7|            writeln!(output)?;
                                          ^0
  116|      7|            return Ok(());
  117|     88|        }
  118|       |
  119|     88|        writeln!(output)?;
                                      ^0
  120|       |
  121|       |        // Function body
  122|     88|        writeln!(output, "{{")?;
                                            ^0
  123|       |
  124|       |        // Dump locals section
  125|     88|        if func.locals.is_empty() == false {
  126|     62|            writeln!(output, "  locals {{")?;
                                                         ^0
  127|    297|            for &local_id in &func.locals {
                               ^235
  128|    235|                if let Some(local) = self.mir.locals.get(&local_id) {
  129|    235|                    let local_type = self.type_to_string(local.type_id);
  130|    235|                    let local_name = self.local_to_string(local_id);
  131|    235|                    writeln!(output, "    {}: {}", local_name, local_type)?;
                                                                                        ^0
  132|      0|                }
  133|       |            }
  134|     62|            writeln!(output, "  }}")?;
                                                  ^0
  135|     26|        }
  136|       |
  137|       |        // Dump basic blocks
  138|    257|        for &block_id in &func.blocks {
                           ^169
  139|    169|            if let Some(block) = self.mir.blocks.get(&block_id) {
  140|    169|                self.dump_block(output, block_id, block)?;
                                                                      ^0
  141|      0|            }
  142|       |        }
  143|       |
  144|     88|        writeln!(output, "}}")?;
                                            ^0
  145|     88|        Ok(())
  146|     95|    }

  148|       |    /// Dump all type definitions
  149|     89|    fn dump_types(&self, output: &mut String) -> Result<(), std::fmt::Error> {
  150|    292|        for (i, mir_type) in self.mir.module.types.iter().enumerate() {
                                           ^89                          ^89

  152|    292|            let type_str = self.type_to_string_with_id(type_id, mir_type);
  153|    292|            writeln!(output, "{}", type_str)?;

  154|       |        }
  155|     89|        Ok(())

  158|       |    /// Convert MIR type to string representation with type ID
  159|    292|    fn type_to_string_with_id(&self, type_id: TypeId, mir_type: &MirType) -> String {
  160|    292|        let type_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(type_id);
  161|    292|        let type_name = format!("%t{}", type_index);
  162|       |
  163|    292|        match mir_type {
  164|     24|            MirType::Void => format!("type {} = void", type_name),
  165|      3|            MirType::Bool => format!("type {} = bool", type_name),
  166|       |
  167|     13|            MirType::I8 => format!("type {} = i8", type_name),
  168|      1|            MirType::I16 => format!("type {} = i16", type_name),
  169|     83|            MirType::I32 => format!("type {} = i32", type_name),
  170|      4|            MirType::I64 => format!("type {} = i64", type_name),
  171|      4|            MirType::U8 => format!("type {} = u8", type_name),
  172|      2|            MirType::U16 => format!("type {} = u16", type_name),
  173|      2|            MirType::U32 => format!("type {} = u32", type_name),
  174|      8|            MirType::U64 => format!("type {} = u64", type_name),
  175|      6|            MirType::F32 => format!("type {} = f32", type_name),
  176|      5|            MirType::F64 => format!("type {} = f64", type_name),
  177|      2|            MirType::F80 => format!("type {} = f80", type_name),
  178|      1|            MirType::F128 => format!("type {} = f128", type_name),
  179|     33|            MirType::Pointer { pointee } => {
  180|     33|                let pointee_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(*pointee);
  181|     33|                format!("type {} = ptr<%t{}>", type_name, pointee_index)
  182|       |            }
  183|     42|            MirType::Array { element, size, .. } => {
  184|     42|                let elem_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(*element);
  185|     42|                format!("type {} = [{}]%t{}", type_name, size, elem_index)
  186|       |            }
  187|       |            MirType::Function {
  188|     24|                return_type,
  189|     24|                params,
  190|     24|                is_variadic,
  191|       |            } => {
  192|     24|                let ret_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(*return_type);
  193|     24|                let mut param_types: Vec<String> = params
  194|     24|                    .iter()
  195|     24|                    .map(|&p| {
                                            ^9
  196|      9|                        let param_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(p);
  197|      9|                        format!("%t{}", param_index)
  198|      9|                    })
  199|     24|                    .collect();
  200|     24|                if *is_variadic {
  201|      4|                    param_types.push("...".to_string());
  202|     20|                }
  203|     24|                format!("type {} = fn({}) -> %t{}", type_name, param_types.join(", "), ret_index)
  204|       |            }
  205|       |            MirType::Record {
  206|     35|                name,
  207|     35|                field_types,
  208|     35|                field_names,
  209|     35|                is_union,
  210|       |                ..
  211|       |            } => {
  212|     35|                let field_strs: Vec<String> = field_names
  213|     35|                    .iter()
  214|     35|                    .zip(field_types.iter())
  215|     62|                    .map(|(fname, fid)| {
                                   ^35
  216|     62|                        let field_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(*fid);
  217|     62|                        format!("{}: %t{}", fname, field_index)
  218|     62|                    })
  219|     35|                    .collect();
  220|     35|                let kind = if *is_union { "union" } else { "struct" };
                                                        ^1               ^34
  221|     35|                format!("type {} = {} {} {{ {} }}", type_name, kind, name, field_strs.join(", "))
  222|       |            }
  223|       |        }
  224|    292|    }

  226|       |    /// Helper function to get type index from TypeId
  227|  1.01k|    fn get_type_index_from_type_id(&self, type_id: TypeId) -> usize {
  228|       |        // Fallback: assume sequential mapping
  229|  1.01k|        (type_id.get() - 1) as usize
  230|  1.01k|    }

  232|       |    /// Dump a global variable
  233|     45|    fn dump_global(&self, output: &mut String, global: &Global) -> Result<(), std::fmt::Error> {

  235|     45|        write!(output, "global @{}: {}", global.name, global_type)?;

                                  ^44

                                      ^10
  240|     10|                write!(output, " = {}", string_repr)?;
                                                                  ^0
  241|       |            } else {

                                                                                     ^0
  243|       |            }
  244|      1|        }
  245|       |
  246|     45|        Ok(())
  247|     45|    }

  249|       |    /// Dump a basic block
  250|    169|    fn dump_block(&self, output: &mut String, block_id: MirBlockId, block: &MirBlock) -> Result<(), std::fmt::Error> {
  251|    169|        writeln!(output)?;
                                      ^0
  252|    169|        writeln!(output, "  {}:", self.block_to_string(block_id))?;
                                                                               ^0
  253|       |
  254|       |        // Dump statements in the block
  255|    485|        for &stmt_id in &block.statements {
                           ^316
  256|    316|            if let Some(stmt) = self.mir.statements.get(&stmt_id) {

  259|    316|                writeln!(output)?;

  260|      0|            }
  261|       |        }
  262|       |
  263|       |        // Dump terminator (MUST be explicit)
  264|    169|        write!(output, "    ")?;
                                            ^0
  265|    169|        self.dump_terminator(output, &block.terminator)?;
                                                                     ^0
  266|    169|        writeln!(output)?;
                                      ^0
  267|       |

  269|    169|    }

  271|       |    /// Dump a statement
  272|    316|    fn dump_statement(&self, output: &mut String, stmt: &MirStmt) -> Result<(), std::fmt::Error> {
  273|    316|        match stmt {
  274|    283|            MirStmt::Assign(place, rvalue) => {
  275|    283|                write!(output, "{} = ", self.place_to_string(place))?;
                                                                                  ^0
  276|    283|                self.dump_rvalue(output, rvalue)?;
                                                              ^0
  277|       |            }
  278|      1|            MirStmt::Store(operand, place) => {
  279|      1|                write!(output, "store ")?;
                                                      ^0
  280|      1|                self.dump_operand(output, operand)?;
                                                                ^0
  281|      1|                write!(output, ", {}", self.place_to_string(place))?;
                                                                                 ^0
  282|       |            }
  283|     26|            MirStmt::Call { target, args, dest } => {
  284|     26|                if let Some(place) = dest {
                                          ^6
  285|      6|                    write!(output, "{} = ", self.place_to_string(place))?;
                                                                                      ^0
  286|     20|                }
  287|     26|                write!(output, "call {}(", self.call_target_to_string(target))?;
                                                                                            ^0
  288|     26|                for (i, operand) in args.iter().enumerate() {
                                   ^16^16
  289|     16|                    if i > 0 {
  290|      6|                        write!(output, ", ")?;
                                                          ^0
  291|     10|                    }
  292|     16|                    self.dump_operand(output, operand)?;
                                                                    ^0
  293|       |                }
  294|     26|                write!(output, ")")?;
                                                 ^0
  295|       |            }
  296|      1|            MirStmt::Alloc(place, type_id) => {
  297|      1|                write!(
  298|      1|                    output,
  299|      1|                    "{} = alloc {}",
  300|      1|                    self.place_to_string(place),
  301|      1|                    self.type_to_string(*type_id)
  302|      0|                )?;
  303|       |            }
  304|      1|            MirStmt::Dealloc(operand) => {
  305|      1|                write!(output, "dealloc ")?;
                                                        ^0
  306|      1|                self.dump_operand(output, operand)?;

  307|       |            }
  308|      1|            MirStmt::BuiltinVaStart(ap, last) => {
  309|      1|                write!(
  310|      1|                    output,
  311|      1|                    "va_start({}, {})",
  312|      1|                    self.place_to_string(ap),
  313|      1|                    self.operand_to_string(last)
  314|      0|                )?;
  315|       |            }
  316|      1|            MirStmt::BuiltinVaEnd(ap) => {
  317|      1|                write!(output, "va_end({})", self.place_to_string(ap))?;
                                                                                    ^0
  318|       |            }
  319|      1|            MirStmt::BuiltinVaCopy(dst, src) => {

  326|       |            }
  327|      1|            MirStmt::AtomicStore(ptr, val, order) => {

  340|       |    /// Dump a terminator

  342|    169|        match terminator {

  346|     23|            Terminator::If(cond, then_block, else_block) => {

  353|      0|                )?;

  355|     99|            Terminator::Return(operand) => match operand {
  356|     81|                Some(op) => {

  362|       |                }

  364|       |            Terminator::Unreachable => {
  365|      2|                write!(output, "unreachable")?;

  366|       |            }
  367|       |        }
  368|    169|        Ok(())
  369|    169|    }

  371|       |    /// Convert MIR type to string representation
  372|    548|    fn type_to_string(&self, type_id: TypeId) -> String {
  373|    548|        if let Some(mir_type) = self.mir.types.get(&type_id) {
  374|    548|            let type_index = self.get_type_index_from_type_id(type_id);
  375|       |
  376|    548|            match mir_type {
  377|     29|                MirType::Void => "void".to_string(),
  378|     11|                MirType::Bool => "bool".to_string(),
  379|       |
  380|     47|                MirType::I8 => "i8".to_string(),
  381|      1|                MirType::I16 => "i16".to_string(),
  382|    195|                MirType::I32 => "i32".to_string(),
  383|      8|                MirType::I64 => "i64".to_string(),
  384|      4|                MirType::U8 => "u8".to_string(),
  385|      4|                MirType::U16 => "u16".to_string(),
  386|      4|                MirType::U32 => "u32".to_string(),
  387|     24|                MirType::U64 => "u64".to_string(),
  388|     29|                MirType::F32 => "f32".to_string(),
  389|     29|                MirType::F64 => "f64".to_string(),
  390|      3|                MirType::F80 => "f80".to_string(),
  391|      0|                MirType::F128 => "f128".to_string(),
  392|     61|                MirType::Pointer { pointee } => {
  393|     61|                    let pointee_type = self.type_to_string(*pointee);
  394|     61|                    format!("ptr<{}>", pointee_type)
  395|       |                }
  396|     31|                MirType::Array { element, size, .. } => {
  397|     31|                    let elem_type = self.type_to_string(*element);
  398|     31|                    format!("[{}]{}", size, elem_type)
  399|       |                }
  400|       |                MirType::Function {
  401|      1|                    return_type,
  402|      1|                    params,
  403|      1|                    is_variadic,
  404|       |                } => {
  405|      1|                    let ret_type = self.type_to_string(*return_type);
  406|      1|                    let mut param_types: Vec<String> = params.iter().map(|&p| self.type_to_string(p)).collect();
                                                                                            ^0   ^0             ^0
  407|      1|                    if *is_variadic {
  408|      0|                        param_types.push("...".to_string());
  409|      1|                    }
  410|      1|                    format!("fn({}) -> {}", param_types.join(", "), ret_type)
  411|       |                }
  412|       |                MirType::Record { .. } => {
  413|       |                    // For aggregate types, use the type ID to keep output concise
  414|     67|                    format!("%t{}", type_index)
  415|       |                }
  416|       |            }
  417|       |        } else {
  418|      0|            format!("unknown_type_{}", type_id.get())
  419|       |        }
  420|    548|    }

  422|       |    /// Convert local ID to string representation
  423|    882|    fn local_to_string(&self, local_id: LocalId) -> String {
  424|    882|        let local = self.mir.locals.get(&local_id);
  425|    882|        let name = match local {
  426|    882|            Some(local) => {
  427|    882|                if let Some(name) = &local.name {
                                          ^450
  428|    450|                    format!("{}", name)
  429|       |                } else {
  430|    432|                    format!("{}", local.id.get())
  431|       |                }
  432|       |            }
  433|      0|            None => format!("{}", local_id.get()),
  434|       |        };
  435|       |
  436|    882|        format!("%{}", name)
  437|    882|    }
  438|       |
  439|       |    /// Convert global ID to string representation
  440|     23|    fn global_to_string(&self, global_id: GlobalId) -> String {
  441|     23|        if let Some(global) = self.mir.globals.get(&global_id) {
  442|     23|            format!("@{}", global.name)
  443|       |        } else {
  444|      0|            format!("@global_{}", global_id.get())
  445|       |        }
  446|     23|    }
  447|       |
  448|       |    /// Convert block ID to string representation
  449|    260|    fn block_to_string(&self, block_id: MirBlockId) -> String {
  450|    260|        format!("bb{}", block_id.get())
  451|    260|    }
  452|       |

  456|     30|            format!("{}", func.name)

  458|      0|            format!("func_{}", func_id.get())

  470|       |    /// Try to format a constant as a string literal if applicable
  471|     44|    fn try_format_as_string_literal(&self, global_name: &str, const_id: ConstValueId) -> Option<String> {
  472|       |        // Only format as string if the global name starts with .L.str (our anonymous string literals)
  473|     44|        if !global_name.starts_with(".L.str") {
  474|     32|            return None;
  475|     12|        }
  476|       |
  477|     12|        if let Some(const_value) = self.mir.constants.get(&const_id)
  478|     12|            && let ConstValueKind::ArrayLiteral(elements) = &const_value.kind
                                                              ^10
  479|       |        {
  480|       |            // Try to convert the array elements to a string
  481|     10|            let mut string_content = String::new();
  482|       |
  483|     83|            for &element_id in elements {
  484|     83|                let element = self.mir.constants.get(&element_id).unwrap();
  485|     83|                if let ConstValueKind::Int(byte) = &element.kind {
  486|     83|                    let byte = *byte as u8;
  487|     83|                    if byte == 0 {
  488|       |                        // Null terminator - end of string
  489|     10|                        break;
  490|     73|                    }
  491|       |
  492|     67|                    match byte {
  493|      6|                        b'\n' => string_content.push_str("\\n"),
  494|      0|                        b'\r' => string_content.push_str("\\r"),
  495|      0|                        b'\t' => string_content.push_str("\\t"),
  496|      0|                        b'\\' => string_content.push_str("\\\\"),
  497|      0|                        b'"' => string_content.push_str("\\\""),
  498|     67|                        b if (32..=126).contains(&b) => string_content.push(b as char),
  499|       |                        _ => {
  500|       |                            // Use hex escape for other non-printable characters
  501|       |                            use std::fmt::Write;
  502|      0|                            write!(&mut string_content, "\\x{:02x}", byte).ok()?;
  503|       |                        }
  504|       |                    }
  505|       |                } else {
  506|       |                    // Non-integer element, not a string
  507|      0|                    return None;
  508|       |                }
  509|       |            }
  510|       |
  511|     10|            if !string_content.is_empty() {
  512|     10|                return Some(format!("const \"{}\"", string_content));
  513|      0|            }
  514|      2|        }
  515|       |
  516|      2|        None
  517|     44|    }
  518|       |
  519|       |    /// Convert constant ID to string representation
  520|    307|    fn const_to_string(&self, const_id: ConstValueId) -> String {
  521|    307|        if let Some(const_value) = self.mir.constants.get(&const_id) {
  522|    307|            match &const_value.kind {
  523|    213|                ConstValueKind::Int(val) => format!("const {}", val),
  524|      6|                ConstValueKind::Float(val) => format!("const {}", val),
  525|      0|                ConstValueKind::Bool(val) => format!("const {}", val),
  526|      1|                ConstValueKind::Null => "const null".to_string(),
  527|     38|                ConstValueKind::Zero => "const zero".to_string(),
  528|     21|                ConstValueKind::StructLiteral(fields) => {
  529|       |                    // Expand struct literal to show field contents
  530|     21|                    let field_strs: Vec<String> = fields
  531|     21|                        .iter()
  532|     32|                        .map(|(field_idx, field_const_id)| {
                                       ^21
  533|     32|                            let field_const_str = self.const_to_string(*field_const_id);
  534|     32|                            format!("{}: {}", field_idx, field_const_str)
  535|     32|                        })
  536|     21|                        .collect();
  537|     21|                    format!("const struct_literal {{ {} }}", field_strs.join(", "))
  538|       |                }
  539|     11|                ConstValueKind::ArrayLiteral(elements) => {
  540|       |                    // Expand array literal to show element contents
  541|     11|                    let element_strs: Vec<String> = elements
  542|     11|                        .iter()
  543|     50|                        .map(|element_const_id| self.const_to_string(*element_const_id))
                                       ^11
  544|     11|                        .collect();
  545|     11|                    format!("const array_literal [{}]", element_strs.join(", "))
  546|       |                }
  547|     11|                ConstValueKind::GlobalAddress(global_id) => {
  548|     11|                    format!("const {}", self.global_to_string(*global_id))
  549|       |                }
  550|      6|                ConstValueKind::FunctionAddress(func_id) => {
  551|      6|                    format!("const {}", self.function_to_string(*func_id))
  552|       |                }
  553|       |            }
  554|       |        } else {
  555|      0|            format!("const unknown_{}", const_id.get())
  556|       |        }
  557|    307|    }

/app/src/mir/validation.rs:
    1|       |//! MIR Validation Pass
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module implements a validation pass that ensures MIR is well-formed
    4|       |//! and ready for code generation. The validation pass checks:
    5|       |//! - All locals have types
    6|       |//! - All blocks end with a Terminator
    7|       |//! - No illegal operations remain
    8|       |//! - MIR is Cranelift-safe
    9|       |
   10|       |use crate::{
   11|       |    ast::NameId,
   12|       |    mir::{
   13|       |        BinaryFloatOp, BinaryIntOp, CallTarget, ConstValue, ConstValueId, ConstValueKind, GlobalId, LocalId,
   14|       |        MirBlockId, MirFunction, MirFunctionId, MirFunctionKind, MirProgram, MirStmt, MirStmtId, MirType, Operand,
   15|       |        Place, Rvalue, Terminator, TypeId, UnaryFloatOp, UnaryIntOp,
   16|       |    },
   17|       |};
   18|       |
   19|       |/// MIR Validation Error
   20|       |#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Clone)]
   21|       |pub enum ValidationError {
   22|       |    /// Illegal operation found in MIR
   23|       |    IllegalOperation(String),
   24|       |    /// Type not found in type table
   25|       |    TypeNotFound(TypeId),
   26|       |    /// Local not found in local table
   27|       |    LocalNotFound(LocalId),
   28|       |    /// Global not found in global table
   29|       |    GlobalNotFound(GlobalId),
   30|       |    /// Function not found in function table
   31|       |    FunctionNotFound(MirFunctionId),
   32|       |    /// Block not found in block table
   33|       |    BlockNotFound(MirBlockId),
   34|       |    /// Statement not found in statement table
   35|       |    StatementNotFound(MirStmtId),
   36|       |    /// Invalid cast operation
   37|       |    InvalidCast(TypeId, TypeId),
   38|       |    /// Function call argument type mismatch
   39|       |    FunctionCallArgTypeMismatch {
   40|       |        func_name: NameId,
   41|       |        arg_index: usize,
   42|       |        expected_type: TypeId,
   43|       |        actual_type: TypeId,
   44|       |    },
   45|       |    /// Constant value is out of range for its type
   46|       |    ConstantValueOutOfRange {
   47|       |        const_id: ConstValueId,
   48|       |        value: i64,
   49|       |        type_id: TypeId,
   50|       |    },
   51|       |}
   52|       |
   53|       |impl std::fmt::Display for ValidationError {
   54|     10|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   55|     10|        match self {
   56|      1|            ValidationError::IllegalOperation(op) => write!(f, "Illegal operation: {}", op),
   57|      1|            ValidationError::TypeNotFound(type_id) => write!(f, "Type {} not found", type_id.get()),
   58|      1|            ValidationError::LocalNotFound(local_id) => write!(f, "Local {} not found", local_id.get()),
   59|      1|            ValidationError::GlobalNotFound(global_id) => write!(f, "Global {} not found", global_id.get()),
   60|      1|            ValidationError::FunctionNotFound(func_id) => write!(f, "Function {} not found", func_id.get()),
   61|      1|            ValidationError::BlockNotFound(block_id) => write!(f, "Block {} not found", block_id.get()),
   62|      1|            ValidationError::StatementNotFound(stmt_id) => write!(f, "Statement {} not found", stmt_id.get()),
   63|      1|            ValidationError::InvalidCast(from, to) => {
   64|      1|                write!(f, "Invalid cast from type {} to type {}", from.get(), to.get())
   65|       |            }
   66|       |            ValidationError::FunctionCallArgTypeMismatch {
   67|      1|                func_name,
   68|      1|                arg_index,
   69|      1|                expected_type,
   70|      1|                actual_type,
   71|       |            } => {
   72|      1|                write!(
   73|      1|                    f,
   74|      1|                    "Function '{}' argument {} type mismatch: expected type {}, got type {}",
   75|       |                    func_name,
   76|       |                    arg_index,
   77|      1|                    expected_type.get(),
   78|      1|                    actual_type.get()
   79|       |                )
   80|       |            }
   81|       |            ValidationError::ConstantValueOutOfRange {
   82|      1|                const_id,
   83|      1|                value,
   84|      1|                type_id,
   85|       |            } => {
   86|      1|                write!(
   87|      1|                    f,
   88|      1|                    "Constant {} value {} is out of range for type {}",
   89|      1|                    const_id.get(),
   90|       |                    value,
   91|      1|                    type_id.get()
   92|       |                )
   93|       |            }
   94|       |        }
   95|     10|    }
   96|       |}
   97|       |
   98|       |/// MIR Validation Pass
   99|       |///
  100|       |/// This pass validates that MIR is well-formed and ready for code generation.
  101|       |/// It performs comprehensive checks but does not modify the MIR.
  102|       |pub struct MirValidator<'a> {
  103|       |    mir: &'a MirProgram,
  104|       |    errors: Vec<ValidationError>,
  105|       |    pointee_to_pointer: hashbrown::HashMap<TypeId, TypeId>,
  106|       |    bool_type: Option<TypeId>,
  107|       |    i32_type: Option<TypeId>,
  108|       |}

  507|    224|    fn validate_rvalue(&mut self, r: &Rvalue) -> Option<TypeId> {

  524|      0|                    UnaryIntOp::BitwiseNot => ta,

  659|      3|    fn are_types_compatible(&self, t1: TypeId, t2: TypeId) -> bool {
  660|      3|        if t1 == t2 {
  661|      1|            return true;
  662|      2|        }
  663|       |
  664|      2|        let (Some(ty1), Some(ty2)) = (self.mir.types.get(&t1), self.mir.types.get(&t2)) else {
  665|      0|            return false;
  666|       |        };
  667|       |
  668|      2|        match (ty1, ty2) {
  669|      0|            (MirType::Pointer { pointee: p1 }, MirType::Pointer { pointee: p2 }) => self.are_types_compatible(*p1, *p2),
  670|       |            (
  671|       |                MirType::Array {
  672|      1|                    element: e1, size: s1, ..
  673|       |                },
  674|       |                MirType::Array {
  675|      1|                    element: e2, size: s2, ..
  676|       |                },
  677|      1|            ) => s1 == s2 && self.are_types_compatible(*e1, *e2),
  678|       |            (
  679|       |                MirType::Function {
  680|      0|                    return_type: r1,
  681|      0|                    params: p1,
  682|      0|                    is_variadic: v1,
  683|       |                },
  684|       |                MirType::Function {
  685|      0|                    return_type: r2,
  686|      0|                    params: p2,
  687|      0|                    is_variadic: v2,
  688|       |                },
  689|       |            ) => {
  690|      0|                v1 == v2
  691|      0|                    && p1.len() == p2.len()
  692|      0|                    && self.are_types_compatible(*r1, *r2)
  693|      0|                    && p1.iter().zip(p2).all(|(&a, &b)| self.are_types_compatible(a, b))
  694|       |            }
  695|      1|            _ => ty1 == ty2,
  696|       |        }
  697|      3|    }

  205|       |    /// Skip tokens until we find a synchronization point.
  206|       |    /// Default behavior: skip aggressively until `;` at depth 0, unmatched delimiter, or EOF.
  207|     45|    fn synchronize(&mut self) {
  208|     45|        self.synchronize_until(&[]);
  209|     45|    }
  210|       |
  211|       |    /// Skip tokens until we find a synchronization point, but stop **before** any
  212|       |    /// token in `stop_before` when at balanced depth. This lets the caller's loop
  213|       |    /// see the delimiter it needs (e.g. `}` for compound statements).
  214|     63|    fn synchronize_until(&mut self, stop_before: &[TokenKind]) {
  215|     63|        let mut brace_depth: i32 = 0;
  216|     63|        let mut paren_depth: i32 = 0;
  217|     63|        let mut any_advance = false;
  218|     63|        let mut stopped_at_stop_token = false;
  219|       |
  220|    193|        while let Some(token) = self.try_current_token() {
                                     ^191
  221|       |            // If we're at balanced depth and the current token is one the caller
  222|       |            // wants to handle, stop WITHOUT consuming it.
  223|    191|            if brace_depth <= 0 && paren_depth <= 0 && stop_before.contains(&token.kind) {
                                                 ^177                ^143        ^143     ^143
  224|      1|                stopped_at_stop_token = true;
  225|      1|                break;
  226|    190|            }
  227|       |
  228|    190|            match token.kind {
  229|      3|                TokenKind::LeftBrace => {
  230|      3|                    brace_depth += 1;
  231|      3|                    self.advance();
  232|      3|                    any_advance = true;
  233|      3|                }
  234|       |                TokenKind::RightBrace => {
  235|      3|                    brace_depth -= 1;
  236|      3|                    self.advance();
  237|      3|                    any_advance = true;
  238|      3|                    if brace_depth < 0 {
  239|      1|                        break; // Unmatched brace, stop here
  240|      2|                    }
  241|       |                }
  242|     13|                TokenKind::LeftParen => {
  243|     13|                    paren_depth += 1;
  244|     13|                    self.advance();
  245|     13|                    any_advance = true;
  246|     13|                }
  247|       |                TokenKind::RightParen => {
  248|     33|                    paren_depth -= 1;
  249|     33|                    self.advance();
  250|     33|                    any_advance = true;
  251|     33|                    if paren_depth < 0 {
  252|     23|                        break; // Unmatched paren, stop here
  253|     10|                    }
  254|       |                }
  255|       |                TokenKind::Semicolon => {
  256|     31|                    self.advance();
  257|     31|                    any_advance = true;
  258|     31|                    if brace_depth == 0 && paren_depth == 0 {
                                                         ^29
  259|     28|                        break;
  260|      3|                    }
  261|       |                }
  262|       |                TokenKind::EndOfFile => {
  263|      8|                    self.advance();
  264|      8|                    any_advance = true;
  265|      8|                    break;
  266|       |                }
  267|     99|                _ => {
  268|     99|                    self.advance();
  269|     99|                    any_advance = true;
  270|     99|                }
  271|       |            }
  272|       |        }
  273|       |
  274|       |        // Force advance only if we didn't advance AND didn't stop at a designated stop token
  275|     63|        if !any_advance && !stopped_at_stop_token {
                                         ^3
  276|      2|            self.advance();
  277|     61|        }
  278|     63|    }

    1|       |//! Declarator parsing module
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles the parsing of C declarators, which are the most complex
    4|       |//! part of C's declaration syntax. Declarators can be nested and include pointers,
    5|       |//! arrays, and functions.
    6|       |
    7|       |use crate::diagnostic::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind};

   20|       |
   21|       |/// Look ahead past a GCC-style `__attribute__((...))` construct without consuming tokens.
   22|       |/// Returns the token immediately following the attribute if the structure is valid, or None.
   23|       |///
   24|       |/// Expects: Attribute (( ... ))
   25|      9|fn peek_past_attribute(parser: &Parser, mut start_offset: u32) -> Option<Token> {
   26|       |    // Check for multiple attributes
   27|       |    loop {
   28|       |        // start_offset points to Attribute
   29|       |        // Skip Attribute
   30|     10|        start_offset += 1;
   31|       |
   32|       |        // Expect ((
   33|       |        // If not ((, then it's not a GCC attribute (or it's ill-formed), stop
   34|     10|        if let Some(t) = parser.peek_token(start_offset) {
   35|     10|            if t.kind != TokenKind::LeftParen {
   36|      1|                return parser.peek_token(start_offset).cloned();
   37|      9|            }
   38|       |        } else {
   39|      0|            return None;
   40|       |        }
   41|      9|        start_offset += 1;
   42|       |
   43|      9|        if let Some(t) = parser.peek_token(start_offset) {
   44|      9|            if t.kind != TokenKind::LeftParen {
   45|      2|                return None;
   46|      7|            }
   47|       |        } else {
   48|      0|            return None;
   49|       |        }
   50|      7|        start_offset += 1;
   51|       |
   52|       |        // Skip balanced parens
   53|      7|        let mut depth = 2; // We saw two LeftParens
   54|       |
   55|     31|        while depth > 0 {
   56|     24|            let t = parser.peek_token(start_offset)?;
                                                                 ^0
   57|     24|            match t.kind {
   58|      1|                TokenKind::LeftParen => depth += 1,
   59|     15|                TokenKind::RightParen => depth -= 1,
   60|      8|                _ => {}
   61|       |            }
   62|     24|            start_offset += 1;
   63|       |        }
   64|       |
   65|       |        // Now we are past one attribute.
   66|       |        // Check if there is another one.
   67|      7|        if let Some(t) = parser.peek_token(start_offset) {
   68|      7|            if t.kind != TokenKind::Attribute {
   69|       |                // Not an attribute, so we are done
   70|      6|                return Some(*t);
   71|      1|            }
   72|       |            // It is an attribute, loop again (start_offset points to it)
   73|       |        } else {
   74|      0|            return None;
   75|       |        }
   76|       |    }
   77|      9|}

    5|       |
    6|       |use crate::ast::literal::Literal;
