   14|       |/// Binding power for Pratt parser operator precedence
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
   16|       |pub(crate) struct BindingPower(u8);
   17|       |
   18|       |impl BindingPower {
   19|       |    pub(crate) const MIN: Self = Self(0);
   20|       |    pub const COMMA: Self = Self(2);
   21|       |    pub const ASSIGNMENT: Self = Self(4);
   22|       |    pub const CONDITIONAL: Self = Self(6);
   23|       |    pub const LOGICAL_OR: Self = Self(8);
   24|       |    pub const LOGICAL_AND: Self = Self(10);
   25|       |    pub const BITWISE_OR: Self = Self(12);
   26|       |    pub const BITWISE_XOR: Self = Self(14);
   27|       |    pub const BITWISE_AND: Self = Self(16);
   28|       |    pub const EQUALITY: Self = Self(18);
   29|       |    pub const RELATIONAL: Self = Self(20);
   30|       |    pub const SHIFT: Self = Self(22);
   31|       |    pub const ADDITIVE: Self = Self(24);
   32|       |    pub const MULTIPLICATIVE: Self = Self(26);
   33|       |    pub const CAST: Self = Self(28);
   34|       |    pub const UNARY: Self = Self(30);
   35|       |    pub const POSTFIX: Self = Self(32);
   36|       |    // pub const PRIMARY: Self = Self(34);
   37|       |}
   38|       |
   39|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   40|       |pub(crate) enum Associativity {
   41|       |    Left,
   42|       |    Right,
   43|       |}
   44|       |
   45|       |/// Pratt parser implementation
   46|       |pub(crate) struct PrattParser;
   47|       |
   48|       |impl PrattParser {
   49|  4.19k|    fn get_binding_power(token_kind: TokenKind) -> Option<(BindingPower, Associativity)> {
   50|  4.19k|        match token_kind {
   51|       |            // Assignment operators (right-associative)
   52|       |            TokenKind::Assign
   53|       |            | TokenKind::PlusAssign
   54|       |            | TokenKind::MinusAssign
   55|       |            | TokenKind::StarAssign
   56|       |            | TokenKind::DivAssign
   57|       |            | TokenKind::ModAssign
   58|       |            | TokenKind::AndAssign
   59|       |            | TokenKind::OrAssign
   60|       |            | TokenKind::XorAssign
   61|       |            | TokenKind::LeftShiftAssign
   62|    169|            | TokenKind::RightShiftAssign => Some((BindingPower::ASSIGNMENT, Associativity::Right)),
   63|       |
   64|       |            // Comma operator (left-associative, lowest precedence)
   65|    525|            TokenKind::Comma => Some((BindingPower::COMMA, Associativity::Left)),
   66|       |
   67|       |            // Conditional operator (right-associative)
   68|     13|            TokenKind::Question => Some((BindingPower::CONDITIONAL, Associativity::Right)),
   69|       |
   70|       |            // Logical operators (left-associative)
   71|     23|            TokenKind::LogicOr => Some((BindingPower::LOGICAL_OR, Associativity::Left)),
   72|     24|            TokenKind::LogicAnd => Some((BindingPower::LOGICAL_AND, Associativity::Left)),
   73|       |
   74|       |            // Bitwise operators (left-associative)
   75|      1|            TokenKind::Or => Some((BindingPower::BITWISE_OR, Associativity::Left)),
   76|      1|            TokenKind::Xor => Some((BindingPower::BITWISE_XOR, Associativity::Left)),
   77|      2|            TokenKind::And => Some((BindingPower::BITWISE_AND, Associativity::Left)),
   78|       |
   79|       |            // Comparison operators (left-associative)
   80|    113|            TokenKind::Equal | TokenKind::NotEqual => Some((BindingPower::EQUALITY, Associativity::Left)),
   81|       |            TokenKind::Less | TokenKind::Greater | TokenKind::LessEqual | TokenKind::GreaterEqual => {
   82|     35|                Some((BindingPower::RELATIONAL, Associativity::Left))
   83|       |            }
   84|       |
   85|       |            // Shift operators (left-associative)
   86|      7|            TokenKind::LeftShift | TokenKind::RightShift => Some((BindingPower::SHIFT, Associativity::Left)),
   87|       |
   88|       |            // Additive operators (left-associative)
   89|     99|            TokenKind::Plus | TokenKind::Minus => Some((BindingPower::ADDITIVE, Associativity::Left)),
   90|       |
   91|       |            // Multiplicative operators (left-associative)
   92|       |            TokenKind::Star | TokenKind::Slash | TokenKind::Percent => {
   93|     32|                Some((BindingPower::MULTIPLICATIVE, Associativity::Left))
   94|       |            }
   95|       |
   96|       |            // Postfix operators
   97|       |            TokenKind::Increment
   98|       |            | TokenKind::Decrement
   99|       |            | TokenKind::LeftParen
  100|       |            | TokenKind::LeftBracket
  101|       |            | TokenKind::Dot
  102|    359|            | TokenKind::Arrow => Some((BindingPower::POSTFIX, Associativity::Left)),
  103|       |
  104|  2.78k|            _ => None,
  105|       |        }
  106|  4.19k|    }
  107|       |}

  110|  3.38k|pub(crate) fn parse_expression(parser: &mut Parser, min_bp: BindingPower) -> Result<ParsedNodeRef, ParseError> {

  329|       |/// Parse GNU statement expression: ({ compound-statement })
  330|      6|fn parse_gnu_statement_expression(parser: &mut Parser, start_loc: SourceLoc) -> Result<ParsedNodeRef, ParseError> {
  331|       |    // Parse the compound statement (parse_compound_statement expects LeftBrace)
  332|      6|    let (compound_stmt, _) = super::statements::parse_compound_statement(parser)?;
                                                                                              ^0
  333|       |
  334|       |    // Expect the closing parenthesis
  335|      6|    let right_paren_token = parser.expect(TokenKind::RightParen)?;
                                                                              ^0
  336|       |
  337|       |    // For GNU statement expressions, the result is the last expression in the compound statement
  338|       |    // We need to extract it from the compound statement
  339|      6|    let result_expr = extract_last_expression_from_compound_statement(parser, compound_stmt);
  340|       |
  341|      6|    let end_loc = right_paren_token.span.end();
  342|      6|    let span = SourceSpan::new(start_loc, end_loc);
  343|       |
  344|      6|    let node = parser.push_node(ParsedNodeKind::GnuStatementExpression(compound_stmt, result_expr), span);
  345|      6|    Ok(node)
  346|      6|}

  348|       |/// Extract the last expression from a compound statement for GNU statement expressions
  349|      6|fn extract_last_expression_from_compound_statement(
  350|      6|    parser: &mut Parser,
  351|      6|    compound_stmt_node_ref: ParsedNodeRef,
  352|      6|) -> ParsedNodeRef {
  353|       |    // Get the compound statement node
  354|      6|    let compound_stmt_node = parser.ast.get_node(compound_stmt_node_ref);
  355|       |
  356|      6|    if let ParsedNodeKind::CompoundStatement(statements) = &compound_stmt_node.kind {
  357|       |        // Find the last expression statement in the compound statement
  358|      7|        for &stmt_ref in statements.iter().rev() {
                                       ^6                ^6
  359|      7|            let stmt_node = parser.ast.get_node(stmt_ref);
  360|      4|            if let ParsedNodeKind::ExpressionStatement(Some(expr)) = &stmt_node.kind {
  361|      4|                return *expr;
  362|      3|            }
  363|       |        }
  364|       |
  365|       |        // If no expression statement found, create a dummy expression
  366|       |        // This shouldn't happen in valid GNU statement expressions
  367|      2|        let dummy_expr = parser.push_node(ParsedNodeKind::Dummy, compound_stmt_node.span);
  368|      2|        return dummy_expr;
  369|      0|    }
  370|       |
  371|       |    // Fallback: create a dummy expression
  372|      0|    parser.push_node(ParsedNodeKind::Dummy, compound_stmt_node.span)
  373|      6|}

  622|       |
  623|     14|fn parse_atomic_op(parser: &mut Parser, op: AtomicOp) -> Result<ParsedNodeRef, ParseError> {
  624|     14|    let start = parser.advance().unwrap().span.start();
  625|     14|    parser.expect(TokenKind::LeftParen)?;
                                                     ^0
  626|     14|    let args = super::utils::expr_patterns::parse_expr_list(parser, BindingPower::ASSIGNMENT)?;
                                                                                                           ^0
  627|     14|    let end = parser.expect(TokenKind::RightParen)?.span.end();
                                                                ^0
  628|     14|    Ok(parser.push_node(ParsedNodeKind::AtomicOp(op, args), SourceSpan::new(start, end)))
  629|     14|}

  323|       |/// Check if a symbol represents a C11 keyword.

  441|       |/// Lexer state machine
  442|       |pub struct Lexer<'src> {

  446|       |
  447|       |impl<'src> Lexer<'src> {
  448|       |    /// Create a new lexer with the given preprocessor token stream
  449|  1.10k|    pub(crate) fn new(tokens: &'src [PPToken]) -> Self {
  450|  1.10k|        Lexer { tokens }
  451|  1.10k|    }
  452|       |
  453|       |    /// Classify a preprocessor token into a lexical token
  454|  22.8k|    fn classify_token(&self, pptoken: &PPToken) -> TokenKind {
  455|  22.8k|        match pptoken.kind {
  456|  8.49k|            PPTokenKind::Identifier(symbol) => {
  457|       |                // Check if it's a keyword
  458|  8.49k|                is_keyword(symbol).unwrap_or(TokenKind::Identifier(symbol))
  459|       |            }
  460|    180|            PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(symbol) => TokenKind::StringLiteral(symbol),
  461|     16|            PPTokenKind::CharLiteral(codepoint, _) => TokenKind::CharacterConstant(codepoint),
  462|  1.63k|            PPTokenKind::Number(value) => {
  463|       |                // Try to parse as integer first, then float, then unknown
  464|  1.63k|                if let Some((int_val, suffix, base)) = literal_parsing::parse_c11_integer_literal(value.as_str()) {
                                           ^1.56k   ^1.56k  ^1.56k
  465|  1.56k|                    TokenKind::IntegerConstant(int_val as i64, suffix, base)
  466|     68|                } else if let Some((float_val, suffix)) = literal_parsing::parse_c11_float_literal(value.as_str()) {
                                                  ^67        ^67
  467|     67|                    TokenKind::FloatConstant(float_val, suffix)
  468|       |                } else {
  469|      1|                    TokenKind::Unknown // Could not parse as integer or float
  470|       |                }
  471|       |            }
  472|  1.10k|            PPTokenKind::Eof => TokenKind::EndOfFile,
  473|      0|            PPTokenKind::Eod => TokenKind::Unknown,
  474|       |            // Handle punctuation tokens using the optimized match-based function
  475|  11.3k|            pptoken_kind => classify_punctuation(pptoken_kind),
  476|       |        }
  477|  22.8k|    }
  478|       |
  479|       |    /// Get all tokens from the stream
  480|  1.10k|    pub(crate) fn tokenize_all(&mut self) -> Vec<Token> {
  481|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Pre-allocate the tokens vector with the capacity of the preprocessor tokens.
  482|       |        // This is a reasonable estimate that reduces the number of reallocations,
  483|       |        // as the number of lexical tokens is usually similar to the number of preprocessor tokens.
  484|  1.10k|        let mut tokens = Vec::with_capacity(self.tokens.len());
  485|  1.10k|        let mut current_token_iter = self.tokens.iter().peekable();
  486|       |
  487|  22.8k|        while let Some(pptoken) = current_token_iter.next() {
  488|  22.8k|            if let PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(symbol) = pptoken.kind {
                                                            ^186
  489|    186|                let start_span = SourceSpan::new_with_length(
  490|    186|                    pptoken.location.source_id(),
  491|    186|                    pptoken.location.offset(),
  492|    186|                    pptoken.length as u32,
  493|       |                );
  494|       |
  495|       |                // Fast path for single string literals
  496|    180|                if !matches!(
  497|    186|                    current_token_iter.peek(),
  498|       |                    Some(PPToken {
  499|       |                        kind: PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(_),
  500|       |                        ..
  501|       |                    })
  502|       |                ) {
  503|    180|                    tokens.push(Token {
  504|    180|                        kind: self.classify_token(pptoken),
  505|    180|                        span: start_span,
  506|    180|                    });
  507|    180|                    continue;
  508|      6|                }
  509|       |
  510|       |                // Slow path: Concatenate adjacent string literals
  511|      6|                let (mut prefix, first_content) = Self::extract_literal_parts(symbol.as_str()).unwrap_or(("", ""));
  512|      6|                let mut content = String::from(first_content);
  513|      6|                let mut final_span = start_span;
  514|       |
  515|     15|                while let Some(next) = current_token_iter.peek() {
  516|     15|                    if let PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(next_symbol) = next.kind {
                                                                    ^9
  517|      9|                        let (next_prefix, next_content) =
  518|      9|                            Self::extract_literal_parts(next_symbol.as_str()).unwrap_or(("", ""));
  519|      9|                        if prefix.is_empty() {
  520|      9|                            prefix = next_prefix;
  521|      9|                        }
                                      ^0
  522|      9|                        content.push_str(next_content);
  523|      9|                        final_span = SourceSpan::new_with_length(
  524|      9|                            next.location.source_id(),
  525|      9|                            next.location.offset(),
  526|      9|                            next.length as u32,
  527|      9|                        );
  528|      9|                        current_token_iter.next();
  529|       |                    } else {
  530|      6|                        break;
  531|       |                    }
  532|       |                }
  533|       |
  534|      6|                tokens.push(Token {
  535|      6|                    kind: TokenKind::StringLiteral(StringId::new(format!("{}\"{}\"", prefix, content))),
  536|      6|                    span: start_span.merge(final_span),
  537|      6|                });
  538|      6|                continue;
  539|  22.6k|            }
  540|       |
  541|       |            // For all other tokens, process normally
  542|  22.6k|            let token = Token {
  543|  22.6k|                kind: self.classify_token(pptoken),
  544|  22.6k|                span: SourceSpan::new_with_length(
  545|  22.6k|                    pptoken.location.source_id(),
  546|  22.6k|                    pptoken.location.offset(),
  547|  22.6k|                    pptoken.length as u32,
  548|  22.6k|                ),
  549|  22.6k|            };
  550|       |
  551|  22.6k|            let is_eof = matches!(token.kind, TokenKind::EndOfFile);
                                       ^21.5k
  552|  22.6k|            tokens.push(token);
  553|  22.6k|            if is_eof {
  554|  1.10k|                break;
  555|  21.5k|            }
  556|       |        }
  557|       |
  558|  1.10k|        tokens
  559|  1.10k|    }
  560|       |
  561|       |    /// Extract parts from a string literal symbol: (prefix, content_without_quotes)
  562|     15|    fn extract_literal_parts(s: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {
  563|     60|        for prefix in ["L", "u8", "u", "U"] {
                                          ^15   ^15  ^15
  564|     60|            if let Some(rest) = s.strip_prefix(prefix)
                                      ^0
  565|      0|                && let Some(inner) = rest.strip_prefix('"')?.strip_suffix('"')
  566|       |            {
  567|      0|                return Some((prefix, inner));
  568|     60|            }
  569|       |        }
  570|     15|        if let Some(inner) = s.strip_prefix('"')?.strip_suffix('"') {
                                                              ^0
  571|     15|            return Some(("", inner));
  572|      0|        }
  573|      0|        None
  574|     15|    }

  354|    160|fn build_parsed_declarator(parser: &mut Parser, declarator: &ParsedDeclarator) -> Result<ParsedDeclRef, ParseError> {

  367|       |            };

  370|     49|                qualifiers: *ptr_qualifiers,

  372|     49|            }))
  373|       |        }

                                                                                ^0

  378|     19|                size: size.clone(),

  381|       |        }

                                                                                ^0

/app/src/parser/struct_parsing.rs:
    1|       |//! Struct and union parsing module
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles parsing of struct and union declarations,
    4|       |//! including member declarations and anonymous structs/unions.
    5|       |
    6|       |use thin_vec::{ThinVec, thin_vec};
    7|       |
    8|       |use crate::ast::parsed::*;
    9|       |use crate::ast::*;
   10|       |use crate::diagnostic::ParseError;
   11|       |use crate::parser::TokenKind;

   13|       |use super::Parser;
   14|       |
   15|       |/// Parse struct or union specifier with context
   16|    365|pub(crate) fn parse_record_specifier_with_context(
   17|    365|    parser: &mut Parser,
   18|    365|    is_union: bool,
   19|    365|    in_struct_member: bool,
   20|    365|) -> Result<ParsedTypeSpecifier, ParseError> {
   21|       |    // Check for __attribute__ after struct/union keyword (GCC extension)
   22|    365|    if parser.is_token(TokenKind::Attribute)
   23|      2|        && let Err(_e) = super::declaration_core::parse_attribute(parser)
                                 ^1
   24|      1|    {
   25|      1|        // For now, ignore attribute parsing errors
   26|    364|    }
   27|       |

/app/src/pp/dumper.rs:
    1|       |//! Preprocessor Dumper module
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module handles dumping preprocessed tokens back to source text,
    4|       |//! with support for line markers and whitespace reconstruction.
    5|       |
    6|       |use crate::pp::{PPToken, PPTokenFlags, PPTokenKind};
    7|       |use crate::source_manager::SourceManager;
    8|       |use std::io::Write;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Dumper for preprocessed output
   11|       |pub struct PPDumper<'a> {
   12|       |    tokens: &'a [PPToken],
   13|       |    source_manager: &'a SourceManager,
   14|       |    suppress_line_markers: bool,
   15|       |}
   16|       |
   17|       |impl<'a> PPDumper<'a> {
   18|       |    /// Create a new preprocessor dumper
   19|      4|    pub(crate) fn new(tokens: &'a [PPToken], source_manager: &'a SourceManager, suppress_line_markers: bool) -> Self {
   20|      4|        Self {
   21|      4|            tokens,
   22|      4|            source_manager,
   23|      4|            suppress_line_markers,
   24|      4|        }
   25|      4|    }
   26|       |
   27|       |    /// Dump preprocessed output to the given writer
   28|      4|    pub(crate) fn dump(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> std::io::Result<()> {
   29|      4|        if self.tokens.is_empty() {
   30|      0|            return Ok(());
   31|      4|        }
   32|       |
   33|       |        // Get the source buffer for the first token
   34|      4|        let first_token = &self.tokens[0];
   35|       |
   36|       |        // Initial heuristic: try to find the first non-macro-expanded token
   37|       |        // to establish the "current file" context. This prevents line markers
   38|       |        // generally being emitted for the file itself if it starts with a macro.
   39|      4|        let mut current_file_id = first_token.location.source_id();
   40|      4|        for token in self.tokens {
   41|      4|            if !token.flags.contains(PPTokenFlags::MACRO_EXPANDED) {
   42|      4|                current_file_id = token.location.source_id();
   43|      4|                break;
   44|      0|            }
   45|       |        }
   46|      4|        let mut current_buffer = self.source_manager.get_buffer(current_file_id);
   47|      4|        let mut last_pos = 0u32;
   48|      4|        let mut last_was_macro_expanded = false;
   49|       |
   50|     28|        for token in self.tokens {
                          ^24
   51|     24|            if token.kind == PPTokenKind::Eof {
   52|      0|                break;
   53|     24|            }
   54|       |
   55|       |            // Handle macro-expanded tokens (Level A: use canonical spelling)
   56|     24|            if token.flags.contains(PPTokenFlags::MACRO_EXPANDED) {
   57|       |                // Heuristic: if we are entering a macro expansion (previous was not macro),
   58|       |                // and there was whitespace at the current position in the source, print a space.
   59|       |                // This preserves separation like "return FOO" -> "return 123".
   60|      2|                if !last_was_macro_expanded {
   61|       |                    // Check if char at last_pos is whitespace
   62|      2|                    if let Some(&byte) = current_buffer.get(last_pos as usize)
   63|      2|                        && (byte as char).is_whitespace()
   64|       |                    {
   65|      2|                        write!(writer, " ")?;
                                                         ^0
   66|      0|                    }
   67|       |                } else {
   68|       |                    // Add space between consecutive macro-expanded tokens (linearization)
   69|      0|                    write!(writer, " ")?;
   70|       |                }
   71|       |
   72|       |                // For macro-expanded tokens, just print the canonical spelling
   73|       |                // No whitespace reconstruction for Level A - these tokens don't have
   74|       |                // meaningful source locations for whitespace calculation
   75|      2|                write!(writer, "{}", token.get_text())?;
                                                                    ^0
   76|      2|                last_was_macro_expanded = true;
   77|       |                // Don't update last_pos for macro-expanded tokens
   78|      2|                continue;
   79|     22|            }
   80|       |
   81|       |            // Check for file transitions and emit line markers
   82|     22|            if token.location.source_id() != current_file_id {
   83|       |                // Emit line marker for file transition (unless suppressed)
   84|      2|                if !self.suppress_line_markers
   85|      2|                    && let Some(file_info) = self.source_manager.get_file_info(token.location.source_id())
   86|       |                {
   87|      2|                    let line = self
   88|      2|                        .source_manager
   89|      2|                        .get_line_column(token.location)
   90|      2|                        .map(|(l, _)| l)
   91|      2|                        .unwrap_or(1);
   92|      2|                    let filename = file_info
   93|      2|                        .path
   94|      2|                        .file_name()
   95|      2|                        .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
   96|      2|                        .unwrap_or("<unknown>");
   97|       |
   98|       |                    // Ensure we start on a new line
   99|      2|                    writeln!(writer)?;
                                                  ^0
  100|      2|                    writeln!(writer, "# {} \"{}\" 1", line, filename)?;
                                                                                   ^0
  101|      0|                }
  102|       |
  103|      2|                current_file_id = token.location.source_id();
  104|      2|                current_buffer = self.source_manager.get_buffer(current_file_id);
  105|      2|                last_pos = token.location.offset();
  106|     20|            }
  107|       |
  108|     22|            let token_start = token.location.offset();
  109|     22|            let token_end = token_start + token.length as u32;
  110|       |
  111|       |            // Print all bytes between last_pos and token_start (whitespace, comments)
  112|     22|            if token_start > last_pos {
  113|     15|                let slice = &current_buffer[last_pos as usize..token_start as usize];
  114|       |                // Convert to string, assuming UTF-8 (preprocessor should ensure this)
  115|     15|                if let Ok(text) = std::str::from_utf8(slice) {
  116|       |                    // Only print slices that are all whitespace to avoid printing directive text
  117|     23|                    if text.chars().all(|c| c.is_whitespace()) {
                                     ^15          ^15
  118|     11|                        write!(writer, "{}", text)?;
                                                                ^0
  119|      4|                    }
  120|      0|                }
  121|      7|            }
  122|       |
  123|       |            // Print the token's raw bytes from source
  124|     22|            let token_slice = token.get_raw_slice(current_buffer);
  125|     22|            if let Ok(text) = std::str::from_utf8(token_slice) {
  126|     22|                write!(writer, "{}", text)?;
                                                        ^0
  127|      0|            }
  128|       |
  129|     22|            last_pos = token_end;
  130|     22|            last_was_macro_expanded = false;
  131|       |        }
  132|       |
  133|      4|        writeln!(writer)?;
                                      ^0
  134|      4|        Ok(())
  135|      4|    }
  136|       |}

   31|      1|    }
   32|       |
   33|       |    /// Resolve an include path to an absolute path
   34|     38|    pub(crate) fn resolve_path(&self, include_path: &str, is_angled: bool, current_dir: &Path) -> Option<PathBuf> {
   35|     38|        if is_angled {
   36|       |            // Angled includes: search angled_includes, then system_path, then framework_path
   37|     16|            self.check_paths(&self.angled_includes, include_path)
   38|     16|                .or_else(|| self.check_paths(&self.system_path, include_path))
                                          ^13  ^13         ^13                ^13
   39|     16|                .or_else(|| self.check_paths(&self.framework_path, include_path))
                                          ^8   ^8          ^8                    ^8
   40|       |        } else {
   41|       |            // Quoted includes: search current_dir, then quoted_includes, then angled_includes, then system_path, then framework_path
   42|     22|            let candidate = current_dir.join(include_path);
   43|     22|            if candidate.exists() {
   44|      3|                return Some(candidate);
   45|     19|            }
   46|     19|            self.check_paths(&self.quoted_includes, include_path)
   47|     19|                .or_else(|| self.check_paths(&self.angled_includes, include_path))
   48|     19|                .or_else(|| self.check_paths(&self.system_path, include_path))

  289|    407|        self.pos >= self.tokens.len()
  290|    407|    }

  297|     55|        if self.peek() == Some(&kind) {
  298|     55|            self.advance();
  299|     55|            Ok(())
  300|       |        } else {
  301|      0|            Err(self.error())
  302|       |        }

  305|       |    /// Returns the current token or an error if at the end of the stream.
  306|    395|    fn current(&self) -> Result<&'a PPToken, PPError> {
  307|    395|        if self.at_end() {
  308|      0|            Err(self.error())
  309|       |        } else {
  310|    395|            Ok(&self.tokens[self.pos])
  311|       |        }
  312|    395|    }

  326|    118|    fn parse_conditional(&mut self) -> Result<PPExpr, PPError> {
  327|    118|        let cond = self.parse_or()?;
                                                ^0
  328|    118|        if self.peek() == Some(&PPTokenKind::Question) {
  329|      5|            self.advance();
  330|      5|            let true_e = self.parse_conditional()?;
                                                               ^0
  331|      5|            self.expect(PPTokenKind::Colon)?;
                                                         ^0
  332|      5|            let false_e = self.parse_conditional()?;
                                                                ^0
  333|      5|            Ok(PPExpr::Conditional(Box::new(cond), Box::new(true_e), Box::new(false_e)))
  334|       |        } else {
  335|    113|            Ok(cond)
  336|       |        }
  337|    118|    }
  338|       |
  339|    118|    fn parse_or(&mut self) -> Result<PPExpr, PPError> {
  340|    118|        self.parse_left_assoc(Self::parse_and, &[(PPTokenKind::LogicOr, BinaryOp::LogicOr)])
  341|    118|    }

  395|       |        )

  398|    171|    fn parse_multiplicative(&mut self) -> Result<PPExpr, PPError> {

  204|       |    /// Get the next character and its position, handling line splicing transparently
  205|       |    /// Line splicing: backslash followed by newline removes both characters
  206|  35.5k|    fn next_char(&mut self) -> Option<(u8, u32)> {
  207|       |        loop {
  208|  35.5k|            let pos = self.position as usize;

  283|       |    /// Peek at the next character without consuming it, handling line splicing
  284|  54.7k|    fn peek_char(&mut self) -> Option<u8> {
  285|  54.7k|        let pos = self.position as usize;
  286|       |
  287|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Fast path for peeking common characters.
  288|       |        // If the next character is not a line splice,
  289|       |        // we can return it directly without the overhead of saving/restoring state.
  290|  54.7k|        if pos < self.buffer.len() {
  291|  53.3k|            let ch = self.buffer[pos];
  292|  53.3k|            if ch != b'\\' {
  293|  53.3k|                return Some(ch);
  294|     14|            }
  295|  1.43k|        }
  296|       |
  297|  1.44k|        let saved_position = self.position;
  298|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Avoid cloning `line_starts` by saving its length.
  299|       |        // `next_char` only ever appends to this vector, so we can restore its
  300|       |        // state by truncating it to its original length. This is a significant
  301|       |        // performance win as `peek_char` is called frequently.
  302|  1.44k|        let saved_line_starts_len = self.line_starts.len();
  303|       |
  304|  1.44k|        let result = self.next_char();
  305|       |
  306|       |        // Restore state
  307|  1.44k|        self.position = saved_position;
  308|  1.44k|        self.line_starts.truncate(saved_line_starts_len);
  309|       |
  310|  1.44k|        result.map(|(ch, _)| ch)
  311|  54.7k|    }
  312|       |
  313|       |    /// ⚡ Bolt: Consolidated operator lexing.
  314|       |    /// This helper function centralizes the logic for lexing single and multi-character operators,
  315|       |    /// significantly reducing code duplication and branching in the main `next_token` function.
  316|       |    /// This improves performance by making the tokenization logic more direct and predictable
  317|       |    /// for the compiler to optimize. It also fixes a bug in the original `...` lexing logic.
  318|  12.4k|    fn lex_operator(&mut self, start_pos: u32, ch: u8, flags: PPTokenFlags) -> PPToken {
  319|  12.4k|        let loc = SourceLoc::new(self.source_id, start_pos);
  320|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Check if we're at the beginning of a line (ignoring whitespace).
  321|       |        // This is crucial for identifying the '#' that starts a preprocessor directive.
  322|  12.4k|        let is_at_start_of_line = self.at_start_of_line;
  323|       |
  324|       |        // Helper macro to reduce boilerplate when creating a token.
  325|       |        macro_rules! token {
  326|       |            ($kind:expr, $len:expr) => {
  327|       |                PPToken::new($kind, flags, loc, $len)
  328|       |            };
  329|       |            ($kind:expr, $len:expr, $custom_flags:expr) => {
  330|       |                PPToken::new($kind, $custom_flags, loc, $len)
  331|       |            };
  332|       |        }
  333|       |
  334|       |        // Helper macro to check the next character and consume it if it matches.
  335|       |        macro_rules! consume_if {
  336|       |            ($c:expr) => {{
  337|       |                if self.peek_char() == Some($c) {
  338|       |                    self.next_char();
  339|       |                    true
  340|       |                } else {
  341|       |                    false
  342|       |                }
  343|       |            }};
  344|       |        }
  345|       |
  346|  12.4k|        match ch {
  347|       |            b'#' => {
  348|    471|                if consume_if!(b'#') {
  349|     13|                    token!(PPTokenKind::HashHash, 2)
  350|       |                } else {
  351|    458|                    let mut token_flags = flags;
  352|    458|                    if is_at_start_of_line {
  353|    455|                        token_flags |= PPTokenFlags::STARTS_PP_LINE;
  354|    455|                        self.in_directive_line = true;
  355|    455|                    }
                                  ^3
  356|    458|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Hash, 1, token_flags)
  357|       |                }
  358|       |            }
  359|       |            b'+' => {
  360|    130|                if consume_if!(b'+') {
  361|     28|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Increment, 2)
  362|    102|                } else if consume_if!(b'=') {
  363|     12|                    token!(PPTokenKind::PlusAssign, 2)
  364|       |                } else {

  366|       |                }
  367|       |            }
  368|       |            b'-' => {
  369|    122|                if consume_if!(b'-') {
  370|     11|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Decrement, 2)
  371|    111|                } else if consume_if!(b'=') {
  372|      6|                    token!(PPTokenKind::MinusAssign, 2)
  373|    105|                } else if consume_if!(b'>') {
  374|     23|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Arrow, 2)
  375|       |                } else {
  376|     82|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Minus, 1)
  377|       |                }

  379|       |            b'*' => {
  380|    474|                if consume_if!(b'=') {
  381|      5|                    token!(PPTokenKind::StarAssign, 2)

  383|    469|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Star, 1)

  404|    682|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Assign, 1)
  405|       |                }

  407|       |            b'!' => {

  409|     72|                    token!(PPTokenKind::NotEqual, 2)

  411|     30|                    token!(PPTokenKind::Not, 1)

  474|      0|                    }

  476|      0|                    self.position = pos_after_first;

  484|  2.50k|            b';' => token!(PPTokenKind::Semicolon, 1),

  495|  25.9k|    pub(crate) fn next_token(&mut self) -> Option<PPToken> {

  636|  25.9k|    fn skip_whitespace_and_comments(&mut self) {
  637|       |        loop {
  638|       |            // Skip whitespace, handling line splicing
  639|       |            // But don't skip newlines if we're in a directive line (let them be processed as tokens)
  640|       |
  641|       |            // ⚡ Bolt: Fast path for skipping common whitespace characters.
  642|       |            // This avoids the overhead of `peek_char` and `next_char` for the majority of whitespace.
  643|  68.1k|            while (self.position as usize) < self.buffer.len() {
  644|  66.8k|                let ch = self.buffer[self.position as usize];
  645|  66.8k|                if ch == b' ' || ch == b'\t' || ch == b'\r' || ch == b'\x0c' || ch == b'\x0b' {
                                               ^28.4k         ^28.4k         ^28.4k           ^28.4k
  646|  38.3k|                    self.position += 1;
  647|  38.3k|                } else if ch == b'\n' && !self.in_directive_line {
                                        ^28.4k         ^4.10k
  648|  3.68k|                    self.position += 1;
  649|  3.68k|                    self.line_starts.push(self.position);
  650|  3.68k|                    self.at_start_of_line = true;
  651|  3.68k|                } else {
  652|       |                    // Trigraphs, line splices, or non-whitespace.
  653|  24.7k|                    break;
  654|       |                }
  655|       |            }
  656|       |
  657|  26.0k|            while let Some(ch) = self.peek_char() {
                                         ^24.7k
  658|  24.7k|                if ch.is_ascii_whitespace() && !(ch == b'\n' && self.in_directive_line) {
                                                               ^420           ^413
  659|      7|                    if ch == b'\n' {
  660|      0|                        self.at_start_of_line = true;
  661|      7|                    }
  662|      7|                    self.next_char();
  663|       |                } else {
  664|  24.7k|                    break;
  665|       |                }
  666|       |            }
  667|       |
  668|  26.0k|            if self.position as usize >= self.buffer.len() {
  669|  1.27k|                break;
  670|  24.7k|            }
  671|       |
  672|       |            // ⚡ Bolt: Fast-path for skipping comment check.
  673|       |            // If the next character is not '/', it cannot be the start of a comment.
  674|       |            // We peek first to avoid the overhead of saving state and multi-character
  675|       |            // consumption for the vast majority of tokens.
  676|  24.7k|            if self.peek_char() != Some(b'/') {
  677|  24.6k|                break;

  681|    164|            let saved_position = self.position;

  683|       |
  684|    164|            let ch1 = self.next_char().map(|(c, _)| c);
  685|    164|            let ch2 = self.next_char().map(|(c, _)| c);
  686|       |
  687|    164|            if ch1 == Some(b'/') && ch2 == Some(b'/') {
  688|       |                // Line comment, skip to end of line
  689|  3.79k|                while let Some((ch, _)) = self.next_char() {
  690|  3.79k|                    if ch == b'\n' {
  691|    103|                        break;
  692|  3.68k|                    }
  693|       |                }
  694|       |                // Continue loop
  695|     61|            } else if ch1 == Some(b'/') && ch2 == Some(b'*') {
  696|       |                // Block comment, skip to */
  697|    873|                while let Some((ch, _)) = self.next_char() {
  698|    873|                    if ch == b'*' && self.peek_char() == Some(b'/') {
                                                   ^36
  699|     36|                        self.next_char(); // consume '/'
  700|     36|                        break;
  701|    837|                    }
  702|       |                }
  703|       |                // Continue loop
  704|       |            } else {
  705|       |                // Not a comment, restore position
  706|     25|                self.position = saved_position;
  707|     25|                self.line_starts.truncate(saved_line_starts_len);
  708|     25|                break;
  709|       |            }
  710|       |        }
  711|  25.9k|    }
  712|       |
  713|       |    /// Consumes characters based on a predicate, building a string.
  714|       |    ///
  715|       |    /// The predicate receives the current state and the character.
  716|       |    /// It should return `true` to consume the character, or `false` to stop.
  717|       |    /// The closure can modify state (e.g., tracking scientific notation 'e').
  718|  1.83k|    fn consume_while<F, S>(&mut self, mut state: S, first_ch: u8, mut pred: F) -> String
  719|  1.83k|    where
  720|  1.83k|        F: FnMut(&mut S, u8) -> bool,
  721|       |    {
  722|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Pre-allocate the vector with a reasonable capacity to avoid
  723|       |        // multiple reallocations during identifier/number lexing.
  724|  1.83k|        let mut chars = Vec::with_capacity(32);
  725|  1.83k|        chars.push(first_ch);
  726|       |        loop {
  727|       |            // ⚡ Bolt: Fast path for direct buffer access when no line splicing is present.
  728|  3.05k|            let pos = self.position as usize;
  729|  3.05k|            if pos < self.buffer.len() {
  730|  3.01k|                let ch = self.buffer[pos];
  731|  3.01k|                if ch != b'?' && ch != b'\\' {
  732|  3.00k|                    if pred(&mut state, ch) {
  733|  1.22k|                        self.position += 1;
  734|  1.22k|                        chars.push(ch);
  735|  1.22k|                        continue;
  736|       |                    } else {
  737|  1.78k|                        break;
  738|       |                    }
  739|      1|                }
  740|     47|            }
  741|       |
  742|     48|            if let Some(ch) = self.peek_char()
                                      ^1
  743|      1|                && pred(&mut state, ch)
  744|       |            {
  745|      1|                chars.push(self.next_char().unwrap().0);
  746|      1|                continue;
  747|     47|            }
  748|     47|            break;
  749|       |        }
  750|       |        // Safety: We assume the caller only consumes valid UTF-8 characters (identifiers/numbers).
  751|  1.83k|        String::from_utf8(chars).unwrap()
  752|  1.83k|    }

  754|       |    /// Generic helper for lexing textual tokens (identifiers, numbers)
  755|  1.83k|    fn lex_textual_token<S, F, K>(
  756|  1.83k|        &mut self,
  757|  1.83k|        start_pos: u32,
  758|  1.83k|        first_ch: u8,
  759|  1.83k|        flags: PPTokenFlags,
  760|  1.83k|        state: S,
  761|  1.83k|        pred: F,
  762|  1.83k|        kind_ctor: K,
  763|  1.83k|    ) -> PPToken
  764|  1.83k|    where
  765|  1.83k|        F: FnMut(&mut S, u8) -> bool,
  766|  1.83k|        K: FnOnce(StringId) -> PPTokenKind,
  767|       |    {
  768|  1.83k|        let text = self.consume_while(state, first_ch, pred);
  769|  1.83k|        let symbol = StringId::new(&text);

  778|       |    /// Try to parse a UCN.
  779|       |    /// Assumes the leading backslash has already been consumed.
  780|       |    /// Returns:
  781|       |    /// - None: Not a UCN (didn't consume anything).
  782|       |    /// - Some(Ok((char, raw_string))): Valid UCN.
  783|       |    /// - Some(Err(raw_string)): Invalid UCN (consumed raw_string).
  784|     53|    fn lex_ucn(&mut self, allow_basic_charset: bool) -> Option<Result<(char, String), String>> {
  785|     53|        let is_u = match self.peek_char() {
                          ^8
  786|      8|            Some(b'u') => true,
  787|      0|            Some(b'U') => false,
  788|     45|            _ => return None,
  789|       |        };
  790|       |
  791|      8|        self.next_char(); // Consume u/U
  792|       |
  793|      8|        let digits_needed = if is_u { 4 } else { 8 };
                                                               ^0
  794|      8|        let mut hex_str = String::new();
  795|       |
  796|      8|        for _ in 0..digits_needed {
  797|     32|            if let Some(ch) = self.peek_char() {
  798|     32|                if ch.is_ascii_hexdigit() {
  799|     32|                    self.next_char();
  800|     32|                    hex_str.push(ch as char);
  801|     32|                } else {
  802|      0|                    break;
  803|       |                }
  804|       |            } else {
  805|      0|                break;
  806|       |            }
  807|       |        }
  808|       |
  809|      8|        let prefix = if is_u { "\\u" } else { "\\U" };
                                                            ^0
  810|      8|        let raw_string = format!("{}{}", prefix, hex_str);
  811|       |
  812|      8|        if hex_str.len() != digits_needed {
  813|      0|            return Some(Err(raw_string));
  814|      8|        }
  815|       |
  816|      8|        let code_point = u32::from_str_radix(&hex_str, 16).unwrap();
  817|       |
  818|       |        // Validation
  819|       |        // 1. Range
  820|      8|        if code_point > 0x10FFFF {
  821|      0|            return Some(Err(raw_string));
  822|      8|        }
  823|       |
  824|       |        // 2. Surrogates
  825|      8|        if (0xD800..=0xDFFF).contains(&code_point) {
  826|      0|            return Some(Err(raw_string));
  827|      8|        }
  828|       |
  829|       |        // 3. Basic Source Character Set
  830|       |        // 0000-009F excluding $, @, `
  831|      8|        if !allow_basic_charset && code_point < 0xA0 && code_point != 0x24 && code_point != 0x40 && code_point != 0x60 {
                                                 ^4                   ^0                    ^0                    ^0
  832|      0|            return Some(Err(raw_string));
  833|      8|        }
  834|       |
  835|      8|        if let Some(c) = char::from_u32(code_point) {
  836|      8|            Some(Ok((c, raw_string)))
  837|       |        } else {
  838|      0|            Some(Err(raw_string))
  839|       |        }
  840|     53|    }
  841|       |
  842|  9.65k|    fn lex_identifier(&mut self, start_pos: u32, first_ch: u8, flags: PPTokenFlags) -> PPToken {
  843|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Pre-allocate the string with a reasonable capacity to avoid
  844|       |        // multiple reallocations during identifier lexing.
  845|  9.65k|        let mut text = String::with_capacity(32);
  846|  9.65k|        let mut length = 0u16;
  847|       |
  848|       |        // Handle first character
  849|  9.65k|        if first_ch == b'\\' {
  850|       |            // Must be a valid UCN to start an identifier
  851|      2|            if let Some(Ok((c, raw))) = self.lex_ucn(false) {
  852|      2|                text.push(c);
  853|      2|                length += raw.len() as u16;
  854|      2|            } else {
  855|       |                // Not a valid UCN start. Return Unknown for the \
  856|      0|                return PPToken::new(
  857|      0|                    PPTokenKind::Unknown,
  858|      0|                    flags,
  859|      0|                    SourceLoc::new(self.source_id, start_pos),
  860|       |                    1,
  861|       |                );
  862|       |            }
  863|  9.65k|        } else if first_ch >= 0x80 {
  864|       |            // UTF-8 start

  866|      2|                let mut bytes = vec![first_ch];

  868|      4|                    if (0x80..0xC0).contains(&continuation_ch) {
  869|      2|                        bytes.push(self.next_char().unwrap().0);
  870|      2|                    } else {
  871|      2|                        break;
  872|       |                    }
  873|       |                }

  875|      2|                    text.push_str(&s);

  881|      0|                        SourceLoc::new(self.source_id, start_pos),
  882|       |                        1,
  883|       |                    );
  884|       |                }
  885|       |            } else {
  886|      0|                return PPToken::new(
  887|      0|                    PPTokenKind::Unknown,
  888|      0|                    flags,
  889|      0|                    SourceLoc::new(self.source_id, start_pos),
  890|       |                    1,
  891|       |                );
  892|       |            }
  893|  9.65k|        } else {
  894|  9.65k|            text.push(first_ch as char);
  895|  9.65k|            length += 1;
  896|  9.65k|        }
  897|       |

                                                                   ^10.8k

  911|  9.56k|                    } else if ch < 0x80 {
  912|       |                        // Other ASCII, stop identifier
  913|  9.56k|                        break;
  914|      0|                    }
  915|      5|                }
  916|     90|            }
  917|       |
  918|       |            // Fallback for tricky cases (Splices, UCNs, UTF-8)
  919|     95|            let ch = match self.peek_char() {
                              ^5
  920|      5|                Some(c) => c,
  921|     90|                None => break,
  922|       |            };

  924|       |            // Check for UCN
  925|      5|            if ch == b'\\' {

  927|      0|                let saved_lines_len = self.line_starts.len();
  928|      0|                self.next_char(); // consume \
  929|       |

  932|      0|                        text.push(c);
  933|      0|                        length += raw.len() as u16;
  934|      0|                    }
  935|       |                    _ => {
  936|       |                        // Not a valid UCN or not a UCN.
  937|       |                        // Backtrack and stop identifier
  938|      0|                        self.position = saved_pos;
  939|      0|                        self.line_starts.truncate(saved_lines_len);
  940|      0|                        break;
  941|       |                    }

  943|      0|                continue;

  956|      0|                    } else {
  957|      0|                        break;
  958|       |                    }
  959|       |                }
  960|       |
  961|      0|                if let Ok(s) = String::from_utf8(bytes) {
  962|      0|                    text.push_str(&s);
  963|      0|                    length += s.len() as u16;

 1017|    293|    fn lex_common_literal_body(&mut self, delimiter: u8, chars: &mut Vec<u8>, has_invalid_ucn: &mut bool) {
 1018|  3.77k|        while let Some((ch, _)) = self.next_char() {
 1019|  3.77k|            chars.push(ch);
 1020|  3.77k|            if ch == delimiter {
 1021|    293|                break; // End of literal
 1022|  3.48k|            } else if ch == b'\\' {
 1023|       |                // Handle escape sequences, including line splicing
 1024|       |

 1026|     49|                match self.lex_ucn(true) {
 1027|      4|                    Some(Ok((_, raw))) => {
 1028|       |                        // Valid UCN. Append raw bytes.
 1029|       |                        // \ is already pushed. raw contains \u...
 1030|       |                        // So append raw[1..]
 1031|      4|                        chars.extend_from_slice(&raw.as_bytes()[1..]);
 1032|      4|                        continue;
 1033|       |                    }

 1035|       |                        // Invalid UCN.
 1036|      0|                        *has_invalid_ucn = true;
 1037|      0|                        chars.extend_from_slice(&raw.as_bytes()[1..]);
 1038|      0|                        continue;
 1039|       |                    }

 1041|     45|                        // Not a UCN (e.g. \n or \t or whatever)
 1042|     45|                        // Fall through to existing logic

 1044|       |                }

 1047|     45|                    chars.push(next_ch);

 1052|       |                    // Consume continuation bytes for valid UTF-8 sequences

 1098|       |    /// Check if a byte is a valid start of a UTF-8 sequence
 1099|       |    /// Returns true if this could be the start of a valid UTF-8 sequence
 1100|     32|    fn is_valid_utf8_start(&self, byte: u8) -> bool {
 1101|       |        // Valid UTF-8 start bytes:
 1102|       |        // 0x00-0x7F: ASCII (single byte, handled elsewhere)
 1103|       |        // 0xC2-0xF4: Start of multi-byte sequence
 1104|       |        // Invalid starts: 0x80-0xBF (continuation bytes), 0xC0, 0xC1, 0xF5-0xFF
 1105|     32|        (0xC2..=0xF4).contains(&byte)
 1106|     32|    }

   66|       |

  278|       |    #[error("File not found: {path}")]
  279|       |    FileNotFound { path: String },

  281|       |    IncludeDepthExceeded,

  287|       |    UnexpectedEndOfFile,

  297|       |    InvalidConditionalExpression,

  299|       |    InvalidLineDirective,
  300|       |    #[error("Multiple #else directives")]
  301|       |    MultipleElse,

  309|       |    InvalidTokenPasting,

  311|       |    ExpectedEod,

  463|       |    /// Try to expand a magic macro (e.g. __LINE__, __FILE__, __COUNTER__)
  464|  9.65k|    fn try_expand_magic_macro(&mut self, token: &PPToken) -> Option<PPToken> {
  465|  9.65k|        let PPTokenKind::Identifier(symbol) = token.kind else {
                                                  ^8.97k
  466|    672|            return None;
  467|       |        };
  468|       |
  469|  8.97k|        let (kind, text) = if symbol == self.directive_keywords.line_macro {
                           ^9    ^9
  470|      2|            let line = self.sm.get_presumed_location(token.location).map(|p| p.0).unwrap_or(1);
  471|      2|            let text = line.to_string();
  472|      2|            (PPTokenKind::Number(StringId::new(&text)), text)
  473|  8.97k|        } else if symbol == self.directive_keywords.file_macro {
  474|      2|            let filename = self

  476|      2|                .get_presumed_location(token.location)
  477|      2|                .and_then(|p| p.2)
  478|      2|                .unwrap_or("<unknown>");
  479|      2|            let text = format!("\"{}\"", filename);
  480|      2|            (PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(StringId::new(&text)), text)
  481|  8.97k|        } else if symbol == self.directive_keywords.counter_macro {
  482|      5|            let text = self.get_next_counter().to_string();
  483|      5|            (PPTokenKind::Number(StringId::new(&text)), text)
  484|       |        } else {
  485|  8.96k|            return None;
  486|       |        };
  487|       |
  488|      9|        Some(PPToken::text(kind, PPTokenFlags::empty(), token.location, &text))
  489|  9.65k|    }
  490|       |
  491|       |    /// Get the next value for __COUNTER__
  492|      5|    fn get_next_counter(&mut self) -> u32 {
  493|      5|        let val = self.counter;
  494|      5|        self.counter += 1;
  495|      5|        val
  496|      5|    }
  497|       |
  498|       |    /// Initialize built-in macros
  499|  1.22k|    fn initialize_builtin_macros(&mut self, current_time: Option<DateTime<Utc>>) {

  770|       |    /// Expect and consume an Eod token or end of file

  815|       |    /// Collect tokens balanced between open and close delimiters.
  816|       |    /// Assumes the opening delimiter has NOT been consumed yet and will consume it.
  817|      6|    fn collect_balanced_tokens(&mut self, open: PPTokenKind, close: PPTokenKind) -> Result<Vec<PPToken>, PPError> {
  818|      6|        self.expect_kind(open)?;
                                            ^0
  819|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Use a small initial capacity to avoid reallocations for common short expressions.
  820|      6|        let mut tokens = Vec::with_capacity(8);
  821|      6|        let mut depth = 1;
  822|     12|        while let Some(t) = self.lex_token() {
  823|     12|            if t.kind == PPTokenKind::Eod {
  824|      0|                return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::UnexpectedEndOfFile, t.location);
  825|     12|            }
  826|     12|            if t.kind == open {
  827|      0|                depth += 1;
  828|     12|            } else if t.kind == close {
  829|      6|                depth -= 1;
  830|      6|                if depth == 0 {
  831|      6|                    return Ok(tokens);
  832|      0|                }
  833|      6|            }
  834|      6|            tokens.push(t);
  835|       |        }
  836|      0|        self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::UnexpectedEndOfFile, self.get_current_location())
  837|      6|    }

  839|       |    /// Helper to extract content of a string literal, stripping quotes.
  840|     25|    fn extract_string_literal_content(
  841|     25|        &self,
  842|     25|        symbol: StringId,
  843|     25|        location: SourceLoc,
  844|     25|        error_kind: PPErrorKind,

                                         ^0   ^0        ^0          ^0
  851|     25|    }

  853|       |    /// Process source file and return preprocessed tokens
  854|  1.21k|    pub(crate) fn process(&mut self, source_id: SourceId, _config: &PPConfig) -> Result<Vec<PPToken>, PPError> {
  855|       |        // Initialize lexer for main file
  856|  1.21k|        let buffer_len = self.sm.get_buffer(source_id).len() as u32;
  857|  1.21k|        let buffer = self.sm.get_buffer_arc(source_id);
  858|       |
  859|  1.21k|        self.lexer_stack.push(PPLexer::new(source_id, buffer));
  860|  1.21k|        self.sm.calculate_line_starts(source_id);
  861|       |
  862|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Pre-allocate the result_tokens vector using a capacity hint based on buffer size.
  863|       |        // A common estimate for C code is around 8 bytes per token.
  864|       |        // This avoids multiple costly reallocations for large source files.
  865|  1.21k|        let mut result_tokens = Vec::with_capacity(buffer_len as usize / 8);
  866|       |
  867|  23.7k|        while let Some(token) = self.lex_token() {
                                     ^22.5k
  868|  22.0k|            match token.kind {
  869|       |                // Handle directive
  870|    440|                PPTokenKind::Hash if !token.flags.contains(PPTokenFlags::MACRO_EXPANDED) => {
  871|    440|                    self.handle_directive()?;
                                                         ^20
  872|       |                }
  873|       |                // Skip tokens when in conditional compilation skip mode
  874|  22.1k|                _ if self.is_currently_skipping() => continue,
                                                              ^83  ^83
  875|       |                // Skip Eod tokens
  876|      1|                PPTokenKind::Eod => continue,
  877|       |                // Handle identifiers (macros, magic macros, _Pragma)
  878|  8.71k|                PPTokenKind::Identifier(symbol) => {
  879|  8.71k|                    if let Some(magic) = self.try_expand_magic_macro(&token) {
                                              ^8
  880|      8|                        result_tokens.push(magic);
  881|  8.70k|                    } else if symbol == self.directive_keywords.pragma_operator {
  882|      2|                        self.handle_pragma_operator()?;
                                                                   ^0
  883|  8.70k|                    } else if let Some(expanded) = self.expand_macro(&token)? {
                                                     ^73                                  ^0
  884|    395|                        for t in expanded.into_iter().rev() {
                                               ^73      ^73         ^73
  885|    395|                            self.pending_tokens.push_front(t);
  886|    395|                        }
  887|  8.63k|                    } else {
  888|  8.63k|                        result_tokens.push(token);
  889|  8.63k|                    }
  890|       |                }
  891|       |                // All other tokens
  892|  13.3k|                _ => result_tokens.push(token),
  893|       |            }
  894|       |        }
  895|       |
  896|       |        // Add EOF token
  897|  1.19k|        result_tokens.push(PPToken::new(
  898|  1.19k|            PPTokenKind::Eof,
  899|  1.19k|            PPTokenFlags::empty(),
  900|  1.19k|            SourceLoc::new(source_id, buffer_len),
  901|       |            0,
  902|       |        ));
  903|       |
  904|  1.19k|        if !self.conditional_stack.is_empty() {
  905|      0|            let loc = self.get_current_location();
  906|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::UnclosedConditional, loc);
  907|  1.19k|        }
  908|       |
  909|  1.19k|        Ok(result_tokens)
  910|  1.21k|    }
  911|       |
  912|       |    /// Get the current location from the lexer stack
  913|     12|    fn get_current_location(&self) -> SourceLoc {
  914|     12|        if let Some(lexer) = self.lexer_stack.last() {
  915|     12|            SourceLoc::new(lexer.source_id, lexer.position)
  916|       |        } else {
  917|      0|            SourceLoc::builtin()
  918|       |        }
  919|     12|    }
  920|       |
  921|      0|    fn get_current_span(&self) -> SourceSpan {
  922|      0|        let loc = self.get_current_location();
  923|      0|        SourceSpan::new(loc, loc)
  924|      0|    }
  925|       |
  926|      0|    fn error(&self, kind: PPErrorKind, span: SourceSpan) -> PPError {
  927|      0|        PPError { kind, span }

  930|      2|    fn error_loc(&self, kind: PPErrorKind, loc: SourceLoc) -> PPError {

  936|       |
  937|      0|    fn emit_error<T>(&self, kind: PPErrorKind, span: SourceSpan) -> Result<T, PPError> {
  938|      0|        Err(PPError { kind, span })
  939|      0|    }
  940|       |
  941|     18|    fn emit_error_loc<T>(&self, kind: PPErrorKind, loc: SourceLoc) -> Result<T, PPError> {

  943|     18|            kind,
  944|     18|            span: SourceSpan::new(loc, loc),

  948|       |    /// Check if we are currently skipping tokens
  949|  22.4k|    fn is_currently_skipping(&self) -> bool {
  950|       |        // Check if any conditional in the stack is currently skipping
  951|  22.4k|        self.conditional_stack.iter().any(|info| info.was_skipping)
  952|  22.4k|    }
  953|       |

  969|     87|                break;

  971|    361|            tokens.push(token);
  972|       |        }
  973|       |

  975|      0|            let loc = self.get_current_location();

  977|     87|        }

  982|       |    /// Evaluate a conditional expression (simplified - handle defined and basic arithmetic)
  983|     87|    fn evaluate_conditional_expression(&mut self, tokens: Vec<PPToken>) -> Result<bool, PPError> {
  984|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Removed redundant filtering of Eod tokens and a buggy optimization.
  985|       |        // parse_conditional_expression already ensures no Eod tokens are present.
  986|       |        // This avoids two allocations and two full clones of the token list.
  987|       |        // The buggy 'defined' optimization was also removed as it incorrectly
  988|       |        // returned early for complex expressions like '#if defined(FOO) && 0'.
  989|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Optimized to take tokens by value, avoiding a redundant `to_vec()` clone.
  990|     87|        if tokens.is_empty() {
  991|       |            // For empty expressions, treat as false
  992|      0|            return Ok(false);
  993|     87|        }
  994|       |
  995|       |        // First, expand macros in the expression
  996|     87|        let mut expanded_tokens = tokens;
  997|     87|        match self.expand_tokens(&mut expanded_tokens, true) {
  998|     87|            Ok(_) => {}
  999|      0|            Err(_e) => {
 1000|       |                // If macro expansion fails, emit diagnostic and treat as false
 1001|      0|                self.report_warning(
 1002|       |                    "Failed to expand macros in conditional expression",
 1003|      0|                    self.get_current_location(),
 1004|       |                );
 1005|      0|                return Ok(false);
 1006|       |            }
 1007|       |        }
 1008|       |
 1009|     87|        self.evaluate_arithmetic_expression(&expanded_tokens)
 1010|     87|    }
 1011|       |
 1012|       |    /// Evaluate a simple arithmetic expression for #if/#elif
 1013|     87|    fn evaluate_arithmetic_expression(&mut self, tokens: &[PPToken]) -> Result<bool, PPError> {
 1014|     87|        if tokens.is_empty() {
 1015|      0|            let loc = self.get_current_location();
 1016|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidConditionalExpression, loc);
 1017|     87|        }
 1018|       |
 1019|     87|        let mut interpreter = Interpreter::new(tokens, self);
 1020|     87|        let result = interpreter.evaluate();
 1021|       |
 1022|     87|        match result {
 1023|     83|            Ok(val) => Ok(val.is_truthy()),
 1024|       |            Err(_) => {
 1025|       |                // For complex expressions that can't be parsed, emit a warning and treat as false
 1026|      4|                self.report_warning(
 1027|       |                    "Invalid conditional expression in preprocessor directive",
 1028|      4|                    self.get_current_location(),
 1029|       |                );
 1030|       |                // Return false for unparseable expressions to allow compilation to continue
 1031|      4|                Ok(false)
 1032|       |            }
 1033|       |        }
 1034|     87|    }

 1036|       |    /// Lex the next token
 1037|  1.21k|    fn pop_finished_lexer(&mut self) -> bool {
 1038|       |        // EOF reached, pop the lexer
 1039|  1.21k|        let popped_lexer = self.lexer_stack.pop().unwrap();
 1040|       |
 1041|       |        // If this was an included file, pop from include stack and decrement depth.
 1042|  1.21k|        if let Some(include_info) = self.include_stack.last()
                                  ^19
 1043|     19|            && include_info.file_id == popped_lexer.source_id
 1044|     19|        {
 1045|     19|            self.include_stack.pop();
 1046|     19|            self.include_depth -= 1;
 1047|  1.19k|        }
 1048|       |
 1049|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Use `take_line_starts` to move the line_starts vector
 1050|       |        // instead of cloning it. This is a performance optimization that
 1051|       |        // avoids a potentially large allocation when a file is finished lexing.
 1052|  1.21k|        self.sm
 1053|  1.21k|            .set_line_starts(popped_lexer.source_id, popped_lexer.take_line_starts());
 1054|       |
 1055|  1.21k|        !self.lexer_stack.is_empty()
 1056|  1.21k|    }

                                  ^571

                                          ^24.4k

 1069|      0|                        self.report_pp_error(err);

 1074|  1.21k|                    if !self.pop_finished_lexer() {
 1075|  1.19k|                        return None;
 1076|     19|                    }
 1077|       |                    // Continue to try the next lexer in the stack

 1100|      8|                            self.skip_directive()

 1102|    165|                            self.execute_directive(kind)
 1103|       |                        }

 1113|    265|    fn handle_conditional_directive(&mut self, kind: DirectiveKind, location: SourceLoc) -> Result<(), PPError> {
 1114|    265|        match kind {
 1115|       |            DirectiveKind::If | DirectiveKind::Ifdef | DirectiveKind::Ifndef => {
 1116|    105|                if self.is_currently_skipping() {

 1121|       |                        DirectiveKind::If => {
 1122|     81|                            let tokens = self.parse_conditional_expression().unwrap_or_default();

 1124|     81|                            self.handle_if_directive(cond)
 1125|       |                        }
 1126|     10|                        DirectiveKind::Ifdef => self.handle_ifdef(),
 1127|     11|                        DirectiveKind::Ifndef => self.handle_ifndef(),
 1128|      0|                        _ => unreachable!(),
 1129|       |                    }
 1130|       |                }
 1131|       |            }
 1132|       |            DirectiveKind::Elif => {
 1133|     11|                if self.should_evaluate_conditional() {
 1134|      6|                    let tokens = self.parse_conditional_expression().unwrap_or_default();
 1135|      6|                    let cond = self.evaluate_conditional_expression(tokens).unwrap_or(false);
 1136|      6|                    self.handle_elif_directive(cond, location)
 1137|       |                } else {
 1138|      5|                    self.handle_elif_directive(false, location)
 1139|       |                }
 1140|       |            }
 1141|     47|            DirectiveKind::Else => self.handle_else(location),
 1142|    102|            DirectiveKind::Endif => self.handle_endif(location),
 1143|      0|            _ => unreachable!(),
 1144|       |        }
 1145|    265|    }

 1179|       |    /// Tokenize the content of a pragma directive
 1180|      5|    fn tokenize_pragma_content(&mut self, pragma_content: &str) -> Vec<PPToken> {
 1181|       |        // Create a buffer for the pragma content
 1182|      5|        let source_id = self

 1184|      5|            .add_buffer(pragma_content.as_bytes().to_vec(), "<_Pragma>", None);

 1187|       |
 1188|       |        // Collect all tokens from the pragma content
 1189|      5|        let mut tokens = Vec::new();
 1190|     19|        while let Some(token) = temp_lexer.next_token() {
                                     ^14
 1191|     14|            if matches!(token.kind, PPTokenKind::Eod | PPTokenKind::Eof) {
 1192|      0|                continue;

 1194|     14|            tokens.push(token);
 1195|       |        }

 1201|      0|            SourceLoc::new(source_id, 0)

 1206|       |

 1210|       |    /// Perform the action of a pragma directive
 1211|      5|    fn perform_pragma(&mut self, pragma_content: &str) {
 1212|      5|        let tokens = self.tokenize_pragma_content(pragma_content);
 1213|       |
 1214|       |        // Push to pending_tokens in reverse order so they come out in correct order
 1215|     19|        for token in tokens.into_iter().rev() {
                                   ^5     ^5          ^5
 1216|     19|            self.pending_tokens.push_front(token);
 1217|     19|        }
 1218|       |
 1219|       |        // Execute pragma handler
 1220|       |        // handle_pragma will consume tokens from pending_tokens
 1221|      5|        if self.handle_pragma().is_err() {
 1222|       |            // If handle_pragma failed (e.g. unknown pragma), it might not have consumed all tokens.
 1223|       |            // We must consume the remaining tokens of this pragma until EOD to ensure they don't leak.
 1224|      0|            while let Some(token) = self.lex_token() {
 1225|      0|                if token.kind == PPTokenKind::Eod {
 1226|      0|                    break;
 1227|      0|                }
 1228|       |            }
 1229|      5|        }
 1230|      5|    }

 1258|    108|    fn check_macro_redefinition(&mut self, name: StringId, name_token: &PPToken, macro_info: &MacroInfo) -> bool {
 1259|    108|        if let Some(existing) = self.macros.get(&name) {
                                  ^4

 1261|       |            // Mask out runtime state flags (USED, DISABLED) that don't affect definition identity.
 1262|       |            // We also exclude BUILTIN from the comparison itself because we want to allow
 1263|       |            // the user to redefine a built-in macro with an identical definition.
 1264|      4|            let identity_flags_mask = MacroFlags::FUNCTION_LIKE | MacroFlags::C99_VARARGS | MacroFlags::GNU_VARARGS;
 1265|       |
 1266|      4|            let is_different = (existing.flags & identity_flags_mask) != (macro_info.flags & identity_flags_mask)
 1267|      4|                || existing.parameter_list != macro_info.parameter_list
 1268|      4|                || existing.variadic_arg != macro_info.variadic_arg
 1269|      4|                || existing.tokens.len() != macro_info.tokens.len()
 1270|      4|                || existing
 1271|      4|                    .tokens
 1272|      4|                    .iter()

 1274|      4|                    .any(|(a, b)| a.kind != b.kind);

 1276|      4|            if existing.flags.contains(MacroFlags::BUILTIN) {
 1277|      2|                if is_different {

 1280|      1|                        name_token.location,
 1281|      1|                    );

 1284|       |                // but we only warn if it was actually different.
 1285|      2|                return false;

 1540|     11|    fn handle_elif_directive(&mut self, condition: bool, location: SourceLoc) -> Result<(), PPError> {
 1541|     11|        if self.conditional_stack.is_empty() {
 1542|      1|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::ElifWithoutIf, location);
 1543|     10|        }

 1545|       |

 1547|      1|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::ElifAfterElse, location);
 1548|      9|        }

 1552|      3|            current.found_non_skipping = true;

 1561|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::ElseWithoutIf, location);
 1562|     47|        }

 1564|       |

 1566|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::MultipleElse, location);
 1567|     47|        }
 1568|       |

 1571|     47|        current.was_skipping = !should_process;
 1572|       |
 1573|     47|        self.expect_eod()
 1574|     47|    }

 1847|       |    /// Helper to convert tokens to their string representation
 1848|     16|    fn tokens_to_string(&self, tokens: &[PPToken]) -> String {
 1849|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Optimized two-pass string building.
 1850|       |        // We use the token's length field for the first pass as it's a fast u16 access.
 1851|     44|        let total_len: usize = tokens.iter().map(|t| t.length as usize).sum();
                          ^16        ^16     ^16    ^16    ^16                        ^16
 1852|       |
 1853|     16|        let mut result = String::with_capacity(total_len);
 1854|     16|        let mut cache = SourceBufferCache::new(self.sm);
 1855|       |
 1856|     60|        for token in tokens {
                          ^44
 1857|     44|            result.push_str(cache.get_token_text(token));
 1858|     44|        }
 1859|     16|        result
 1860|     16|    }

 1862|       |    /// Helper to create a virtual buffer and expand tokens within it
 1863|     96|    fn expand_virtual_buffer(
 1864|     96|        &mut self,
 1865|     96|        tokens: &[PPToken],
 1866|     96|        name: &str,
 1867|     96|        location: SourceLoc,

 1941|       |    /// Parse macro arguments from the current lexer
 1942|     48|    fn parse_macro_args_from_lexer(&mut self, macro_info: &MacroInfo) -> Result<Vec<Vec<PPToken>>, PPError> {
 1943|     48|        let token = self.expect_token()?;
                                                     ^0
 1944|     48|        if token.kind != PPTokenKind::LeftParen {
 1945|      0|            self.pending_tokens.push_front(token);
 1946|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidMacroParameter, token.location);
 1947|     48|        }
 1948|       |
 1949|     48|        let mut args = Vec::with_capacity(macro_info.parameter_list.len());
 1950|     48|        let mut current_arg = Vec::new();
 1951|     48|        let mut depth = 0;
 1952|       |
 1953|    177|        while let Some(t) = self.lex_token() {
 1954|     29|            match t.kind {
 1955|      0|                PPTokenKind::Eof => break,
 1956|      2|                PPTokenKind::LeftParen => {
 1957|      2|                    depth += 1;
 1958|      2|                    current_arg.push(t);
 1959|      2|                }
 1960|     50|                PPTokenKind::RightParen if depth > 0 => {
                                                                 ^2
 1961|      2|                    depth -= 1;
 1962|      2|                    current_arg.push(t);
 1963|      2|                }
 1964|       |                PPTokenKind::RightParen => {
 1965|     48|                    if !current_arg.is_empty() || !args.is_empty() {
                                                                ^1
 1966|     48|                        args.push(current_arg);
 1967|     48|                    }
                                  ^0

 1970|     48|                    let valid = if macro_info.variadic_arg.is_some() {
 1971|      6|                        args.len() >= expected
 1972|       |                    } else {
 1973|     42|                        args.len() == expected
 1974|       |                    };
 1975|       |
 1976|     48|                    if valid {
 1977|     48|                        return Ok(args);
 1978|      0|                    }
 1979|       |
 1980|      0|                    return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidMacroParameter, macro_info.location);
 1981|       |                }

                                                             ^28
 1983|     28|                    args.push(std::mem::take(&mut current_arg));
 1984|     28|                }
 1985|     97|                _ => current_arg.push(t),
 1986|       |            }

 1988|       |
 1989|      0|        self.emit_error(PPErrorKind::UnexpectedEndOfFile, self.get_current_span())
 1990|     48|    }

 2065|     55|        macro_info: &MacroInfo,
