 2127|       |    /// Perform token pasting logic for the ## operator, including GNU comma swallowing.
 2128|     12|    fn perform_token_pasting(
 2129|     12|        &mut self,
 2130|     12|        macro_info: &MacroInfo,
 2131|     12|        left: Option<PPToken>,
 2132|     12|        right_token: &PPToken,
 2133|     12|        args: &[Vec<PPToken>],
 2134|     12|    ) -> Result<(Vec<PPToken>, bool), PPError> {
 2135|     12|        let right_tokens = if let PPTokenKind::Identifier(sym) = right_token.kind {
 2136|     12|            self.get_macro_param_tokens(macro_info, sym, args, right_token.location)
 2137|     12|                .unwrap_or(Cow::Borrowed(std::slice::from_ref(right_token)))
 2138|       |        } else {
 2139|      0|            Cow::Borrowed(std::slice::from_ref(right_token))
 2140|       |        };
 2141|       |
 2142|     12|        if right_tokens.is_empty() {
 2143|       |            // Right operand is empty (placemarker).
 2144|      4|            let is_comma = left.as_ref().is_some_and(|t| t.kind == PPTokenKind::Comma);
                                                                       ^3        ^3
 2145|      4|            let is_variadic =
 2146|      4|                matches!(right_token.kind, PPTokenKind::Identifier(s) if macro_info.variadic_arg == Some(s));
                              ^2                                                 ^2                                     ^2
 2147|       |
 2148|      4|            if is_comma && is_variadic {
                                         ^2
 2149|       |                // GNU Comma Swallowing extension: swallow the comma.
 2150|      2|                Ok((Vec::new(), false))
 2151|      2|            } else if let Some(l) = left {
                                             ^1
 2152|       |                // Standard behavior: paste left with empty -> left.
 2153|      1|                Ok((vec![l], true))
 2154|       |            } else {
 2155|      1|                Ok((Vec::new(), false))
 2156|       |            }
 2157|      8|        } else if let Some(l) = left {
                                         ^5
 2158|       |            // Right is not empty, Left is not empty: paste left with right[0].
 2159|      5|            let mut pasted = self.paste_tokens(&l, &right_tokens[0])?;
                                                                                  ^0
 2160|      5|            let pasted_count = pasted.len();
 2161|      5|            pasted.extend(right_tokens.iter().skip(1).copied());
 2162|      5|            Ok((pasted, pasted_count > 0 || right_tokens.len() > 1))
                                                          ^0
 2163|       |        } else {
 2164|       |            // Right is not empty, Left is empty: empty ## right -> right.
 2165|      3|            Ok((right_tokens.into_owned(), true))
 2166|       |        }
 2167|     12|    }

 2169|       |    /// Stringify tokens for # operator
 2170|      4|    pub(crate) fn stringify_tokens(&self, tokens: &[PPToken], location: SourceLoc) -> Result<PPToken, PPError> {
 2171|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Use a two-pass approach to build the stringified token efficiently.
 2172|      4|        let mut total_len = 2; // For the opening and closing quotes
 2173|      4|        let mut cache = SourceBufferCache::new(self.sm);
 2174|       |
 2175|     10|        for (i, token) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^4     ^4     ^4
 2176|     10|            if i > 0 && token.flags.contains(PPTokenFlags::LEADING_SPACE) {
                                      ^6          ^6
 2177|      4|                total_len += 1;
 2178|      6|            }
 2179|     10|            let bytes = cache.get_token_bytes(token);
 2180|     35|            for &b in bytes {
                               ^25
 2181|     25|                match b {
 2182|      2|                    b'"' | b'\\' => total_len += 2,
 2183|     23|                    _ => total_len += 1,
 2184|       |                }
 2185|       |            }
 2186|       |        }
 2187|       |
 2188|      4|        let mut result = String::with_capacity(total_len);
 2189|      4|        result.push('"');
 2190|       |
 2191|      4|        cache.reset();
 2192|     10|        for (i, token) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^4     ^4     ^4
 2193|     10|            if i > 0 && token.flags.contains(PPTokenFlags::LEADING_SPACE) {
                                      ^6          ^6
 2194|      4|                result.push(' ');
 2195|      6|            }
 2196|       |
 2197|     10|            let bytes = cache.get_token_bytes(token);
 2198|     10|            let mut last_start = 0;
 2199|     25|            for (j, &b) in bytes.iter().enumerate() {
                                         ^10   ^10    ^10
 2200|     25|                if b == b'"' || b == b'\\' {
                                              ^24
 2201|      2|                    if j > last_start {
 2202|      2|                        result.push_str(unsafe { std::str::from_utf8_unchecked(&bytes[last_start..j]) });
 2203|      2|                    }
                                  ^0
 2204|      2|                    result.push('\\');
 2205|      2|                    result.push(b as char);
 2206|      2|                    last_start = j + 1;
 2207|     23|                }
 2208|       |            }
 2209|     10|            if last_start < bytes.len() {
 2210|     10|                result.push_str(unsafe { std::str::from_utf8_unchecked(&bytes[last_start..]) });
 2211|     10|            }
                          ^0
 2212|       |        }
 2213|       |
 2214|      4|        result.push('"');
 2215|       |
 2216|      4|        Ok(PPToken::new(
 2217|      4|            PPTokenKind::StringLiteral(StringId::new(&result)),
 2218|      4|            PPTokenFlags::empty(),
 2219|      4|            location,
 2220|      4|            result.len() as u16,
 2221|      4|        ))
 2222|      4|    }

 2224|       |    /// Paste tokens for ## operator
 2225|      5|    fn paste_tokens(&mut self, left: &PPToken, right: &PPToken) -> Result<Vec<PPToken>, PPError> {
 2226|       |        // Get text of both tokens
 2227|      5|        let left_buffer = self.sm.get_buffer(left.location.source_id());
 2228|      5|        let left_start = left.location.offset() as usize;
 2229|      5|        let left_end = left_start + left.length as usize;
 2230|      5|        let left_text = if left_end <= left_buffer.len() {
 2231|      5|            unsafe { std::str::from_utf8_unchecked(&left_buffer[left_start..left_end]) }
 2232|       |        } else {
 2233|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidTokenPasting, left.location);
 2234|       |        };
 2235|       |
 2236|      5|        let right_buffer = self.sm.get_buffer(right.location.source_id());
 2237|      5|        let right_start = right.location.offset() as usize;
 2238|      5|        let right_end = right_start + right.length as usize;
 2239|      5|        let right_text = if right_end <= right_buffer.len() {
 2240|      5|            unsafe { std::str::from_utf8_unchecked(&right_buffer[right_start..right_end]) }
 2241|       |        } else {
 2242|      0|            return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidTokenPasting, right.location);
 2243|       |        };
 2244|       |
 2245|       |        // ⚡ Bolt: Avoid redundant format! and clone by building the byte buffer directly.
 2246|      5|        let mut virtual_buffer = Vec::with_capacity(left_text.len() + right_text.len());
 2247|      5|        virtual_buffer.extend_from_slice(left_text.as_bytes());
 2248|      5|        virtual_buffer.extend_from_slice(right_text.as_bytes());
 2249|      5|        let virtual_id = self
 2250|      5|            .sm
 2251|      5|            .add_virtual_buffer(virtual_buffer, "pasted-tokens", None, FileKind::PastedToken);
 2252|       |
 2253|       |        // Create a temporary lexer to lex the pasted text
 2254|      5|        let buffer = self.sm.get_buffer_arc(virtual_id);
 2255|      5|        let mut lexer = PPLexer::new(virtual_id, buffer);
 2256|       |
 2257|      5|        let mut tokens = Vec::new();
 2258|     11|        while let Some(token) = lexer.next_token() {
                                     ^6
 2259|      6|            if matches!(token.kind, PPTokenKind::Eod | PPTokenKind::Eof) {
 2260|      0|                continue;
 2261|      6|            }
 2262|      6|            tokens.push(token);
 2263|       |        }
 2264|       |
 2265|       |        // If the result is not a valid preprocessing token, the behavior is undefined.
 2266|       |        // We will return whatever tokens we got.
 2267|      5|        Ok(tokens)
 2268|      5|    }

 2547|       |    /// Push a conditional that is lazily skipped (nested in a skipped block)
 2548|      3|    fn push_skipped_conditional(&mut self) {
 2549|       |        // Equivalent to handle_if_directive(false)
 2550|      3|        let info = PPConditionalInfo {
 2551|      3|            was_skipping: self.is_currently_skipping(),
 2552|      3|            found_else: false,
 2553|      3|            found_non_skipping: false, // Condition treated as false
 2554|      3|        };
 2555|      3|        self.conditional_stack.push(info);
 2556|       |        // Force skipping for this level
 2557|      3|        self.set_skipping(true);
 2558|      3|    }
 2559|       |
 2560|       |    /// Check if we should evaluate conditional expression (e.g. for #elif)
 2561|     11|    fn should_evaluate_conditional(&self) -> bool {
 2562|       |        // We should evaluate ONLY if no parent is skipping
 2563|       |        // The current level (which we are about to replace with elif) is at index len()-1.
 2564|       |        // The parent is at index len()-2.
 2565|     11|        if self.conditional_stack.len() > 1 {
 2566|      2|            let parent_index = self.conditional_stack.len() - 2;
 2567|      2|            let parent_skipping = self.conditional_stack[parent_index].was_skipping;
 2568|      2|            if parent_skipping {
 2569|      2|                return false;
 2570|      0|            }
 2571|      9|        }
 2572|       |
 2573|       |        // And if we haven't found a true branch in this level yet
 2574|      9|        if let Some(current) = self.conditional_stack.last() {
                                  ^8
 2575|      8|            !current.found_non_skipping
 2576|       |        } else {
 2577|      1|            false
 2578|       |        }
 2579|     11|    }

 2581|    123|    fn is_recursive_expansion(&self, location: SourceLoc, macro_name: &str) -> bool {
 2582|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Optimization: avoid format! and Path allocation in a hot loop.
 2583|       |        // We check the path directly by string prefix/suffix which is much faster.
 2584|    138|        std::iter::successors(self.sm.get_file_info(location.source_id()), |info| {
                      ^123                  ^123    ^123          ^123     ^123
 2585|    138|            info.include_loc.and_then(|loc| self.sm.get_file_info(loc.source_id()))
                                                          ^15     ^15           ^15 ^15
 2586|    138|        })
 2587|    123|        .take(100)
 2588|    138|        .any(|info| {
                       ^123
 2589|       |            // Bolt ⚡: Fast-path: Only macro expansion buffers can be recursive.
 2590|       |            // This avoids expensive path-to-string conversions for real source files.
 2591|    138|            if info.kind != FileKind::MacroExpansion {
 2592|    127|                return false;
 2593|     11|            }
 2594|       |
 2595|     11|            let path_str = info.path.to_str().unwrap_or("");
 2596|       |            // Bolt ⚡: Prefix "<macro_" is 7 chars, suffix ">" is 1 char. Total 8.
 2597|     11|            path_str.len() == macro_name.len() + 8
 2598|      0|                && path_str.starts_with("<macro_")
 2599|      0|                && path_str.ends_with('>')
 2600|      0|                && &path_str[7..path_str.len() - 1] == macro_name
 2601|    138|        })
 2602|    123|    }

 2604|    103|    fn create_virtual_buffer_tokens(
 2605|    103|        &mut self,
 2606|    103|        tokens: &[PPToken],
 2607|    103|        macro_name: &str,
 2608|    103|        trigger_location: SourceLoc,
 2609|    103|    ) -> Vec<PPToken> {
 2610|       |        // Bolt ⚡: Optimized macro expansion token creation.
 2611|       |        // This function is a hot path during macro expansion. We've optimized it by:
 2612|       |        // 1. Using a single pass to build the virtual buffer and collect token metadata.
 2613|       |        // 2. Caching SourceId lookups and 'is_pasted' checks to avoid redundant HashMap
 2614|       |        //    lookups and string comparisons for tokens from the same source.
 2615|       |        // 3. Avoiding redundant calls to `get_text()` by using the buffer lengths directly.
 2616|       |        // 4. Pre-allocating all necessary vectors with the correct capacity.
 2617|       |
 2618|       |        // Pass 0: Sum up lengths for capacity hint.
 2619|    482|        let total_upper_bound: usize = tokens.iter().map(|t| t.length as usize).sum();
                          ^103               ^103    ^103   ^103   ^103                       ^103
 2620|    103|        let mut buffer = Vec::with_capacity(total_upper_bound);
 2621|       |
 2622|       |        // Metadata to avoid re-calculating things in the final mapping pass.
 2623|       |        // (is_pasted, offset_in_new_buffer, length_in_new_buffer)
 2624|    103|        let mut token_metadata = Vec::with_capacity(tokens.len());
 2625|       |
 2626|    103|        let mut last_is_pasted_sid = None;
 2627|    103|        let mut last_is_pasted_val = false;
 2628|       |
 2629|       |        {
 2630|    103|            let mut cache = SourceBufferCache::new(self.sm);
 2631|       |
 2632|    585|            for t in tokens {
                              ^482
 2633|    482|                let sid = t.location.source_id();
 2634|       |
 2635|       |                // Optimized is_pasted check with caching.
 2636|    482|                let is_pasted = if Some(sid) == last_is_pasted_sid {
 2637|    316|                    last_is_pasted_val
 2638|       |                } else {
 2639|    166|                    let val = self
 2640|    166|                        .sm
 2641|    166|                        .get_file_info(sid)
 2642|    166|                        .is_some_and(|info| info.kind == FileKind::PastedToken);
                                                          ^154         ^154
 2643|    166|                    last_is_pasted_sid = Some(sid);
 2644|    166|                    last_is_pasted_val = val;
 2645|    166|                    val
 2646|       |                };
 2647|       |
 2648|    482|                let start_offset = buffer.len() as u32;
 2649|       |
 2650|       |                // Build buffer efficiently using cache
 2651|    482|                let bytes = cache.get_token_bytes(t);
 2652|    482|                buffer.extend_from_slice(bytes);
 2653|       |
 2654|    482|                let len = (buffer.len() as u32 - start_offset) as u16;
 2655|    482|                token_metadata.push((is_pasted, start_offset, len));
 2656|       |            }
 2657|       |        }
 2658|       |
 2659|    103|        let virtual_id = self.sm.add_virtual_buffer(
 2660|    103|            buffer,
 2661|    103|            &format!("macro_{}", macro_name),
 2662|    103|            Some(trigger_location),
 2663|    103|            FileKind::MacroExpansion,
 2664|       |        );
 2665|       |
 2666|       |        // Final pass: Construct new tokens using the pre-calculated metadata.
 2667|    103|        tokens
 2668|    103|            .iter()
 2669|    103|            .zip(token_metadata)
 2670|    482|            .map(|(t, (is_pasted, offset, len))| {
                           ^103
 2671|    482|                let loc = if is_pasted {
 2672|      6|                    t.location
 2673|       |                } else {
 2674|    476|                    SourceLoc::new(virtual_id, offset)
 2675|       |                };
 2676|       |
 2677|    482|                PPToken::new(t.kind, t.flags | PPTokenFlags::MACRO_EXPANDED, loc, len)
 2678|    482|            })
 2679|    103|            .collect()
 2680|    103|    }

 2714|     48|    fn parse_macro_definition_params(
 2715|     48|        &mut self,
 2716|     48|        macro_name: &str,
 2717|     48|    ) -> Result<(MacroFlags, Vec<StringId>, Option<StringId>), PPError> {
 2718|     48|        let mut flags = MacroFlags::FUNCTION_LIKE;
 2719|     48|        let mut params = Vec::new();
 2720|     48|        let mut variadic = None;
 2721|       |
 2722|       |        'param_parsing: loop {
 2723|     80|            let param_token = self.expect_token()?;
                                                               ^0
 2724|     80|            match param_token.kind {
 2725|      0|                PPTokenKind::RightParen => break,
 2726|       |                PPTokenKind::Ellipsis => {
 2727|      3|                    flags |= MacroFlags::C99_VARARGS;
 2728|      3|                    variadic = Some(StringId::new("__VA_ARGS__"));
 2729|      3|                    if !matches!(self.lex_token().map(|t| t.kind), Some(PPTokenKind::RightParen)) {
                                      ^0
 2730|      0|                        return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidMacroParameter, param_token.location);
 2731|      3|                    }
 2732|      3|                    break;
 2733|       |                }
 2734|     77|                PPTokenKind::Identifier(sym) => {
 2735|     77|                    if params.contains(&sym) {
 2736|      0|                        return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidMacroParameter, param_token.location);
 2737|     77|                    }
 2738|     77|                    params.push(sym);
 2739|       |
 2740|     77|                    let sep = self.expect_token()?;
                                                               ^0
 2741|     77|                    match sep.kind {
 2742|     32|                        PPTokenKind::Comma => continue,
 2743|     43|                        PPTokenKind::RightParen => break,
 2744|       |                        PPTokenKind::Ellipsis => {
 2745|      2|                            variadic = Some(params.pop().unwrap());
 2746|      2|                            flags |= MacroFlags::GNU_VARARGS;
 2747|      2|                            if !matches!(self.lex_token().map(|t| t.kind), Some(PPTokenKind::RightParen)) {
                                              ^0
 2748|      0|                                return self.emit_error_loc(PPErrorKind::InvalidMacroParameter, sep.location);
 2749|      2|                            }
 2750|      2|                            break;
 2751|       |                        }
 2752|       |                        _ => {
 2753|       |                            // Not a standard parameter list separator; treat as start of body.
 2754|       |                            // This pushes back both the separator and the identifier.
 2755|      0|                            self.pending_tokens.push_front(sep);
 2756|      0|                            self.pending_tokens.push_front(param_token);
 2757|      0|                            break 'param_parsing;
 2758|       |                        }
 2759|       |                    }
 2760|       |                }
 2761|       |                _ => {

 2764|      0|                        param_token.location,
 2765|       |                    );

 2768|      0|                    while let Some(t) = self.lex_token() {
 2769|      0|                        if matches!(t.kind, PPTokenKind::Comma | PPTokenKind::RightParen) {
 2770|      0|                            self.pending_tokens.push_front(t);
 2771|      0|                            break;
 2772|      0|                        }
 2773|       |                    }
 2774|       |                }

 2776|       |        }
 2777|       |
 2778|     48|        Ok((flags, params, variadic))
 2779|     48|    }
 2780|       |}
 2781|       |
 2782|       |/// Helper to cache source buffers for efficient token text retrieval
 2783|       |struct SourceBufferCache<'a> {
 2784|       |    sm: &'a SourceManager,
 2785|       |    last_sid: Option<SourceId>,
 2786|       |    last_buffer: Option<&'a [u8]>,

 2788|       |

                          ^534

  398|      1|                    expected: lhs_str,
  399|      1|                    found: rhs_str,
  400|      1|                },
  401|       |            );
  402|       |            // Still record conversions so the assignment works
  403|      1|            self.record_implicit_conversions(lhs_ty, rhs_ty, rhs_ref);
  404|      1|            true
  405|     15|        } else if self.is_discarding_pointer_qualifiers(lhs_ty, rhs_ty, rhs_ref) {

  407|      1|            let rhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(rhs_ty);
  408|      1|            self.report_warning(
  409|      1|                span,

  411|      1|                    expected: lhs_str,
  412|      1|                    found: rhs_str,
  413|      1|                },
  414|       |            );
  415|      1|            self.record_implicit_conversions(lhs_ty, rhs_ty, rhs_ref);
  416|      1|            true
  417|       |        } else {
  418|     14|            let lhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(lhs_ty);

  431|       |    /// Check if the assignment is between pointers where target qualifiers are discarded
  432|     17|    fn is_discarding_pointer_qualifiers(&self, lhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ty: QualType, _rhs_ref: NodeRef) -> bool {
  433|       |        // Both must be pointers (or array/function that decay to pointers)
  434|     17|        if !lhs_ty.is_pointer() {
  435|     14|            return false;
  436|      3|        }
  437|       |
  438|       |        // Resolve implicit decay for RHS
  439|      3|        let rhs_pointee = if rhs_ty.is_array() {
  440|       |            // Array qualifiers apply to the element type
  441|      0|            self.registry
  442|      0|                .get_array_element(rhs_ty.ty())
  443|      0|                .map(|elem| QualType::new(elem, rhs_ty.qualifiers()))
  444|      3|        } else if rhs_ty.is_function() {
  445|      0|            Some(rhs_ty) // Function decays to pointer to function (unqualified)
  446|      3|        } else if rhs_ty.is_pointer() {
  447|      2|            self.registry.get_pointee(rhs_ty.ty())
  448|       |        } else {
  449|      1|            None
  450|       |        };
  451|       |
  452|      3|        if let Some(rhs_base) = rhs_pointee {
                                  ^2
  453|      2|            let lhs_base = self.registry.get_pointee(lhs_ty.ty()).unwrap();

  455|       |            // Check compatibility ignoring top-level qualifiers of the pointed-to type

  457|      2|                if lhs_base.ty() == self.registry.type_void || rhs_base.ty() == self.registry.type_void {
  458|      0|                    true
  459|       |                } else {
  460|      2|                    self.registry.is_compatible(
  461|      2|                        QualType::unqualified(lhs_base.ty()),
  462|      2|                        QualType::unqualified(rhs_base.ty()),
  463|       |                    )
  464|       |                };
  465|       |

  467|       |                // Return true if qualifiers are DISCARDED (rhs has something lhs doesn't)
  468|      1|                return !lhs_base.qualifiers().contains(rhs_base.qualifiers());
  469|      1|            }
  470|      1|        }
  471|       |
  472|      2|        false

  475|       |    /// Check if the mismatch is between pointers to different struct types
  476|     18|    fn is_incompatible_struct_pointer_types(&self, lhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ty: QualType) -> bool {
  477|       |        // Both must be pointers
  478|     18|        if !lhs_ty.is_pointer() || !rhs_ty.is_pointer() {
                                                 ^4
  479|     15|            return false;
  480|      3|        }
  481|       |
  482|       |        // Get the pointed-to types
  483|      3|        let lhs_pointee = self.registry.get_pointee(lhs_ty.ty());
  484|      3|        let rhs_pointee = self.registry.get_pointee(rhs_ty.ty());

  488|      3|            let lhs_pointee_ty = self.registry.get(lhs_p.ty());
  489|      3|            let rhs_pointee_ty = self.registry.get(rhs_p.ty());
  490|       |
  491|      3|            if let (TypeKind::Record { .. }, TypeKind::Record { .. }) = (&lhs_pointee_ty.kind, &rhs_pointee_ty.kind) {
  492|       |                // They must be different types (not compatible)
  493|      1|                return lhs_p.ty() != rhs_p.ty();

  495|      0|        }

  497|      2|        false
  498|     18|    }

  500|     98|    fn check_scalar_condition(&mut self, condition: NodeRef) {
  501|     98|        let Some(mut cond_ty) = self.visit_node(condition) else {
  502|      0|            return;
  503|       |        };

                                               ^96     ^96

  508|      2|            {

  510|      2|                    self.ast.get_span(condition),
  511|      2|                    SemanticErrorKind::AddressOfArrayAlwaysTrue { name: *name },
  512|      2|                );
  513|      2|            }
                          ^0
  514|      2|            cond_ty = self.registry.decay(cond_ty, TypeQualifiers::empty());

  516|      2|            self.semantic_info.types[condition.index()] = Some(cond_ty);
  517|     96|        }
  518|       |
  519|     98|        if !cond_ty.is_scalar() {
  520|      4|            self.report_error(

  522|      4|                SemanticErrorKind::TypeMismatch {

  525|      4|                },

  527|     94|        }

  542|      6|        self.loop_depth -= 1;

  621|    286|    fn visit_unary_op(&mut self, op: UnaryOp, operand_ref: NodeRef, span: SourceSpan) -> Option<QualType> {
  622|    286|        let operand_ty = self.visit_node(operand_ref)?;
                                                                   ^0
  623|       |
  624|    286|        match op {
  625|       |            UnaryOp::AddrOf => {
  626|     34|                if !self.is_lvalue(operand_ref) {
  627|      2|                    self.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::NotAnLvalue);
  628|      2|                    return None;
  629|     32|                }
  630|     32|                if self.is_bitfield(operand_ref) {
  631|      1|                    self.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::AddressOfBitfield);
  632|      1|                    return None;
  633|     31|                }
  634|     31|                if self.is_register_variable(operand_ref) {
  635|      2|                    self.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::AddressOfRegister);
  636|      2|                    return None;
  637|     29|                }
  638|       |                // Taking address of array or function: result is pointer to array/function (no decay)
  639|     29|                Some(QualType::unqualified(self.registry.pointer_to(operand_ty)))
  640|       |            }
  641|       |            UnaryOp::Deref => {
  642|    167|                let actual_ty = if operand_ty.is_array() || operand_ty.is_function() {
  643|    156|                    let decayed = self.registry.decay(operand_ty, TypeQualifiers::empty());
  644|    156|                    self.push_conversion(operand_ref, Conversion::PointerDecay { to: decayed.ty() });
  645|    156|                    decayed
  646|       |                } else {
  647|     11|                    operand_ty
  648|       |                };
  649|       |
  650|    167|                if actual_ty.is_pointer() {
  651|    163|                    self.registry.get_pointee(actual_ty.ty())
  652|       |                } else {
  653|      4|                    let type_kind = &self.registry.get(actual_ty.ty()).kind;

  657|      4|                            ty: type_kind.to_string(),
  658|      4|                        },
  659|       |                    );
  660|      4|                    None
  661|       |                }
  662|       |            }
  663|       |            UnaryOp::Real | UnaryOp::Imag => {
  664|      4|                if let TypeKind::Complex { base_type } = &self.registry.get(operand_ty.ty()).kind {
                                                         ^2
  665|      2|                    Some(QualType::new(*base_type, operand_ty.qualifiers()))
  666|      2|                } else if operand_ty.is_real() {
  667|      2|                    if op == UnaryOp::Real {
  668|      1|                        Some(operand_ty)
  669|       |                    } else {
  670|       |                        // __imag__ on real type returns zero of that type
  671|      1|                        Some(self.registry.strip_all(operand_ty))
  672|       |                    }
  673|       |                } else {
  674|      0|                    let type_kind = &self.registry.get(operand_ty.ty()).kind;
  675|      0|                    self.report_error(
  676|      0|                        span,
  677|      0|                        SemanticErrorKind::InvalidUnaryOperand {
  678|      0|                            ty: type_kind.to_string(),
  679|      0|                        },
  680|       |                    );
  681|      0|                    None
  682|       |                }
  683|       |            }

  685|      4|                self.check_lvalue_and_modifiable(operand_ref, operand_ty, span);

  689|     44|                if operand_ty.is_arithmetic() {

  691|       |                    // and the result has the promoted type.
  692|     44|                    let promoted = self.apply_and_record_integer_promotion(operand_ref, operand_ty);
  693|       |

  696|     44|                    if stripped.qualifiers() != promoted.qualifiers() {

  698|      0|                            operand_ref,
  699|      0|                            Conversion::QualifierAdjust {
  700|      0|                                from: promoted.qualifiers(),
  701|      0|                                to: stripped.qualifiers(),
  702|      0|                            },
  703|      0|                        );
  704|     44|                    }
  705|     44|                    Some(stripped)
  706|       |                } else {

  711|      0|                            ty: type_kind.to_string(),
  712|      0|                        },
  713|       |                    );
  714|      0|                    None
  715|       |                }
  716|       |            }
  717|       |            UnaryOp::LogicNot => {

  719|     26|                Some(QualType::unqualified(self.registry.type_int))

  721|       |            UnaryOp::BitNot => {

  740|    584|    fn apply_and_record_integer_promotion(&mut self, node_ref: NodeRef, ty: QualType) -> QualType {
  741|    584|        let promoted = integer_promotion(self.registry, ty);
  742|    584|        if promoted.ty() != ty.ty() {
  743|     41|            self.push_conversion(
  744|     41|                node_ref,
  745|     41|                Conversion::IntegerPromotion {
  746|     41|                    from: ty.ty(),
  747|     41|                    to: promoted.ty(),
  748|     41|                },
  749|     41|            );
  750|    543|        }
  751|    584|        promoted
  752|    584|    }
  753|       |
  754|    263|    fn analyze_binary_operation_types(
  755|    263|        &mut self,
  756|    263|        op: BinaryOp,
  757|    263|        lhs_promoted: QualType,
  758|    263|        rhs_promoted: QualType,
  759|    263|        span: SourceSpan,
  760|    263|    ) -> Option<(QualType, QualType)> {
  761|     39|        match op {
  762|       |            // Pointer + pointer is invalid (C11 6.5.6)
  763|     43|            BinaryOp::Add if lhs_promoted.is_pointer() && rhs_promoted.is_pointer() => {
                                                                        ^4           ^4         ^1
  764|      1|                let lhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(lhs_promoted);
  765|      1|                let rhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(rhs_promoted);
  766|      1|                self.report_error(
  767|      1|                    span,
  768|      1|                    SemanticErrorKind::InvalidBinaryOperands {
  769|      1|                        left_ty: lhs_str,
  770|      1|                        right_ty: rhs_str,
  771|      1|                    },
  772|       |                );
  773|      1|                None
  774|       |            }
  775|       |            // Pointer + integer = pointer
  776|     42|            BinaryOp::Add if lhs_promoted.is_pointer() && rhs_promoted.is_integer() => {
                                                                        ^3           ^3         ^3
  777|       |                // Void pointer arithmetic is invalid (C11 6.5.6)
  778|      3|                if let Some(pointee) = self.registry.get_pointee(lhs_promoted.ty())
  779|      3|                    && pointee.is_void()
  780|       |                {
  781|      1|                    let lhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(lhs_promoted);
  782|      1|                    let rhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(rhs_promoted);
  783|      1|                    self.report_error(
  784|      1|                        span,
  785|      1|                        SemanticErrorKind::InvalidBinaryOperands {
  786|      1|                            left_ty: lhs_str,
  787|      1|                            right_ty: rhs_str,
  788|      1|                        },
  789|       |                    );
  790|      1|                    return None;
  791|      2|                }
  792|      2|                Some((lhs_promoted, lhs_promoted))
  793|       |            }
  794|     39|            BinaryOp::Add if lhs_promoted.is_integer() && rhs_promoted.is_pointer() => {
                                                                        ^32          ^32        ^0
  795|       |                // Void pointer arithmetic is invalid (C11 6.5.6)
  796|      0|                if let Some(pointee) = self.registry.get_pointee(rhs_promoted.ty())
  797|      0|                    && pointee.is_void()
  798|       |                {
  799|      0|                    let lhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(lhs_promoted);
  800|      0|                    let rhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(rhs_promoted);
  801|      0|                    self.report_error(
  802|      0|                        span,
  803|      0|                        SemanticErrorKind::InvalidBinaryOperands {
  804|      0|                            left_ty: lhs_str,
  805|      0|                            right_ty: rhs_str,
  806|      0|                        },
  807|       |                    );
  808|      0|                    return None;
  809|      0|                }
  810|      0|                Some((rhs_promoted, rhs_promoted))
  811|       |            }
  812|       |
  813|       |            // Pointer - integer = pointer
  814|     23|            BinaryOp::Sub if lhs_promoted.is_pointer() && rhs_promoted.is_integer() => {
                                                                        ^2           ^2         ^2
  815|       |                // Void pointer arithmetic is invalid (C11 6.5.6)
  816|      2|                if let Some(pointee) = self.registry.get_pointee(lhs_promoted.ty())
  817|      2|                    && pointee.is_void()
  818|       |                {
  819|      1|                    let lhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(lhs_promoted);
  820|      1|                    let rhs_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(rhs_promoted);
  821|      1|                    self.report_error(
  822|      1|                        span,
  823|      1|                        SemanticErrorKind::InvalidBinaryOperands {
  824|      1|                            left_ty: lhs_str,
  825|      1|                            right_ty: rhs_str,
  826|      1|                        },
  827|       |                    );
  828|      1|                    return None;
  829|      1|                }
  830|      1|                Some((lhs_promoted, lhs_promoted))
  831|       |            }
  832|       |
  833|       |            // Pointer - pointer = integer (ptrdiff_t)
  834|     21|            BinaryOp::Sub if lhs_promoted.is_pointer() && rhs_promoted.is_pointer() => {
                                                                        ^0           ^0         ^0
  835|      0|                Some((QualType::unqualified(self.registry.type_int), lhs_promoted))
  836|       |            }
  837|       |
  838|       |            // Pointer/Integer comparisons
  839|       |            BinaryOp::Equal | BinaryOp::NotEqual => {

 1003|    264|    fn resolve_binary_operation_types(
 1004|    264|        &mut self,
 1005|    264|        op: BinaryOp,
 1006|    264|        lhs_ref: NodeRef,
 1007|    264|        rhs_ref: NodeRef,
 1008|    264|        lhs_ty: QualType,
 1009|    264|        rhs_ty: QualType,
 1010|    264|        span: SourceSpan,

 1012|       |        // Perform array/function decay first

                                              ^263   ^263

 1021|      0|            rhs_decayed = self.registry.decay(rhs_ty, TypeQualifiers::empty());

 1153|    947|    fn check_assignment_constraints(&self, lhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ref: NodeRef) -> bool {
 1154|       |        // 0. Identical types
 1155|    947|        if lhs_ty.ty() == rhs_ty.ty() {
 1156|    677|            return true;
 1157|    270|        }
 1158|       |
 1159|       |        // 1. Arithmetic types
 1160|    270|        if lhs_ty.is_arithmetic() && rhs_ty.is_arithmetic() {
                                                   ^192   ^192
 1161|    181|            return true;

 1163|       |
 1164|       |        // 2. Structure or Union types

                                               ^86    ^86

                                                       ^3     ^3             ^2

 1168|       |
 1169|       |        // 3. Pointers
 1170|     83|        if lhs_ty.is_pointer() {

 1172|      7|                return true;
 1173|     67|            }
 1174|       |
 1175|       |            // Resolve implicit decay for RHS to check compatibility
 1176|     67|            let rhs_pointee = if rhs_ty.is_array() {
 1177|       |                // Array qualifiers apply to the element type
 1178|      4|                self.registry
 1179|      4|                    .get_array_element(rhs_ty.ty())
 1180|      4|                    .map(|elem| QualType::new(elem, rhs_ty.qualifiers()))

 1182|      3|                Some(rhs_ty) // Function decays to pointer to function (unqualified)
 1183|     60|            } else if rhs_ty.is_pointer() {
 1184|     59|                self.registry.get_pointee(rhs_ty.ty())
 1185|       |            } else {
 1186|      1|                None

                                      ^66

                                                                             ^63

 1222|    949|    fn record_implicit_conversions(&mut self, lhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ty: QualType, rhs_ref: NodeRef) {
 1223|    949|        let mut current_rhs_ty = rhs_ty;
 1224|       |
 1225|       |        // 1. Null pointer constant conversion (0 or (void*)0 -> T*)
 1226|    949|        if lhs_ty.is_pointer() && self.is_null_pointer_constant(rhs_ref) {
                                                ^110 ^110                     ^110

 1229|      7|                self.push_conversion(
 1230|      7|                    rhs_ref,
 1231|      7|                    Conversion::PointerCast {
 1232|      7|                        from: self.registry.type_void_ptr,
 1233|      7|                        to: lhs_ty.ty(),
 1234|      7|                    },
 1235|      7|                );
 1236|      7|            }
                          ^6
 1237|     13|            return;
 1238|    936|        }
 1239|       |
 1240|       |        // 2. Array/Function-to-pointer decay
 1241|    936|        if lhs_ty.is_pointer() && (current_rhs_ty.is_array() || current_rhs_ty.is_function()) {
                                                 ^97            ^97           ^93            ^93
 1242|      7|            current_rhs_ty = self.registry.decay(current_rhs_ty, TypeQualifiers::empty());
 1243|      7|            self.push_conversion(
 1244|      7|                rhs_ref,

 1246|      7|                    to: current_rhs_ty.ty(),

 1248|      7|            );

                                                               ^681

                                       ^425

 1267|       |                | NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::Float { .. })

                                                                         ^814           ^814

                                                                   ^97            ^97

 1698|     42|    fn visit_index_access(&mut self, arr_ref: NodeRef, idx_ref: NodeRef) -> Option<QualType> {
 1699|     42|        let arr_ty = self.visit_node(arr_ref)?;
                                                           ^0
 1700|     42|        let idx_ty = self.visit_node(idx_ref)?;
                                                           ^0
 1701|       |
 1702|       |        // Handle both arr[idx] and idx[arr] (subscripting is commutative in C)
 1703|       |        // One must be a pointer/array, the other must be an integer
 1704|     42|        let (sequence_ty, _index_ty) = if arr_ty.is_pointer() || arr_ty.is_array() {
                                                                               ^41    ^41
 1705|     40|            (arr_ty, idx_ty)
 1706|      2|        } else if idx_ty.is_pointer() || idx_ty.is_array() {
                                                       ^1     ^1
 1707|      2|            (idx_ty, arr_ty)
 1708|       |        } else {
 1709|       |            // Neither is a pointer/array, this is an error - but visit_node already processed both
 1710|      0|            return None;
 1711|       |        };
 1712|       |
 1713|     42|        if sequence_ty.is_array() {

 1923|      9|    fn visit_switch_statement(&mut self, cond: NodeRef, body: NodeRef) -> Option<QualType> {
 1924|      9|        let cond_ty = self.visit_node(cond);
 1925|      9|        self.switch_depth += 1;
 1926|      9|        self.switch_cases.push(HashSet::new());
 1927|      9|        self.switch_default_seen.push(false);
 1928|       |
 1929|      9|        if let Some(ty) = cond_ty {
 1930|      9|            let effective_ty = if !ty.is_integer() {
 1931|      1|                self.report_error(
 1932|      1|                    self.ast.get_span(cond),
 1933|      1|                    SemanticErrorKind::InvalidSwitchCondition {
 1934|      1|                        ty: self.registry.display_qual_type(ty),
 1935|      1|                    },
 1936|       |                );
 1937|      1|                QualType::unqualified(self.registry.type_int)
 1938|       |            } else {
 1939|      8|                ty
 1940|       |            };
 1941|      9|            self.switch_orig_cond_types.push(effective_ty);
 1942|      9|            let promoted = self.apply_and_record_integer_promotion(cond, effective_ty);
 1943|      9|            self.switch_cond_types.push(promoted);
 1944|      0|        } else {
 1945|      0|            let int_ty = QualType::unqualified(self.registry.type_int);
 1946|      0|            self.switch_orig_cond_types.push(int_ty);
 1947|      0|            self.switch_cond_types.push(int_ty);
 1948|      0|        }
 1949|       |
 1950|      9|        self.visit_node(body);
 1951|       |

 1953|      9|        self.switch_orig_cond_types.pop();

 1955|      9|        self.switch_cases.pop();
 1956|      9|        self.switch_depth -= 1;
 1957|      9|        None
 1958|      9|    }

 2206|      7|    fn visit_ternary_op(&mut self, cond: NodeRef, then: NodeRef, else_expr: NodeRef) -> Option<QualType> {
 2207|      7|        self.visit_node(cond);
 2208|      7|        let then_ty = self.visit_node(then);
 2209|      7|        let else_ty = self.visit_node(else_expr);
 2210|       |
 2211|      7|        if let (Some(mut t), Some(mut e)) = (then_ty, else_ty) {
 2212|       |            // C11 6.5.15p5: If both the second and third operands have arithmetic type, the result type that would be determined by the usual arithmetic conversions...
 2213|       |            // But array-to-pointer decay happens before that (lvalue conversion).
 2214|       |
 2215|      7|            if t.is_array() || t.is_function() {
 2216|      0|                let decayed = self.registry.decay(t, TypeQualifiers::empty());
 2217|      0|                self.push_conversion(then, Conversion::PointerDecay { to: decayed.ty() });
 2218|      0|                t = decayed;
 2219|      7|            }
 2220|       |
 2221|      7|            if e.is_array() || e.is_function() {
 2222|      0|                let decayed = self.registry.decay(e, TypeQualifiers::empty());
 2223|      0|                self.push_conversion(else_expr, Conversion::PointerDecay { to: decayed.ty() });
 2224|      0|                e = decayed;
 2225|      7|            }
 2226|       |
 2227|      7|            let result_ty = match (t, e) {
 2228|      7|                (t, e) if t.is_arithmetic() && e.is_arithmetic() => usual_arithmetic_conversions(self.registry, t, e),
                               ^2 ^2                         ^5^5                 ^2                           ^2             ^2 ^2
 2229|      5|                (t, e) if t.ty() == e.ty() => Some(t),
                               ^0 ^0                        ^0
 2230|      5|                (t, _) if t.ty() == self.registry.type_void => Some(t),
                               ^0                                            ^0
 2231|      5|                (_, e) if e.ty() == self.registry.type_void => Some(e),
                                  ^1                                         ^1
 2232|      4|                (t, _) if t.is_pointer() && self.is_null_pointer_constant(else_expr) => Some(t),
                               ^2                         ^2   ^2                       ^2            ^2
 2233|      2|                (_, e) if e.is_pointer() && self.is_null_pointer_constant(then) => Some(e),
 2234|      0|                (t, e) if t.is_pointer() && e.is_pointer() => {
 2235|       |                    // C11 6.5.15: pointer to void and pointer to object -> pointer to void
 2236|      0|                    if t.ty() == self.registry.type_void_ptr || e.ty() == self.registry.type_void_ptr {
 2237|      0|                        Some(QualType::unqualified(self.registry.type_void_ptr))
 2238|       |                    } else {
 2239|       |                        // Should check compatibility, for now just use one or common
 2240|      0|                        Some(t)
 2241|       |                    }
 2242|       |                }
 2243|      0|                _ => usual_arithmetic_conversions(self.registry, t, e),
 2244|       |            };
 2245|       |
 2246|      7|            if let Some(res) = result_ty {
 2247|       |                // Don't record conversions for void types
 2248|      7|                if !res.is_void() {
 2249|      6|                    self.record_implicit_conversions(res, t, then);
 2250|      6|                    self.record_implicit_conversions(res, e, else_expr);
 2251|      6|                }
                              ^1
 2252|      7|                return Some(res);
 2253|      0|            }
 2254|       |
 2255|      0|            let t_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(t);
 2256|      0|            let e_str = self.registry.display_qual_type(e);
 2257|      0|            self.report_error(
 2258|      0|                self.ast.get_span(cond),
 2259|      0|                SemanticErrorKind::TypeMismatch {
 2260|      0|                    expected: format!("compatible types for ternary operator ({} and {})", t_str, e_str),
 2261|      0|                    found: "incompatible types".to_string(),
 2262|      0|                },
 2263|       |            );
 2264|      0|            None
 2265|       |        } else {
 2266|      0|            None
 2267|       |        }
 2268|      7|    }

 2832|     40|    fn compute_offsetof_recursive(&mut self, node_ref: NodeRef, current_ty: &mut QualType, offset: &mut i64) -> bool {
 2833|     40|        let kind = *self.ast.get_kind(node_ref);
 2834|     40|        match kind {
 2835|     16|            NodeKind::Dummy => true,
 2836|     20|            NodeKind::MemberAccess(base, member_name, is_arrow) => {
 2837|     20|                debug_assert!(!is_arrow, "offsetof does not support arrow operator");
                                                       ^0
 2838|       |
 2839|     20|                if !self.compute_offsetof_recursive(base, current_ty, offset) {
 2840|      0|                    return false;
 2841|     20|                }
 2842|       |
 2843|     20|                let record_ty = current_ty.ty();
 2844|       |
 2845|     20|                if !record_ty.is_record() {

 2848|      2|                        SemanticErrorKind::MemberAccessOnNonRecord {
 2849|      2|                            ty: self.registry.display_qual_type(QualType::unqualified(record_ty)),
 2850|      2|                        },
 2851|       |                    );
 2852|      2|                    return false;
 2853|     18|                }
 2854|       |

/app/src/semantic/const_eval.rs:
    1|       |//! Constant expression evaluation
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This module provides the functionality to evaluate constant expressions
    4|       |//! at compile time, as required by the C11 standard for contexts like
    5|       |//! static assertions and array sizes.
    6|       |
    7|       |use crate::ast::{Ast, BinaryOp, NodeKind, NodeRef, UnaryOp, literal};
    8|       |use crate::semantic::{QualType, SemanticInfo, SymbolKind, SymbolTable, TypeRegistry};
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Context for constant expression evaluation
   11|       |pub(crate) struct ConstEvalCtx<'a> {
   12|       |    pub(crate) ast: &'a Ast,
   13|       |    pub(crate) symbol_table: &'a SymbolTable,
   14|       |    pub(crate) registry: &'a TypeRegistry,
   15|       |    pub(crate) semantic_info: Option<&'a SemanticInfo>,
   16|       |}
   17|       |
   18|       |impl<'a> ConstEvalCtx<'a> {
   19|    108|    fn get_resolved_type(&self, node_ref: NodeRef) -> Option<QualType> {

                                  ^105

   25|    108|    }
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Evaluate a constant expression node to an i64 value
   29|  13.9k|pub(crate) fn eval_const_expr(ctx: &ConstEvalCtx, expr_node_ref: NodeRef) -> Option<i64> {
   30|  13.9k|    let node_kind = ctx.ast.get_kind(expr_node_ref);
   31|    905|    match node_kind {
   32|    868|        NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::Int { val, .. }) => Some(*val),
   33|      3|        NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::Char(val)) => Some(*val as i64),
   34|    291|        NodeKind::Ident(_, sym_ref) => {
   35|    291|            let symbol = ctx.symbol_table.get_symbol(*sym_ref);
   36|    291|            if let SymbolKind::EnumConstant { value } = &symbol.kind {
                                                            ^6
   37|      6|                Some(*value)
   38|       |            } else {
   39|    285|                None
   40|       |            }
   41|       |        }
   42|    261|        NodeKind::BinaryOp(op, left_ref, right_ref) => {
   43|    261|            let left_val = eval_const_expr(ctx, *left_ref)?;
                              ^92                                       ^169
   44|       |
   45|       |            // Short-circuiting logic
   46|     92|            match op {
   47|       |                BinaryOp::LogicAnd => {
   48|      4|                    if left_val == 0 {
   49|      1|                        return Some(0);
   50|      3|                    }
   51|      3|                    let right_val = eval_const_expr(ctx, *right_ref)?;
                                                                                  ^0
   52|      3|                    return Some((right_val != 0) as i64);
   53|       |                }
   54|       |                BinaryOp::LogicOr => {
   55|      5|                    if left_val != 0 {
   56|      3|                        return Some(1);
   57|      2|                    }
   58|      2|                    let right_val = eval_const_expr(ctx, *right_ref)?;
                                                                                  ^0
   59|      2|                    return Some((right_val != 0) as i64);
   60|       |                }
   61|     83|                _ => {}
   62|       |            }
   63|       |
   64|     83|            let right_val = eval_const_expr(ctx, *right_ref)?;
                              ^82                                         ^1
   65|       |
   66|       |            // Determine if operation should be unsigned
   67|     82|            let (is_unsigned, is_unsigned_cmp) = if let Some(left_ty) = ctx.get_resolved_type(*left_ref) {
                                                                           ^79

   69|     79|                let is_unsigned = !ty_obj.is_signed() && ty_obj.is_int();
                                                                       ^36

                                                           ^43
   71|       |            } else {
   72|      3|                (false, false)
   73|       |            };
   74|       |
   75|     82|            match op {
   76|      7|                BinaryOp::Add => Some(left_val.wrapping_add(right_val)),
   77|      6|                BinaryOp::Sub => Some(left_val.wrapping_sub(right_val)),
   78|      8|                BinaryOp::Mul => Some(left_val.wrapping_mul(right_val)),
   79|       |                BinaryOp::Div => {
   80|      4|                    if right_val == 0 {
   81|      1|                        None
   82|      3|                    } else if is_unsigned {
   83|      2|                        Some((left_val as u64).wrapping_div(right_val as u64) as i64)
   84|       |                    } else {
   85|      1|                        Some(left_val.wrapping_div(right_val))
   86|       |                    }
   87|       |                }
   88|       |                BinaryOp::Mod => {
   89|      2|                    if right_val == 0 {
   90|      0|                        None
   91|      2|                    } else if is_unsigned {
   92|      1|                        Some((left_val as u64).wrapping_rem(right_val as u64) as i64)
   93|       |                    } else {
   94|      1|                        Some(left_val.wrapping_rem(right_val))
   95|       |                    }
   96|       |                }
   97|     14|                BinaryOp::Equal => Some((left_val == right_val) as i64),
   98|     20|                BinaryOp::NotEqual => Some((left_val != right_val) as i64),
   99|       |                BinaryOp::Less => {
  100|      8|                    if is_unsigned_cmp {
  101|      5|                        Some(((left_val as u64) < (right_val as u64)) as i64)
  102|       |                    } else {

  104|       |                    }

  109|       |                    } else {

  111|       |                    }
  112|       |                }

  116|       |                    } else {

  118|       |                    }
  119|       |                }
  120|       |                BinaryOp::GreaterEqual => {
  121|      2|                    if is_unsigned_cmp {
  122|      0|                        Some(((left_val as u64) >= (right_val as u64)) as i64)
  123|       |                    } else {
  124|      2|                        Some((left_val >= right_val) as i64)
  125|       |                    }
  126|       |                }
  127|       |                // LogicAnd/LogicOr handled above
  128|      1|                BinaryOp::BitOr => Some(left_val | right_val),
  129|      1|                BinaryOp::BitAnd => Some(left_val & right_val),
  130|      1|                BinaryOp::BitXor => Some(left_val ^ right_val),

  257|      1|fn eval_offsetof(ctx: &ConstEvalCtx, ty: QualType, expr_ref: NodeRef) -> Option<i64> {
  258|      1|    let mut current_ty = ty;
  259|      1|    let mut offset = 0i64;
  260|       |
  261|      2|    fn walk(ctx: &ConstEvalCtx, node_ref: NodeRef, current_ty: &mut QualType, offset: &mut i64) -> bool {
  262|      2|        match *ctx.ast.get_kind(node_ref) {
  263|      1|            NodeKind::Dummy => true,
  264|      1|            NodeKind::MemberAccess(base, member_name, is_arrow) => {
  265|      1|                debug_assert!(!is_arrow, "offsetof does not support arrow operator");
                                                       ^0
  266|       |
  267|      1|                if !walk(ctx, base, current_ty, offset) {
  268|      0|                    return false;
  269|      1|                }
  270|       |
  271|      1|                let record_ty = current_ty.ty();
  272|       |
  273|      1|                if !record_ty.is_record() {
  274|      0|                    return false;
  275|      1|                }
  276|       |
  277|      1|                let mut flat_members = Vec::new();
  278|      1|                let mut flat_offsets = Vec::new();
  279|      1|                let ty_obj = ctx.registry.get(record_ty);
  280|      1|                ty_obj.flatten_members_with_layouts(ctx.registry, &mut flat_members, &mut flat_offsets, 0);
  281|       |
  282|      2|                if let Some(idx) = flat_members.iter().position(|m| m.name == Some(member_name)) {
                                          ^1     ^1                  ^1
  283|      1|                    *offset += flat_offsets[idx] as i64;
  284|      1|                    *current_ty = flat_members[idx].member_type;
  285|      1|                    true
  286|       |                } else {
  287|      0|                    false
  288|       |                }
  289|       |            }
  290|      0|            NodeKind::IndexAccess(base, index) => {
  291|      0|                if !walk(ctx, base, current_ty, offset) {
  292|      0|                    return false;
  293|      0|                }
  294|       |
  295|      0|                let Some(elem_ty) = ctx.registry.get_array_element(current_ty.ty()) else {
  296|      0|                    return false;
  297|       |                };
  298|      0|                let Some(index_val) = eval_const_expr(ctx, index) else {
  299|      0|                    return false;
  300|       |                };
  301|       |
  302|      0|                let layout = ctx.registry.get_layout(elem_ty);
  303|      0|                *offset += index_val * (layout.size as i64);
  304|      0|                *current_ty = QualType::unqualified(elem_ty);
  305|      0|                true
  306|       |            }
  307|      0|            _ => false,
  308|       |        }
  309|      2|    }
  310|       |
  311|      1|    if walk(ctx, expr_ref, &mut current_ty, &mut offset) {
  312|      1|        Some(offset)
  313|       |    } else {
  314|      0|        None
  315|       |    }
  316|      1|}

    6|       |/// Performs the "usual arithmetic conversions" as specified in C11 6.3.1.8.
    7|    216|pub(crate) fn usual_arithmetic_conversions(ctx: &mut TypeRegistry, lhs: QualType, rhs: QualType) -> Option<QualType> {
    8|    216|    let lt = lhs.ty();
    9|    216|    let rt = rhs.ty();
   10|       |
   11|    216|    if lt.is_floating() || rt.is_floating() {
                                         ^190^190
   12|     52|        let get_real_type = |registry: &TypeRegistry, ty: TypeRef| {
                          ^26
   13|     52|            if ty.is_complex() {
   14|     35|                match &registry.get(ty).kind {
   15|     35|                    TypeKind::Complex { base_type } => Some(*base_type),
   16|      0|                    _ => unreachable!("ICE: Complex type has non-complex kind"),
   17|       |                }
   18|       |            } else {
   19|     17|                Some(ty)
   20|       |            }
   21|     52|        };
   22|       |
   23|     26|        let lr = get_real_type(ctx, lt)?;
                                                     ^0
   24|     26|        let rr = get_real_type(ctx, rt)?;
                                                     ^0
   25|       |
   26|     26|        let lb = lr.builtin();
   27|     26|        let rb = rr.builtin();
   28|       |
   29|     26|        let common_real = match (lb, rb) {
   30|      1|            (Some(BuiltinType::LongDouble), _) | (_, Some(BuiltinType::LongDouble)) => ctx.type_long_double,
   31|      9|            (Some(BuiltinType::Double), _) | (_, Some(BuiltinType::Double)) => ctx.type_double,
   32|     16|            _ => ctx.type_float,
   33|       |        };
   34|       |
   35|     26|        if lt.is_complex() || rt.is_complex() {
                                            ^8 ^8
   36|     19|            return Some(QualType::unqualified(ctx.complex_type(common_real)));
   37|       |        } else {
   38|      7|            return Some(QualType::unqualified(common_real));
   39|       |        }
   40|    190|    }
   41|       |
   42|       |    // Both types are promoted to at least 'int' or 'unsigned int'
   43|    190|    let lp = integer_promotion(ctx, lhs);
   44|    190|    let rp = integer_promotion(ctx, rhs);
   45|    190|    if lp == rp {
   46|    148|        return Some(lp);
   47|     42|    }
   48|       |
   49|     42|    let lbp = lp.ty().builtin()?;
                                             ^0
   50|     42|    let rbp = rp.ty().builtin()?;
                                             ^0
   51|     42|    let (ls, lr) = (lbp.is_signed(), lbp.rank());
   52|     42|    let (rs, rr) = (rbp.is_signed(), rbp.rank());
   53|       |
   54|       |    // Same signedness: higher rank wins
   55|     42|    if ls == rs {
   56|      5|        return Some(if lr >= rr { lp } else { rp });
                                                ^3          ^2
   57|     37|    }
   58|       |
   59|       |    // Different signedness: Higher rank usually wins; if ranks are equal, unsigned wins.
   60|     37|    let (ut, ur, st, sr) = if ls { (rp, rr, lp, lr) } else { (lp, lr, rp, rr) };
                                                 ^7^7  ^7                   ^30^30 ^30
   61|     37|    if ur >= sr {
   62|     37|        Some(ut)
   63|       |    } else {
   64|       |        // If the signed type can represent all values of the unsigned type, it wins.
   65|       |        // For currently supported targets (e.g. 32-bit int, 64-bit long), this holds.
   66|      0|        Some(st)
   67|       |    }

   19|    155|pub(crate) fn parse_string_literal(name: NameId) -> ParsedStringLiteral {

   26|    143|    } else if let Some(s) = raw.strip_prefix("U\"") {
                                     ^6

   28|    137|    } else if let Some(s) = raw.strip_prefix("u8\"") {

   30|    134|    } else if let Some(s) = raw.strip_prefix("\"") {

/app/src/semantic/lowering.rs:
    1|       |//! SemanticLowering
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Responsibility
    4|       |//! -> VarDecl/RecordDecl/EnumDecl/TypedefDecl, FunctionDef -> Function)
    5|       |//! - Scope Construction

    9|       |//!
   10|       |//! This module implements the semantic lowering phase that bridges the gap between the
   11|       |//! grammar-oriented parser AST and the type-resolved semantic AST (HIR). The lowering
   12|       |//! phase handles all C-style declaration forms

   38|       |/// Recursively apply parsed declarator to base type
   39|    407|fn apply_parsed_declarator(
   40|    407|    current_type: QualType,
   41|    407|    declarator_ref: ParsedDeclRef,
   42|    407|    ctx: &mut LowerCtx,
   43|    407|    span: SourceSpan,
   44|    407|    decl_ctx: DeclaratorContext,

   47|       |
   48|    407|    match declarator_node {
   49|    331|        ParsedDeclaratorNode::Identifier => current_type,
   50|     44|        ParsedDeclaratorNode::Pointer { qualifiers, inner } => {
   51|       |            // Pointer
   52|       |            // Apply Pointer modifier to the current type first (Top-Down)
   53|     44|            let pointer_type = ctx.registry.pointer_to(current_type);
   54|       |            // Pointer type is always compatible with restrict, but we use checked merge anyway for consistency
   55|     44|            let modified_current =
   56|     44|                ctx.merge_qualifiers_with_check(QualType::unqualified(pointer_type), qualifiers, span);
   57|     44|            apply_parsed_declarator(modified_current, inner, ctx, span, decl_ctx)
   58|       |        }
   59|     19|        ParsedDeclaratorNode::Array { size, inner } => {
   60|       |            // Array
   61|       |            // Apply Array modifier to the current type
   62|       |            // Propagate qualifiers from the element type to the array type (C11 6.7.3)
   63|       |
   64|       |            // C11 6.7.6.2p1: qualifiers and static in array declarator only allowed in function parameters
   65|       |            // and only in the outermost array type derivation.
   66|     19|            let has_quals = match &size {
   67|     11|                ParsedArraySize::Expression { qualifiers, .. } => !qualifiers.is_empty(),
   68|      0|                ParsedArraySize::Star { qualifiers } => !qualifiers.is_empty(),
   69|      4|                ParsedArraySize::VlaSpecifier { qualifiers, .. } => !qualifiers.is_empty(),
   70|      4|                ParsedArraySize::Incomplete => false,
   71|       |            };
   72|     19|            let has_static = matches!(size, ParsedArraySize::VlaSpecifier { is_static: true, .. });
                                           ^1
   73|       |
   74|     19|            if has_static || has_quals {
                                           ^18

                                                 ^0
   77|       |
   78|      4|                if !decl_ctx.in_parameter {
   79|      2|                    if has_static {
   80|      1|                        ctx.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::ArrayStaticOutsideParameter);
   81|      1|                    }
   82|      2|                    if has_quals {
   83|      1|                        ctx.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::ArrayQualifierOutsideParameter);
   84|      1|                    }
   85|      2|                } else if !is_outermost {
   86|      0|                    if has_static {
   87|      0|                        ctx.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::ArrayStaticNotOutermost);
   88|      0|                    }
   89|      0|                    if has_quals {
   90|      0|                        ctx.report_error(span, SemanticErrorKind::ArrayQualifierNotOutermost);
   91|      0|                    }

   96|     19|            let array_type_ref = ctx.registry.array_of(current_type.ty(), array_size);
   97|     19|            let qualified_array = ctx
   98|     19|                .registry
   99|     19|                .merge_qualifiers(QualType::unqualified(array_type_ref), current_type.qualifiers());

  101|       |        }

  474|       |/// Convert a ParsedType to a TypeRef
  475|    331|fn convert_to_qual_type(
  476|    331|    ctx: &mut LowerCtx,
  477|    331|    parsed_type: ParsedType,
  478|    331|    span: SourceSpan,
  479|    331|    in_parameter: bool,

  492|       |

  494|    331|        qualified_base,
  495|    331|        declarator_ref,
  496|    331|        ctx,
  497|    331|        span,
  498|    331|        DeclaratorContext { in_parameter },
  499|       |    );

  504|    354|fn resolve_record_tag(

  506|    354|    tag: Option<NameId>,
  507|    354|    is_union: bool,
  508|    354|    is_definition: bool,

  514|       |    };
  515|       |

  535|      0|                    },

  613|       |/// Recursively validates that there are no duplicate member names, descending into anonymous records.

  687|     29|fn choose_enum_type(registry: &TypeRegistry, enumerators: &[EnumConstant]) -> TypeRef {
  688|     29|    if enumerators.is_empty() {
  689|      0|        return registry.type_int;
  690|     29|    }
  691|       |
  692|     29|    let mut min = 0i64;
  693|     29|    let mut max = 0i64;
  694|     29|    let mut first = true;
  695|       |
  696|     78|    for e in enumerators {
                      ^49
  697|     49|        if first {
  698|     29|            min = e.value;
  699|     29|            max = e.value;
  700|     29|            first = false;
  701|     29|        } else {
  702|     20|            if e.value < min {
  703|      1|                min = e.value;
  704|     19|            }
  705|     20|            if e.value > max {
  706|     18|                max = e.value;
  707|     18|            }
                          ^2
  708|       |        }
  709|       |    }
  710|       |
  711|       |    // C11 6.7.2.2p4:
  712|       |    // Each enumerated type shall be compatible with char, a signed integer type, or an unsigned integer type.
  713|       |    // The choice of type is implementation-defined...
  714|       |
  715|       |    // We prioritize unsigned int if all values are non-negative and fit.
  716|       |    // This helps with strict pointer compatibility checks against unsigned int *.
  717|     29|    let uint_max = 4294967295i64;
  718|     29|    if min >= 0 && max <= uint_max {
                                 ^23
  719|     20|        return registry.type_int_unsigned;
  720|      9|    }
  721|       |
  722|       |    // Default to int if it fits (or if min < 0)
  723|      9|    let int_min = -2147483648i64;
  724|      9|    let int_max = 2147483647i64;
  725|      9|    if min >= int_min && max <= int_max {
                                       ^7
  726|      3|        return registry.type_int;
  727|      6|    }
  728|       |
  729|       |    // For larger values, fallback to long long
  730|      6|    if min >= 0 {
  731|      3|        return registry.type_long_long_unsigned;
  732|      3|    }
  733|       |
  734|      3|    registry.type_long_long
  735|     29|}
  736|       |
  737|     29|fn complete_enum_symbol(
  738|     29|    ctx: &mut LowerCtx,
  739|     29|    tag: Option<NameId>,
  740|     29|    type_ref: TypeRef,
  741|     29|    enumerators: Vec<EnumConstant>,
  742|     29|) -> Result<(), SemanticError> {
  743|       |    // Determine the underlying type
  744|     29|    let base_type = choose_enum_type(ctx.registry, &enumerators);
  745|       |
  746|       |    // Update the type in AST and SymbolTable using the proper completion function
  747|     29|    ctx.registry.complete_enum(type_ref, enumerators, base_type);
  748|     29|    ctx.registry.ensure_layout(type_ref)?;
                                                      ^0
  749|       |
  750|     29|    if let Some(tag_name) = tag
                              ^27
  751|     27|        && let Some((entry_ref, _)) = ctx.symbol_table.lookup_tag(tag_name)
  752|       |    {
  753|     27|        let entry = ctx.symbol_table.get_symbol_mut(entry_ref);
  754|     27|        entry.is_completed = true;
  755|     27|        if let SymbolKind::EnumTag { is_complete } = &mut entry.kind {
  756|     27|            *is_complete = true;
  757|     27|        }
                      ^0
  758|      2|    }
  759|     29|    Ok(())

  962|       |/// Merge base types according to C type combination rules
  963|  2.40k|fn merge_base_type(
  964|  2.40k|    existing: Option<QualType>,
  965|  2.40k|    new_type: QualType,
  966|  2.40k|    ctx: &mut LowerCtx,
  967|  2.40k|    span: SourceSpan,
  968|  2.40k|) -> Option<QualType> {
  969|  2.40k|    match existing {
  970|  2.26k|        None => Some(new_type),
  971|    146|        Some(existing_ref) => {
  972|    146|            let existing_type = ctx.registry.get(existing_ref.ty());
  973|    146|            let new_type_info = ctx.registry.get(new_type.ty());
  974|       |
  975|    146|            match (&existing_type.kind, &new_type_info.kind) {
  976|    146|                (TypeKind::Builtin(existing_builtin), TypeKind::Builtin(new_builtin)) => {
  977|    146|                    match (existing_builtin, new_builtin) {
  978|       |                        // 1. Same types (redundancy)
  979|    146|                        (a, b) if a == b => {
                                       ^1 ^1         ^1
  980|       |                            // C99/C11: int int is NOT allowed, but long long is.
  981|       |                            // However, many compilers allow redundant specifiers.
  982|       |                            // In Cendol, we'll allow it if they are identical,
  983|       |                            // EXCEPT for types that already have a combined form (like long long).
  984|      1|                            if *a == BuiltinType::Long {
  985|      1|                                Some(QualType::unqualified(ctx.registry.type_long_long))
  986|       |                            } else {
  987|      0|                                Some(existing_ref)
  988|       |                            }
  989|       |                        }
  990|       |
  991|       |                        // 2. Handle Signed as a modifier
  992|      1|                        (BuiltinType::Signed, BuiltinType::Int) => Some(new_type),
  993|      0|                        (BuiltinType::Int, BuiltinType::Signed) => Some(existing_ref),

 1395|       |/// Apply declarator transformations to a base type
 1396|  3.15k|fn apply_declarator(
 1397|  3.15k|    base_type: QualType,
 1398|  3.15k|    declarator: &ParsedDeclarator,
 1399|  3.15k|    ctx: &mut LowerCtx,
 1400|  3.15k|    span: SourceSpan,
 1401|  3.15k|    spec_info: &DeclSpecInfo,
 1402|  3.15k|    decl_ctx: DeclaratorContext,
 1403|  3.15k|) -> QualType {
 1404|  3.15k|    match declarator {
 1405|    186|        ParsedDeclarator::Pointer(qualifiers, next) => {
 1406|    186|            let ty = ctx.registry.pointer_to(base_type);
 1407|       |            // Checked merge
 1408|    186|            let modified_ty = ctx.merge_qualifiers_with_check(QualType::unqualified(ty), *qualifiers, span);
 1409|    186|            if let Some(next_decl) = next {
 1410|    186|                apply_declarator(modified_ty, next_decl, ctx, span, spec_info, decl_ctx)
 1411|       |            } else {
 1412|      0|                modified_ty
 1413|       |            }
 1414|       |        }
 1415|  2.04k|        ParsedDeclarator::Identifier(_, qualifiers) => ctx.merge_qualifiers_with_check(base_type, *qualifiers, span),
 1416|    154|        ParsedDeclarator::Array(base, size) => {
 1417|       |            // C11 6.7.6.2 Array declarators
 1418|       |            // "The element type shall not be an incomplete or function type."

 2422|    123|            ParsedNodeKind::FunctionCall(func, args) => {
 2423|       |                // Reserve a slot for the FunctionCall node to ensure parent < child index (when necessary)
 2424|       |                // If we have a target slot for the result, we can use it directly?
 2425|       |                // But FunctionCall needs to know ranges of args.
 2426|       |                // The structure is: CallNode -> FuncExpr, Arg1, Arg2...
 2427|       |                // FuncExpr and Args can be anywhere, but Args must be contiguous.

 3009|     21|    fn deduce_array_size_full(&self, init_node: NodeRef, element_type: TypeRef) -> Option<usize> {
 3010|     21|        match self.ast.get_kind(init_node) {
 3011|     11|            NodeKind::InitializerList(list) => {
 3012|       |                // Special case: array of character type initialized by { "string" }
 3013|     11|                if list.init_len > 0 {
 3014|     11|                    let first_item_ref = list.init_start;
 3015|     11|                    if let NodeKind::InitializerItem(item) = self.ast.get_kind(first_item_ref)
 3016|     11|                        && item.designator_len == 0
 3017|     10|                        && let NodeKind::Literal(literal::Literal::String(s)) = self.ast.get_kind(item.initializer)
                                                                                        ^4
 3018|       |                    {
 3019|      4|                        let parsed = crate::semantic::literal_utils::parse_string_literal(*s);
 3020|      4|                        let string_elem_type = match parsed.builtin_type {
 3021|      1|                            BuiltinType::Char => self.registry.type_char,
 3022|      1|                            BuiltinType::Int => self.registry.type_int,
 3023|      1|                            BuiltinType::UShort => self.registry.type_short_unsigned,
 3024|      1|                            BuiltinType::UInt => self.registry.type_int_unsigned,
 3025|      0|                            _ => self.registry.type_char,
 3026|       |                        };
 3027|       |
 3028|      4|                        if self.registry.is_compatible(
 3029|      4|                            QualType::unqualified(element_type),
 3030|      4|                            QualType::unqualified(string_elem_type),
 3031|       |                        ) {
 3032|      4|                            return Some(parsed.size);
 3033|      0|                        }
 3034|      7|                    }
 3035|      0|                }

 3037|      7|                let mut max_index: i64 = -1;
 3038|      7|                let mut current_index: i64 = 0;
 3039|      7|                let eval = |e| const_eval::eval_const_expr(&self.const_ctx(), e);
                                             ^3                          ^3                 ^3

 3042|       |
 3043|     24|                while let Some(item_ref) = iter.peek().copied() {
                                             ^17

 3045|      0|                        iter.next();

 3047|       |                    };

 3056|      0|                                if s_v > e_v {
 3057|      0|                                    return None;

 3060|       |                            }
