 3093|       |
 3094|     19|    fn consume_initializers<I>(
 3095|     19|        &self,
 3096|     19|        element_type: TypeRef,
 3097|     19|        iter: &mut std::iter::Peekable<I>,
 3098|     19|        allow_array_designator: bool,
 3099|     19|    ) where
 3100|     19|        I: Iterator<Item = NodeRef>,
 3101|       |    {
 3102|       |        // Check if there are more items
 3103|     19|        let Some(item_ref) = iter.peek().copied() else {
 3104|      0|            return;
 3105|       |        };
 3106|       |
 3107|     19|        let NodeKind::InitializerItem(init) = self.ast.get_kind(item_ref) else {
 3108|       |            // Should not happen in valid AST
 3109|      0|            return;
 3110|       |        };
 3111|       |
 3112|     19|        if init.designator_len > 0 {
 3113|       |            // Check for array designators
 3114|      3|            match self.ast.get_kind(init.designator_start) {
 3115|       |                NodeKind::Designator(Designator::ArrayIndex(_))
 3116|       |                | NodeKind::Designator(Designator::GnuArrayRange(_, _)) => {
 3117|      3|                    if !allow_array_designator {
 3118|      0|                        return;
 3119|      3|                    }
 3120|       |                    // If allowed, we proceed to consume this item (and potentially others)
 3121|       |                }
 3122|       |                _ => {
 3123|       |                    // Field designator.
 3124|       |                    // For now, we assume field designators consume one item from the list.
 3125|       |                    // (Strictly we should check if it belongs to current struct, but for size counting
 3126|       |                    // we assume valid C code).
 3127|      0|                    iter.next();
 3128|      0|                    return;
 3129|       |                }
 3130|       |            }
 3131|     16|        }
 3132|       |
 3133|       |        // Check braced initializer
 3134|     19|        if let NodeKind::InitializerList(_) = self.ast.get_kind(init.initializer) {
 3135|      2|            iter.next();
 3136|      2|            return;
 3137|     17|        }
 3138|       |
 3139|       |        // Brace elision logic
 3140|     17|        let type_kind = &self.registry.get(element_type).kind;
 3141|     17|        match type_kind {
 3142|      2|            TypeKind::Record { members, .. } => {
 3143|      2|                let mut is_first_member = true;
 3144|      2|                for member in members.iter() {
 3145|      2|                    let allow = allow_array_designator && is_first_member;
 3146|      2|                    self.consume_initializers(member.member_type.ty(), iter, allow);
 3147|      2|                    is_first_member = false;
 3148|       |
 3149|       |                    // If we stopped consuming, we should also stop for this record

   29|       |/// Represents a resolved symbol entry from the symbol table.
   30|       |/// This structure is typically populated during the semantic analysis phase.
   31|       |/// Symbol are stored in a separate Vec<Symbol> with SymbolRef references.
   32|       |/// invariant:
   33|       |/// - Variable / Typedef / Function: type_info is meaningful
   34|       |/// - EnumConstant: type_info = int (unqualified)
   35|       |/// - Label / RecordTag / EnumTag: type_info = TypeKind::Error
   36|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   37|       |pub struct Symbol {
   38|       |    pub name: NameId,
   39|       |    pub kind: SymbolKind, // e.g., Variable, Function, Typedef
   40|       |    pub type_info: QualType,
   41|       |    pub scope_id: ScopeId, // Reference to the scope where it's defined
   42|       |    pub def_span: SourceSpan,
   43|       |    pub def_state: DefinitionState,
   44|       |    pub is_completed: bool,
   45|       |}

  146|       |        // A medium-sized C translation unit can easily have hundreds of symbols and multiple scopes.
  147|    780|        let mut table = SymbolTable {

  160|    667|    pub(crate) fn push_scope(&mut self) -> ScopeId {
  161|    667|        let new_scope_id = ScopeId::new(self.next_scope_id).unwrap();
  162|    667|        self.next_scope_id += 1;
  163|       |

  167|    667|            ..Default::default()

  175|       |        );

  177|    667|    }

  179|    667|    pub(crate) fn pop_scope(&mut self) -> Option<ScopeId> {
  180|    667|        let current_scope_id_before_pop = self.current_scope_id;
  181|    667|        let current_scope = &self.scopes[current_scope_id_before_pop.get() as usize - 1];
  182|    667|        if let Some(parent) = current_scope.parent {
  183|    667|            self.current_scope_id = parent;
  184|    667|            debug!(
  185|      0|                "SymbolTable: Popped scope. Old current_scope_id: {}, New current_scope_id: {}",
  186|      0|                current_scope_id_before_pop.get(),
  187|      0|                self.current_scope_id.get()
  188|       |            );
  189|    667|            Some(parent)
  190|       |        } else {
  191|      0|            debug!("SymbolTable: Attempted to pop global scope. No change.");
  192|      0|            None
  193|       |        }
  194|    667|    }

  239|  4.72k|    pub(crate) fn lookup(&self, name: NameId, start_scope: ScopeId, ns: Namespace) -> Option<(SymbolRef, ScopeId)> {
  240|  4.72k|        let mut scope_id = start_scope;

  478|       |    /// Merge a new symbol entry with an existing one in the global scope.
  479|       |    /// This implements C11 6.9.2 for handling tentative definitions, extern declarations, and actual definitions.
  480|    958|    fn merge_global_symbol(&mut self, name: NameId, mut new_entry: Symbol) -> Result<SymbolRef, SymbolTableError> {
  481|    958|        let global_scope = ScopeId::GLOBAL;
  482|       |
  483|       |        // Check if symbol already exists in global scope
  484|    958|        if let Some(existing_ref) = self.fetch(name, global_scope, Namespace::Ordinary) {
                                  ^36
  485|     36|            let existing = self.get_symbol_mut(existing_ref);
  486|       |
  487|       |            // Verify kinds match
  488|     36|            match (&existing.kind, &new_entry.kind) {
  489|     13|                (SymbolKind::Variable { .. }, SymbolKind::Variable { .. }) => {}
  490|     21|                (SymbolKind::Function { .. }, SymbolKind::Function { .. }) => {}
  491|       |                _ => {
  492|       |                    // Mismatched kinds
  493|      2|                    debug!("Symbol '{}' redefinition: different kinds", name);
                                         ^0
  494|      2|                    return Err(SymbolTableError::InvalidRedefinition {
  495|      2|                        name,
  496|      2|                        existing: existing_ref,
  497|      2|                    });
  498|       |                }
  499|       |            }
  500|       |
  501|       |            // Check alignment compatibility (Variables only)
  502|       |            if let SymbolKind::Variable {
  503|     13|                alignment: new_align, ..
  504|     34|            } = &new_entry.kind
  505|       |                && let SymbolKind::Variable {
  506|     13|                    alignment: existing_align,
  507|       |                    ..
  508|     13|                } = &existing.kind
  509|       |            {
  510|     13|                match (existing_align, new_align) {
  511|      0|                    (Some(a), Some(b)) if a != b => {
  512|      0|                        return Err(SymbolTableError::InvalidRedefinition {
  513|      0|                            name,
  514|      0|                            existing: existing_ref,
  515|      0|                        });
  516|       |                    }
  517|      0|                    (None, Some(b)) => {
  518|       |                        // Inherit alignment from new declaration
  519|      0|                        if let SymbolKind::Variable { alignment, .. } = &mut existing.kind {
  520|      0|                            *alignment = Some(*b);
  521|      0|                        }
  522|       |                    }
  523|     13|                    _ => {}
  524|       |                }
  525|     21|            }
  526|       |
  527|       |            // Apply C11 merging rules
  528|     34|            match (existing.def_state, new_entry.def_state) {
  529|       |                (DefinitionState::Defined, DefinitionState::Defined) => {
  530|       |                    // Multiple actual definitions - error
  531|      2|                    debug!("Multiple definitions of '{}'", name);
                                         ^0
  532|      2|                    return Err(SymbolTableError::InvalidRedefinition {
  533|      2|                        name,
  534|      2|                        existing: existing_ref,
  535|      2|                    });
  536|       |                }
  537|       |
  538|       |                (DefinitionState::Defined, _) => {
  539|       |                    // Already defined, ignore new declaration/tentative definition
  540|      1|                    debug!("Ignoring redundant declaration for already-defined '{}'", name);
                                         ^0
  541|       |                }
  542|       |
  543|       |                (_, DefinitionState::Defined) => {
  544|       |                    // Upgrade to defined
  545|     14|                    debug!("Upgrading to defined for '{}'", name);
                                         ^0
  546|     14|                    existing.def_state = DefinitionState::Defined;
  547|     14|                    if let SymbolKind::Variable { initializer, .. } = &mut new_entry.kind
                                                                ^1
  548|       |                        && let SymbolKind::Variable {
  549|      1|                            initializer: existing_init,
  550|       |                            ..
  551|      1|                        } = &mut existing.kind
  552|      1|                    {
  553|      1|                        *existing_init = *initializer;
  554|     13|                    }
  555|       |                }
  556|       |
  557|       |                (DefinitionState::Tentative, DefinitionState::Tentative)
  558|       |                | (DefinitionState::Tentative, DefinitionState::DeclaredOnly)
  559|       |                | (DefinitionState::DeclaredOnly, DefinitionState::DeclaredOnly) => {
  560|       |                    // No change to def_state
  561|     15|                    debug!("Merging similar or weaker definition for '{}'", name);
                                         ^0
  562|       |                }
  563|       |
  564|       |                (DefinitionState::DeclaredOnly, DefinitionState::Tentative) => {
  565|       |                    // Upgrade to tentative
  566|      2|                    debug!("Upgrading extern declaration to tentative for '{}'", name);
                                         ^0
  567|      2|                    existing.def_state = DefinitionState::Tentative;
  568|       |                }
  569|       |            }
  570|       |
  571|     32|            Ok(existing_ref)
  572|       |        } else {
  573|       |            // Symbol doesn't exist, add it
  574|    922|            debug!(
  575|      0|                "Adding new global symbol '{}' with def_state {:?}",
  576|       |                name, new_entry.def_state
  577|       |            );
  578|    922|            Ok(self.add_symbol(name, new_entry))
  579|       |        }
  580|    958|    }

  122|    787|    fn create_builtin(&mut self) {
  123|       |        // Reset types to just dummy to ensure order
  124|    787|        self.types.truncate(1);
  125|       |
  126|       |        // Must match BuiltinType enum values 1..16 sequentially
  127|       |
  128|       |        // 1: Void
  129|    787|        self.type_void = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::Void));
  130|       |
  131|       |        // 2: Bool
  132|    787|        self.type_bool = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::Bool));
  133|       |
  134|       |        // 3: Char (signed)
  135|    787|        self.type_char = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::Char));
  136|       |
  137|       |        // 4: SChar (explicit signed char)
  138|    787|        self.type_schar = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::SChar));
  139|       |
  140|       |        // 5: UChar
  141|    787|        self.type_char_unsigned = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::UChar));
  142|       |
  143|       |        // 6: Short
  144|    787|        self.type_short = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::Short));
  145|       |
  146|       |        // 7: UShort
  147|    787|        self.type_short_unsigned = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::UShort));
  148|       |
  149|       |        // 8: Int
  150|    787|        self.type_int = self.alloc_builtin(TypeKind::Builtin(BuiltinType::Int));

  338|    686|    fn reconstruct_pointee(&self, r: TypeRef) -> TypeRef {
  339|    686|        debug_assert!(r.is_inline_pointer());
  340|    686|        let depth = r.pointer_depth();
  341|    686|        if depth > 1 {
  342|       |            // Decrement depth
  343|      3|            TypeRef::new(r.base(), r.class(), depth - 1, 0).unwrap()
  344|       |        } else {
  345|       |            // Depth becomes 0. Class becomes Class of Base.
  346|       |            // Look up base in registry.
  347|    683|            let base_idx = r.base();
  348|    683|            let base_type = &self.types[base_idx as usize];
  349|    683|            let base_class = base_type.kind.to_class();
  350|    683|            TypeRef::new(base_idx, base_class, 0, 0).unwrap()
  351|       |        }
  352|    686|    }
  353|       |
  354|  1.53k|    fn reconstruct_element(&self, r: TypeRef) -> TypeRef {
  355|  1.53k|        debug_assert!(r.is_inline_array());
  356|       |        // Array becomes non-array (arr=0). Class becomes Class of Base.
  357|  1.53k|        let base_idx = r.base();
  358|  1.53k|        let base_type = &self.types[base_idx as usize];
  359|  1.53k|        let base_class = base_type.kind.to_class();
  360|  1.53k|        TypeRef::new(base_idx, base_class, 0, 0).unwrap()
  361|  1.53k|    }

  366|  1.33k|    pub(crate) fn pointer_to(&mut self, base: QualType) -> TypeRef {
  367|       |        // Try inline if unqualified
  368|  1.33k|        if base.qualifiers().is_empty() {
  369|  1.26k|            let base_ty = base.ty();
  370|       |            // 1. If base is Inline Pointer (depth 1..2), we can increment depth (max 3).
  371|  1.26k|            if base_ty.is_inline_pointer() {
  372|     11|                let depth = base_ty.pointer_depth();
  373|     11|                if depth < 3 {
  374|     10|                    return TypeRef::new(base_ty.base(), TypeClass::Pointer, depth + 1, 0).unwrap();
  375|      1|                }
  376|  1.25k|            }
  377|       |
  378|       |            // 2. If base is Simple (Ptr=0, Arr=0), we can make Inline Pointer depth 1.
  379|  1.25k|            if base_ty.pointer_depth() == 0 && base_ty.array_len().is_none() {
                                                             ^1.25k              ^1.25k
  380|  1.22k|                return TypeRef::new(base_ty.base(), TypeClass::Pointer, 1, 0).unwrap();
  381|     22|            }
  382|     75|        }
  383|       |
  384|       |        // Fallback to Registry
  385|     97|        if let Some(&ptr) = self.pointer_cache.get(&base) {
                                   ^28
  386|     28|            return ptr;
  387|     69|        }
  388|       |
  389|     69|        let ptr = self.alloc(Type::new(TypeKind::Pointer { pointee: base }));
  390|     69|        self.pointer_cache.insert(base, ptr);
  391|     69|        ptr
  392|  1.33k|    }

  518|  1.63k|    pub(crate) fn get_layout(&self, ty: TypeRef) -> Cow<'_, TypeLayout> {
  519|  1.63k|        if ty.is_inline_pointer() {
  520|     10|            return Cow::Owned(TypeLayout {
  521|     10|                size: 8,
  522|     10|                alignment: 8,
  523|     10|                kind: LayoutKind::Scalar,
  524|     10|            });
  525|  1.62k|        }
  526|       |
  527|  1.62k|        if ty.is_inline_array() {
  528|    120|            let elem = self.reconstruct_element(ty);
  529|    120|            let elem_layout = self.get_layout(elem);
  530|    120|            let len = ty.array_len().unwrap() as u64;
  531|    120|            return Cow::Owned(TypeLayout {
  532|    120|                size: elem_layout.size * len, // Potential overflow if not careful, but C rules apply
  533|    120|                alignment: elem_layout.alignment,
  534|    120|                kind: LayoutKind::Array { element: elem, len },
  535|    120|            });
  536|  1.50k|        }
  537|       |
  538|  1.50k|        let idx = ty.index();
  539|  1.50k|        match self.types[idx].layout.as_ref() {
  540|  1.50k|            Some(x) => Cow::Borrowed(x),
  541|      0|            None => panic!("ICE: TypeRef {ty} layout not computed. make sure layout is computed in previous phase"),
  542|       |        }
  543|  1.63k|    }

  545|    104|    pub(crate) fn get_array_layout(&self, ty: TypeRef) -> (u64, u64, TypeRef, u64) {

  553|  21.1k|    pub(crate) fn ensure_layout(&mut self, ty: TypeRef) -> Result<Cow<'_, TypeLayout>, SemanticError> {
  554|  21.1k|        if ty.is_inline_pointer() {

  559|    914|            }));
  560|  20.2k|        }
  561|       |
  562|  20.2k|        if ty.is_inline_array() {
  563|       |            // Recursive check
  564|    869|            let elem = self.reconstruct_element(ty);
  565|    869|            let elem_layout = self.ensure_layout(elem)?; // returns Cow
                              ^868                                  ^1
  566|    868|            let len = ty.array_len().unwrap() as u64;
  567|    868|            let size = elem_layout.size * len;
  568|       |
  569|    868|            return Ok(Cow::Owned(TypeLayout {
  570|    868|                size,
  571|    868|                alignment: elem_layout.alignment,
  572|    868|                kind: LayoutKind::Array { element: elem, len },
  573|    868|            }));
  574|  19.3k|        }
  575|       |

                          ^13.6k                          ^1.62k
  582|  13.6k|        self.types[idx].layout = Some(layout);
  583|       |
  584|  13.6k|        Ok(Cow::Borrowed(self.types[idx].layout.as_ref().unwrap()))
  585|  21.1k|    }

  587|  15.2k|    fn compute_layout(&mut self, ty: TypeRef) -> Result<TypeLayout, SemanticError> {
  588|  15.2k|        if self.layout_in_progress.contains(&ty) {
  589|      1|            return Err(SemanticError {
  590|      1|                span: SourceSpan::dummy(),
  591|      1|                kind: SemanticErrorKind::RecursiveType { ty },
  592|      1|            });
  593|  15.2k|        }
  594|       |
  595|  15.2k|        self.layout_in_progress.insert(ty);
  596|  15.2k|        let result = self.compute_layout_internal(ty);
  597|  15.2k|        self.layout_in_progress.remove(&ty);
  598|  15.2k|        result
  599|  15.2k|    }
  600|       |
  601|  15.2k|    fn compute_layout_internal(&mut self, ty: TypeRef) -> Result<TypeLayout, SemanticError> {
  602|       |        // We clone Kind to release borrow on self
  603|  15.2k|        let type_kind = self.get(ty).kind.clone();
  604|       |
  605|  15.2k|        let layout = match type_kind {
                          ^13.6k
  606|  14.9k|            TypeKind::Builtin(b) => match b {
  607|       |                BuiltinType::Void => {
  608|    791|                    return Err(SemanticError {
  609|    791|                        span: SourceSpan::dummy(),
  610|    791|                        kind: SemanticErrorKind::SizeOfIncompleteType { ty },
  611|    791|                    });
  612|       |                }

  779|    183|    fn compute_record_layout(&mut self, members: &[StructMember], is_union: bool) -> Result<TypeLayout, SemanticError> {
  780|    183|        let mut max_align = 1;
  781|    183|        let mut current_size = 0;
  782|    183|        let mut field_layouts = Vec::with_capacity(members.len());
  783|       |        // For C11 6.7.2.1p18 flexible array check:
  784|       |        // "the last element of a structure with more than one named member may have an incomplete array type"
  785|       |        // But incomplete array types are NOT allowed in unions.
  786|       |        // We will check validity as we iterate.
  787|       |        // Note: The count of members might include anonymous struct/union members which are technically members.
  788|       |
  789|    183|        let member_count = members.len();
  790|       |
  791|    276|        for (i, member) in members.iter().enumerate() {
                                         ^183    ^183   ^183
  792|    276|            let member_ty = member.member_type.ty();
  793|       |
  794|       |            // Special handling for flexible array member (FAM)
  795|       |            // Need to check if it is incomplete array
  796|       |            // We can't use is_complete because that recurses. We check TypeKind directly.
  797|       |            // Bolt ⚡: Optimized to match on reference to avoid cloning ArraySizeType.
  798|    276|            let type_info = self.get(member_ty);
  799|    276|            if let TypeKind::Array { element_type, size } = &type_info.kind {
                                                   ^22           ^22
  800|     22|                if matches!(size, ArraySizeType::Variable(_)) {
                                 ^21
  801|      1|                    return Err(SemanticError {
  802|      1|                        span: member.span,
  803|      1|                        kind: SemanticErrorKind::UnsupportedFeature {
  804|      1|                            feature: "variably modified type (VLA) as struct member".to_string(),
  805|      1|                        },
  806|      1|                    });
  807|     21|                }
  808|       |
  809|     21|                if matches!(size, ArraySizeType::Incomplete) {
                                 ^17
  810|      4|                    let elem_ty = *element_type;
  811|      4|                    drop(type_info);
  812|      4|                    if is_union {
  813|       |                        // Incomplete types not allowed in union (FAM is only for structures)
  814|      1|                        return Err(SemanticError {
  815|      1|                            span: member.span,
  816|      1|                            kind: SemanticErrorKind::UnsupportedFeature {
  817|      1|                                feature: "incomplete/VLA array in union".to_string(),
  818|      1|                            },
  819|      1|                        });
  820|      3|                    }
  821|       |
  822|       |                    // Must be last member
  823|      3|                    if i != member_count - 1 {
  824|      1|                        return Err(SemanticError {
  825|      1|                            span: member.span,
  826|      1|                            kind: SemanticErrorKind::FlexibleArrayNotLast,
  827|      1|                        });
  828|      2|                    }
  829|       |
  830|       |                    // Must have at least one other named member.
  831|       |                    // Or rather, "structure with more than one named member".
  832|       |                    // If this is the only member, it's invalid.
  833|      2|                    if member_count < 2 {
  834|      1|                        return Err(SemanticError {
  835|      1|                            span: member.span,
  836|      1|                            kind: SemanticErrorKind::FlexibleArrayInEmptyStruct,
  837|      1|                        });
  838|      1|                    }
  839|       |
  840|       |                    // If valid FAM:
  841|       |                    // Size of structure is as if FAM was omitted.
  842|       |                    // But we must respect its alignment for the struct's alignment.
  843|       |                    // We need to get the element type to find alignment.
  844|      1|                    let elem_layout = self.ensure_layout(elem_ty)?;
                                                                               ^0
  845|       |
  846|      1|                    max_align = max_align.max(elem_layout.alignment);
  847|       |
  848|       |                    // FAM has size 0 for layout purposes of the struct size,
  849|       |                    // but its offset is where it would start.
  850|       |                    // The standard says: "size of the structure is as if the flexible array member were omitted"
  851|       |                    // This means current_size stays as is (after padding for alignment of FAM? No, omitted means omitted).
  852|       |                    // "except that it may have more trailing padding than the omission would imply"
  853|       |                    // Usually this is interpreted as: sizeof(struct) = max(sizeof(struct_without_fam), offsetof(fam)).
  854|       |                    // Or simply: layout the FAM, but don't increment current_size by its size (which is unknown/0).
  855|       |                    // But we might need to add padding to current_size to reach FAM alignment?
  856|       |                    // "as if the flexible array member were omitted" implies we don't even add padding for it?
  857|       |                    // BUT "except that it may have more trailing padding".
  858|       |                    // Most compilers align the end of the struct to the alignment of the FAM.
  859|       |
  860|       |                    // Let's compute offset.
  861|      1|                    let offset = (current_size + elem_layout.alignment - 1) & !(elem_layout.alignment - 1);
  862|      1|                    field_layouts.push(FieldLayout { offset });
  863|       |
  864|       |                    // We do NOT update current_size with FAM size (which is effectively 0 or variable).
  865|       |                    // But we might update current_size to offset?
  866|       |                    // GCC: sizeof(struct { int x; int y[]; }) == 4.
  867|       |                    //      sizeof(struct { char c; int y[]; }) == 4 (aligned to 4).
  868|       |                    // So we do align current_size.
  869|      1|                    current_size = offset;
  870|       |
  871|      1|                    continue;
  872|     17|                }
  873|    254|            }
  874|    271|            drop(type_info);
  875|       |
  876|    271|            let layout = match self.ensure_layout(member_ty) {
                              ^267
  877|    267|                Ok(l) => l,
  878|      4|                Err(e) => {
  879|      4|                    let new_e = match e.kind {
  880|      2|                        SemanticErrorKind::SizeOfIncompleteType { .. } => SemanticError {
  881|      2|                            span: member.span,
  882|      2|                            kind: SemanticErrorKind::IncompleteType {
  883|      2|                                ty: self.display_type(member_ty),
  884|      2|                            },
  885|      2|                        },
  886|       |                        SemanticErrorKind::SizeOfFunctionType => {
  887|      1|                            if let Some(name) = member.name {
  888|      1|                                SemanticError {
  889|      1|                                    span: member.span,
  890|      1|                                    kind: SemanticErrorKind::MemberHasFunctionType { name },
  891|      1|                                }
  892|       |                            } else {
  893|      0|                                e
  894|       |                            }
  895|       |                        }
  896|      1|                        _ => e,
  897|       |                    };
  898|      4|                    return Err(new_e);
  899|       |                }
  900|       |            };
  901|    267|            let mut member_align = layout.alignment;
  902|    267|            if let Some(req_align) = member.alignment {
                                      ^8
  903|      8|                member_align = member_align.max(req_align as u64);
  904|    259|            }
  905|    267|            max_align = max_align.max(member_align);
  906|       |
  907|    267|            if is_union {
  908|     20|                current_size = current_size.max(layout.size);
  909|     20|                field_layouts.push(FieldLayout { offset: 0 });
  910|    247|            } else {
  911|    247|                // Align current_size to member's alignment to find its offset
  912|    247|                let offset = (current_size + member_align - 1) & !(member_align - 1);
  913|    247|                field_layouts.push(FieldLayout { offset });
  914|    247|                current_size = offset + layout.size;
  915|    247|            }
  916|       |        }
  917|       |
  918|       |        // Final size is padded to the record's max alignment
  919|    175|        let final_size = (current_size + max_align - 1) & !(max_align - 1);
  920|       |
  921|    175|        Ok(TypeLayout {
  922|    175|            size: final_size,
  923|    175|            alignment: max_align,
  924|    175|            kind: LayoutKind::Record {
  925|    175|                fields: Arc::from(field_layouts),
  926|    175|                is_union,
  927|    175|            },
  928|    175|        })
  929|    183|    }

  931|    575|    pub(crate) fn decay(&mut self, qt: QualType, ptr_qualifiers: TypeQualifiers) -> QualType {
  932|    575|        let ty_ref = qt.ty();
  933|    575|        let (element_type, is_array, is_function) = {
  934|    575|            let type_info = self.get(ty_ref);
  935|    575|            match &type_info.kind {
  936|    102|                TypeKind::Array { element_type, .. } => (Some(*element_type), true, false),
  937|    167|                TypeKind::Function { .. } => (None, false, true),
  938|    306|                _ => (None, false, false),
  939|       |            }
  940|       |        };
  941|       |
  942|    575|        if is_array {
  943|       |            // Correct logic: Array of T decays to Pointer to T.
  944|       |            // Qualifiers on Array apply to the element in the resulting pointer type.
  945|    102|            let elem_qt = QualType::new(element_type.unwrap(), qt.qualifiers());
  946|    102|            let ptr = self.pointer_to(elem_qt);
  947|       |            // Apply the extracted pointer qualifiers (e.g. from static/const inside [])
  948|    102|            QualType::new(ptr, ptr_qualifiers)
  949|    473|        } else if is_function {
  950|    167|            let ptr = self.pointer_to(qt);
  951|    167|            QualType::new(ptr, ptr_qualifiers)
  952|       |        } else {
  953|    306|            qt
  954|       |        }
  955|    575|    }
  956|       |
  957|    145|    pub(crate) fn strip_all(&self, qt: QualType) -> QualType {
  958|    145|        QualType::unqualified(qt.ty())
  959|    145|    }

  961|  4.96k|    pub(crate) fn merge_qualifiers(&self, base: QualType, add: TypeQualifiers) -> QualType {
  962|  4.96k|        QualType::new(base.ty(), base.qualifiers() | add)
  963|  4.96k|    }

  965|    203|    pub(crate) fn is_compatible(&self, a: QualType, b: QualType) -> bool {
  966|    203|        if a == b {
  967|    137|            return true;
  968|     66|        }
  969|       |
  970|     66|        if a.qualifiers() != b.qualifiers() {
  971|     20|            return false;
  972|     46|        }
  973|       |
  974|     46|        let ty_a_ref = a.ty();
  975|     46|        let ty_b_ref = b.ty();
  976|       |
  977|     46|        if ty_a_ref == ty_b_ref {
  978|      0|            return true;
  979|     46|        }
  980|       |
  981|     46|        let type_a = self.get(ty_a_ref);
  982|     46|        let type_b = self.get(ty_b_ref);
  983|       |
  984|     46|        match (&type_a.kind, &type_b.kind) {
  985|      6|            (TypeKind::Enum { base_type, .. }, _) => self.is_compatible(QualType::new(*base_type, a.qualifiers()), b),
  986|      0|            (_, TypeKind::Enum { base_type, .. }) => self.is_compatible(a, QualType::new(*base_type, b.qualifiers())),
  987|       |            (
  988|       |                TypeKind::Array {
  989|      2|                    element_type: elem_a,
  990|      2|                    size: size_a,
  991|       |                },
  992|       |                TypeKind::Array {
  993|      2|                    element_type: elem_b,
  994|      2|                    size: size_b,
  995|       |                },
  996|       |            ) => {
  997|      2|                if !self.is_compatible(QualType::unqualified(*elem_a), QualType::unqualified(*elem_b)) {
  998|      0|                    return false;
  999|      2|                }
 1000|      2|                match (size_a, size_b) {
 1001|      1|                    (ArraySizeType::Incomplete, _) => true,
 1002|      0|                    (_, ArraySizeType::Incomplete) => true,
 1003|      1|                    (ArraySizeType::Constant(sa), ArraySizeType::Constant(sb)) => sa == sb,
 1004|      0|                    (ArraySizeType::Star, _) => true,
 1005|      0|                    (_, ArraySizeType::Star) => true,
 1006|      0|                    _ => false,
 1007|       |                }
 1008|       |            }
 1009|       |            (
 1010|       |                TypeKind::Function {
 1011|      0|                    return_type: ret_a,
 1012|      0|                    parameters: params_a,
 1013|      0|                    is_variadic: var_a,
 1014|       |                    ..
 1015|       |                },
 1016|       |                TypeKind::Function {
 1017|      0|                    return_type: ret_b,
 1018|      0|                    parameters: params_b,
 1019|      0|                    is_variadic: var_b,
 1020|       |                    ..
 1021|       |                },
 1022|       |            ) => {
 1023|      0|                if var_a != var_b {
 1024|      0|                    return false;
 1025|      0|                }
 1026|      0|                if !self.is_compatible(QualType::unqualified(*ret_a), QualType::unqualified(*ret_b)) {
 1027|      0|                    return false;
 1028|      0|                }
 1029|      0|                if params_a.len() != params_b.len() {
 1030|      0|                    return false;
 1031|      0|                }
 1032|      0|                for (p_a, p_b) in params_a.iter().zip(params_b.iter()) {
 1033|       |                    // Ignore top-level qualifiers on parameters
 1034|      0|                    let type_a = QualType::unqualified(p_a.param_type.ty());
 1035|      0|                    let type_b = QualType::unqualified(p_b.param_type.ty());
 1036|      0|                    if !self.is_compatible(type_a, type_b) {
 1037|      0|                        return false;
 1038|      0|                    }
 1039|       |                }
 1040|      0|                true
 1041|       |            }
 1042|      8|            (TypeKind::Pointer { pointee: p_a }, TypeKind::Pointer { pointee: p_b }) => self.is_compatible(*p_a, *p_b),
 1043|     30|            _ => false,
 1044|       |        }
 1045|    203|    }
 1046|       |
 1047|     47|    pub(crate) fn composite_type(&mut self, a: QualType, b: QualType) -> Option<QualType> {
 1048|     47|        if a.qualifiers() != b.qualifiers() {
 1049|      1|            return None;
 1050|     46|        }
 1051|       |
 1052|     46|        if a.ty() == b.ty() {
 1053|     36|            return Some(a);
 1054|     10|        }
 1055|       |
 1056|     10|        let type_a = self.get(a.ty());
 1057|     10|        let type_b = self.get(b.ty());
 1058|       |
 1059|     10|        match (&type_a.kind, &type_b.kind) {
 1060|       |            (
 1061|       |                TypeKind::Array {
 1062|      4|                    element_type: elem_a,
 1063|      4|                    size: size_a,
 1064|       |                },
 1065|       |                TypeKind::Array {
 1066|      4|                    element_type: elem_b,
 1067|      4|                    size: size_b,
 1068|       |                },
 1069|       |            ) => {
 1070|      4|                let elem_a = *elem_a;
 1071|      4|                let elem_b = *elem_b;
 1072|      4|                let size_a = size_a.clone();
 1073|      4|                let size_b = size_b.clone();
 1074|       |
 1075|      4|                drop(type_a);
 1076|      4|                drop(type_b);
 1077|       |
 1078|      4|                let composite_elem =
 1079|      4|                    self.composite_type(QualType::unqualified(elem_a), QualType::unqualified(elem_b))?;
                                                                                                                   ^0
 1080|      4|                let composite_size = match (size_a, size_b) {
                                  ^3
 1081|      2|                    (ArraySizeType::Incomplete, s) => s,
 1082|      1|                    (s, ArraySizeType::Incomplete) => s,
 1083|      1|                    (ArraySizeType::Constant(sa), ArraySizeType::Constant(sb)) if sa == sb => {
                                                           ^0                           ^0             ^0
 1084|      0|                        ArraySizeType::Constant(sa)
 1085|       |                    }
 1086|      0|                    (ArraySizeType::Star, s) => s,
 1087|      0|                    (s, ArraySizeType::Star) => s,
 1088|      1|                    _ => return None,
 1089|       |                };

 1162|  1.08k|    pub(crate) fn is_complete(&self, ty: TypeRef) -> bool {
 1163|  1.08k|        if ty.is_inline_pointer() {
 1164|    110|            return true;

 1167|       |            // Array is complete if element is complete

 1171|     67|            return self.is_complete(elem);

 1179|     18|                if let ArraySizeType::Incomplete = size {

 1258|    300|    pub(crate) fn display_type(&self, ty: TypeRef) -> String {
 1259|    300|        if ty.is_inline_pointer() {

 1262|    280|        }

   14|       |/// Type representation (for semantic analysis)
   15|       |/// This is a canonical type, distinct from TypeSpecifier which is a syntax construct.
   16|       |/// Types are stored in a separate Vec<Type> with TypeRef references.
   17|       |/// invariant:
   18|       |/// - layout == None for incomplete types
   19|       |/// - layout is computed according to C abstract machine rules
   20|       |/// - layout may differ from MIR layout

  121|    528|    pub(crate) fn truncate_int(&self, val: i64) -> i64 {
  122|    528|        if !self.is_int() && !self.is_pointer() {
                                           ^4
  123|      3|            return val;
  124|    525|        }
  125|       |
  126|    525|        let width = if let Some(layout) = &self.layout {
  127|    525|            layout.size * 8
  128|       |        } else {
  129|       |            // Fallback for builtin types if layout is not yet computed
  130|      0|            match &self.kind {
  131|      0|                TypeKind::Builtin(b) => match b {
  132|      0|                    BuiltinType::Bool => 1,
  133|      0|                    BuiltinType::Char | BuiltinType::SChar | BuiltinType::UChar => 8,
  134|      0|                    BuiltinType::Short | BuiltinType::UShort => 16,
  135|      0|                    BuiltinType::Int | BuiltinType::UInt | BuiltinType::Signed => 32,
  136|      0|                    _ => 64,
  137|       |                },
  138|      0|                TypeKind::Pointer { .. } => 64,
  139|      0|                TypeKind::Enum { .. } => 32,
  140|      0|                _ => 64,
  141|       |            }
  142|       |        };

  147|       |

  149|    499|        let truncated = (val as u64) & mask;

  156|     15|        }
  157|       |
  158|    497|        truncated as i64

  299|       |
  300|       |/// Opaque reference to a canonical type.
  301|       |/// Internally index + 1 (NonZeroU32 for niche optimization).
  302|       |#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]
  303|       |#[repr(transparent)]
  304|       |pub struct TypeRef(NonZeroU32);
  305|       |
  306|       |impl TypeRef {
  307|       |    // Layout:
  308|       |    // bit  0..=17  (18 bit) → BASE INDEX
  309|       |    // bit 18..=20  (3 bit)  → TYPE CLASS
  310|       |    // bit 21..=22  (2 bit)  → POINTER DEPTH (0..3)
  311|       |    // bit 23..=27  (5 bit)  → ARRAY LEN INLINE (0..31)
  312|       |
  313|       |    const BASE_BITS: u32 = 18;
  314|       |    const CLASS_BITS: u32 = 3;
  315|       |    const PTR_BITS: u32 = 2;
  316|       |    const ARR_BITS: u32 = 5;
  317|       |
  318|       |    const BASE_SHIFT: u32 = 0;
  319|       |    const CLASS_SHIFT: u32 = Self::BASE_BITS;
  320|       |    const PTR_SHIFT: u32 = Self::CLASS_SHIFT + Self::CLASS_BITS;
  321|       |    const ARR_SHIFT: u32 = Self::PTR_SHIFT + Self::PTR_BITS;
  322|       |
  323|       |    const BASE_MASK: u32 = (1 << Self::BASE_BITS) - 1;
  324|       |    const CLASS_MASK: u32 = (1 << Self::CLASS_BITS) - 1;
  325|       |    const PTR_MASK: u32 = (1 << Self::PTR_BITS) - 1;
  326|       |    const ARR_MASK: u32 = (1 << Self::ARR_BITS) - 1;
  327|       |
  328|       |    #[inline]
  329|  20.3k|    pub(crate) fn new(base_index: u32, class: TypeClass, ptr_depth: u8, arr_len: u32) -> Option<Self> {
  330|  20.3k|        if base_index == 0 || base_index > Self::BASE_MASK {
  331|      0|            return None; // Base index must be non-zero and fit in 18 bits
  332|  20.3k|        }
  333|  20.3k|        if ptr_depth as u32 > Self::PTR_MASK {
  334|      1|            return None;
  335|  20.3k|        }
  336|  20.3k|        if arr_len > Self::ARR_MASK {
  337|      1|            return None;
  338|  20.3k|        }
  339|       |
  340|       |        // Validate combinations
  341|  20.3k|        match class {
  342|       |            TypeClass::Builtin => {
  343|  16.8k|                if ptr_depth != 0 || arr_len != 0 {
                                                   ^16.8k
  344|      1|                    return None;
  345|  16.8k|                }
  346|       |            }
  347|       |            TypeClass::Pointer => {
  348|  1.31k|                if arr_len != 0 {
  349|      1|                    return None;
  350|  1.31k|                }
  351|       |                // Pointer depth 0 means "Registry Pointer" (valid)
  352|       |                // Pointer depth 1..3 means "Inline Pointer" (valid)
  353|       |            }
  354|       |            TypeClass::Array => {
  355|       |                // Array len 0 means "Registry Array" (valid)
  356|       |                // Array len 1..31 means "Inline Array" (valid)
  357|    880|                if ptr_depth != 0 {
  358|      1|                    return None;
  359|    879|                }
  360|       |            }
  361|       |            TypeClass::Function | TypeClass::Record | TypeClass::Enum | TypeClass::Typedef | TypeClass::Complex => {
  362|  1.27k|                if ptr_depth != 0 || arr_len != 0 {
  363|      0|                    return None;
  364|  1.27k|                }
  365|       |            }
  366|       |        }
  367|       |
  368|  20.3k|        let mut raw = 0u32;
  369|  20.3k|        raw |= base_index & Self::BASE_MASK;
  370|  20.3k|        raw |= (class as u32) << Self::CLASS_SHIFT;
  371|  20.3k|        raw |= (ptr_depth as u32) << Self::PTR_SHIFT;
  372|  20.3k|        raw |= (arr_len) << Self::ARR_SHIFT;
  373|       |
  374|  20.3k|        NonZeroU32::new(raw).map(TypeRef)
  375|  20.3k|    }
  376|       |
  377|       |    // Unsafe constructor for internal use / tests
  378|       |    #[inline]
  379|  69.7k|    pub(crate) unsafe fn from_raw_unchecked(n: u32) -> Self {
  380|  69.7k|        unsafe { TypeRef(NonZeroU32::new_unchecked(n)) }
  381|  69.7k|    }
  382|       |
  383|       |    /// Create a dummy TypeRef (value 1) for initialization placeholders.
  384|       |    /// This is safe because 1 is a valid NonZeroU32, though the TypeRef might not be valid in the registry yet.
  385|       |    #[inline]
  386|  16.5k|    pub(crate) fn dummy() -> Self {
  387|  16.5k|        unsafe { Self::from_raw_unchecked(1) }
  388|  16.5k|    }
  389|       |
  390|       |    #[inline]
  391|    121|    pub(crate) fn get(self) -> u32 {
  392|    121|        self.raw()
  393|    121|    }
  394|       |
  395|       |    #[inline]
  396|  54.7k|    pub(crate) fn base(self) -> u32 {
  397|  54.7k|        (self.0.get() >> Self::BASE_SHIFT) & Self::BASE_MASK
  398|  54.7k|    }
  399|       |
  400|       |    #[inline]
  401|   120k|    pub(crate) fn class(self) -> TypeClass {
  402|   120k|        TypeClass::from_u32((self.0.get() >> Self::CLASS_SHIFT) & Self::CLASS_MASK)
  403|   120k|    }
  404|       |
  405|       |    #[inline]
  406|  5.64k|    pub(crate) fn pointer_depth(self) -> u8 {
  407|  5.64k|        ((self.0.get() >> Self::PTR_SHIFT) & Self::PTR_MASK) as u8
  408|  5.64k|    }
  409|       |
  410|       |    #[inline]
  411|  6.81k|    pub(crate) fn array_len(self) -> Option<u32> {
  412|  6.81k|        let val = (self.0.get() >> Self::ARR_SHIFT) & Self::ARR_MASK;
  413|  6.81k|        if val == 0 { None } else { Some(val) }
                                    ^2.29k        ^4.51k
  414|  6.81k|    }
  415|       |

  534|       |#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]
  535|       |#[repr(transparent)]

  537|       |
  538|       |impl Default for QualType {
  539|     13|    fn default() -> Self {
  540|       |        // Use raw value 1 (which maps to TypeRef(1)) as default to avoid NonZeroU32(0) UB
  541|     13|        QualType(1)
  542|     13|    }
  543|       |}

  545|       |// bits  0..=27  → TypeRef (28 bit)
  546|       |// bits 28..=31  → qualifiers (4 bit)

  548|       |impl QualType {
  549|       |    const QUAL_BITS: u32 = 4;
  550|       |    const QUAL_SHIFT: u32 = 28;
  551|       |    const TY_MASK: u32 = (1 << Self::QUAL_SHIFT) - 1;
  552|       |
  553|       |    #[inline]
  554|  16.6k|    pub(crate) fn new(ty: TypeRef, quals: TypeQualifiers) -> Self {

  556|  16.6k|        debug_assert!(ty.raw() <= Self::TY_MASK);
  557|       |
  558|  16.6k|        QualType((ty.raw() & Self::TY_MASK) | ((quals.bits() as u32) << Self::QUAL_SHIFT))
  559|  16.6k|    }

  561|       |    #[inline]

  633|       |const _: () = assert!(std::mem::size_of::<QualType>() == 4);

  635|       |/// The kind of type
  636|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default)]
  637|       |pub enum TypeKind {
  638|       |    Builtin(BuiltinType),
  639|       |    Complex {
  640|       |        base_type: TypeRef,
  641|       |    },
  642|       |    Pointer {
  643|       |        pointee: QualType,
  644|       |    },
  645|       |    Array {
  646|       |        element_type: TypeRef,
  647|       |        size: ArraySizeType,
  648|       |    },
  649|       |    Function {
  650|       |        return_type: TypeRef,
  651|       |        parameters: Arc<[FunctionParameter]>,
  652|       |        is_variadic: bool,
  653|       |        is_noreturn: bool,
  654|       |    },
  655|       |    Record {
  656|       |        tag: Option<NameId>,
  657|       |        members: Arc<[StructMember]>,
  658|       |        is_complete: bool,
  659|       |        is_union: bool,
  660|       |    },
  661|       |    Enum {
  662|       |        tag: Option<NameId>,
  663|       |        base_type: TypeRef,
  664|       |        enumerators: Arc<[EnumConstant]>,
  665|       |        is_complete: bool,
  666|       |    },
  667|       |    #[default]
  668|       |    Error,
  669|       |}
  670|       |
  671|       |impl TypeKind {
  672|      1|    pub(crate) fn builtin(&self) -> Option<BuiltinType> {
  673|      1|        match self {
  674|      1|            TypeKind::Builtin(b) => Some(*b),
  675|      0|            _ => None,
  676|       |        }
  677|      1|    }
  678|       |
  679|  18.2k|    pub(crate) fn to_class(&self) -> TypeClass {
  680|  18.2k|        match self {
  681|  16.8k|            TypeKind::Builtin(_) | TypeKind::Error => TypeClass::Builtin,
  682|     32|            TypeKind::Complex { .. } => TypeClass::Complex,
  683|     69|            TypeKind::Pointer { .. } => TypeClass::Pointer,
  684|     76|            TypeKind::Array { .. } => TypeClass::Array,
  685|    913|            TypeKind::Function { .. } => TypeClass::Function,
  686|    299|            TypeKind::Record { .. } => TypeClass::Record,
  687|     30|            TypeKind::Enum { .. } => TypeClass::Enum,
  688|       |        }
  689|  18.2k|    }
  690|       |}
  691|       |
  692|       |impl Display for TypeKind {
  693|     17|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
  694|     17|        match self {
  695|     15|            TypeKind::Builtin(b) => match b {
  696|      0|                BuiltinType::Void => write!(f, "void"),
  697|      0|                BuiltinType::Bool => write!(f, "_Bool"),
  698|      1|                BuiltinType::Char => write!(f, "char"),
  699|      0|                BuiltinType::SChar => write!(f, "signed char"),

   69|       |/// Represents a range in the source file.
   70|       |/// Packed representation (64 bits total):
   71|       |/// - Bits 0-23: Offset (24 bits) - Max 16 MiB

   83|       |impl SourceSpan {

   86|       |    const SOURCE_ID_BITS: u64 = 24;
   87|       |
   88|       |    const OFFSET_MASK: u64 = (1 << Self::OFFSET_BITS) - 1;
   89|       |    const LENGTH_MASK: u64 = (1 << Self::LENGTH_BITS) - 1;
   90|       |    const SOURCE_ID_MASK: u64 = (1 << Self::SOURCE_ID_BITS) - 1;
   91|       |
   92|       |    const LENGTH_SHIFT: u64 = Self::OFFSET_BITS;

   94|       |

   97|       |    const MAX_SOURCE_ID: u32 = Self::SOURCE_ID_MASK as u32;
   98|       |
   99|  15.5k|    pub(crate) fn new(start: SourceLoc, end: SourceLoc) -> Self {

  102|       |            // we cannot represent the span correctly in our packed format.

  105|  15.5k|        }

  109|  15.5k|    }

  111|  38.4k|    pub(crate) fn new_with_length(source_id: SourceId, offset: u32, length: u32) -> Self {

  121|       |

  389|       |        };

  459|       |    /// Get the source text for a given span
  460|       |    /// Since we only support UTF-8, we can assume the bytes are valid UTF-8
  461|       |    #[cfg(test)]
  462|      2|    pub(crate) fn get_source_text(&self, span: SourceSpan) -> &str {
  463|      2|        let buffer = self.get_buffer(span.source_id());
  464|      2|        let start = span.start().offset() as usize;
  465|      2|        let end = span.end().offset() as usize;
  466|       |
  467|      2|        if start <= end && end <= buffer.len() {
  468|      1|            unsafe { std::str::from_utf8_unchecked(&buffer[start..end]) }
  469|       |        } else {
  470|      1|            panic!("Invalid span range");
  471|       |        }
  472|      1|    }

  474|       |    /// Get line and column for a source location
  475|     30|    pub(crate) fn get_line_column(&self, loc: SourceLoc) -> Option<(u32, u32)> {
  476|     30|        let file_info = self.get_file_info(loc.source_id())?;
                          ^29                                            ^1
  477|     29|        let offset = loc.offset();
  478|       |
  479|     29|        let line_starts = &file_info.line_starts;
  480|     29|        if line_starts.is_empty() {
  481|       |            // If line_starts not calculated yet, assume single line starting at 0
  482|      1|            return Some((1, offset + 1));
  483|     28|        }
  484|       |
  485|       |        // Use partition_point which performs a binary search
  486|     84|        let idx = line_starts.partition_point(|&start| start <= offset);
                          ^28   ^28         ^28
  487|       |
  488|     28|        if idx == 0 {
  489|      1|            return Some((0, 1));
  490|     27|        }
  491|       |
  492|       |        // idx is the index of the first element GREATER than offset.
  493|       |        // The line index corresponds to the element immediately preceding usage.
  494|     27|        let line_idx = idx - 1;
  495|     27|        let line_start = line_starts[line_idx];
  496|     27|        let column = offset - line_start;
  497|       |
  498|     27|        Some((line_idx as u32 + 1, column + 1)) // 1-based indexing
  499|     30|    }
  500|       |
  501|       |    /// Get the presumed location (logical line and file) for a source location
  502|      7|    pub(crate) fn get_presumed_location(&self, loc: SourceLoc) -> Option<(u32, u32, Option<&str>)> {
  503|      7|        let file_info = self.get_file_info(loc.source_id())?;
                          ^6                                             ^1
  504|      6|        let (physical_line, column) = self.get_line_column(loc)?;
                                                                             ^0
  505|       |
  506|      6|        let (logical_line, logical_file) = file_info.line_map.presumed_location(physical_line);
  507|       |
  508|       |        // If no logical file specified, use the physical filename
  509|      6|        let filename = logical_file.or_else(|| file_info.path.to_str());
                                                             ^5             ^5
  510|       |
  511|      6|        Some((logical_line, column, filename))
  512|      7|    }
  513|       |}
  514|       |
  515|       |/// ⚡ Bolt: Two-pass optimization to avoid multiple reallocations of line_starts vector.
  516|  1.33k|fn compute_line_starts(buffer: &[u8]) -> Vec<u32> {
  517|       |    // First pass: count newlines to determine required capacity.
  518|   105k|    let newline_count = buffer.iter().filter(|&&b| b == b'\n').count();
                      ^1.33k          ^1.33k ^1.33k ^1.33k                   ^1.33k
  519|  1.33k|    let mut line_starts = Vec::with_capacity(newline_count + 1);
  520|  1.33k|    line_starts.push(0); // First line starts at offset 0
  521|       |
  522|       |    // Second pass: populate line starts.
  523|   105k|    for (i, &byte) in buffer.iter().enumerate() {
                                    ^1.33k ^1.33k ^1.33k
  524|   105k|        if byte == b'\n' {
  525|  4.08k|            line_starts.push((i + 1) as u32);
  526|   101k|        }
  527|       |    }
  528|  1.33k|    line_starts
  529|  1.33k|}

  535|       |    #[test]
  536|      1|    fn test_empty_and_dummy() {
  537|      1|        let span = SourceSpan::empty();
  538|      1|        assert_eq!(span.start(), SourceLoc::builtin());
  539|      1|        assert_eq!(span.end(), SourceLoc::builtin());
  540|      1|        assert_eq!(span.source_id().to_u32(), 1);
  541|       |
  542|      1|        let dummy = SourceSpan::dummy();
  543|      1|        assert_eq!(dummy, span);
  544|       |
  545|      1|        let merged = span.merge(dummy);
  546|      1|        assert_eq!(merged, span);
  547|      1|    }
  548|       |
  549|       |    #[test]
  550|      1|    fn test_builtin() {
  551|      1|        let span = SourceSpan::empty();
  552|      1|        assert!(span.is_source_id_builtin());
  553|       |
  554|      1|        let builtin = SourceLoc::builtin();
  555|      1|        let other = SourceLoc::new(SourceId::new(2), 0);
  556|       |
  557|      1|        let merged = SourceSpan::new(builtin, builtin).merge(SourceSpan::new(other, other));
  558|      1|        assert_eq!(
  559|       |            merged,
  560|      1|            SourceSpan::empty(),
  561|      0|            "Merging spans from different source IDs should return the first span unchanged"
  562|       |        );
  563|      1|    }
